title: Amenor
content:
The Creation of Amenor
A document detailing the creation of Amenor.

    Taldarin no longer needs to fear the oppression of Darkheart. Thus, the time for expansion has come. Amenor will be our gift to you, a grand world of beauty and power. May the lives of Amenor be many and long.
    — The Nine, Creator Deities.

 
Darkheart's Fall
On the 10th of Drakwhite, 73 BA, 4th Era at 15:00 the Marked Ones and the Nine managed to defeat the entity know as Darkheart. Darkheart was the first creature to ever exist and it had long sought vengeance on the Nine for their banishment of it. It had captured their divine forms and tortured them while the Champions of the Nine, the Marked Ones, sought to save them.   The Marked Ones, which include the Artificer Barry; the Warlock/Sorceress Irwena; the Bard Mae; the Sorceress Vaei and the Fighter Voniss, had found the Paragons of the Nine and collected their divine essences within them. With the essences of the Nine, the Marked Ones took a stand against Darkheart and managed to free the divine forms of the Nine.   The Nine could then make Darkheart mortal and the Marked Ones finished it off, saving Taldarin from a future without Creator Deities. Together, the fourteen of them escaped Darkheart's crumbling plane of existence and the Nine declared that, with the threat gone, it was time to expand the world.  
Taldarin, The First World
It had always been the intention of the Nine to make a place for all kinds of lives to thrive. Though Taldarin was beloved by them, it was a project far too small for their scopes. They had intented to make a world that was much larger, much more wondrous but Darkheart's return had halted their creation.   Now that their champions had saved it, the nine Creator Deities continued their previously halted projected. Their first creation, the world of Taldarin would still exist but only be a part of the entire world. As the Nine grew the world beyond the borders of Taldarin and it's people took to the seas to explore the new world, Taldarin would earn it's current title: Taldarin, the First World.   Some people remained on what is now the continent of Taldarin, continuing their established lives there without the fear of Darkheart. Many more took upon themselves the task of exploring the new world, a world that was waiting for it's inhabitants.  
Amenor is Forged
The Nine established themselves in the Elloweth Theater in the multiversal city of Sigil as they started to craft the new world. Nothing prohibited them from fully realizing their dream of crafting a world that could be enjoyed by all. And so, as they began, it took almost a decade of creation before they considered themselves content.   It was in the ninth year that they finished their work. The Nine had expanded the world of Taldarin into the world of Amenor and they watched as people began to settle this new frontier. They smiled as lives started, began anew or continued in the new world.   It was then that a realization hit them. The Nine understood that their work is complete. They have fulfilled their ultimate goal, they have crafted the world of Amenor and they have seen the lives that it began to house. With nothing more to achieve, they had no more reason to remain close to Amenor.  
The Commence
As the Nine realized that they were fulfilled, they made the decision to leave Amenor to house it's many lives. Before they left though, they understood that something needed to control Amenor. The Nine drew their powers from Amenor and they are intristically tied to it, but if they left it would be vulnerable.   As such, before the Nine took their leave, they forged the first deities in order to protect and cultivate Amenor. The Nine combined their essences in all permutations to craft the first deities that would overlook Amenor and it's people.   With the creation of the these gods, the people of Amenor quickly took to worshipping them and the Nine saw that Amenor was self-sustaining. With Amenor created and the Amenorean pantheon born, the Nine took a step back as the first era of Amenor began. This event, the creation of the first deities and the Nine leaving, which began the first era of Amenor, is known as The Commence.
  An artistic rendition of the Nine forging Amenor from the stage of the Elloweth Theater in Sigil.
Date of Setting
Years 0-9 ADD, 0th Era
Related People

    Arbaraen
    Chandryl
    Draktyl
    Dwandrin
    Elfyrean
    Gordfein
    Heyla
    Huwander
    Tyrabane

  The Marked Ones, slayers of Darkheart.

title: General Rules
content:
General Rules
A document to detail general rules that apply whenever we play.
General Rules
Below is a list of general rules that apply to each of our sessions.  

    Magic items are priced according to Samuel's webpage.
    You can only hold an action, not a bonus action. If the condition is triggered, your reaction is also consumed. Holding a spell counts as concentrating on it. If the condition is not triggered by the start of your next turn, the spell is wasted or released (your choice, if it is released you can still cast another leveled spell that turn). Either way, the spell slot is consumed. You can also hold the Dash action, allowing you to move up to double your speed as normal.
    Reviving a creature is not an automatic success. Assuming the soul is still willing, the caster must make an ability check using their spellcasting modifier and beat a DC of 10. (The DC increases if the creature has been revived before).
    Flanking is applied for both you and the enemies you face. Whenever you and an ally stand on opposite sides of an enemy, they are considered flanked and you both get a +2 to hit. A Gargantuan (or larger) creature cannot be flanked.
    You cannot use Deception or Insight on another player.
    You can't cast a spell stealthily. If it requires somatic or verbal components, you need to speak at a certain volume and perform certain movements that can't be masked unless you have the sorcerers Subtle Spell feature.
    Most checks in combat can be made with disadvantage as a bonus action or straight roll as an action.
    If you fall unconscious during combat and are brought back to consciousness in the same combat, you must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution Saving Throw or gain a point of exhaustion.

title: Introduction To Amenor
content:
Introduction to Amenor
A document containing introductory information about the world, Amenor.
Welcome to Amenor
Welcome to Amenor, a land that was first created on the 18th of December, 2024 and first conceived of a year or two before that. The idea of mine is that Amenor is a massive world, large enough to hold multiple campaigns. I plan for the campaigns to be about the size of Wilma's Campaign, Orinor, perhaps a bit shorter or a bit longer. This document will contain some general introductory information about the world of Amenor.  
Creation Myth
It is well known to all people of Amenor how the world was created. In the beginning, before anything else existed, there came to be a creature known as Darkheart. The first creature in existence, it eventually created a world of it's own. Unbeknownst to it however, that place could support life. From the life that was born rose nine creatures that were able to banish Darkheart. These are the nine creator deities who, after their victory, created what came to be known as Taldarin, the First World.   Eventually Darkheart returned from his banishment, causing era-defining chaos in Taldarin. Fortunately however, five heroes that were chosen of the Nine rose to challenge Darkheart and it's quest for vengeance. These heroes, the Marked Ones, fought valiantly and defeated Darkheart, thus allowing the Nine to complete their creation of the world.   They expanded the world of Taldarin, creating new lands and wonders. In these prosperous times, the people of Taldarin grew unbounded and moved to settle these new lands. The world left the four eras before Amenor (BA) and entered into the time after Darkhearts death (ADD) as the world of Amenor was born.  
Time Count
 
Eras
The world of Amenor counts their time in eras, defined by great events. The first four eras happened when only Taldarin existed and their years are marked by BA, meaning "Before Amenor". For example, if one refers to the third year of Taldarin in the fourth era, one would write 3 BA, 4th Era.   After the world was expanded into Amenor, the years are marked by ADD, meaning "After Darkhearts Death" and the eras all have names. For example, if one refers to the third year of Amenor's first era, the Era of Settlement, one would write 3 ADD, 1st Era or 3 ADD, Era of Settlement.  
Years & Months
In Amenor, the years are counted within each era. Until an era-defining event occurs, the years simply increase, one at a time. One year is divided into nine different months, each one named after one of the Nine, see below.  

    Drakwhite, the first month of the year, named after Draktyl.
    Dawnrise, the second month of the year, named after Dwandrin.
    Heysatus, the third month of the year, named after Heyla.
    Chandran, the fourth month of the year, named after Chandryl.
    Arbamand, the fifth month of the year, named after Arbaraen.
    Gordrand, the sixth month of the year, named after Gordfein.
    Huwalain, the seventh month of the year, named after Huwander.
    Elfyrend, the eight month of the year, named after Elfyrean.
    Tyrafrost, the ninth month of the year, named after Tyrabane.

 
Tendays & Days
These months are all 40 days long. Each section of 10 days is called a tenday and it is Amenor's equivalent to a week. The three final days of the tenday are referred to as "tenends" and is Amenor's equivalent to a weekend. How people work during the tenday differs greatly throughout Amenor but to say that everyone is free during the tenends would be incorrect. Each day of the tenday also has a unique name, derived from the names of the creator deities, see below.  

    Arday, the first day of the tenday, named after Arbaraen.
    Drylday, the second day of the tenday, named after Chandryl.
    Drakday, the third day of the tenday, named after Draktyl.
    Dwanday, the fourth day of the tenday, named after Dwandrin.
    Fyreaday, the fifth day of the tenday, named after Elfyrean.
    Feinday, the sixth day of the tenday, named after Gordfein.
    Heyday, the seventh day of the tenday, named after Heyla.
    Anderday, the eight day of the tenday, named after Huwander.
    Tyraday, the ninth day of the tenday, named after Tyrabane.
    Nineday, the tenth day of the tenday, named after the Nine.

Pantheon
In the world of Amenor, there are four categories of deities. These are elaborated upon below. An important note to make is that all of the deities of the Forgotten Realms (such as Sélune, Bahamut, Lathander, e.t.c.) are considered Demigods. They all exist in Amenor with their established stories, rivalries and relationships but they have little to no following, even though they are still very powerful creatures.  

    Creator Deities - The Nine, makers of both Taldarin and Amenor.
    Prime Deities - Powerful gods with a large following.
    Lesser Deities - Less powerful gods with a smaller following.
    Demigods - Powerful creatures with a small to non-existent following.

 
Creator Deities
The creator deities are a unique class of deities. There are only nine of them, that being the Nine that created Taldarin and Amenor. They are immensely powerful and well-respected. Though they are still believed to exist, they have little to no interaction with, or influence on, the world of Amenor and it's people. The power of the Nine is not drawn from followers, but from Amenor directly. This means that despite the fact that no one prays to the Creator Deities, they still retain their power. Among the ranks of the Creator Deities are:  

    Arbaraen, the Goddess of Life & Aasimars.
    Chandryl, the Goddess of Chaos & Changelings.
    Draktyl, the God of Justice & Dragonborns.
    Dwandrin, the God of Creation & Dwarves.
    Elfyrean, the Goddess of Nature & Elves.
    Gordfein, the God of Magic & Gnomes.
    Heyla, the Goddess of Luck & Halflings.
    Huwander, the God of Time & Humans.
    Tyrabane, the God of Death & Tieflings.

 
Prime Deities
These are the most powerful of Amenorean pantheon. Most of them were born of the Creator Deities and they gain their power by the belief of people. That means that they are only considered Prime Deities because so many people of Amenor believe in them. It is important to note that some of them also draw power from the existence of things in Amenor that fall in their domain. For example, Cazerni, the God of War, is always stronger when Amenor is embroiled in war. Among the ranks of the Prime Deities are:  

    Alora - Caretaker, Goddess of Spring, Fertility & Growth.
    Aqua - Wavemother, Goddess of Water & Grief.
    Cazerni - God General, God of War.
    Dar'Ishir - Frostbaron, God of Winter & Cruelty.
    Eladrey - Fall, God of Autumn & Time.
    Firenthra - Unraveling Weaver, Goddess of Souls.
    Fórein - Forgemaster, God of the Forge & Creation.
    Ignis - Father Flame, God of Fire & Passion.
    Irwena - Lady Luck, Goddess of Luck & Fortune.
    Jale'Qar - Weavemaker, God of Magic.
    Kannder - Arbiter, Goddess of Justice & Law.
    Libram - Keeper of Scrolls, God of Knowledge.
    Maar - The Great End, God of Death.
    Mae - The Everchanging, Goddess of Cunning and Chaos.
    Mornal - Divine Fulcrum, Goddess of Balance.
    Qandezar - Lifedrinker, Goddess of Decay & Rot.
    Soyal - Lightfather, God of the Sun & Light.
    Terra - Rockbreaker, God of the Earth & Gems.
    Varileth - Fatestitcher, God of Fate.
    Velyndra & Vortharax - Dawnscale/Duskscale, God of Dragons.
    Ventus - Mother Wind, Goddess of Wind & Change.
    Virá - Lover, Goddess of Summer, Beauty & Love.
    Wynderialdessa - The Cosmic Matriarch, Goddess of the Moon & Stars.
    Ze'Tren - ???, God of Secrets.

 
Lesser Deities
These are the less powerful gods of the world of Amenor. Most of them were still born of the Creator Deities and they too gain their power by the belief of people. That means that they are only considered Lesser Deities so long as people of Amenor believe in them. They are not as powerful as the Prime Deities since they do not have as many followers. It is important to note that some of them also draw power from the existence of things in Amenor that fall in their domain. For example, Cazerni, the God of War, is always stronger when Amenor is embroiled in war. Among the ranks of the Lesser Deities are:  

    Alaric - The Eternal Nightmare, God of Dreams.
    Brilja - Lady Brilliant, Goddess of Constructs & Inventions.
    Cáhlid - Great Shepherd, God of Beasts.
    Dilora - Hearthmother, Goddess of Food.
    Elyzari - Taleweaver, Goddess of Stories.
    Guinebrut - Butcherer, God of Blood & Slaughter.
    Nechlar - The Great Hoarder, God of Greed.
    Obiterra - Doombringer, God of Disasters & Destruction.
    Perreni - Greatmaster, God of Perfection & Skill.
    Presch - Willtaker, God of Tyranny & Domination.
    Puzkera - Plaugebearer, God of Plauges, Poison & Disease.
    Raschal - The Unending, God of Undeath.
    Ronashand - Darkskulker, God of Theft & Shadows.
    Serra - Joybringer, God of Gifts & Music.
    Therion & Liora - Jewelfather, God of Jewelry and Craftsmanship.
    Uynga - Lady of Chains, Goddess of Torture & Pain.
    Velissara - Midnight Mistress, Goddess of Ecstasy & Hedonism.
    Wayleen - Wanderlust, God of Travel.
    Xaner - Life of the Party, God of Debauchery.
    Yllí - Aunt Fey, Goddess of Children & Pranks.
    Zeschla - Webmother, Goddess of Fear, Despair & Spiders.

 
Demigods
Demigods are technically not really gods, they are simply powerful creatures. While Demigods may have accrued a following, they are not ascended to the level of god because the following is not large enough. Demigods are particularly powerful creatures that have transcended the convential means of the world. An example of a Demigod could be a very old and powerful dragon, or a fighter of such prowess that it cannot be compared to mortals creatures.  
World Information
The world of Amenor was created from the existing world of Taldarin. Taldarin now remains as a lone island within the massive world of Amenor and is referred to as one of the regions. There are a total of 11 regions in Amenor, a summary of them can be seen below.  

    Acadia, the largest region in Amenor, a place characterized by grassland.
    Dar'Cortya, a region of snow and ice in the northeast part of the map.
    Elezari, a large jungle region in the southeast part of the map.
    Jyndera, a region of desert and scorching sand in the southern part of the map.
    Neolora, a region characterized by hills and cliffs in the eastern part of the map.
    Oriceré, an archipelago region in the center of the map.
    Taldarin, the first world and the place where all of Amenor stemmed from.
    The Ashlands, a region recently devastated by a volcanic eruption.
    The Crooked Tooth, a small, icy region in the northwestern part of the map.
    The Grayhold, a previously lustrous region now turned into a wasteland of stone.
    Qanya, a small region northeast of Acadia, characterized by marshland.

 The world is round, meaning that you can sail all the way around the world.  
Life & Death in Amenor
Life in Amenor is filled with many strange experiences and every mortal lives their life differently. Despite that, there are some things that happen to all almost all mortals. Naturally, there are exceptions and those who are exempt but the overwhelming majority of people are subject to what is described here.  
Aging
Firstly, mortals age. Different races age at different speeds and live to different ages. All of this is because of the Goddess of Decay and Rot, Qandezar. In year 285 ADD, Era of Longevity she "solved" the problem of immortality in Amenor, which allowed her to slowly drain the life of all living things. She is the reasons that plants wither, food spoils and mortals age. Mortals age and die at different speeds due to her preferences of the lifeblood that she absorbs. For example, she really likes to absorb the life of humans while she dislikes the life of elves. Thus, humans have a shorter lifespan than elves do.  
Death
Secondly, mortals die. As mentioned previously, different races die at different ages but death comes for all mortals in the end. When your time is up, your soul leaves your body via the power of the God of Death, Maar. Maar employs his many reapers to take the soul from the body and bring it before the Goddess of Justice, Kannder. Once brought before her, the ultimate fate of the soul is judged.   With ultimate neutrality and as the final arbiter, Kannder judges all the actions, thoughts and deeds of the souls life before uttering her final decree. There are three different decrees that she can give:  

    Good - The soul is judged to be good and a choice is given to it. If it desires, it can move on from life on Amenor and live eternally in the divine realm of a deity that it worships. The degree of worship is unimportant to Kannder though most likely not to the deity whose realm the soul intends to go to. The other option is that the soul reincarnates into a new body and a new life on Amenor. The soul retains no memories of its previous life.
    Neutral - The soul is judged to be neutral and no choice is given to it. The soul reincarnates into a new body and a new life on Amenor. The soul retains no memories of its previous life.
    Evil - The soul is judged to be evil and no choice is given to it. It is thrown into the clutches of a random evil deity, forced to that deity's divine domain. There they are made into subservient creatures that answer to every whim of their new master. Though the soul retains no memory of its past life, it is not uncommon for the deity to dissect their mind in order to let them remember what they have lost, as a form of mental torture in addition to the physical one.

  No matter what is decreed, as soon as the matter of the souls ultimate fate is settled, Kannder hands it over to the Goddess of Souls, Firenthra. Firenthra takes the soul into her care, looking it over for any potential damage or error. She fixes anything she deems to be wrong, carefully studying the soul before her. Once done, she then carries the soul with her to its ultimate destination, whether it be to a divine realm or back to Amenor.   There are some exceptions to the ultimate fate of souls. For example, most warlocks have had their soul claimed by another entity already and, upon the warlocks death, Maar, Kannder and Firenthra have nothing to do with it. Furthermore, mortals who are devoted to evil deities may also be plucked from Amenor upon their death before Maar intervenes and given a more glorious role than eternal servitude given to most evil mortals.  
Cosmology
The cosmology of Amenor is very similar to that of the Forgotten Realms. The Material Plane with its reflections of the Feywild and the Shadowfell exist in the middle of the Elemental Chaos and the Etheral Plane surrounds that. The Astral Plane then covers the space between these Inner Planes and the Outer Planes. The Outer Planes is a collection of divine realms and Sigil.   There are only two differences between the cosmology of the Forgotten Realms and Amenor. The first is that each of the different planes is a divine realm. This means that there is a god of the Plane of Earth, of the Shadowfell, the Astral Plane and so on.   Secondly, there are many more divine realms in the Outer Planes. With a total of forty-five gods, each with their own realm, there thus exists more divine realms in Amenor than there does in the Forgotten Realms. There are also two exceptions to the fact that each plane has its own god: The Abyss and The Nine Hells. These are the same way that they are in the Forgotten Realms, only that the creatures that control them are Demigods with a lot of infighting.

title: Timeline
content:
Go to World

History of Amenor

The Creation of Amenor

... to 9 ADD

Darkheart has been slain and the Nine set about the expansion of the world. Taldarin, the First World is expanded into Amenor as the Nine continue the creation they always had planned.

The Creation of Amenor

The Creation of the First Admyl

06 of Drakwhite 1 ADD

In the year following Darkhearts defeat, one of the members of the Marked Septet, Barry Longfeet, creates the first Artificially Disciplined Mechanical Yearning Lightbringer (ADMYL). The first model is crude, unsustainable and does not have sentience but it is a major milestone in the creation of this new race.

OTHER CONNECTIONS

Related Species

Admyls

The Creation of Amenor

Irwena's Apotheosis

17 of Drakwhite 8 ADD to 18 of Drakwhite 8 ADD

The Marked Septet engage in combat with the divine mother of Irwena, one of the members of the group. The combat is fought in Amenor, destroying a part of the yet to be finished world. In the end, the tyrannical mother is defeated and the Nine realize that they must step away from Amenor and create a new pantheon. Irwena is offered the position as the Goddess of Luck and her ascendance quickly follows.

CHARACTERS

    Irwena

The Creation of Amenor

Mae's Apotheosis

18 of Drakwhite 8 ADD to 19 of Drakwhite 8 ADD

The Marked Septet engage in combat with the divine mother of Irwena, one of the members of the group. The combat is fought in Amenor, destroying a part of the yet to be finished world. In the end, the tyrannical mother is defeated and the Nine realize that they must step away from Amenor and create a new pantheon. Mae is offered the position as the Goddess of Cunning and Chaos and her ascendance quickly follows.

CHARACTERS

    Mae

The Creation of Amenor

The Commence

20 of Drakwhite 8 ADD to 23 of Gordrand 1 ADD

The Nine finalize their creation of Amenor and perfect it. They then merge their essences through every possible permutation in order to make the Amenorean pantheon. Once done, they open the way to Amenor and let the people of Taldarin flood into its lands. With a content smile, they leave.

CHARACTERS

    Chandryl
    Arbaraen
    Draktyl
    Huwander
    Gordfein
    Elfyrean
    Dwandrin
    Heyla
    Tyrabane

The Era of Settlement

1 ADD to 203 ADD

The first Era of Amenor. The Nine open the way for the people of Taldarin and thousands upon thousands leave the First World to explore these new lands. Over the course of a bit more than a hundred years, a steady stream of immigrants make their way across uncharted waters and towards a new life. They find the lands of Amenor and spread far and wide across, constructing settlements as they go. After another hundred years or so, the situation on Amenor starts to calm down. Barely any more immigrants move from Taldarin and new settlements have been established on Amenor. The time of settling is over.

The Era of Settlement

Taldarin Migration

22 of Drakwhite 2 ADD to 16 of Elfyrend 105 ADD

The way to Amenor is opened by the Nine and people from Taldarin start to make their way towards these new lands, a migration that lasted over a hundred years.

The Era of War

1 ADD to 8 ADD

The third era of Amenor, characterized by war and strife. The divine decision to have the mortals of Amenor age causes a great divide between the people. Some believe it to be a good thing as all life must end eventually, that is what makes life worth living. Others argue that Amenor should be a paradise and one should not have to worry about all the problems that comes with aging, especially death. The heated arguments eventually lead to the taking up of arms and Amenors falls into a seven year-long war. The warring on Amenor causes Cazerni to gain more and more power. Eventually, his thirst for battle is so great that he descends upon Amenor and joins the fight. He battles everyone, no matter their stance on the matter and he slaughters enough to bring the war to a standstill, essentially committing suicide. The mortals of Amenor take stock of the survivors who are far fewer than a mortal-waged war ever could have wrought. They agree to a truce and start to clean up the disaster.

The fifth era of Amenor, characterized by a cleft in the divine. At the beginning of this era the population of Amenor has grown to a number larger than the population of Taldarin ever reached. Throughout the era it would continue to grow but many mortals would fall victims to the plans of the malicious divine. With such a large population, the evil-aligned deities of Amenor had many subjects to enact their plans upon and so they did, while the good-aligned did their best to protect mortals. This conflict between the divine would last for almost a thousand years and leave lasting impacts not only on the mortals of Amenor but on the divine. Eventually the good deities of Amenor would stand fast before the mortals and the evil would take a step back, resorting to more subtle plans in the future.

Hayle then speaks orders into a Sending Stone and Ironbellow has the greatest volcanic eruption Amenor has ever seen. title: Andesoar
content:
Andesoar
A document detailing the village of Andesoar.

    Andesoar is a village on the western caost of Acadia. It sits just at the mouth of a the river which holds the Merdepths. The village is a quaint little place, with the nearby forest giving a fair amount of isolation from the rest of Acadia.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Elf 	63
Human 	51
Gnome 	27
Dwarf 	22
Tiefling 	14
Other 	31
 
Geography
Andesoar is settled just on the western coast of Acadia, with the blue oceans spread out as far as one can see in that direction. To the south and east though, oceans of green grass reach far. This green ocean is obstructed by a large forest of deciduous trees that grows right near the village. To the north of Andesoar flows a wide river, one that flows towards the ocean from the Merdepths.  
Culture
Andesoar's global identity and culture are nothing specifically noteworthy. The large forest and wide river nearby makes it a rather picturesque and calm place. Beyond this apparent quiet and simple life that Andesoar presents though, there have long been rumors that Andesoar has a close relation with the Merdepths. Even though this is double speculation, as it is not even known whether the Merdepths even hold intelligent life, the rumors have attached themselves strongly to Andesoar.  
History
The village of Andesoar was settled in the Era of Growth, when Acadia was growing greatly. Since then, it has been a quiet village that has not had any particularly impactful historical influences.

title: Biviul
content:
Biviul
A document detailing the village of Biviul.

    Biviul is a village on the western coast of Acadia. It is settled just south of a forest, a bit out of the way from Acadia's main road. The village is small and unassuming, without any form of strong identity, just a place to live a simple life in.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	21
Elf 	14
Halfling 	8
Aasimar 	5
Gnome 	5
Dwarf 	3
Other 	9
 
Geography
Biviul is settled just upon the western coast of Acadia and so the ocean stretches out in that latitude from the village. Southeast of the village is the Evergreen Sea, the grassy fields that stretch out akin to the ocean itself. North of Biviul is a deciduous forest that sit at the base of a winding cliff which box Biviul in a little.  
Culture
Biviul is a such a small village that it doesn't have any discernable culture. In reality, it is just a place where a few wooden homes sit and a couple of families, who all know each other, live. Life is simple, quiet and slow as everyone pitches in to help keep the village alive.  
History
Biviul was settled rather recently, in the Era of Peace. It's young age along with its small population means that it has not had any form of notable historical impact, it has simply been a quiet place to live.

title: Caldera
content:
Caldera
A document detailing the village of Caldera.

    Caldera is a small village in the midst of The Crooked Tooth. It lies just north of a great pine forest and just south of a large cliffscape. Approaching the settlement is nearly impossible by boat and quite difficult by foot.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Goliath 	87
Dragonborn 	56
Human 	31
Gnome 	19
Halfling 	13
Other 	14
 
Geography
Caldera is settled in the middle of the main landmass of The Crooked Tooth, northwest of Wehran. With the mainland being very narrow at this point, the ice-cold water is not far off in either direction of the village, which keeps the temperature down.   Winds blow in from across the ice floes and beat straight through the village, making it even colder. The ground is frozen for almost all of the year and there are only a few days each year that the village is not entirely covered in snow. To the north of the village lies a region of cliffs and unexplored, snowy wastes.   To the south of Caldera is a large forest of snow-covered pine trees. The forest is dense and difficult to navigate through. Ever harder to navigate through though is the archipelago to the east of the village where the islands and jutting cliffs kill many ships.  
Culture
The fact that Caldera is covered in snow for almost all of the year makes it an appealing place for those who enjoy winter. A large portion of the inhabitants are silver and white dragonborns, as well as goliaths that fare well in the cold weathers.   With that said though, it is a tough life to lead in a cold place like this. An essetially eternal winter means that not everyone is cut out for life in Caldera, something that is reflected in its low population numbers. Only about 200 people call Caldera their home and very few ever move here.   As such, Caldera is a very close-knit village. Most everyone knows each other and helps each other through the cold winter. Their homes are built with lumber from the nearby forest, with slanted roofs and careful insulation. Smoke rises far above the village at most times of the day as hearths provide much-needed warmth to their occupants.  
History
Caldera was settled year 330 ADD, in the Era of Growth. It was settled by people from Wehran who sought to explore the lands north of their home. They made a new home north of the great forest and in the many years since, the population of Caldera has remained around 200 people.

title: Corum
content:
Corum
A document detailing the village of Corum.

    Corum is a village in southern Acadia. It is settled on the coast, just south of the Gray Smile and the Sunlake. It is a small village dedicated to fishing and providing outsiders with a chance to explore the nearby islands.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	51
Halfling 	36
Elf 	27
Tiefling 	22
Dwarf 	12
Other 	31
 
Geography
The geography near Corum is one of three parts. Firstly, there are the large deciduous forest that grow along the coast on either side of the settlement. Secondly, beyond the grassy plains of the Evergreen Sea sits the Gray Smile, the mountains looming large over the small village. Finally there is the ocean south of the settlement, dotted with many small islands.  
Culture
Corum is a rather small village whose people make their livelihood from fishing, farming and hunting. It is not too uncommon that travelers visit the village for a boat tour around the nearby small islands either, which help Corum's economy. This positive interaction with primarily the rest of Acadia makes Corum a wealthy village for its size and they are very happy for it, just as they are very happy to receive visitors and travelers.  
History
Corum was settled late in the Era of Growth. Since then, it's had a quiet and calm history. It has not really had any larger impact upon the history of Acadia either, though it's docks were occasionally used for warships during The Trade War.

title: Dereli
content:
Dereli
A document detailing the village of Dereli.

    Dereli is a small village in the northern reaches of The Grayhold. It sits at the foot of a large, snowy mountain that provides some cover from the winds that beat across the Frostreach Glacier. The village was established as a last bastion after the events of The Ravaging of The Greenreaches. It holds a lot of history but has little contact with the rest of Amenor.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	17
Goliath 	8
Dwarf 	7
Gnome 	6
Dragonborn 	2
 
Geography
Dereli is settled in the northern reaches of the Frostreach Glacier. It lies many miles from all other forms of civilization, a solitary settlement in frozen reaches. Though the village lies just by the coast it's dock is no more than a mere bridge as none ever sail to or from Dereli.   The cold sea stretches out to the north of the village while the frozen glacier stretches as far as the eye can see in every other direction. Breaking up all this flatness is a small mountain chain with high peaks just east of the village. Dereli lies nestled at its very foot, the rising mountain providing much needed cover from the winds that beat across the glacier.   Dereli is the northernmost settlement in all of Amenor. Snow covers the thatched roofs of the homes every day of the year. The homes sit upon frozen, unmoving ice as snow piles up around them. The few pines that grow upon the slopes of the nearby mountain are the source of the eternal plumes of smoke that rise from the hearths.  
Culture
The culture of Dereli is a complicated one. All who live here are descendants of those who survived Puzkera's attack on The Greenreaches and this loss has formed the settlement. It was built in a very mournful time and that grief has been passed down through each generation.   The defining feature of Dereli's culture is without a doubt isolation. The 40 people that live here are like one big family but to them, they are the only 40 people in the world who exist. Their life is inexorably tied to Dereli through their ancestry, as well as their living family members.   Then again, even if one wanted to leave they would step out from the cover of the mountain and see the Frostreach Glacier stretch endlessly towards the horizon. Even though they know there is another world out there, a daunting journey dissuades any attempts or desire to reconnect with it.  
History
As mentioned previously, Dereli was settled by the few survivors of the Ravaging of The Greenreaches. They fled away from the plague up north where the disease had nothing to cling onto and quickly froze to death. Their determined minds and ingenuity kept them from doing the same as they established the village in the Era of Divine Division. Ever since then, very few descendants have ever returned to the rest of Amenor's life.

title: Elley
content:
Elley
A document detailing the village of Elley.

    Elley is a village in the southeastern reaches of Acadia. It is settled upon the eastern shore of the Sunlake, a couple of miles northeast of the Gray Smile. It is a small fishing village often used a port for those who cross the Sunlake.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	76
Elf 	44
Halfling 	39
Dwarf 	38
Gnome 	25
Other 	27
 
Geography
The geography near Elley is dominated by the Sunlake. It stretches out to the west of the village, a blue field as far as the eye can see. Just south of village is a large forest that sits between it and the Gray Smile. Elley sits a few miles inland from the elevated part of the cliff that curls around the Sunlake but at the base of that cliff, the Evergreen Sea stretches out.  
Culture
Elley is a small village with no particularly strong identity. Most people spend their days fishing upon the lake, farming the fields or hunting in the nearby forest. Some also work the docks as it is not too uncommon to pass through Elley when traveling to Sunhaven from the east. Beyond that though, Elley is a simple and quiet village for a simple and quiet life, one that is not too far from the larger cities in Acadia either.  
History
Elley was settled in the Era of Growth, during the time that Acadia's popuation was greatly increasing. Since then, the village has not grown any considerable amount as most people choose to live in the other settlements that sit upon the shore of the Sunlake.

title: Feir
content:
Feir
A document detailing the village of Feir.

    Feir is a village in the western reaches of Jyndera. It is settled just at the eastern foot of the mountain range that sits upon the western coast of the region. It is partially built into the mountains, allowing the village to avoid most of the suns scorching heat.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Gnome 	43
Dwarf 	39
Dragonborn 	22
Tiefling 	17
Human 	16
Other 	34
 
Geography
The immediate geography near Feir is defined by two things: desert and mountain. To the east, the sandy expanse of Wynderialdessa's Wastes stretch out, a sea of schorched ground as far as the eye can see. To the west of the village, rises a large mountain chain. These mountains used to be of gray stone but are now covered in much ash from the eruption of Ironbellow, ash that occasionally blows down to the village.  
Culture
The culture of Feir is defined in large parts around their settling within the mountains. If one does not appreciate stonework, mining or carefully studying stone, then one would most likely not be comfortable in Feir. The homes are rough and sharp, carved out of stone and all homes do not even have furniture of wood or comfortable beds to due the distance between the village and other settlements and the difficulty of crossing the desert.  
History
Feir was settled rather recently, in the Era of Peace. The village was established by dreamers who hope to find wealth within the mountains. As of now, they have had no such luck and the village struggles on to keep everything afloat and everyone fed.
Founding Date
13th of Huwalain, 310 ADD, Era of Peace
Type
Village
Population
171
Location under
Jyndera
Related Historical Events

    3126 ADD
    Settling of Feir

title: GréChen
content:
Gréchen
A document detailing the village of Gréchen.

    Gréchen is a lone village in the region of Elezari. It lies on the second largest island in the region, settled in the middle of a large plateau. The village is the only part of the island that is explored, leaving the rest of it a mystery.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Elf 	51
Human 	47
Dragonborn 	43
Tiefling 	22
Dwarf 	21
Other 	31
 
Geography
Gréchen sits upon a large X-shaped plateau in the middle of the island, many miles from the coast. At the base of the plateau, the jungle grows dense and rich while it is slightly more sparse upon the plateau itself, though only slightly. It is this jungle that spreads out on every side of the village, leaving it in the midst of a green ocean.  
Culture
Gréchen's location is very much intentional and strongly defines the culture of the village. Though it is perhaps not as isolated as Kalte or Dereli, it is mostly certainly quite difficult to reach. Gréchen has little to no contact with the outside world and updates on events of the village rarely reach the mainland.   The village was built in the middle of the jungle, in the middle of an island for a reason. People in the village simply wish to live a quiet, undisturbed life and they do not wish to do so in a place where they might freeze to death. This isolation has made the village very tight-knit but travelers who manage to make their way to Gréchen are often welcomed with warm greetings though, seeing as they must have a good reason for making the trip.  
History
Gréchen was initially settled by people who wished to get away from the chaos of civilization during the Era of Divine Division . Since then, the village has been self-sustaining and has not really grown beyond the births and deaths of the founders bloodlines.
Founding Date
22nd of Huwalain, 384 ADD, Era of Divine Division
Type
Village
Population
215
Location under
Elezari
Related Historical Events

    2326 ADD
    Settling of Gréchen

title: Hendas
content:
Hendas
A document detailing the village of Hendas.

    Hendas is a village in the northwestern reaches of Neolora. It lies on an island just south of the border to Dar'Cortya. It is a calm and quiet place, a village for those who like to live in peaceful isolation.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Elf 	92
Halfling 	76
Human 	54
Dwarf 	30
Tiefling 	26
Other 	19
 
Geography
Hendas is settled on a solitary island, many miles away from the mainland. As such, the most constant feature of the nearby geography is the surrounding ocean. Its waves crash upon the rocky shores of the island and its waters provide life to the greenery upon the island.   Though the shores are rocky and cliffy, the rest of the island is a flat plain. Farmlands dot this sea of grass and dirt, centered around the village. Many deciduous trees can be found on the island, often growing near the coast. A larger forest can be found just near the village.   Hendas itself is settled upon the cliffs of the coast. The homes are meticulously crafted upon the narrow and slanted stone cliffs of the shore. Cobblestone steps lead at odd angles through the homes of the village, avoiding the worst of the crashing waves and eroding rock.  
Culture
The culture of Hendas revolves around a simple way of life. The people here desire to be free of the noise, pollution and people of the larger cities and instead lead a smaller life in peace. Hendas is a farming village the encapsulates this idea perfectly.   Everyone in the village knows each other and helps each other out. The days are spent toiling at the farm or fishing in the sea, while the nights are spent on the front porch, enjoying the sunset and a hard day of simple work. This culture makes Hendas a bit wary of outsiders, at least before they are able to ascertain whether or not they share the same ideas and ideals.  
History
The village of Hendas was settled in the Era of Settlement. It was one of the first settlements in the region of Neolora and since then it has pretty much stagnated in its growth, much to the approval of its people.
Founding Date
24th of Huwalain, 12 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Village
Population
297
Location under
Neolora
Related Historical Events

    20 ADD
    Settling of Hendas

title: Innhold
content:
Innhold
A document detailing the village of Innhold.

    Innhold is a village in the middle of Acadia. It lies right in the middle of the Evergreen Sea, grassy fields stretching out in every direction from it. The village is known for its many and large inns that house the many travelers that pass through the settlement.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	93
Halfling 	68
Dwarf 	33
Elf 	24
Gnome 	17
Other 	26
 
Geography
The geography near Innhold is dominated by one thing: grassy fields. There are a few small forest that grow upon this green sea but mostly it is just plains of windswept grass. When standing upon the balconies of the inn rooms here, one can see nothing but fields of grass in every direction, lost in a sea of green.  
Culture
The culture of Innhold is heavily dependent on the travelers that pass through the village. Since Innhold is settled nearly in the middle of Acadia, most of Acadia's larger roads pass through the settlement and thus a great number of people pass through. These travelers can rest their weary heads and tired feet in some inns that are uncharacteristically nice for a village. The people of the village work either at one of the many inns, or in the farms that produce food for both the village and all the inns.  
History
The settlement of Innhold was settled in the Era of Growth. Acadia's population was growing quickly and it was becoming apparent that most people traveled through the midst of the Evergreen Sea. It was then that Innhold was settled, as a way to capitalize on the many travelers that passed through.
Founding Date
21st of Arbamand, 1058 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Village
Population
261
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    1560 ADD
    Settling of Innhold

title: Iveré
content:
Iveré
A document detailing the village of Iveré.

    Iveré is a village in the southern reaches of Elezari's mainland. It lies just at the end of a snaking river that diverges from the Slithering Serpent, many miles northeast of the village. The river makes travel to Iveré possible but approaching it through the jungle is nearly impossible, trekking through many many miles of dense jungle.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Elf 	47
Human 	34
Halfling 	23
Aasimar 	16
Tiefling 	11
Dwarf 	9
Other 	16
 
Geography
The geopgraphy of Iveré is very uniform. The river that reaches the village is very important for the self-sustaining of Iveré but by the time it has flowed all the way to the village, it has thinned considerably. It is a thin river that just barely allows a ship to turn around.   Beyond this river, Iveré is surrounded on all sides by the jungles of Elezari. The massive trees rise far above the wooden homes and the dense vegetation that grows makes Iveré a sort of secluded glade in the verdant growth of the jungle.  
Culture
The culture in Iveré is one that promotes a simple life. The people of the village live away from most of the troubles of the world, those problems tend to not concern the lives of the people in Iveré. In as small of a village as Iveré is, people pitch in in many different ways.   They cultivate the land, farm, hunt, build water mills and the like in order to keep life moving smoothly in the village. The surrounding jungle is rich with both flora and fauna, letting life in the village thrive. Life and the culture in Iveré is centered around simplicity, about just enjoying life and living.  
History
Iveré was settled in the final years of the Era of Growth. A small group of settlers established the village looking for a nice, quiet life. Since then, them and their descendants have gotten exactly that through Iveré's calm history.

title: Juva
content:
Juva
A document detailing the village of Juva.

    Juva is a village in the northern parts of Qanya. It is settled at the foot of a mountain chain that rises along the coast. It is a small mining village that leads a simple life, bisected by mountain and swamp.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Dwarf 	51
Human 	48
Gnome 	34
Elf 	27
Halfling 	19
Other 	26
 
Geography
Juva's immediate geography is dominated by two things: mountain and swamp. The village sits partially upon the dark green bog ground, with its vegetation and willow trees somehwat occluding the settlement. The other half of the village rests upon the foot of a mountain, the gray stone gray stone being a stark contrast to the dark green ground.  
Culture
The culture in Juva is one of community. The homes are built closely together and many of them are even built wall in wall with their neighbors, with a door between. Being such a small village, the names and faces of the inhabitants is one of the first things children in Juva learn. This closeness in the village means that everyone immediately recognizes when a traveler passes through, though it does not make them unwelcome by any means.  
History
Juva was settled in the Era of Growth. Since then, the village has extracted a few riches from within the mountain but nothing incredibly eye-catching. The village has simply done their own thing, providing Qanya with as much gems and stone to trade as possible.
Founding Date
20th of Heysatus, 591 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Village
Population
205
Location under
Qanya
Related Historical Events

    1093 ADD
    Settling of Juva

title: Kjera
content:
Kjera
A document detailing the village of Kjera.

    Kjera is a village in the eastern reaches of Neolora. It is settled in The Howling Cliffs, just upon the edge of a steep cliff. Though the village only lies a number of miles east of Hértaux, the rivers and cliffs make approach difficult.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	35
Elf 	29
Halfling 	26
Gnome 	21
Tiefling 	17
Half-Orc 	13
Other 	16
 
Geography
The area around Kjera is called The Howling Cliffs, which gives quite an accurate description of the surrounding geography. The plains are windstruck and bare, with many patches of dirt and rock piercing through the grass. Not many trees grow upon the rocky plains and no larger forests can be found nearby.   Unsurprisingly, the most defining feature of The Howling Cliffs are the cliffs themselves. These cliffs form steep canyons and passages that make traversal through the area very difficult if one does not follow the paths. This has made Kjera a rather isolate village despite its relative proximity to other settlements.  
Culture
The people who first settled Kjera were people who were searching for their fortune. They were hoping to find valuable rocks and gems in the exposed cliffs. As is evident with the low population, this never turned out to be quite the fortunate venture.   Nevertheless, the culture of excavators and rock miners lives on in Kjera. It is by no means as focused on and obsessed with rocks as Kech'Nal is, but many would consider it a close second. Nowadays, the areas around Kjera provide a natural workout, both in working the hardened earth and in scaling the cliffs which has mingled with the stonecunning to provide a unique identity.  
History
Kjera was settled by people who sought their fortunes outside of existing settlements. There was an influx of these types of people during the Era of Longevity, when people did not age. As such, Kjera was settled in the early years of that era. Since then, it has slowly changed as a settlement, but barely grown.

title: Lendech
content:
Lendech
A document detailing the village of Lendech.

    Lendech is a village in the western part of Neolora. It is settled just upon the cliffs of a great ravine, overlooking the descent. It is the westernmost settlement on Neolora's mainland and its distance to the other settlements isolates it a bit.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	103
Elf 	76
Dwarf 	55
Halfling 	41
Gnome 	38
Other 	27
 
Geography
Lendech is settled just upon the edge of a steep cliff. It overlooks the ravine below, giving the village a nice view of the surrounding landscape. Approaching the village from the east is very difficult due to this dangerous terrain. Meanwhile, approach from the west is not entirely easy either.   To the west lies a big forest of deciduous trees that shield the village from the winds blowing in from the sea. The density of the forest also makes the approach to the village quite difficult. As such, the geography surrounding Lendech isolates it a bit from the rest of Neolora.  
Culture
The culture of Lendech is mirrored in its geography. Difficult to reach, but if one makes the effort, they can be rewarded with a beautiful village and stunning views. This describes the people of Lendech quite well. They enjoy the slight solitude that comes from Lendechs geographical location, but also do not desire to be too far away from the rest of the world.  
History
Lendech was settled late in the Era of Settlement. Since its inception, it has slowly grown to a comfortable size and remained such since. Its remote nature has made its history calm and quiet.

title: Makan & Wokan
content:
Makan & Wokan
A document detailing the villages of Makan & Wokan.

    Makan and Wokan are two villages situated in the middle of Rimefrost Glacier, in the region of Dar'Cortya. They sit at either end of a large gorge in the glacier and though they are separated by distance, their culture is very similar. The biggest difference lies in the fact that there only lives men in Makan and only women in Wokan.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Goliath 	78
Human 	66
Gnome 	57
Dwarf 	42
Dragonborn 	33
Other 	8
 
Geography
Makan and Wokan have very much the same geography that surrounds the villages. Settled at either end of a large gorge in the glacier, the villages are nearcly identical. The cliffs of the gorge shield the villages from most of the wind and harsh weather that blows across the Rimefrost Glacier.   There is not much variation of the geography that surrounds the villages. Whether homes are built into the sides of the icy cliffs or constructed just upon the glacier itself, nothing but ice still surrounds them. The cliffs that rise far above the homes are of ice and climbing the stone steps up from the gorge leads to the glacier, a landscape of ice in every direction as far as the eye can see.  
Culture
Makan and Wokan have quite a strange culture as compared to the rest of Amenor. Though they are technically two villages, the inhabitants don't really consider it so. The only reason that two villages exist is because they have split their population in half, making the men live in Makan and the women live in Wokan.   The reason for this strange decision harkons back to the history of Kalte, and how it's fast growth lead to many difficulties. The solution of separating the men and women were thus proposed as many left Kalte to live better lives elsewhere.   The two villages cooperate as if their were one entity. Goods, food and other necessities are traded freely between the two villages. In order to limit interaction between the men and women however, this is often done by a single person from each village, meeting halfway with their sled dogs. The continued existence of the villages is ensured by a yearly celebration, where the two villages come together. They spend a few days together, celebrating life and ensuring it continues. The children borne from this meeting live with their mothers for the first year of their life before the boys of them are escorted to their village and the girls stay.   The largest difference between Makan and Wokan arises perhaps not in their culture but in their way of living. The villages have the same values and interests, seeing life on the glacier as a great peaceful life. However, the way of life in a village with men and a village with women certainly differs.   In Makan, every man is trained to be a hunter, to stalk the glacier and track prey. Makan's proximity to the ocean also makes it such that some are trained as fishermen. This focus on activities outside the village leads to it being quite bare. Homes are crudely constructed and rarely decorated and the entire village is gruff in it's demeanor.   In Wokan however, there are far less hunters and there are no fishers. Instead, many women perform duties that can be done in the village. Things like making warm clothes from pelts and furs or improving homes. This means that Wokan is not nearly as bare of a village as Makan is. Homes are made with care and often decorated, both interior and exterior.  
History
The villages of Makan and Wokan were settled after people left Kalte due to its growth and internal differences. This was during the Era of Growth, as people spread to the furthest reaches of Amenor. Since their founding, Makan and Wokan have had rather quiet histories as people are content with the harsh but peaceful lives they live here.

title: Mern
content:
Mern
A document detailing the village of Mern.

    Mern is a village in the middle region of The Grayhold. It is situated on the eastern side of The Winding Peaks, on a stretch of land that is surrounded by rivers. Though a stone bridge leads further north into the Frostreach Glacier and the settlement of Dereli, Mern is generally considered to be the northern marker of civilization in The Grayhold.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	32
Gnome 	27
Tiefling 	19
Halfling 	11
Other 	10
 
Geography
Mern is situated some miles east of The Winding Peaks. The rivers that run to the north and south of the village are large enough to make its location essentially a peninsula. Like most other settlement in The Grayhold, there is little more than gray stone that surrounds Mern.   The large rivers break up this sea of gray with a moving blue, while the occasional pine tree adds a splash of green but one would be hard pressed to find any more colours in Mern.  
Culture
The culture in Mern is characterized by a calm friendliness. The small amount of people living here means that no great details shine through in the architecture. Instead the wooden homes that sit here mostly look the same, whether the residents are halfling or human.   Life in Mern is calm and quiet. There are no surprises, no shouting markets or loud town criers. It is a village that gets by with fishing but does not aim for much more. Mern is a place for people who seek peace and quiet, those who desire uneventful days and friendly neighbors. Of course, it helps if you like fish as well.  
History
The settlement of Mern was established when The Grayhold used to be known as The Greenreaches, during the Era of Settlement. It used to be a simple fishing and farming village as far north as people wanted to settle in The Greenreaches. Since it was resettled it is now nothing more than a small, quiet village sustaining itself on fishing from the nearby rivers.
Founding Date
8th of Gordrand, 125 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Village
Population
99 people
Location under
The Grayhold
Related Historical Events

    133 ADD
    Settling of Mern

title: Nor'Da
content:
Nor'Da
A document detailing the village of Nor'Da.

    Nor'Da is a village in the northeastern reaches of Oriceré. The village is settled on a small island in the Peridot North, with an island of mountains between it and the capital. Nor'Da is a small fishing village built in the forest of its island.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	62
Elf 	51
Dwarf 	49
Tiefling 	46
Gnome 	41
Halfling 	36
Other 	64
 
Geography
Settled on a small island in Oriceré's archipelago, there is no doubt about the fact that the most defining characteristic of the geography near Nor'Da are the surrounding waters. Beyond this sea though, Nor'Da's island is dominated by a dense forest of deciduous trees. The village itself is settled in a man-made clearing on the coast, with trees shading the village all around.  
Culture
The culture of Nor'Da was rather unremarkable in its early years. It was a small village settled by people who disliked the bigger settlements and it got by on fishing its waters and hunting its forest. Though its livelihood has not changed since then, its global identity certainly has. Over time, Nor'Da has become known as a beautiful, calm and serene place to move if you are expecting kids. Life is slow and simple, letting you focus on raising your kids who will have a great time playing in the forest with the many other kids that are raised in the village.  
History
Nor'Da was settled in the Era of Growth, by settlers who wished to get away from the hustle and bustle of the larger settlements. Since then, the village has grown considerably after it has gotten the reputation it has today. Even with that though, it is a simple and calm place that has little impact on Oriceré's history.

title: Teh'Fal
content:
Teh'Fal
A document detailing the village of Teh'Fal.

    Teh'Fal is a village in the southern part of Neolora. It is settled on the southern coast of Xelley Island, one of three settlements on the island. Though settled on the coast and equipped with a reasonably large docks, the other two settlements see most of the ships that pass into Neolora. 


 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	141
Elf 	118
Halfling 	67
Dwarf 	63
Gnome 	41
Other 	53

 
Geography
Xelley Island is bisected by a large cliff, making the eastern part higher above the sea than the western. As Teh'Fal lies upon the eastern side of the island, this makes the nearby coastline quite rocky. However, the land that stretches north from the settlement is a flat, grassy plain.
  This make the land north of the village quite farmable, although it remains a difficulty to get water in from the cliffy coast. To the west is a large forest of deciduous tree that reach all the way from the village to the cliffs edge.
 
Culture
Like the rest of Xelley Island, the population of Teh'Fal longs for independence from the rest of Neolora. This historical struggle has made them headstrong, patient and firm in their beliefs. Furthermore, many of them consider themselves atheists, uncaring for the divine.
 
History
Teh'Fal was settled in the Era of Settlement, around the same time as the other settlements on Xelley Island. At its inception, it quickly grew to around 400 people and has since then enjoyed a quiet stagnancy. Despite this quiet, the islands struggle for independence has given it a shaky history.

title: Vel'Nar
content:
Vel'Nar
A document detailing the village of Vel'Nar.

    Vel'Nar is a village in the southwestern reaches of Acadia. It is settled on the coast, with forests growing on either side of the village. The village is very new, established in the current era as a place to treat soldiers wounded from The Ruiner's War.

 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	73
Elf 	51
Dwarf 	48
Halfling 	33
Gnome 	24
Other 	37
 
Geography
The geography near Vel'Nar is rather varied for a settlement in Acadia. The ocean spreads out southeast of the settlement while deciduous forests grow on either side of the village. To the north of the village, the stretches of the Evergreen Sea spread out. The rapid and recent creation of Vel'Nar along with its main purpose also means that the nearby landscape is dominated by haphazard, quickly put togetether wooden buildings.  
Culture
The culture in Vel'Nar is born entirely from the purpose of the village's establishment. The village exists for the purpose of taking care of wounded soldiers and mages from the Ruiner's War. As such, the village is filled with medics, healers, priests and such, as well as the wounded from the war. This makes the atmosphere in the village rather sullen, an air mixed with hope of the survivors and the defeat of the dying.  
History
Vel'Nar was settled recently, just a year into the Ruiner's War. This makes the history of the village rather short, only its birth as a place of survival from the war, the notable event.

title: Wastillé
content:
Wastillé
A document detailing the village of Wastillé.

    Wastillé is a village in the northwesternmost parts of Acadia. It is settled just at the foot of a large mountain chain that sits on the Acadia's coast. Much of Wastillé's livelihood is gained from mining the nearby mountains.


 
Demographics
Species 	Amount
Human 	122
Dwarf 	118
Elf 	109
Halfling 	67
Gnome 	53
Other 	26

 
Geography
Wastillé is settled just at the foot of a large mountain chain, which dominates most of the nearby geography. The gray mountains rise far above the simple homes of the village, blocking the any quick access to the coast. Beyond the rising mountains, green grass fields stretch out as far as the eye can see south of the settlement. The occasional trees stand upon this green sea, but it is mostly flat.
 
Culture
The culture of Wastillé centers much around the mining operations it performs. People tend to either work in the mines, work to accomodate the miners or work to provide food for the village and its miners. Wastillé is settled a bit out of the way and sees very little travelers come through. The opinion on this is divided through the people of the settlement, most of the miners seem to be perfectly content with the little corner of the world that they are carving out. The other inhabitants however, would perhaps not mind some more, and different, business.
 
History
Wastillé was settled sometime into the Era of Growth, It was part of the multiple settlements that popped up in Acadia during that era, when the region was continuing its quick growth from past eras. Since then, Wastillé has had little impact on the history of Acadia nor has anything remarkable happened in the village itself. A few men and women have gone to join a war now and in the past. Beyond that though, Wastillé has remained calm.
Founding Date
35th of Chandran, 533 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Village
Population
495
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    1035 ADD
    Settling of Wastillé

title: Aldemar
content:
Aldemar
A document detailing the town of Aldemar.

    Aldemar is the northernmost town in Qanya. It is settled on the coast of a small lake borne of a river that flows in from the ocean. Journeying to the settlement is not the easiest as one has to tredge through dense forests or pass over the river via the Gray Crossing.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	25%
Elf 	23%
Gnome 	21%
Tiefling 	14%
Half-Orc 	7%
Other 	10%
 
Geography
Aldemar is settled on the coast of a lake, its murky, vegetated waters stretching out south from the settlement. To the east grows a dense forest of willow trees, making travel to and from the town difficult in that direction. To the east though, the swamp is open and plain but to reach the settlement from there, one has to pass through the dangerous Gray Crossing.  
Culture
The culture in Aldemar is mostly borne from the difficulty that exists in reaching the settlement. Since not many travel Aldemar, as there is not much reason to either, the town has had to become mostly self-sufficient. With the nearby waters and forest, they get by quite well on their own and have become rather proud of that exact fact. As such, the inhabitants of Aldemar are rather prideful about the settlement and its history, a fact they happily show the occasional traveler or discuss among themselves.  
History
The settlement of Aldemar was settled rather early in the Era of Growth. Since then, the town has msotly kept to themselves and have had little influence on Qanya's history, instead just leading their own life in the region.

title: Breakwind
content:
Breakwind
A document detailing the town of Breakwind.

    Breakwind is a town in the southern reaches of The Grayhold. It is a coastal city, settled on the point of The Grayhold that is the closest to the middle of Amenor. Settled out on a peninsula, the town is surrounded by more blue than gray, an uncommon sight in The Grayhold.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	21%
Dwarf 	20%
Gnome 	17%
Elf 	15%
Halfling 	13%
Other 	14%
 
Geography
Breakwind is settled in the southern reaches of The Grayhold, being the settlement on the continent that is closest to the rest of Amenor. It’s placed upon the edge of a peninsula, making it one of the few settlements in The Grayhold that is surrounded by more blue than gray.   The surrounding land of the peninsula has little in way of breaking the stretches of gray though. The occasional pine trees dot the land with some green but beyond that the land is flat. An ocean of gray stretching out to the north and an actual ocean in all other directions.  
Culture
Breakwind’s proximity to the rest of Amenor, along with its predominant human population has led to it having no strong, discernable identity. It is a mix of cultural ideas and beliefs, coming from the mixed population or the frequent ship travel in and out of the city.   With that said though, it is not unlike the other settlements in The Grayhold. A bit sleepy and quiet with a relatively small population. Not much happens in the town, whose inhabitants have a fondness for stone and fish. The biggest difference is perhaps the amount of people that have different hobbies, other than stone and fish, due to the settlement's proximity to the rest of Amenor.  
History
Breakwind was initially settled in the Era of Settlement but was abandoned after The Ravaging of The Greenreaches. Since the ravaging, the city has been slowly, very slowly, growing back towards the size it used to hold. The difference between The Green reaches and The Grayhold is perhaps examplified best in the difference of growth for Breakwind. It used to be a pristine location people wanted to settle down in. Now, it is much more gray and gloomy.

title: Celiera
content:
Celiera
A document detailing the town of Celiera.

    Celiera is a town in the northern reaches of Neolora. It is one of the three settlements settled around the Triloch, the smallest of the three. Its cooperation with the other two settlements around the lakes has made it the biggest town in all of Amenor.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	23%
Human 	18%
Dwarf 	17%
Halfling 	16%
Aasimar 	14%
Other 	12%
 
Geography
The geography around Celiera has many different aspects. The Triloch is the most obvious of them, its clear waters stretching out south of the settlement. Just north of the town, along the banks of one of three arms that snake away from the Triloch is a forest of oak. On the other side of this river is a large region of hills and uneven terrain. They do not rise as high as the mountains southeast of the town however.  
Culture
The culture in Celiera is one that is shared between all the three settlements around the Triloch. These three places have been built around the lake and it has been instrumental in their growth, both for natural resources and trade. As a result of this fortune, many of the citizens in these three settlements are appreciative and respectful of the surrounding nature. People cultivate gardens and grow vegetables even within the stone walls. In general, the people who live around the Triloch are considered grateful, nice and respectable people that know to appreciate not only themselves and others, but also the world around them.  
History
Celiera was settled a long time ago, around the same time as the other two settlements of the Triloch, in the Era of Settlement. Since then, the town has grown considerably due to the natural bounty and cooperation of the Triloch settlements. Celiera kept out of The Mirrored War and its complications, managing to avoid the historical suffering of southern Neolora.
Founding Date
37th of Elfyrend, 30 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Town
Population
~6300
Location under
Neolora

title: Desteré
content:
Desteré
A document detailing the town of Desteré.

    Desteré is a town in the middle of the Dar'Cortya region of Amenor. It is situated upon the largest island in the lake Ishir's Depths and is a town that lies just at the very edge of winter. Being situated on an island and having access to the oceans of Amenor, has made it a shipping town. 

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Tiefling 	26%
Elf 	20%
Human 	14%
Halfling 	11%
Goliath 	8%
Dragonborn 	8%
Other 	13%
 
Geography
The geography around Desteré comes in three variations: snow, tundra or water. The settlement lies just south of where the most of the snow remains throughout the year, meaning that the pine forest in the northern parts of the island is almost always covered in snow.   Where there is not snow or pine on the island, there is gray stone. The cold ground of the tundra leaves little ability for farming or working the land. Surrounding the island are the cold, dark waters of the lake. During the colder parts of the year, when the water has yet to freeze, it takes on a dark gray hue that very closely matches the color of the tundra stone.  
Culture
The culture in Desteré is based largely upon its large shipping operations. The import and export of goods that travel through the town does a lot for introducing many cultures, views and ideas into the town. As such, Desteré is a rather mixed city that sees a lot of different traditions.   Above all though, is the tiefling influence in the city. Of all the settlements in Amenor, Desteré is the one with the largest percentage of its population as tieflings. This phenomenon is most likely a snowballing effect that comes from the history of its settling.   Nevertheless, the amount of tieflings in the town makes it a significant influence on the culture. Traditions and beliefs that are central to many tieflings are celebrated in Desteré and it is, in many circles, considered a haven for tieflings.  
History
The town of Desteré was first settled in the later stages of the Era of Growth. It would likely have been settled earlier if not for Shipcutter Estuary. For a long time, it was regarded basically as suicide to try to navigate a ship through this narrow mouth. It was first done by a particularly courageous tiefling captain however, and in memory of his achievement, he founded Desteré. Since then, the town grew into a significant place for tieflings as it slowly expanded through shipping enterprises over the years.

title: Echtar
content:
Echtar
A document detailing the town of Echtar.

    Echtar is a town in the middle of Jyndera. It is settled in the middle of the desert that is Wynderialdessa's Wastes, with sand in every direction. The town is rather old but has essentially stagnated as it cannot support any more inhabitants due to the harsh conditions.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Tiefling 	31%
Dragonborn 	27%
Halfling 	21%
Gnome 	17%
Dwarf 	2%
Other 	2%
 
Geography
The geography arond Echtar is incredibly simple to describe and imagine: sand. The town is settled in the middle of Wynderialdessa's Wastes and there is nothing but sand, desert and dunes in every direction. The ground is flat and the sun beams down relentlessly upon the settlement.  
Culture
The culture of Echtar is formed from the rough conditions that are imposed upon life there. One does not live in Echtar if they are weak-willed or desire an easy life. Food, water and other basic necessities have to be fought for, or journeys far through the desert have to be made.   The harsh life and great heat that is prominent in Echtar has made it a common place for creatures with natural resistance to heat to live. There are many dragonborns with red, brass or gold scales as well as tieflings that make their home in Echtar, as they are able to withstand the heat better than other species. The rest of the population is mostly smaller folk as their homes simply require less resources to build, resources that are difficult to get to Echtar.  
History
Echtar was settled in the Era of Longevity by a number of tiefling settlers that wanted to get away from the more hectic city life. Since then, the town has grown into a rather small population compared to other town in Amenor but is not quite able to grow any further due to the limited resources out in the middle of th desert.
Founding Date
22nd of Tyrafrost, 143 ADD, Era of Longevity
Type
Town
Population
~2200
Location under
Jyndera
Related Historical Events

    353 ADD
    Settling of Echtar

title: Faeiere
content:
Faeiere
A document detailing the town of Faeiere.

    Faeiere is a town in the southern reaches of Elezari. It lies just at the mouth of a river that splits from the Slithering Serpent and flows out into the ocean. As such, it is a town that most people pass when sailing through Elezari.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	24%
Human 	20%
Dragonborn 	16%
Gnome 	13%
Tiefling 	13%
Other 	14%
 
Geography
Faeiere lies just at the mouth of a splitting river and the waters flow around and encapsulate the town on the northern side. On the southern side, the dense jungles and massive trees spread out towards the Lifespring Mountains. Though the nearby terrain is simply jungle or river, there is a lot of beauty in it.   Small rivers have been dug to flow through Faeiere and the nearby jungle plants have been well cultivated, resulting in the geography being a beautiful, natural sanctuary. Exotic plants and flowers grow in abundance in the town, giving it an otherwordly beauty.  
Culture
The culture of Faeiere is one focused on appearances. The people of the town have a fascination with the natural beauty of the surrounding jungle. In fact, the name Faeiere is derived from the Fey, as a nod to the enchanting beauty of the Fey that Faeiere is attempting to replicate.   Homes are built onto the trunks of the massive jungle trees, making the city quite vertical as well. The stairs that curl around the bark are decorated with pink and purple flowers and colorful golden lanterns hang from hand-made branches while waterfalls splash into the rivers below.   It is such otherworldy beauty that is the cornerstone of Faeiere's culture. It's people deal cultivate strange plants and wondrous flowers, decorating the town and the nearby jungle and rivers with the result of their labor. More than almost any other place on the Material Plane does Faeiere resemble the strange beauty of the Feywild, but without the hidden danger.  
History
Faeiere was settled only a few years after Dragon's End, when settlers followed the Slithering Serpent downstream, looking for more places to settle. Since then Faeiere has grown and formed its identity as a beautiful town over the course of many years.

title: Foleyn
content:
Foleyn
A document detailing the town of Foleyn.

    Foleyn is a town in the region of Elezari, one of two settlements on Crescent Island. Foleyn is a small and unremarkable town that gets by on trade as well as what the nearby waters and jungle provide.
