Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	43%
Dwarf 	21%
Halfling 	19%
Elf 	10%
Gnome 	3%
Other 	4%
 
Geography
Foleyn is settled upon the northern part of Crescent Island, just southeast of the northern part of the mountain chain. A wide river flows inland from the ocean, passing Foleyn on the northern side. To the east is a wide strait that separates the island from the mainland,   South of Foleyn, the dense jungle stretches out. The heavy foliage along with the uneven terrain makes traversing south of the settlement a challenge for most and the area between the two settlements remains largely unexplored, even to this day.  
Culture
Foleyn is a rather unremarkable, small town. It is not so small that every citizen knows every other, nor is it large enough that businesses and people get lost in the chaos. The town sees a fair amount of trade, both with ships sailing through the strait as well as with the other settlement on Crescent Island with which Foleyn retains a strong relationship.   Foleyn is best described by the elvish word "lagom", meaning just the right amount. There is nothing particularly impressive or outstanding about the town or its people, but it is also not insufficient by any means. It is simply a calm and quiet place where one live without worry.  
History
Foleyn was settled late in the Era of Growth. Since then, the settlement has seen a slow and steady growth with no notable historical events.
Founding Date
28th of Tyrafrost, 1366 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Town
Population
~5700
Location under
Elezari
Related Historical Events

    1868 ADD
    Settling of Foleyn

title: Gruun
content:
Gruun
A document detailing the town of Gruun.

    Gruun is a town in the southwestern reaches of Dar'Cortya. It lies on a peninsula just east of Rimewoods, where a stone bridge connects it to the mainland. The town is rather unremarkable but holds a bit of a rivalry towards Whitepass.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	31%
Halfling 	24%
Dragonborn 	16%
Dwarf 	15%
Goliath 	9%
Other 	7%
 
Geography
Gruun is settled on a peninsula to the east of Rimewoods and the west of Ishir's Depths. The narrow piece of land stretches southward, forming the waters around it into the shape of a U. To the southwest, a long stone bridge connects the settlement to the mainland, making approach far faster than traveling through the whole peninsula.   Though the large forest of Rimewoods sits only a few miles to the west of Gruun, the lake cuts off the many trees. As such, only small patches of pine forests surround the city. The surrounding geography is rather flat and easily traversable as there are many miles to the closest mountains.  
Culture
The culture of Gruun is easy to forget, as it lacks any distinct qualities. The town is mostly defined as a group of people living off the game of the Rimewoods and nearby lakes. More than anything else, it is seen as a thriving community of surviors in the tundra, ones that lack strong identities beyond their own lives.   If something can be said of the culture of Gruun, it should be credited to the rather large halfling population. The fact that 24% of the population are halflings are reflected in the homes and architecture of the town. Amidst the normal sized homes are often many smaller halfling homes, built between the larger homes.   Perhaps the most notable thing about Gruun is one that is centered around another town in Dar'Cortya, Whitepass. Gruun was established many years before Whitepass but, despite the colder temperature and harsher environment, Whitepass has grown larger than Gruun. This had led to a feeling of inadequacy in some of Gruun's population, establishing a bit of rivalry against Whitepass.  
History
Gruun is one of the oldest settlements in Dar'Cortya, settled a few hundred years into the Era of Growth. Since then it has had a slow and steady growth as people foraged more and more food.
Founding Date
15th of Huwalain, 556 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Town
Population
~2600
Location under
Dar'Cortya
Related Historical Events

    1058 ADD
    Settling of Gruun

title: HéRtaux
content:
Hértaux
A document detailing the town of Hértaux.

    Hértaux is a town in the northern reaches of Neolora, along the winding river of Zeschla's Embrace. It is a daughter settlement to Ilértaux, an extension of that city.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	25%
Elf 	21%
Dwarf 	14%
Halfling 	13%
Tiefling 	11%
Aasimar 	10%
Other 	6%
 
Geography
Hértaux is settled, just like its mother settlement, at a dividing point of Zeschla's Embrace. Traveling south along the river, one would first encounter Ilértaux and then at the next point of the rivers splitting, one would encounter Hértaux.   The geography nearby is dominated by large plains of grass and dirt, broken by the surrounding wide rivers. They flow to the east and west of the settlement while the plains stretch to the south. A forest of oak tress grows along the bank of the eastern river, being a bit smaller than the forest south of Ilértaux.  
Culture
Given that the rivers of Zeschla's Embrace are one of the largest shipping lanes in Amenor, a great deal of people and traditions pass through Hértaux. As such, the influence on the culture of the settlement is many-fold and is reflected not only in the architecture of Hértaux, but also in its people.   To speak about Hértaux entirely as its own settlement is slightly false however. It is an offshoot of Ilértaux, a daughter settlement built to manage the quickly growing population. Much of Hértaux's identity is inherited from its mother, evident in both its people and its rule. Though they are self-governed, Hértaux's leadership still answer to that of Ilértaux's.  
History
Hértaux was settled relatively recently, at least in comparison to most other cities in Neolora. During the Era of Growth, Ilértaux was growing at an unsustainable rate and this was the cause of Hértaux's birth. Since then it has followed in the footsteps of its mother settlement, with a quiet and calm history.
Founding Date
26th of Huwalain, 849 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Town
Population
~4200
Location under
Neolora
Related Historical Events

    1351 ADD
    Settling of Hértaux

title: Ildros
content:
Ildros
A document detailing the town of Ildros.

    Ildros is a town in the western reaches of Qanya. It is settled on the northern coast of Mistmire, which has contributed to the towns reputation. The town is well known for its spiritual nature and its connection to supernatural forces.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Tiefling 	22%
Elf 	19%
Aasimar 	16%
Human 	14%
Halfling 	11%
Other 	18%
 
Geography
The nearby geography near Ildros is rather varied. To the northwest rises a few, low mountains, just north of a dense forest of willow trees. Such a forest grows near the settlement as well, along its eastern edge. The most notable nearby geography though is certainly Mistmire. This fog-blanketed lake lies south of the settlement, its waters quietly washing upon the shore.  
Culture
The culture of Ildros has been heavily influenced by Mistmire. This lake is always covered in a thick mist and its waters move even when the wind is calm. This strange lake caused a lot of the early settlers of Ildros to take up practices in occultism, mysticism and spiritual connections. This reputation grew and more and more people came to the town for this, whether it was to experience the spiritual town first-hand or practice their own craft. Today Ildros is the place to be for anyone who is interested in spirits, mystical, occult or otherwordly creatures.  
History
Ildros was settled in the early years of the Era of Longevity. In its first few years of existence, when people did not die of old age, life in the town was slow and simple. As the Era of Longevity ended however, and death was reintroduced as a natural concept, Ildros's shaping into a spiritual town began. Their interaction with and study of Mistmire spawned an obsession with the supernatural for many in the town and, from that, the town has only grown.
Founding Date
25th of Tyrafrost, 5 ADD, Era of Longevity
Type
Town
Population
~6200
Location under
Qanya
Related Historical Events

    215 ADD
    Settling of Ildros

title: Jelore
content:
Jelore
A document detailing the town of Jelore.

    Jelore is a town in the eastern reaches of Acadia, near the Greenshade Descent. It is settled in the midst of a hilly region of the Evergreen Sea. This placement of the settlement makes it somewhat difficult to approach, but is also incorporated in the architecture of the town.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Halfling 	31%
Dwarf 	29%
Gnome 	26%
Human 	4%
Elf 	3%
Other 	7%
 
Geography
The immediate geography around Jelore is rather different than most other settlements in Acadia. There are many miles to the closest body of water and many small forests that dot the surrounding landscape. Most notably, the nearby terrain is certainly not flat. Hills rise and lower near the town, creating a very uneven geography near the settlement.  
Culture
Jelore is a rather unique town, mostly due to the place it was settled. The rising and falling hills that the town is in are used by the majority of homes as well. Homes are built into the hills, a small garden on top of the hill around the chimney that rises from the home beneath.   These homes are often built in smaller sizes due to the amount of headroom available beneath a hill. As such, it naturally came to be that most of the population that lives in Jelore are shorter folk. The few "regular-sized" people that live here have normal homes amidst the hills but Jelore's global identity is certainly based upon its large population of short folk and their homes.  
History
Jelore was settled in the Era of Settlement. Even upon its inception, people started to build homes into the hills of the landscape and establish the short culture. Since then, the town has retained this identity and not grown considerably either.

title: Kalte
content:
Kalte
A document detailing the town of Kalte.

    Kalte is a town in the most northeastern part of Amenor's mainland, in the region of Dar'Cortya. It sits nestled into a valley between the winding Frostwall Mountains, just at the edge of the Rimefrost Glacier. It is a remote town, populated by what most people would consider as barbarians. 

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Goliath 	25%
Dragonborn 	20%
Dwarf 	18%
Human 	14%
Gnome 	12%
Other 	11%
 
Geography
Kalte is settled as far northeast as is possible on Amenor's mainland. It lies in a valley that is naturally formed by the curving of the northern Frostwall Mountains, shielding it from the harsh winds that blow across the glacier. The town is built partially upon the ice of the glacier and partially upon the stone of the mountainside.   The nearby mountains provide some much needed change in the nearby geography, breaking up the endless flatness of the glacier. The occasional pine tree grows upon the mountainside but even then, the area surrounding Kalte is mostly a barren, frigid wasteland, eternally covered in snow.  
Culture
The town of Kalte would be considered uncultured by most. It certainly has its own identity and culture, formed from their remarkable resilience and ability to survive the freezing lands. However, most people disregard it and instead consider them ruthless savages.   Some of these ideas are born simply from the necessary cruelty one needs to display to survive in the frozen north, but it is generally true that the people of Kalte are unwelcoming. They consider most of the Rimefrost Glacier and Frostwall Mountains to be under their ownership. As a result, they are often rude and barbaric towards outsider, almost attacking on sight.  
History
Kalte was the first settlement that was formed in Dar'Cortya, founded in the Era of War. It was initially formed by people who fled from the all-encompassing war of the era, getting as far away as they could. Since then, it has slowly grown into an isolate community of people that consider the north their own.   In the later stages of the Era of Growth, the people of Kalte became too many for the town itself to sustain. The limited resources available could not feed all the growing people. This problem led to internal strife and conflicts, which eventually resulted in many people leaving Kalte behind them and forming the villages of Makan & Wokan.
Founding Date
26th of Arbamand, 3 ADD, Era of War
Type
Town
Population
~1700
Location under
Dar'Cortya
Related Historical Events

    498 ADD
    Settling of Kalte

title: Kech'Nal
content:
Kech'Nal
A document detailing the town of Kech'Nal.

    Kech'Nal is a town in the southeastern reaches of The Grayhold. It is situated upon an island with two long stone bridges connecting it to the mainland. The city has a strong dwarven influence and it is built almost entirely by the gray stone that is so common in The Grayhold.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Dwarf 	38%
Human 	23%
Gnome 	18%
Tiefling 	11%
Other 	10%
 
Geography
Kech'Nal is settled on an island in the southeastern reaches of The Grayhold. It is surrounded by the mainland on all sides except to the southeast where some small islands float. Water flows from the Deeploch out into the open sea on either side of Kech'Nal, making it apt for fishing.   The town is settled close to the middle of the island while a dockyard is established at the very southeastern edge of the island, an hours journey away. The town sits at the narrowest point of the island, its walls reaching from coast to coast.   Three long stone bridges connect Kech'Nal to the rest of The Grayhold, two to the southwest and one to the north. The geography around Kech'Nal is endlessly gray, broken only by the dark waters that surround the island.  
Culture
The settlement of Kech'Nal has a strong dwarvish influence, mostly due to the large population of dwarves that live here. The buildings are, without exception, made of gray stone and built squarely. Decorations, while intricate, are sharp and colorless giving the city the distinct gray color theme it is known for.   Though the town has a large population of shorter species, there are enough taller species that everything is built large. This causes the dwarves and gnomes of the city to appear a little out of place to those unfamiliar with Kech'Nal. That is, at least, until they start talking about how their ancestors built this town with their own two hands.   The dwarven culture runs deep in Kechn'Nal, infecting everyone there. To live in Kech'Nal is to have a passion about stonework and masonry, or to be a fish out of water. Not only are the building made from stone but a great number of stone statues dot the city streets, the work of intrepid stonecrafters.  
History
Kech'Nal was intially settled long ago, in the Era of Settlement. Back then it looked very different to what it does now. It was not until after the settlement was abandoned and then reclaimed that the fascination with stonework began.
Founding Date
19th of Heysatus, 103 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Town
Population
~3500 people
Location under
The Grayhold
Related Historical Events

    111 ADD
    Settling of Kech'Nal

title: Lordona
content:
Lordona
A document detailing the town of Lordona.

    Lordona is a town in the middle reaches of Acadia. It lies in the middle of the Evergreen Sea, settled on the western edge of the lake that lies just south of the Gravewoods. The town is well known for its many herbalists and alchemical proficiency.


 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	20%
Gnome 	19%
Elf 	17%
Halfling 	16%
Dwarf 	14%
Other 	14%

 
Geography
Lordona is settled close to the middle of Acadia and thus sits in the midst of the Evergreen Sea. The grassy green fields stretch out to the south and west of the settlement, while a large lake lies to the east. Lordona is settled just at the eastern shore of this lake and just south of the Gravewoods and its accompanying mountain chain. A couple of miles southwest of the town lies the large forest of the Wolfwood, the trees of which rise from the green plains.
 
Culture
The culture in Lordona is largely dependent on the towns alchemical prowess. Lordona used to be a rather regular town, where the population was spread out between farmers, fishers, hunters and other normal occupations. However, when the Gravewoods withered, Lordona discovered an opportunity where the neighboring Soizie only saw problems.
  The desecration of the Gravewoods did destroy the ground and turned the area into a dangerous territory filled with undead. However, some of the more brave members of the population found that many unique flowers, mushrooms and other ingredients now grow upon the once hallowed ground. With this realization, Lordona quickly adapted and became known for its high-quality potions and poisons. Many took up jobs as hebalists, while the most cunning and brave venture into the dangerous Gravewoods from time to time.
 
History
Lordona was settled in the late years of the Era of Longevity. As mentioned, the town had a rather quiet history with nothing particularly remarkable happening until the desecration of the Gravewoods. After that, they found a strong identity as herbalists and potionmakers.

title: Monloe
content:
Monloe
A document detailing the town of Monloe.

    Monloe is a town in the western reaches of Acadia. It is settled in the midst of a forest, at the shore of a lake with mountains rising on the other side. This varied terrain makes Monloe a very beautiful and serene place, which is what it is known for.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	24%
Gnome 	21%
Human 	19%
Halfling 	13%
Tiefling 	9%
Other 	14%
 
Geography
The geography surrounding Monloe is its most defining characteristic and strongest attribute. Monloe is settled in a small opening on the southern side of forest of deciduous trees. It sits just upon the shores of a beautiful lake with crystal clear water. The lake itself is formed from the mountains that rise to the southern side of it so that when one stands one the shore, it is just about possible to see the cloud-topped mountains one the other side.  
Culture
The culture of Monloe is centered around its nearby geography. People who live in Monloe are usually of two types: ones who appreciate beauty, calm and quiet. These people come from all walks of life to simply appreciate what Monloe has to offer. The other type are those consider themselves entwined with the natural forces of the world. Monloe is in many ways a town settled in the midst of a natural sanctuary and thus there are many druids who make their home here.  
History
Monloe was settled in the early years of the Era of Growth. Since then, Monloe has remained a natural sanctuary in many ways. Its population cares mostly about themselves and keeping the natural order around their own settlement. They have outright denounced Acadia's war efforts in the past as well as now and tend to just keep to themselves.
Founding Date
30th of Huwalain, 52 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Town
Population
~6400
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    554 ADD
    Settling of Monloe

title: Navalja
content:
Navalja
A document detailing the town of Navalja.

    Navalja is a town in the eastern reaches of Xelley Island. It lies upon the northeastern coast, making it the settlement on the island that is closest to the rest of Neolora. Like the rest of the settlements on Xelley Island, it has had an eventful history.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	25%
Elf 	23%
Dwarf 	20%
Gnome 	14%
Halfling 	11%
Other 	7%
 
Geography
Navalja lies along the strait that divides Xelley Island from Neolora's mainland. The waters are well traversed and and well explored, giving much bounty to the town. The coast that the settlement lies upon is a cliffy, rocky shore that makes approaching the settlement via ship somewhat treacherous.   To the south of the town, a large area of hills stretch across the grassy plains that make up the island. The hilly terrain is bare and not much vegetation grows upon them. However, passing through the hills annd heading further south leads one to larger forests.  
Culture
The culture of Navalja is shared across the three settlements upon Xelley Island. Their shared history has formed a close connection between the settlements, despite their distances. Furthermore, the rift between them and the mainland settlements has only grown in time.   Most of the people of Navalja are headstrong, independent and self-sufficient people that pride themselves on these qualities. While they may refuse help one too many times when they require it, their ambition should not be discounted. Finally, due to the history of the region, many people in Navalja as well as the rest of Xelley Island declare themselves to be atheists.  
History
Navalja was settled in the midst of the Era of Settlement. The town grew quickly with its geographical advantages but was bounded by the amount of nearby farmable land. For multiple eras, the town enjoys a nice and calm history.   Tensions between Xelley Island and Neolora's mainland grow however and in the Era of Divine Division, they bubble over. Southern Neolora is divided during The Divine War, half of them still believing in the deities while the others say to abandon them, leading to The Mirrored War. During this time, Xelley Island attempts to claim independence from the rest of Neolora, under the guidance of Xelley Lightforged. The attempt fails, and Xelley Island remains a part of Neolora to this day.
Founding Date
19th of Gordrand, 67 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Town
Population
~5000
Location under
Neolora
Related Historical Events

    75 ADD
    Settling of Navalja

title: Oena
content:
Oena
A document detailing the town of Oena.

    Oena is a town in the eastern reaches of Qanya. It is settled upon the eastern coast of the region and serves as a port and trading town that often represents the entire region. The town sits upon a small peninsula away from the mainland.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	31%
Dwarf 	27%
Halfling 	16%
Elf 	12%
Gnome 	10%
Other 	4%
 
Geography
Oena is settled upon a small peninsula that stretches out from the eastern coast of Qanya. The peninsula is small enough to essentially only hold the town itself and little else. Though a few willow trees grow upon the peninsula, it is mostly just the town and its docks. To the west of the settlement though, upon the mainland, grows a larger forest of willow trees, making Oeana surrounded by both blue waters and a green forest.  
Culture
The culture of Oena is mostly defined by the amount of ships that pass through the town. It is first and foremost a port town and most wares that Qanya export tend to leave the region via the docks at Oena. The town also lies rather close to the center of Amenor, making it a rather easy stop for most trading ships that sail Amenor's seas. This amount of seafaring traffic has defined the town and a large amount of the inhabitants work at or in some relation to the docks.  
History
The town of Oena was settled a while into the Era of Growth. It was settled with the intention of serving as a port town, a way for Qanya to get their wares out into the rest of Amenor more easily. Since its settling, the town has served exactly that purpose but has remained a rather modest size.

title: Penchla
content:
Penchla
A document detailing the town of Penchla.

    Penchla is a town in the easternmost reaches of Neolora. It is a harbor town, situated on the coast just south of The Howling Cliffs. Despite laying far to the east, the coastal access has made Penchla a staple in shipping.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	25%
Dwarf 	22%
Halfling 	16%
Gnome 	13%
Human 	7%
Dragonborn 	6%
Other 	11%
 
Geography
Penchla lies just on the coast, northeast of The Metal Mountains and south of The Howling Cliffs. Though the town lies not in The Howling Cliffs, the surrounding geography is still rather hilly and cliffy. The town lies upon a plateau, where a large forest grows around the edge of the cliff.   To the northwest of Penchla, land stretches out far from the settlement. To the southeast however, the wide ocean stretches as far as the eye can see. Lying just on the coast to Amenors largest ocean has defined the settlement and its growth.  
Culture
The culture of Penchla is mainly influenced by two things. Firstly, with coastal access the town has a lot of interaction with other settlements in Amenor and is thus influenced by the passing cultures of different places.   Secondly, the most important influence on the culture of Penchla is borne from its namesake. The name comes from how tightly they hold their money, how they pinch their pennies. These penny-pinching, stingy people that live in Penchla greatly influence the culture with their minimal spending of gold.  
History
Penchla was settled in the Era of Settlement, along with many other of Neolora's settlements. Since then it has slowly grown to its current size and has accrued a good deal of wealth among its populace.

title: Port Imodal
content:
Port Imodal
A document detailing the town of Port Imodal.

    Port Imodal is a town in southern Acadia. It lies on the southern coast of the region and is the eastmost settlement of the region as well. As is evident by the name, the town is highly-traficked port where many ships pass through.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	23%
Elf 	20%
TIefling 	17%
Dwarf 	14%
Halfling 	11%
Other 	15%
 
Geography
The geography near Port Imodal is mostly dominated by the ocean. The town is settled on a wide peninsula, with the ocean in every direction except to the north. Growing along the coast is a forest that rises upon the grassy plains of the Evergreen Seas but mostly it is the uninterrupted ocean that has defined Port Imodal and its history.  
Culture
Port Imodal's culture is a port town. It is heavily dependent on and and centered around the many ships that pass through the settlements. Most ships that leave Acadia do so through Chandé and often pass by Port Imodal for resupplying and delivering goods. In essence, Port Imodal's location makes it one of the most heavily traficked ports in Amenor.   This has had a very clear impact on the culture. The amount of inns and taverns in the town are many more than in most other settlements of this size. The docks are large and filled with life as ships moor or sail away, people who peddle wares or run market stands. Compared to the docks, the rest of the town is sleepy and quiet.  
History
Port Imodal was settled late in the Era of Settlement. It was settled by people who realized its advantageous position towards the rest of Amenor and since its inception, it has served the function of port town just as was intended. During The Trade War, Port Imodal saw a lot of naval warfare upon its shores but beyond that, it has simply remained a place where a lot of trading passes through.

title: Reylee
content:
Reylee
A document detailing the town of Reylee.

    Reylee is a town in the western reaches of Oriceré. It is settled upon a small island in the Jade West, in the shadow of some mountains that rise on an island to the south. Its own island is small and dominated by a large forest.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	23%
Elf 	20%
Dwarf 	19%
Halfling 	16%
Gnome 	14%
Other 	6%
 
Geography
The most defining geographical characteristic is certainly the waters that surround the small island, as is common for the settlements in Oriceré. Beyond that though, Reylee is settled on an island that is dominated by a large forest of deciduous trees. This forest stands upon most of the island except for the northern shore, where the town lies.  
Culture
The culture in Reylee is a mix between farmers, fishers, hunters and merchants. The farmers make their livelihood upon the fertile grass along the northern shore, while the fishermen have plenty of spots to catch fish and more. As they sit in their rowboats, they often see the large trading and cargo ships that go between Cel'Ki and western Amenor. These ships often take a quick stop in Reylee for restocking and a proper nights sleep, allowing the merchants of the town to conduct their business. Finally, the forth group of people in Reylee are the ones who make their livelihood from hunting beasts and other critters in the forest on the island. Together, these four groups make up Reylee, a town of mutual respect, no matter your job or background.  
History
Reylee was settled in the Era of Growth, a few years before Nor'Da. Reylee was settled as an expansion of Cel'Ki, initally planned as a natural stop for trade routes between western Amenor and Cel'Ki. Though it still fulfills this purpose today, it has also gained its own identity.   Though the town is quite calm now, such was not the case during The Trade War. Much of the clashes between Acadia and Oriceré took place in the Jade West and Reylee became a natural garrison for Oriceré during the war. Remnants of this military architecture and culture can still be found among the buildings of the town and the discipline of its people.

title: Seand
content:
Seand
A document detailing the town of Seand.

    Seand is a town in the northwestern reaches of Jyndera. It is settled below the ground level of the sands, at the bottom of a cliff. This makes it well protected against the winds that blow accross Wynderialdessa's Wastes which has let the town prosper relatively well.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	21%
Halfling 	19%
Tiefling 	16%
Dragonborn 	13%
Dwarf 	11%
Other 	20%
 
Geography
Seand is settled at the bottom of a cliff, so. the immediate geography is defined by the rising cliff walls. These red walls rise far above the village and only one small path leads up the side of the cliff and out into the desert. Besides the cliffs walls on each side of the settlement, there is the sand and stone beneath the feet of the inhabitants, that define the barren geography near the settlement.  
Culture
The culture of Seand is one that is heavily defined by self-sufficiency. The location of the settlement made it somewhat impervious to the sandstorms that frequently blow through Wynderialdessa's Wastes, but the sand from these storms often fell straight down on top of the settlement from the cliffs above. Having to deal with this problem, one that no one else was having, was the start of Seand's independence. Since then, Seand has been quick to decline help of most kinds and almost everyone who lives there believes that each person should take care of themselves. Those who ask for help, they are weak.  
History
Seand was settled rather late in the Era of Growth. It was settled by a few stubborn and determined settlers who were determined to make a life for themselves in the harsh desert sands. Since then, the towns philosophy of disliking help has kept them to themselves, without any major historical impacts.

title: Tarell
content:
Tarell
A document detailing the town of Tarell.

    Tarell is a town set in the western reaches of Neolora. It lies at the only division of the western river that feeds into the Triloch, just south of The Mark. Its proximity to the landmark has made it a bit of a tourist destination.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Halfling 	30%
Human 	21%
Elf 	16%
Dragonborn 	12%
Aasimar 	11%
Other 	10%
 
Geography
Lying just at the forking point of a large river makes the geography surrounding Tarell quite varied. The large rivers that snake on either side of the settlement are notable features that bring much bounty. South of the settlement, a large forest of oak trees grows alongside the bank of the river.   The most notable piece of geography brought to mind when one considers Tarell would be what lies north of the river however. The picturesque mountains and forest that sit west of the Triloch, holding the long-since passed events of The Marked Septet in The Mark, the crater left by their final fight.  
Culture
Much of the culture in Tarell is borne from its proximity to The Mark, and hence its function as a tourist city. Over the years of Amenor's history, the deeds of The Marked Septet have become a piece of household history, leading to many who desire to see the location of their final fight.   Tarell has thus become a town with more accommodations for outsiders than most other towns of its size. There are more inns and tavern, general stores selling wandering equipment and horses with stables and the like. The population of Tarell reflects this as well, generally being a friendly people.  
History
Tarell was settled in the Era of Settlement, along with many of the other settlements in Neolora. Its geographical location along with the profit to be earned from tourists has since made it grown considerably, to be one of the largest towns in all of Amenor.

title: Te'Phra
content:
Te'Phra
A document detailing the town of Te'Phra.

    Te'Phra is a town in the northwestern reaches of Acadia. It lies just at the eastern foot of a large mountain chain that sits upon Acadia's coast. With the large mountains nearby, as well as a large forest, the town makes its livelihood from lumbering and mining.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	23%
Dwarf 	22%
Elf 	15%
Gnome 	15%
Halfling 	9%
Other 	6%
 
Geography
The town of Te'Phra is settled just upon the slanting where a mountain rises from the ground. This large mountain chain dominates the immediate geography north of the town, with the mountains rising high into the sky. To the east is a large forest of deciduous trees that stretch all the way to the coastline. Beyond the trees and mountains, the flat grass fields characteristic to Acadia, stretch out south of Te'Phra.  
Culture
Te'Phra is much like the bigger brother of Wastillé. Like it, much of the culture revolves around the mining operations in the nearby mountains, though the operations here are a bit larger in scale. Furthermore, Te'Phra does a lot of lumber work thanks to the large nearby forest, also allowing for a bit more serene and peaceful environment in the woods than that of the deep mineshafts. Not that many people make the trek all the way to Te'Phra, but those who do find a hard-working settlement whose exports of ore and wood are impressive for a town of its size.  
History
Te'Phra was settled in the Era of Settlement. It's proximity to Taldarin made it one of the earlier settlements in Amenor. Since then, Te'Phra has remained a moderately sized town focused on mining and lumber. Their work load has waxed and waned throughout Acadia's history and wars, but they have always kept working.
Founding Date
19th of Dawnrise, 160 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Town
Population
~3800
Location under
Acadia

title: Verendar
content:
Verendar
A document detailing the town of Verendar.

    Verendar is a town in the souther reaches of Neolora. It lies just at the mouth of one of the largest rivers of the region, making it a harbor town both in terms of trade and defense. Lying just between The Faithful Forest and Heathen Hills, Verendar has a war-torn history.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	22%
Elf 	19%
Dwarf 	14%
Halfling 	13%
Aasimar 	12%
Other 	20%
 
Geography
Verendar is situated on the coast of southern Neolora, just at the mouth of a large river. The waters that flow past the town provide it with much of its livelihood in forms of fish, watermills and more. The land to the west, along the coast, tends to be quite fertile and is home to quite a few farms as well though.   To the north lies the area of Heathen Hills, a large hilly region with historical significance to Neolora. Many faded paths still lead into these torn hills though few venture there anymore. On the other side of the river, to the south of the settlement lies the large Faithful Forest, the antithesis of Heathen Hills.  
Culture
Verendar is a smaller town, though it has been militarily important throughout history. This idea lives on through the town and there is an abnormally large guard presence for a town of its size. Many of the inhabitants make a good living by providing for these forces as well, making Verendar a more militaristic settlement than most other towns.   The focus on military power has left some of the other aspects of the town underfunded, which as led to a larger unemployment rate and larger slums in Verendar than is common. This is also the reason for its large mix in population, as it has gathered up many unfortunate outcasts who try to make their way, living off Verendar's streets.  
History
Verendar was settled in the Era of Settlement, along with many other settlements in Neolora. Initially the town grown quickly, supported by the life of the waters and nearby fertile ground.   During the Era of Divine Division however, it would become one of the most greatly desired locations in all of Amenor, with three warring factions craving it. During The Mirrored War and The Tectonic Skirmishes, Verendar traded hands many times and the continued clashes here left it broken when the wars finally ended, with a lot of reconstruction to do. To this day, Verendar is still thought of in terms of military power, rather than a settlement, by many people.

title: Vitrienné
content:
Vitrienné
A document detailing the town of Vitrienné.

    Vitrienné is a town in the eastern reaches of Elezari, situated just northeast of the head of the Slithering Serpent. It lies off the main road that leads through northern Elezari and requires a rather difficult journey through miles of dense jungle to reach.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	24%
Dwarf 	21%
Human 	20%
Halfling 	16%
Gnome 	11%
Other 	8%
 
Geography
Though the terrain to reach Vitrienné is dense and difficult to traverse, the immediate geopgraphy by the town is not nearly as rampant. Vitrienné is settled on the western edge of a massive glade in the jungle, at the center of which sits the large mountain Gemgrave.   This glade has almost no trees growing in it and the few that do are not nearly as large as the towering ones in the rest of the surrounding jungle. A few bushes and shrubs dot the emerald ground of the glade but mostly it is just a sea of short grass that then gives way to the slopes of the gray mountain. Vitrienné sits just at the foot of Gemgrave, half of the town being built on the green plain and the other half climbing the mountainside.  
Culture
Vitrienné is certainly a bit of a secluded town and it does not experience many visitors or travelers due to the difficulty of traversing the jungle. The culture in Vitrienné clearly conveys the sense that they are fine with this arrangement, as is evident by the strong sense of community in the town.   That is not to say that they are rude or unwelcoming towards visitors though. However, the accomodations for travelers are rather bare and as a visitor one feels just like an outsider. The entire town is very close-knit and there is simply a sense of being on the outside when visiting, even though one feels welcomed.   Beyond this sense of comunity though, there is a lot of value placed upon material beauty in Vitrienné. Gemgrave is so named due to the large amount of gems that can be found deep inside the mountain and extraction, treatment and use of these gems is a core value of Vitrienné.  
History
Vitrienné was settled in the late years of the Era of Growth. It took a couple of years before the riches of Gemgrave were discovered but once that happened, the town grew rather quickly as people came seeking work and riches. Since then, the slight isolation of the settlement is the only reason it has not grown larger as Gemgrave still gives much bounty.
Founding Date
18th of Arbamand, 1224 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Town
Population
~6500
Location under
Elezari
Related Historical Events

    1726 ADD
    Settling of Vitrienné

title: Wallsmount
content:
Wallsmount
A document detailing the town of Wallsmount.

    Wallsmount is a town in southern Acadia. It is settled just north of Cazerni's Hollow, near the border to The Ashlands. The town is a military establishment, a place for restocking and a final line to hold in The Ruiner's War.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	27%
Elf 	17%
Dwarf 	17%
Tiefling 	14%
Goliath 	11%
Other 	14%
 
Geography
The geography north of Wallsmount is dominated by the Evergreen Seas, the grassy fields stretching out as far as the eyes can see. As the town is settled on the land bridge between Acadia and The Ashlands, there is ocean on either side of the.   The most notable geography near Wallsmount though is the terrain south of the settlement. There lies Cazerni's Hollow, the no mans land of the Ruiner's War. Temporary fortifications, trenches and Fireball-charred grounds dominate this barren battlefield.  
Culture
The culture of Wallsmount is entirely dominated by the Ruiner's War. To refer to it as a town is technically correct but in truth, it is much more of a military encampment. The homes are quickly constructed in simple styles and are still outnumbered by the tents. Trenches and fortified towers dot the surrounding landscape and soldiers flood the taverns looking for a drink to numb their pain. Though the population of Wallsmount is only about a few thousand, there are many many more that reside in the town and in tents around it.  
History
Wallsmount was initially settled in the Era of Growth, during Acadia's period of great growth. Back then, Wallsmount was a simple farming town and it remained as such until the start of the Ruiner's War. It is in these last few years that the town has been entirely transformed.

title: Wehran
content:
Wehran
A document detailing the town of Wehran.

    Wehran is a town in the southern reaches of The Crooked Tooth. It is located on the tundra coast, surrounded by cold waters and falling snow. The treacherous waters and cruel landscape has made it one of the younger settlements in Amenor.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Goliath 	27%
Human 	20%
Dragonborn 	16%
Gnome 	14%
Halfling 	11%
Other 	12%
 
Geography
Wehran is settled on the tundra in the very southern reaches of The Crooked Tooth. It lies just on the coast, where people made landfall so many years ago when they first made it to the archipelago. The town has a large port that sees a decent amount of traffic as people ship wares to Wehran that cannot be found in the snowy reaches.   To the west of the town is a forest of pine trees. They stand on the cold, gray ground and provide Wehran with much appreciated shelter from the cold winds that blow through the archipelago. The situation is the same with the jutting cliffs and mountain that sits north of the town.   For most of the year, Wehran is covered in snow. Only during the warmest parts of Amenor's summer does the snow fully melt away. Even then, the nearby mountains peaks do not melt so even in the summer one can enjoy a bit of sledding.  
Culture
The harsh weather of The Crooked Tooth makes Wehran a challenging place to live, a place where a fireplace in the home is nearly a necessity. This makes the settlement a perfect place for those who can withstand the cold temperatures and beating winds.   This is clearly reflected in the population of the town, as many goliaths as well as silver and white dragonborns make their home here. The homes are constructed with care and precision, characterized by slanted roofs such that the snow should fall off. All homes are wooden, constructed with lumber from the nearby forests and none are rather spectacular, their distinctive feature often hidden under a layer of snow.   The harsh weather tends to produce two types of people: those who are equally harsh and those who are overly compassionate. Most folks who live in Wehran tend to fall into one of these two camps, but the tough weather does inspire a great deal of cooperation.  
History
Wehran was settled by the first people who discovered the archipelago of The Crooked Tooth. Their ships made landfall on the southern shore of the main landmass and that is where Wehran was established. This happened in year 303 ADD of the Era of Growth and the town has had a rather quite and slow growth since then.

title: Yssoné
content:
Yssoné
A document detailing the town of Yssoné.

    Yssoné is a city in the northern reaches of Acadia. It is settled at the end of a wide river that flows in from the ocean, north of the Gravewoods. Despite its pictoresque surroundings, Yssoné is well known for its more brutal ways.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Dwarf 	18%
Human 	17%
Goliath 	16%
Tiefling 	16%
Gnome 	14%
Other 	19%
 
Geography
The geography near Yssoné has a little bit of everything. A wide river flows southeast of the settlement, providing much growth for the dense forest that grows along its western bank. Great fields of green grass stretch out north of Yssoné, abrupted by a wide cliff that splits the land. To say that the landscape near Yssoné is beautiful is a very accurate description.  
Culture
The culture in Yssoné is very peculiar. In most ways, it is a simpke town with its farmers, fishermen, blacksmiths and the like. However, over the history of the settlement, pit fighting has become increasingly popular. So much so, in fact, that it is the thing that the town is most known for.   Most evenings there are multiple taverns that hold various pit fighting. Most of these fights are non-lethal but there are those that are to the death. Many people come from far away to either watch or participate in pit-fighting tournaments.  
History
Yssoné was settled in the late years of the Era of Settlement. The history of Yssoné was very quiet and calm for a long time, which perhaps is a reason for its the fascination with fighting it would develop.   Throughout Acadia's history, Yssoné often entered into a deep depression during times of war as a large portion of the people who participated in the pit fighting, who were in turn a large portion of the population, left to join. This holds true today as The Ruiner's War has made things quiet and slow in Yssoné.
Founding Date
5th of Arbamand, 187 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Town
Population
~4700
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    195 ADD
    Settling of Yssoné

title: Aliendor
content:
Aliendor
A document detailing the city of Aliendor.

    Aliendor is a city in the western reaches of Elezari. It lies upon the largest island of the region, in the middle of Crescent Rise. Though it is settled far inland, the city has easy access to the ocean by the way of two large rivers.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	23%
Elf 	21%
Dwarf 	18%
Tiefling 	16%
Halfling 	14%
Other 	8%
 
Geography
Aliendor lies just at the meeting point of two large rivers that flow down from Crescent Rise. These wide rivers flow past and meet east of the settlement, flowing away to the ocean. To the west of the city is a dense, hilly jungle. The trees grow tall on the uneven earth and flora grows unbounded on the ground. Beyond the jungle, the Crescent Rise stands tall, encapsulating and shielding Aliendor.  
Culture
The culture in Aliendor is a mix of metropolis civilization and natural sanctuary. On one hand, it lies quite far from most of Amenor's other settlements, quite deep in the jungle. This contributes to the natural sanctuary culture, a place to get away from the chaos and bustle of the city street and enjoy the natural beauty that grows just outside the city walls.   On the other hand, the city has grown a lot since its settling, both due to a large influx of population as well as having rather accesible trading routes with the rest of Amenor. This contributes to the large metropolitan scale of Aliendor, to bustling streets and growing businesses.   One can consider Aliendor a perfect place for people who desire both a little piece of quiet and the hustle and bustle of a large city. The culture represents this as well, providing many opportunities for entertainment and career within its walls and many opportunities for a more peaceful life outside its walls.  
History
Aliendor was first settled during the Era of Growth. Being settled rather out of the way, the years of the Era of Divine Division did little for the growth of the settlement. A trade agreement with Xell during the Era of Peace however, made Aliendor grow quite quickly and since then, it has been a rather large city.
Founding Date
25th of Elfyrend, 1271 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
City
Population
~11 000
Location under
Elezari
Related Historical Events

    1773 ADD
    Settling of Aliendor

title: Bayn
content:
Bayn
A document detailing the city of Bayn.

    Bayn is a city in the northern reaches of Neolora. It is one of the three settlements that lie on the shore of the Triloch, settled in the southwestern part of the lake. Bayn is the middle child of the three settlements around the Triloch, in every sense of the word. 

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	20%
Elf 	19%
Dwarf 	18%
Halfling 	18%
Gnome 	16%
Other 	9%
 
Geography
The geopgraphy around Bayn is quite varied. Naturally you have the Triloch with its deep, far-reaching waters that stretch out to the east of the city. Along its shores, a forest of oak and birch grows amidst the plains that lay south of Bayn. The Triloch flows past the city with one of its three arms, the wide river separating Bayn from the rising peaks east of The Mark.  
Culture
The culture in Bayn is one that is shared between all the three settlements around the Triloch. These three places have been built around the lake and it has been instrumental in their growth, both for natural resources and trade. As a result of this fortune, many of the citizens in these three settlements are appreciative and respectful of the surrounding nature.   People cultivate gardens and grow vegetables even within the stone walls. In general, the people who live around the Triloch are considered grateful, nice and respectable people that know to appreciate not only themselves and others, but also the world around them.  
History
Bayn was initially settled along with the other two Triloch settlements in the Era of Settlement. Since then, the city has grown considerably due to the natural bounty and cooperation of the Triloch settlements. Bayn mostly kept out of The Mirrored War and its complications, managing to avoid the historical suffering of southern Neolora.

title: Chandé
content:
Chandé
A document detailing the city of Chandé.

    Chandé is a city in the southern reaches of Acadia. It is settled on the southern coast, just at the mouth of the river that flows from the ocean into the Sunlake. The city is a hub for sailing and ships, whether they are military, trade or civilian.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	25%
Dwarf 	24%
Elf 	19%
Tiefling 	17%
Halfling 	11%
Other 	4%
 
Geography
The most important geographical feature near Chandé is the ocean and the nearby river. The river flows past the city on the western side while the ocean spreads out to the south. The nearby land is dominated by the grassy fields of the Evergreen Seas, though with a few nearby forests. The grasslands that surround the city now have been unearthed by a great deal of deforesting that takes place when constructing naval vessels.  
Culture
The culture of Chandé is heavily defined by seafaring. All three of the large cities in Acadia that lay on the shores of the Sunlake pass through Chandé by way of the river when they wish to interact with the outside world, which has made it the de facto place for ships and their knowledge, no matter the type of ship. Chandé's proficiency in seafaring has made it a great trading hub, filled with wares from every corner of Amenor.   While Port Imodal sees a lot of ships pass through, Chandé is often the final destination for those ships. While the population of Chandé is reasonably high, there are often hundreds, if not thousands, more in the city that have sailed there to trade and sell wares.  
History
Chandé was settled in the early years of Era of Growth. Throughout most of that era, the city grew steadily as much of Acadia's notable traffic passed through the city. In the Era of Divine Division however, when The Trade War broke out, Chandé became Acadia's hub for naval warfare. Forests were chopped down, military vessels were built and researched upon and it clearly defined Chandé's future. Even to this day, Chandé is defined by their seafaring skill and the military naval hub has been established once more for The Ruiner's War.

title: Diroux
content:
Diroux
A document detailing the city of Diroux.

    Diroux is a city in the northern reaches of Elezari's mainland. It was the first settlement settled in the region and it served as a staging point to reclaim the region from the dragons that once ruled it. The construction of the city still reveals its original military intent, now mixed with an influence of a trading post and gate-town.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	31%
Elf 	24%
Tiefling 	16%
Dragonborn 	12%
Dwarf 	11%
Other 	6%
 
Geography
Diroux lies on the main road that connects the capitals of Neolora and Elezari, a road that has been created by the felling of many, many jungle trees. This well-traveled road leads straight through the town and is the only part of the landscape that breaks up the sea of green that surrounds the settlement.   The trees of the jungle nearby Diroux naturally vary in height, some being shorter than the walls that encircle the city but most of them towering far above. The extensive canopies form a natural ceiling over the surrounding geography but have been trimmed away right above the city to allow the sunlight to more easily illuminate.   Traveling along the main road is easy enough and one would be joined by many other travelers. Veering off this beaten path is a much more difficult task though. Vines grow long on the thick bark of the jungle trees and slither across the ground. Bushes and shrubbery grow tall and wide amidst the base of the trees and make traversal with horse nearly impossible. The jungle is a verdant growth of beauty and nature, in its most unkept state.  
Culture
Diroux has a friendly atmosphere with many different people who welcome any and all travelers. Being a settlement that one almost has to pass through if they wish to travel through Elezari on foot, there are many different activities and establishments for travelers which also helps Diroux's economy.   Most people who live in Diroux and are not simply passing through can be divided into one of three camps. Firstly, there are those that are the descendants of the settlers who struggled in Dragon's Destiny. They are incredibly proud of the feats of their anscestors and often tend to the parts of Diroux's defences that remain, the ones that now serve to reinforce Diroux as a gate-city.   Secondly, there are those that consider the beauty of the surrounding nature. They have considerable gardens in their backyards and enjoy taking long walks through the unmarked paths that slither around in the jungle near the settlement. Finally, there are those who perpetuate the economic well-being of Diroux. The traders, merchants and craftsmen that make good business from the many people that pass through the city.  
History
Diroux was settled in the Era of Growth. It was the first settlement to be successfully established in Elezari, initially settled as a foothold in the region during the events of Dragon's Destiny. Since then, the city has steadily grown and gotten more and more of a trading post influence.

title: Elendar
content:
Elendar
A document detailing the city of Elendar.

    Elendar is a city near the middle of Acadia. It sits on the northeastern curve of the Sunlake. Though the city has a large population with varied livelihoods, it is well known for its many learned scholars.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	27%
Elf 	26%
Gnome 	19%
Dwarf 	11%
Halfling 	10%
Other 	7%
 
Geography
Elendar is settled in the northeastern part of the Sunlake, where it curves around. This means that though it sits on the southern shore of the lake, a few miles south of the city, the lake stretches out as well. To the east of Elendar is a forest of deciduous trees but beyond that, there is only the Evergreen Sea.  
Culture
Elendar is a city that is well positioned for many different professions. The Evergreen Sea provides many opportunites for farming on fertile ground near the Sunlake. The lake itself is large and provides plenty of chance for fishing and the like, while the nearby forest gives chances for hunting and such.   Though a large part of Elendar's population is divided across professions such as these, it is not what the city is best known for. Over time, it has become a haven for scholarly studies of all types. Wizards, researchers, teachers and the like can be found en masse in Elendar and the city has a higher amount of libraries and learned places than any other settlement in Amenor.  
History
Elendar was settled among the first cities after The Commence during the Era of Settlement. Since then, the city has slowly gained the learned identity it has today, over time. Throughout Acadia's history, much of the battle plans and inventions for the wars took place in Elendar.
Founding Date
38th of Elfyrend, 16 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
City
Population
~12 300
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    24 ADD
    Settling of Elendar

title: Fe'Dalar
content:
Fe'Dalar
A document detailing the city of Fe'Dalar.

    Fe'Dalar is a city in the southern reaches of Qanya. It is the first settlement one would reach when traveling down the Greenhold Descent from Acadia, settled in the midst of the swamp. In more recent times, it has become characterized by a large econimic class divide.


 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	23%
Elf 	22%
Dwarf 	18%
Gnome 	13%
Halfling 	11%
Other 	13%

 
Geography
Fe'Dalar is settled in the middle of the swamp. A couple of miles to the southwest is the Greenshade Descent, leading up to Acadia but beyond that, there is nothing but murky green in every direction. A forest of willow trees grows around the city, whiel a small lake filled with vegetation and insects lies to the northwest. West of the city, past this lake, the landscape turns hilly and uneven with the flora growing at odd angles.
 
Culture
Officially, the culture of Fe'Dalar is centered around the beauty that can be found even in a swamp. The city is quick to promote its creations that help one live a life in the swamp while also looking beautiful. In truth though, Fe'Dalar has a bit of problem with economic differences. The city has grown large enough that building homes for everyone on the marsh ground would not only be impractical but also difficult. As such, homes have been built upon massive trees that stretch through the swamp and the economic divide between the people who still live on the ground and the ones who live above them is quite distinct. The tensions between these two groups are very real in the settlement but officials do their best to prevent in-fighting, often taking the side of those with money.
 
History
Fe'Dalar was settled in the Era of Settlement, the first settlement that settled in the region. It was created by settlers from Acadia and the city did quite well for many years. Arguably, the city did a little too well over time and the amount of people who came to settle down in Fe'Dalar started to become unsustainable. Over time, a class divide started to form in the city, one that is very prevalent now but equally suppressed by the authorities.

title: Goldwatch
content:
Goldwatch
A document detailing the city of Goldwatch.

    Goldwatch is a city in the souther reaches of Acadia. It is settled a few miles west of the Sunlake, at the foot of some large mountains. These mountains have proved enormously profitable in yielding gold, which as defined Goldwatch entirely.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	21%
Dwarf 	20%
Gnome 	18%
Elf 	16%
Halfling 	13%
Other 	12%
 
Geography
The geography around Goldwatch is quite varied but also rather narrow compared to most other settlements in Acadia. While they often have fields of grassy plains stretching out, Goldwatch is settled between a steep cliff to the north and a wide river to the south. To the west of the settlement is the most important part of the terrain though, the Goldwatch Mountains where the city has earned its riches.  
Culture
As is hinted at by the name, Goldwatch is a city that is greatly defined by gold. From the nearby mountains, the city has extracted enormous amounts of gold, enough to decorate their city with. The streets of Goldwatch are covered in sheets of gold, the homes decored with golden filigree and the many, many bank buildings are topped with golden cupolas.   The incredible wealth of Goldwatch is what defined it, not just in appearance but also in way of life. There are more banks in Goldwatch than there are in the rest of Amenor combined and very few people in Goldwatch actually work a farm, fish or forage for food. Goldwatch and it's citizens know how to spend their money and are not above flaunting their wealth whenever they have the chance. Naturally there exists those who hold onto their money for dear life but unlike Penchla, these are few and far between.  
History
Goldwatch was settled in the Era of Growth. It was not long after the settling of the city that the very fortunate mines of the Goldwatch Mountains were opened. This led to a boom in Goldwatch's population and for a few years during the Era of Growth, they even outnumbered the capital. Eventually though, the great influx of gold, jobs and people would calm down. Throughout history, Goldwatch's rich coffers have been very helpful for all the problems that Acadia's has faced.
Founding Date
23rd of Huwalain, 255 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
City
Population
~15 000
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    757 ADD
    Settling of Goldwatch

title: Gul'DáRa
content:
Gul'Dára
A document detailing the city of Gul'Dára.

    Gul'Dára is a city in the eastern reaches of Acadia. It lies just at the mouth of a river as well as on the coast of the region. The city has grown considerably over time and does a lot of trading with Taldarin. 

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	27%
Elf 	24%
Halflingd 	16%
Gnome 	13%
Dwarf 	11%
Aasimar 	6%
Other 	3%
 
Geography
The geography near Gul'Dára is dominated by terrain that is harder to traverse. To the east of the city rises a massive mountain chain that separates the regions, while the oceans spread out to the north. To the west is a small forest that grows along the coast and up to the walls of the city. The terrain to the south of the city remains easily traversable though, as grassy green fields stretch out in that direction.  
Culture
Gul'Dára is a city that lacks any strong specialization or identity. Some people work on farms south of the city, plowing the fields and tending cattle. Some people work to the east of the city, mining in the nearby mountains. Some hunt in the forest to the west or fish in the waters to the north. None of these practices are particularly overpowering in Gul'Dára's culture, instead just creating a well-rounded city. That is mostly the global identity of the settlement as well: a good place to live, where nothing particularly interesting ever happens but you will have a good life.  
History
Gul'Dára was settled in the middle of the Era of Growth, when Acadia's population was greatly increasing. Since then, the city has grown considerably and has become one of the largest cities in all of Amenor. Despite this growth and large population, the city has had a rather quite history with not particularly remarkable historical events.
Founding Date
26th of Huwalain, 860 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
City
Population
~15 700
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    1362 ADD
    Settling of Gul'Dára

title: Hotienné
content:
Hotienné
A document detailing the city of Hotienné.

    Hotienné is a city in the western reaches of Elezari. It lies just on the coast, being the city in Elezari that is closest to the rest of Amenor. As such, it has become a mixture of Elezari's culture and the rest of Amenor.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	19%
Elf 	18%
Aasimar 	14%
Dragonborn 	13%
Gnome 	11%
Dragonborn 	11%
Other 	14%
 
Geography
Unlike most of the other settlements in Elezari, Hotienné is not only surrounded by jungle. Settled on the coast, it is half surrounded by the oceans of Amenor, half surrounded by dense jungle. Though they are very different in many aspects, the oceans of blue and oceans of green both provide Hotienné with much bounty.  
Culture
Hotienné is settled on the western coast of Elezari, significantly closer to most of Amenor than any other settlement in the region. Though travel to Dragonhold via the main road that leads through Neolora, or via ship upon the Slithering Serpent is common, sailing to Hotienné is often much closer.   As such, Hotienné has become a trading post and mixing pot for Elezari and the rest of Amenor. Many ships that trade with Elezari do so by traveling to Hotienné where the wares are then carried further by Elezari's internal merchants. This large amount of business and trade going through Hotienné has been instrumental in its growth and shaped its culture.   Hotienné has become a mix of a little bit of everything. There is fur and enchanted ice from Dar'Cortya, rare rocks and gems from Grayhold, cattle and produce from Neolora and more. The settlement has something for almost every type of traveler, whether they be arcane or martial, lawful or chaotic. More than any of the cultural influences on Hotienné though, is the culture that anyone can make a fortune. The streets are filled with wanna-be merchants and peddlers, each and every one of them attempting to seize (or avoid) the interest of the many travelers that pass through the city.  
History
Hotienné was settled in the final years of the Era of Growth. It's early years mirrored that of the first settlement in Elezari, with The Divine War stunting growth in Hotienné. It was not until the end of the war that Hotienné could begin to grow, and quickly at that. Since then, the city has had a calm history, barring a recent incident with corrupt leadership that was swiftly handled.

title: IléRtaux
content:
Ilértaux
A document detailing the city of Ilértaux.

    Ilértaux is a city in the northern reaches of Neolora. It lies just at the second division of the Zeschla's Embrace, the river flowing on either side of the city. Lying along a large shipping lane has contributed to the growth of the settlement.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	22%
Elf 	20%
Dwarf 	17%
Tiefling 	14%
Halfling 	11%
Aasimar 	10%
Other 	6%
 
Geography
Ilértaux is settled upon the plains of Neolora. The fields of grass and dirt stretch far out from the settlement, broken only by the nearby river and forest. To the south of the city lies a large forest of oak trees, growing along the bank of the river and providing Ilértaux with many natural resources.   The most defining trait of the nearby geography is undoubtedly the rivers that flow on either side of the city. Zeschla's Embrace splits just north of the city, as two large rivers flow south, past it. The western river cuts through the plains, slightly isolating Ilértaux while the eastern river does the opposite, acting as a large shipping lane that connects the city to the rest of Amenor.  
Culture
The culture of Ilértaux is influenced by a great many things. The many ships that pass through bring goods and influences from other parts of Amenor, while the large population of the city forms their own traditions, ideals and ideas.   With that said though, the large amount of cultural influences to Ilértaux leaves it a city without a singular strong identity. The traditions of many different species and people can be found in Ilértaux. One thing that should be stated about the city though is its lack of hostility towards others.   Many of the southern settlements in Neolora have had a history of animosity towards outsiders or people who are different. Ilértaux has been far enough away from these conflicts to not be influenced by them. Its lack of complicated history makes it a bit of a friendlier place.  
History
Ilértaux was settled early in the Era of Settlement. Its geographical location made it a tempting place to settle and has since contributed to its growth. Unlike some of the southern cities in Neolora, it has had a relatively uncomplicated history and has not been involved in any major conflicts.

title: Jazerai
content:
Jazerai
A document detailing the city of Jazerai.

    Jazerai is a large city in the middle of Neolora. It is settled upon the a large cliff that rises from the plains. This cliff, Serpent's Skull, has the appearance of the head of a snake and Jazerai sits at its very tip.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	19%
Dwarf 	17%
Gnome 	16%
Tiefling 	15%
Half-Orc 	13%
Dragonborn 	11%
Other 	7%
 
Geography
Jazerai is settled upon the very edge of the cliffs of Serpent's Skull. The sharp cliffs rise on every side of the settlement, cradling and protecting it. The city looks out over the plains below that stretch as far as the eye can see. Beyond the road that snakes past the cliffs to the east, Jazerai seems to be isolated in a sea of green.   Though the plains beneath the fertile and fine for farming, the ground upon the cliff itself are far more stone-filled and difficult to toil. As such, the few farms that surround the city are not enough to sustain it and therefore Jazerai relies much on trade with the other settlements in Neolora, using specially designed lifts to ascend and descend the cliffs quickly.  
Culture
Being located close to the middle of Neolora, the culture of Jazerai sees many influences from other settlements nearby. Remnants of the non-believers and believers war in Neolora's history are also visible in the traditions of the city, having a large amount of the population that have no faith in the Amenorean pantheon.   What Jazerai is best known for however, is much in line with the namesake of its cliff. Outside Jazerai, the city is known as somewhat of a criminal pit and inside its walls, most of the population would agree. Though officials like to say the opposite, Jazerai has the largest amount of organized crime, thefts, murders and more, in all of Amenor. Walking its streets, the tense air, peering eyes and rough streets certainly tell the same tale.  
History
Jazerai was settled in the Era of Settlement. In the beginning of its history, it did not have the reputation it did today. It's central location made it grow fast and the city was well constructed and respected. It was during and after The Mirrored War, when many of the normal population left to fight and was replaced by refugees, the injured, old and fools that Jazerai started to devolve into a wretched hive of scum and villainy.
Founding Date
26th of Huwalain, 88 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
City
Population
~7600
Location under
Neolora
Related Historical Events

    96 ADD
    Settling of Jazerai

title: Koenadar
content:
Koenadar
A document detailing the city of Koenadar.

    Koenadar is a city in the northern reaches of The Ashlands. It is one of only two surviving settlements after the eruption of Ironbellow and now houses many who have fled their destroyed homes.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	24%
Dwarf 	21%
Elf 	19%
Gnome 	13%
Halfling 	12%
Other 	11%
 
Geography
Koenadar is settled at the base of the land bridge that connects the Ashlands with Acadia. The land bridge is rather wide though and there are multiple miles to either coast from the settlement.   A forest grows on each coast, now reduced to just ash-covered, smothered, leafless trees. Any trees that grew nearby Koenadar have been cut down in order to help build homes for the refugees and fortifications against Acadia.   The proximity of the settlement to Acadia has made it the first line of defence for the region. As such, what truly dominates the nearby geography of Koenadar are the man-made structures. Large watchtowers, long walls, fields of traps and extensive trenches define the landscape, all of it covered in a layer of gray ash.  
Culture
The culture that Koenadar used to have has mostly faded away to now become a war and disaster-torn place. Refugees cramp the city streets, tents rise from the ash outside the walls all the while guards, soldiers and mercenaries fight a war based out of the city.   There are simply too many people living in the city at the moment. Beyond the usual citizens, there are the refugees who escaped the destruction of their home and the soldiers, hired or otherwise, who have come to fight a war on the doorstep of the city.  
History
Koenadar was first settled in the Era of Settlement. Since then, it has followed the history of the Ashlands closely with the other settlements of the region. Now, after the recent eruption and subsequent declaration of war, it has turned into torn shadow of its former self.

title: Loftal
content:
Loftal
A document detailing the city of Loftal.

    Loftal is a city in the western reaches of The Grayhold. It is the second largest settlement on the continent, just a bit smaller than the capital of the region, Grayhold. The city is settled inland, just at the dividing point of the largest river of the region, allowing easy access for ships.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	17%
Dwarf 	16%
Gnome 	16%
Goliath 	13%
Elf 	12%
Halfling 	11%
Other 	15%
 
Geography
The geography surrounding Loftal is perhap the most varied of any settlement in The Grayhold. Many rivers flow past its northern walls and join to one wide river that crests past to the west. Past the wide river, a hilly area sits before a region of sheer stone cliffs.   The elevation from the nearby mountains to the southeast slowly diminish towards the settlement, seating it at the bottom of a long, faint descent. Tall pine trees grow in the shade of these mountains and a forest stretches out beyond the eastern walls of the settlement, bounded by the cresting river.  
Culture
Loftal's inhabitants are greatly varied, leading to many different customs and traditions carried out by its people. No clear architecture defines the city nor do any special celebrations. Loftal is a city that has a place for all types of people.   That said however, it is still a city in The Grayhold and, try as they might, it is very difficult to escape the clutches this barren land hold over you. Though painted roofs and wooden homes may distract you otherwise, the gray ground and surrounding stone are unmistakable. Most people get by with fishing or trading with ships, unable to cultivate on their own continent.  
History
Loftal was settled in the Era of Settlement. It is one of the earliest settlements in the history of Amenor, settled as early 3 ADD. Like the other settlements of The Grayhold, it was abandoned after The Ravaging of The Greenreaches. Since it's resettling though, it has grown faster than all the other settlements in The Grayhold, though not to anywhere near what it used to be.
Founding Date
20th of Heysatus, 3 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
City
Population
~7200
Location under
The Grayhold
Related Historical Events

    12 ADD
    Settling of Loftal

title: Martee
content:
Martee
A document detailing the city of Martee.

    Martee is a city on the western coast of Acadia. The city is settled just south of a long mountain chain and sits just west of the Wolfwood. Though the city does a lot of fishing as well as some mining, it is mostly known for its hunters.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	18%
Elf 	17%
Halfling 	15%
Dwarf 	15%
Gnome 	14%
Other 	21%
 
Geography
Martee is settled just on the western coast of Acadia. The oceans spread out as far as the eye can see to the west of the city, while a mountain range towers over the city to the north. To the east of Martee lies the Evergreen Sea, the grassy fields of Acadia that stretch out in a similar fashion to the ocean. This green ocean is however broken up by the large forest of the Wolfwood which lies only a few miles east of the city.  
Culture
Martee's placement upon the coast of Acadia intrinsically makes it do a lot of fishing and the like. It also has a close trading relationship with Penchla but travel via ship to other settlements takes a long time and thus does not have a particularly strong trading identity.   Instead, the proximity of the Wolfwood is what has defined Martee's culture. Though the percentage of the population that actually leave the city and hunt within these woods is relatively small, the city has still become known for these people. The brave and cunning hunters and rangers that make the Wolfwood their primary home and Martee their secondary.  
History
Martee was settled in the Era of Settlement. Since then, the city has had no major historical influence or events. Over time, its global identity has been shaped around the few men and women of the population that regularly enter the Wolfwood. Over the course of Acadia's history, these ranger often took up roles as scouts and the like in Acadia's wars. And just like when they venture into the Wolfwood, not all returned from these wars.

title: Novacera
content:
Novacera
A document detailing the city of Novacera.

    Novacera is a settlement in the western reaches of Jyndera. It is settled upon the western coast of the region, just north of a large bay. It is the second oldest city in the region, established as a way to more easily trade with The Ashlands.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	22%
Dwarf 	20%
Tiefling 	18%
Dragonborn 	16%
Gnome 	11%
Other 	13%
 
Geography
The territory near Novacera is defined by two things: the ocean and the desert. To the west of the city is the ocean between Jyndera and the Ashlands, the waters dotted with some small islands. To the east of the city is the desert that is Wynderialdessa's Wastes, where many dunes create a sloped landscape of sand.  
Culture
The culture in Novacera is greatly defined by help. As it was initially established to help have better trading with the Ashlands, the city is entirely based on the concept of helping each other. This sentiment has spread through the inhabitants and history of the city, making it an incredibly friendly place. People are always happy too see their friends and outsiders alike, and are always quick to offer help to any who need it.  
History
Novacera was settled towards the end of the Era of Settlement, after the formation of The Ashlands and Jyndera's allyship. Since then, the city has stood strong and grown considerably due to the bounties of the nearby ocean as well as the trading with the Ashlands.

title: On'Tha
content:
On'Tha
