A document detailing the city of On'Tha.

    On'Tha is a city in southern Neolora. It lies just at the end of one of Neolora's largest rivers, on a peninsula as the river splits just south of the city. On'Tha is also closely situated to both the Heathen Hills and The Faithful Forest, which has dominated its history.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	18%
Elf 	16%
Gnome 	15%
Dwarf 	14%
Halfling 	13%
Tiefling 	12%
Other 	12%
 
Geography
Settled on a peninsula with moving waters all around, On'Tha is a city that is provided much by the nearby waters. That is not to say the plains of the peninsula are poor farming grounds, for they are not, but the ship traffic, fishing and water-powered inventions are perhaps simply more useful.   To the northwest of the city lies a large lake, where one half of the river feeds into. The other half comes flowing from the top of a small mountain chain to the southeast of On'Tha. Finally, On'Tha lies just east of the Heathen Hills and The Faithful Forest. While it lies closer to the forest, the river flows between them while the peninsula reaches up into Heathen Hills where it connects with the mainland.  
Culture
The culture of On'Tha is a varied one. Some influences of distant cultures are brought here by trading ships, some come from the northern settlements and some remain from the days of warfare in On'Tha's history. It is thus difficult to describe the culture of On'Tha as one definitive thing, when there are so many small pieces that create it. Though signs of old warfare are still visible in the city, it is not on the forefront of many peoples minds, instead being replaced by new traditions and ideas, making On'Tha a city where many different cultures can be found.  
History
On'Tha was settled in the Era of Settlement. The many nearby natural resources made it quickly grow and for many years it had a peaceful history, as the capital of Neolora. That was until the Era of Divine Division and The Mirrored War, where the city became a battle ground for the belivers and non-believers. Though these fights left much of the city crumbled, its prior riches made rebuilding quick and mostly easy when the time of peace came once more but not quite so that they managed to keep the title of capital, losing that to Adlora.

title: Pearltide
content:
Pearltide
A document detailing the city of Pearltide.

    Pearltide is a city in northern Acadia. It lies on the coast of the region, just south of Taldarin. The city is well known for its many bounties extracted from the cove it is settled near, especially the pearls.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	31%
Elf 	19%
Halfling 	16%
Tiefling 	13%
Dragonborn 	11%
Other 	10%
 
Geography
The immediate geography near Pearltide is certainly dominated by the cove. Pearltide is settled just at the southern edge of Pearlcove and its waters see a lot of ship traffic and work. To the west and east of the cove are large deciduous forest while the immediate area around Pearltide are more flat. Acadia's characteristic grass plains stretch out around the city and south of it.  
Culture
Pearltide has a culture that is, in large parts, grounded in the many beautiful pearls they have access to. There is a fascination with beauty and appearance, something that becomes very clear when walking among the streets of the city and noticing the many decorations upon homes and streets alike.   Though much of Pearltide's global identity is based upon their pearls and beauty, such jewelry is often reserved for nobles and the wealthy. The people who actually harvest these pearls, work the docks or nearby farms often do not have the money to waste upon such frivolous items. Though the economic gap between these groups is not necessarily larger than in other places, the amount of jewelry makes it quite apparent, which has only fueled tensions between the two groups.  
History
Pearltide was the first settlement ever settled after The Commence. It was settled in the third year of the Era of Settlement, as settlers from Targaraen first reached the lands of Acadia. Despite being the oldest city in all of Amenor's expansion, its obsession with beauty and appearance makes it look much newer than it actually is.   Throughout its history, the tension between the working class and the nobles have fluctuated. Though most diplomatic meetings are performed with raised voices and clenched fists, the groups have never come to blows.
Founding Date
23rd of Dawnrise, 3 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
City
Population
~13 000
Location under
Acadia
Related Historical Events

    11 ADD
    Settling of Pearltide

title: Redemount
content:
Redemount
A document detailing the city of Redemount.

    Redemount is a city in the northern parts of Acadia. It is settled just at the foot of a large mountain chain, just besides a large river that flows from the mountains out into the ocean. The mountains and the river are what make up the livelihood of the city, providing quite a bit of bounty.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	28%
Dwarf 	26%
Elf 	14%
Gnome 	12%
Halfling 	10%
Other 	10%
 
Geography
The immediate geography around Redemount is dominated by the large chain of mountains that rise above the rooftops of the city. Beyond the mountains themselves, they also shape the landscape around, as a large river flows down its face and past the eastern side of the city. North of the city lies a few small forests, but mostly the characteristice green fields of grass stretch out.  
Culture
Redemount's culture is largely based around the mining operations that are performed in the nearby mountains. Most of the homes are constructed by the stone that is excavated as tunnels are mined. The bounty from within the mountains is rather impressive as well and the down is well decorated with gems and the like. Furthermore, the nearby river provides many opportunities for relaxation, making Redemount a place both of work and rest. As such, the culture in Redemount is strongly distinctive during different times of the day, being a rather strict city during the day and a colorful and vibrant city during the night.  
History
Redemount was settled rather early in the Era of Settlement. The location was rather advantageous and the mountain quickly proved bountiful. Since the settling of the city, it has been a valuable asset in the history of Acadia. The steel and iron extracted from within the mountain has contributed a great deal to Acadia's war efforts and the like. Beyond that though, the city's placement deep within the region has always kept it safe and rather uneventful.

title: Soizie
content:
Soizie
A document detailing the city of Soizie.

    Soizie is a city in the middle of Acadia. It is settled just south of the Gravewoods, on the eastern side of the lake. It is a relatively large city, one that is defined by its religious presence and faith.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	25%
Elf 	17%
Gnome 	13%
Dragonborn 	12%
Dwarf 	12%
Other 	21%
 
Geography
Soizie is settled close to the middle of Acadia and thus sits in the midst of the Evergreen Sea. The grassy green fields stretch out to the south and east of the settlement, while a large lake lies to the west. Soizie is settled just at the eastern shore of this lake and just south of the Gravewoods and its accompanying mountain chain.  
Culture
Soizie's culture is heavily defined by the gods of Amenor. Faith and belief are the two most important factors for most people in Soizie, whether it be faith in the divine or faith in other people. Despite this open and accepting nature, Soizie has had quite a few controversies.   This is also due to the strong religious presence in the city. As a matter of fact, the city is one of the few settlements in Amenor where there exists shrines or temples to almost any god. Those who worship loving deities like Virá walk the streets among those who preach the chaos of Obiterra.   The mere fact that these evil deities have places of worship has made Soizie controversial in the eyes of Amenor. In addition, it has also made Soizie have some tensions between their own inhabitants. In fact, the nearby Gravewoods used to be an important part of Soizie's culture, a place to pay respect to the honored dead. When the forest changed in the years of the Era of Peace, many accused followers of Raschal for its downfall, causing even further strife. For the most part though, there are very few that pray to such chaotic and evil deities and therefore most of the city gets along.  
History
Soizie was settled in the Era of Settlment. The settlers who created the city had an overrepresentation of priests and holy people and it is from this that Soizie's religious nature was born. Throughout the history of Acadia, Soizie has mostly abstained from the war efforts and thus only had their own minor internal conflicts to resolve.

title: Taran
content:
Taran
A document detailing the city of Taran.

    Taran is a city in the eastern part of Neolora. It is a unique settlement, in that it was founded by the first Admyl, Tara, hence the name. It is nestled in The Metal Mountains which also gain their name from the fact that only Admyls live in Taran.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Admyl 	99%
Other 	1%
 
Geography
Taran is nestled into The Metal Mountains, sitting just above the foothills and looking out over the surrounding plains. The sea of grass to the west of the city is vast yet empty. Unlike most other cities in Amenor, the existence of Taran is no betrayed by nearby farms and the like, as Admyls do not need to farm.   This makes the area to the west of the city quite empty and unexplored, while the mountains that the city is built into are well explored. The Admyls of Taran have excavated many minerals and gemstones from within the mountains and have explored much of the chain.  
Culture
As it perhaps expected due to its population, the culture of Taran is unique in all of Amenor. Though Admyls can be found scattered throughout other settlements, there are rarely enough of them in one place to make their traditions and culture an important place of that settlement.   Taran is differen, of course. Comprised of almost only Admyl's, it is a haven for those with metal skin who might not be quite as welcome everywhere else. Though the city is large, it is bonded through its people and a strong sense of unity is central to the culture of Taran. Though outsiders are welcome, they are met with skeptical eyes and worrying gazes until they can prove themselves a friend of Admyls.  
History
Taran was established in the Era of War, after Tara's Enlightenment. It was Tara, along with 73 other Admyls that first claimed stake to The Metal Mountains and started construction of their haven. Being such a small number, construction took a long time but the lack of aging made their numbers slowly grow. Today, Taran is a grand city filled with thousands of Admyls.   Unfortunately, their history has not been very smooth and simple. Some of the people of Neolora, coming out of the Era of Longevity now as aging beings hated the Admyls for having what they now had lost. This group of people waged many smaller wars against Taran and the Admyls, never quite overcoming the ingenuity of the city but always dealing some damage.   Then, when these thoughts eventually started to disappear, they were quickly replaced with the disdain that Admyls have no souls. More sieges against Taran took place, scattered over many years. Eventually, even these ended but the matter of Admyls souls remains a topic of great debate and heated feelings even to this day.
Founding Date
13th of Gordrand, 6 ADD, Era of War
Type
City
Population
~8500
Location under
Neolora
Related Historical Events

    501 ADD
    Settling of Taran

title: Venbaaz
content:
Venbaaz
A document detailing the city of Venbaaz.

    Venbaaz is a settlement in the southern parts of Jyndera, settled upon the small island that lies in Clearwater Bay. The city is well known for its innovations and inventions that have helped Jyndera prosper in Amenor.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	23%
Gnome 	22%
Human 	18%
Dwarf 	15%
Tiefling 	12%
Halfling 	4%
Other 	6%
 
Geography
The geography near Venbaaz is mostly defined by the nearby waters of Clearwater Bay. They surrond the island that the city is settled upon and give it an almost ethereal look with how clear the waters are. The land itself is not much different from the rest of Wynderialdessa's Wastes, and is far less bountiful than the waters.  
Culture
Venbaaz is a city that is defined by inventions and innovations in general. When the city was originally settled, it was discovered that the nearby waters held many beautiful pearls and gems, as well as fish but they were also very cloudly and difficult to fish in due to all the sand that was constantly being blown in the waters.   This problem is what started Venbaaz's streak of innovation. Since then, they have made inventions their lifeblood and people move from all over Amenor to take part in its trade shows, culture and possible jobs. Beyond the few that work simple, honest jobs, almost every person in Venbaaz has ties to, or is someone who has contributed to the many inventions that Jyndera patents.  
History
Venbaaz was settled in the Era of Settlement, by settlers from Taldarin who sailed north from Jerun. It was not long after its birth that Venbaaz earned its reputation of invention haven, when it found a way to make the waters in the bay clear and thus catch much more fish than normal.   Since then, Venbaaz has simply continued to create inventions, finding efficient ways to store food, manipulating sun rays in order to create automated machinery and much more, many things that all of Jyndera has adopted to help them with the harsh desert life.
Founding Date
24th of Chandran, 83 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
City
Population
~10 000
Location under
Jyndera
Related Historical Events

    91 ADD
    Settling of Venbaaz

title: Xell
content:
Xell
A document detailing the city of Xell.

    Xell is a city in southern Neolora. It is the second-largest settlement in all of Neolora as well as the westernmost one. This also makes it the closest point of contact between Neolora and most of Amenor and its strategic location has caused strife in the past.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	27%
Elf 	22%
Dwarf 	16%
Halfling 	12%
Gnome 	11%
Tiefling 	7%
Other 	5%
 
Geography
Xell lies on the western coast of Xelley Island, which is the lower part of the island. This makes the western shore near the city much flatter and the nearby waters much easier to navigate. While the ocean stretches out to the west, a large plain of grass and dirt stretches out in the other direction, allowing for farms that sustain the large population. To the north of the settlement is a large oak forest that provides much bounty for the city as well.  
Culture
Being the easiest access to Neolora for most of Amenor, many ships pass through the large city. This coming and going of wares and ideas has certainly influenced the culture of Xell, though certainly not as much as its history has.   Xelley Island, with its largest city at the helm, has fought for its independence from the rest of Neolora for a long time. Though they have not achieved it yet, their continued struggle has made the people definitively headstrong and stubborn, traits they are well known for.  
History
Xell was settled along with the other two settlements on Xelley Island in the Era of Settlement. Since then, the city has grown a lot, both from its nearby natural resources as well as people coming from the rest of Amenor. Beyond this growth though, it has had a shaky history in its fight for independence.

title: Yldesar
content:
Yldesar
A document detailing the city of Yldesar.

    Yldesar is a city in northern Oriceré. It is not quite settled in the Tourmaline North but sits closer to the center of the archipelago, on an island just north of the capital island. The island that the town sits upon is and oblong island dominated by hills and forests.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	24%
Halfling 	21%
Elf 	18%
Aasimar 	15%
Tiefling 	12%
Dwarf 	6%
Other 	4%
 
Geography
The geography of Yldesar is naturally dominated by the surroundings waters, especially considering how oblong the island is. The terrain upon the island is defined by forests of deciduous trees that grow upon uneven, hilly ground. The city itself is built upon the hills of the island, with the forests growing outside its walls and along the coast of the island.  
Culture
Yldesar is a city that is skilled at seafaring in all its forms, whether it be simple cargo ships or massive naval warfare vessels. Its greatest focus is that of naval warfare though, a role that it attained during the Trade War. Ever since then, Yldesar has been the hub of naval innovation and city that is quite defined by a military presence. Even today, when the region is not at war, this military culture lives on in the city through its efficiency and disciplined, somewhat cold, population.  
History
Yldesar was settled in the Era of Growth, only a few years after Cel'Ki. For much of its early years, it had a bit of a friendly rivalry with the capital, both settlements pushing each other to innovate upon the seas. This made them the leading experts upon Amenor's seas and eventually the rivalry turned into a clearly defined relation during The Trade War. Yldesar took upon itself the task of being the hub of naval warfare and innovation, while Cel'Ki took on the role of capital.
Founding Date
38th of Chandran, 107 ADD, Era of Longevity
Type
City
Population
~9300
Location under
Oriceré
Related Historical Events

    317 ADD
    Settling of Yldesar

title: Adlora
content:
Adlora
A document detailing the capital city Adlora.

    Adlora is the capital of Neolora, situated in its northern reaches. It is one of the three settlements around the Triloch, sitting just where the largest river from Zeschla's Embrace spills into the lake. Adlora is the second largest city in all of Amenor and its peaceful history has made it quite prosperous.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	19%
Elf 	15%
Dwarf 	15%
Halfling 	12%
Gnome 	11%
Tiefling 	10%
Aasimar 	10%
Other 	8%
 
Geography
The geography surrounding Adlora is somewhat varied, with the most noteable quality being the Triloch. It stretches out west from the capital like a sea, while the river connected to it flows past the city to the north. Oak trees grow along the banks of the river and lake, forming oblong forests on either side. The immediate area surrounding Adlora is covered by farms and pastures that are built upon the flat reaches of the plains.  
Culture
The culture in Adlora is one that is shared between all the three settlements around the Triloch. These three places have been built around the lake and it has been instrumental in their growth, both for natural resources and trade. As a result of this fortune, many of the citizens in these three settlements are appreciative and respectful of the surrounding nature. People cultivate gardens and grow vegetables even within the stone walls.   In general, the people who live around the Triloch are considered grateful, nice and respectable people that know to appreciate not only themselves and others, but also the world around them. Furthermore, being the capital of Neolora, Adlora has a large amount of business and people that pass through it. The entire city is vibrant and busy, and opportunities to make friends, money, or both are plenty.   With alabaster towers, packed streets, many satellite homes and farms and its large population, Adlora is an archetypical capital. It encapsulates much of the culture of the region of Neolora and is home to enough people that almost everyone has some form of representation.  
History
Adlora was settled a long time ago, around the same time as the other two settlements of the Triloch, in the Era of Settlement. Since then, the city has grown considerably due to the natural bounty and cooperation of the Triloch settlements. Adlora kept out of The Mirrored War and its complications, managing to avoid the historical suffering of southern Neolora and as a direct result of that, it became the capital of Neolora in the years following the war and has remained such ever since.

title: Alitrá
content:
Alitrá
A document detailing the capital city Alitrá.

    Alitrá is the capital of Jyndera. It is settled in the northeasternmost part of the region, at the very typ of the Sandtrench. With the rest of Jyndera being rather inhospitable, it Alitrá holds about 42% of Jyndera's entire population.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Elf 	18%
Human 	16%
Gnome 	14%
Tiefling 	12%
Dragonborn 	10%
Halfling 	8%
Aasimar 	6%
Other 	16%
 
Geography
The geography near Alitrá is dominated by tall mountain ranges. Both to the west and the south of the settlement a long mountain chain runs along the coast. Only in the valley between these mountains, which is a cliffy region covered in sand, known as the Sandstretch is the land somewhat traversible. To the east of the city, the ocean stretches out towards Oricere and Elezari which has let the capital establish trade with the other regions.  
Culture
The culture in Alitrá is mix of many things, as tends to happen when so many people live in one place. There are influences of every race and type of person in Alitrá, and there are activities and gatherings for people of any walk of life. There is however an overarching culture that lies above all the smaller others in the city, and that is the belief of hard work in difficult scenarios. Life in Jyndera is not easy, especially compared to life in Acadia, for example. As such, the people of Alitrá value hard work, determination and grit very highly, as that is what is required in these harsh landscapes. Furthermore, it is not uncommon for someone from Alitrá to consider Acadians weak and spoonfed.  
History
Alitrá was the first settlement established on Jyndera, in the Era of Settlement. The harsh conditions of Wynderialdessa's Wastes kept few people from striking out on their own and thus the city grew quickly. The oceans provided easy access to the other regions, as well as plenty of bounties themselves. The nearby mountains also make Alitrá a very defensible position and it has managed to easily withstand a few assaults throughout its history.

title: Cel'Ki
content:
Cel'Ki
A document detailing the capital city Cel'Ki.

    Cel'Ki is the capital of the region of Oriceré. It is settled essentially in the middle of the region, upon the largest island in the archipelago. Its location within the region is very apt, as it is certainly the heart of Oriceré, housing more than half of the regions population.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	17%
Elf 	15%
Dwarf 	14%
Halfling 	13%
Gnome 	13%
Tiefling 	11%
Aasimar 	10%
Other 	7%
 
Geography
Cel'Ki is settled upon the larget island in Oriceré's archipelago, lying in the middle of the region. Though it remains true that the most defining geographical feature is the surrounding waters, the size of the island makes the geography near Cel'Ki more varied than around the other settlements in the archipelago. The island itself is defined by deciduous trees and sharp cliffs that rise from fertile grass ground. Cel'Ki itself is settled upon a cliff on the coast, overlooking the waves as they crash and mix with the waterfalls from the settlement, against the stone far beneath.  
Culture
The culture of Cel'Ki is, by extension of its large population, what defines the culture of Oriceré. Cel'Ki is a large settlement with a host of different people from different places. Lying so close to the center of Amenor, with so many ships that sail to Cel'Ki has made it a bit of a melting pot of different cultures and people. In Cel'Ki it is true, more than any other place in Amenor, that anyone can find anything that they are looking for, no matter if its a specific toy from their childhood or a certain pastry their grandmother used to make.   So many ships sail to and from Cel'Ki, bearing wares from every corner of Amenor, something that only enhances the diversity of the settlement. Beyond this diversity though, there is still a central culture in Cel'Ki that revolves around seafaring. Most of the original population have a fascination with ships and innovation in that space, whether it be military or civil. Cel'Ki is known as the place to go if you are looking for something that is hard to find, or if you have an interest in seafaring and life upon the docks and seas.  
History
Cel'Ki was settled in the Era of Longevity, a number of years before Yldesar. The two settlements had a friendly rivarly in the early years of their life, spurring each other on to make better and better ways to travel the seas. At this point, Oriceré had no capital and was not even a recognized region, just a collection of islands with two settlements in them. Eventually though, The Trade War would bond together the two cities and Cel'Ki would take on the role of capital and with the funds it earned from the war, its growth would skyrocket. As the war then ended, Cel'Ki was kept rather intact, allowing it to have a very strong jumping-off point when reestablishing trade, which has led to its size and diversity today.

title: Dragonhold
content:
Dragonhold
A document detailing the capital city Dragonhold.

    Dragonhold is the captial of the region of Elezari. It is settled southwest of the neck of the Slithering Serpent, upon the remains of the first attempted settlement in the region. The name, Dragonhold, is earned from the establishing of the capital by the defeat of the dragons that once ruled Elezari.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	16%
Elf 	15%
Aasimar 	14%
Tiefling 	13%
Dwarf 	12%
Half-Orc 	10%
Halfling 	10%
Other 	10%
 
Geography
Dragonhold's immediate geography is two-fold; jungle and river. The Slithering Serpent splits just south of the capital, flowing past both on the western and eastern side. To the north, the characteristic dense jungles of Elezari grow. The trees rise tall, vines grow long and the bushes sit dense upon the ground, making traversal difficult, with the exception of the cleared main road that leads north into Neolora.  
Culture
Dragonhold is the heart of Elezarian culture. It is a mix of natural beauty and appreciation, calm and serene geography and determined people. Most of the nature that surrounds Dragonhold is untouched and unexplored, its natural beauty left completely intact.   The city is a sprawling metropolis, with high walls and impressive defenses that harkon back to the days of its establishment, Crafted almost entirely from stone, the city stands as an oasis in the ocean of green that surrounds it.   More than any other city in Amenor, Dragonhold's culture forges determined and skilled people. The spirit of the settlers and heroes that fought tooth and nail to establish the settlement lives on it its people. Most who live in Dragonhold are determined and stubborn people who pursue excellence in everything, themselves above all.   This causes outsiders to sometimes experience Dragonhold as a rough and intense city, though the skill of its artisans, artists and fighters cannot be denied. And even then, the intense nature of Dragonhold's people mixes in almost a mystical way with the natural beauty of its location, forming a distinct and memorable capital.  
History
In the middle of the Era of Settlement, settlers from Neolora sought their way south in order to establish more settlements. What they ended up finding was the region of Elezari, ruled by dragons. Despite their bravery, they failed to establish a settlement under the dragons spite and it would take many years before it was attempted again.   The second attempt took place in the middle of the Era of Growth, first settling Diroux as an outpost and then pushing further south, all the way to where that first settle was attempted so many years ago. Thanks to their bravery and determination, these settlers eventually managed to hold back the dragons and establish Dragonhold in the late years of the Era of Growth.
Founding Date
3rd of Chandran, 1113 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Capital
Population
~18 500
Location under
Elezari
Related Historical Events

    1616 ADD
    Settling of Dragonhold

title: Grayhold
content:
Grayhold
A document detailing the capital city Grayhold.

    Grayhold is the capital of The Grayhold region. Named after the region itself, Grayhold encapsulates the virtues and essences of this gray region. Grayhold is settled south of the Winding Peaks, in the middle of every other settlement in The Grayhold.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Dwarf 	15%
Human 	15%
Goliath 	13%
Gnome 	12%
Elf 	11%
Halfling 	10%
Tiefling 	9%
Other 	15%
 
Geography
Grayhold lies in the middle of The Grayhold, situated as the middle point between all the other settlements in the region, excluding Dereli. The city sits quite far south of the Winding Peaks, having a collection of tall mesas to its immediate north instead. Similar mesas rise to the southeast as well and together with the pine trees that dot their base, the city is slightly shielded from the winds that blow across the gray and dreary landscape.   Most of the terrain that surrounds Grayhold though is nothing more than stretches of cold, gray rock. This is shown most clearly in the fact that no farms or homes sit outside the walls of the city. Instead, all of the population resides within the city walls.  
Culture
As the central rendevouz of the entire region, Grayhold is the melting pot where all the ideas, ideals and virtues of the people of The Grayhold, mix. The results of this is a capital city that encompasses its region's values and culture perfectly. It is no coincidence that the city is named after the region itself.   Grayhold is a city that values honorable traits such as hard work and helping others. Perhaps not as great of a virtue but still very important to the people of Grayhold are things like fish and stone, the most common objects in The Grayhold which manage to find a new meaning of complexity here.   Though the region surrounding Grayhold is dreary and perhaps the city itself is slightly grim and colorless, these depressive traits are not perpetrated by its people. They have hope still that The Grayhold can be returned to the former glory of The Greenreaches and that such a future is reached by hard work and cooperation. In a word, the people of Grayhold are optimists, not pessimists.  
History
Grayhold was originally settled in the Era of Settlement. Back then it was a thriving capital, going by a different name: Greenhold. After The Ravaging of The Greenreaches, Grayhold was left abandoned and not resettled until relatively recently. The city has been built back up to an impressive sight and its people are determined to reclaim its former name.
Founding Date
23rd of Arbamand, 131 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Capital
Population
~13 000
Location under
The Grayhold
Related Historical Events

    139 ADD
    Settling of Grayhold

title: Oryen
content:
Oryen
A document detailing the capital city Oryen.

    Oryen is the capital of the region of Qanya. It is settled in the very middle of the region, just north of the Inverted Mountains. Oryen is the largest settlement in the region but not particularly large for a capital.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	21%
Elf 	19%
Gnome 	18%
Hafling 	13%
Dwarf 	10%
Tiefling 	6%
Aasimar 	4%
Other 	9%
 
Geography
The geography nearby Oryen is rather varied. West of the settlement, the plain swamp stretches out. Wetland, vegetation and insects fill the flat ground. Oryen is settled upon the western shore of a large lake, formed by a river that flows in from the ocean. All around this lake grows a forest of willow trees, surrounding the settlement by dense forests. Partially thanks to this lake, the vegetation growing near Oryen is plenty and moving through the nearby area is somewhat difficult.  
Culture
The culture in Oryen is somewhat varied because even though it may not be the largest capital, it still houses enough people to bring in influences from all types of people. Overall though, Oryen is generally seen as a kind, respectful and grateful town, where people are happy and welcoming. Though the surrounding swamps may not be the easiest place to live, there is plenty of natural resources for life to thrive and thus the settlement thrives as well. After all, leaving Oryen and traveling to Acadia is not particularly difficult of a journey so the people who remain are the people who wish to live in here. Oryen is also known for its unique construction, where the city is built in many different levels, upon man-made platforms that are meant to keep houses out of the treacherous, soft ground.  
History
Oryen was the second settlement established in Qanya, settled in the Era of Settlement, a few years after Fe'Dalar. The central placement of the settlement quickly made it a natural point for everyone in Qanya to visit, travel to or from and the status as capital naturally came from this. This natural role as capital has also been further defined by the people and leaders of Oryen, to the point that everyone in the region pretty much considers Oryen the heart and mind of Qanya.   Throughout the history of the region, Oryen has taken on the role of a protector, benefactor, parent and other similar roles for the other settlements in the region. It has always done its best to help the other settlements become as strong as they could be, sometimes even to their own detriment. The city suffered quite a bit during the Era of Divine Division, where a group known as Qandezar's Betrayers took control of the city for a number of years, before losing it once more as the other settlements rallied against them.

title: Sunhaven
content:
Sunhaven
A document detailing the capital city Sunhaven.

    Sunhaven is the capital of the region of Acadia. It is settled on the shore of the Sunlake, just north of the westernmost point of the Gray Smile. It is the largest settlement in all of Amenor, and its egotistical name clearly shows how proud the capital is over its own strength.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	17%
Elf 	15%
Dwarf 	15%
Halfling 	12%
Gnome 	11%
Aasimar 	9%
Tiefling 	7%
Other 	14%
 
Geography
Sunhaven is settled just at the shore of the Sunlake, which stretches out to the east of the settlement as far as the eye can see. Beyond its southern walls stands a large deciduous forest of tree, in the shade of the tall mountains of the Gray Smile. To the west of the capital fields of grass, the Evergreen Sea, reach far. Farms dot the landscape to the west along the road, while many man-made islands dot the waters of the Sunlake. Though the city of Sunhaven exists within its own walls, its reach through satellite buildings and creations goes far beyond its walls.  
Culture
To describe Sunhaven and its people with one word is perhaps not entirely right, but calling them prideful is incredibly apt. People who are not from Sunhaven can often find the inhabitants arrogant and self-centered, while they themselves would only describe themselves as people with a great love for their home.   It is however no secret that Sunhaven considers itself the greatest settlement in all of Amenor. Such a claim is subjective of course, but Sunhaven is undenaibly a grand city. Its walls reach high, its buildings built in the sturdiest material, decorated by the most skilled artisans. Its people are many, mixed and happy allowing for a great variety of activites to perform, places to see and people to meet. Even when Amenor gets colder and darker as winter comes ever closer, Sunhaven seems to stay warm and bright for just a little bit longer than other places at the same longitude.  
History
Sunhaven was not the first settlement established in Acadia, nor has it always been the largest one. It has always been well positioned though, with somewhat easy access from all the other settlements in the region. The city was originally settled in the Era of Settlement, at the end of the Taldarin Migration. As a matter of fact, it was the settling of Sunhaven that marked the end of that period of time.   Since then, the city has only steadily grown and over time it has grown to be the largest settlement in all of Amenor. It has been the seat of power of the region since its initial settling and its power has always been apparent, whether you have ever visited the city or not.

title: Vyr
content:
Vyr
A document detailing the capital city Vyr.

    Vyr is the captial of The Ashlands. It is situated in the eastern part of the island, nestled into the crevice of a mountain range. It used to be the second biggest city on the island before the eruption but as the capital fell, it earned the title of the largest city, as well as capital.


 
Species 	Percentage
Dwarf 	16%
Human 	15%
Elf 	15%
Gnome 	14%
Halfling 	13%
Tiefling 	11%
Aasimar 	9%
Other 	7%

 
Geography
Vyr sits just at the foot of large collection of mountains that stand between it and Ironbellow. The city is then cradled by large rocky spires that rise to the east of it, creating a little pocket for the city. It is this geography that protected it from much of the worst effects of the eruptions and why it still stands relatively unharmed today.
  That is not to say that it went unaffected however. The ground is still covered in ash, the ground is partially cracked and the jutting stone spires were partially broken apart during the eruption, raining down large pieces of rock upon the city.
 
Culture
Vyr used to be a large mining settlement, unearthing minerals and gems from within the nearby mountains. Farms lined the ground along the foot of the mountains, creating a very pictoresque settlement. The city was known for its fine stonework and gifted artists, especially painters.
  Since the eruption though, the city became the captial and has been tasked with so many other issues that its former identity has been lost. Refugees have made their way to the new capital and people have had to open their homes to the ones who have lost theirs. Tents are set outside the city walls and many soldiers walk the streets as officials plan strategies for the war that has enraptured them.
 
History
Vyr was settled long ago, in the Era of Settlement. Since then, it grew rather quickly as a place with much fine art but also many opportunites for work. It followed along the history of the Ashlands and quickly became the capital after Ironbellow's Fate.
Founding Date
26th of Heysatus, 114 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Capital
Population
~27 000
Location under
The Ashlands
Related Historical Events

    122 ADD
    Settling of Vyr

title: Whitepass
content:
Whitepass
A document detailing the capital city Whitepass.

    Whitepass is a city in the middle of Dar'Cortya, the capital of the region. It lies in the only pass through the Frostwall Mountains and has earned its name from that fact. It is a border, one that connects the tundra region of Dar'Cortya and Rimefrost Glacier.

 
Demographics
Species 	Percentage
Human 	22%
Dwarf 	18%
Elf 	17%
Gnome 	15%
Goliath 	14%
Other 	14%
 
Geography
Whitepass lies southwest of the Rimefrost Glacier, just beyond its edge. It is nestled in a narrow pass through the Frostwall Mountains, being a place that one has to travel through if they desire to get to the glacier. The mountains rise tall on either side of the settlement, making Whitepass an oblong city.   Though the town does not lie on the glacier, it is still covered in snow almost all through the year. The gray ground is very rarely seen and the case is the same for the surrounding mountains that provide the white barrier to the Rimefrost Glacier.  
Culture
The culture of Whitepass is a mix of two types of people. The first are the ones who enjoy the calm and quiet of the north, the ones who have moved away from the larger cities to take part of snow and peaceful times. The second are the ones who have moved south through the Rimefrost Glacier, those who have the wits to survive in cold and harsh environments.   In Whitepass, these two types of people mix in order to make a city that is well fortified against the cold, snow and harsh weather that occur this far north in Amenor, but is also well designed. The fortifications are both sturdy and pretty and there are more activities for the population to partake in beyond hunting or fishing.  
History
Whitepass is the youngest settlement in Dar'Cortya, founded late in the Era of Growth. It was founded by a union of people who came from the southern and northern settlements of Dar'Cortya. Since then, it has grown at an impressive rate, becoming the only city in the region.
Founding Date
26th of Gordrand, 1387 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
City
Population
~8100
Location under
Dar'Cortya
Related Historical Events

    1889 ADD
    Settling of Whitepass

title: Acadia
content:
Acadia
A document detailing Acadia, a region of Amenor.

    Acadia is a region in Amenor, situated in the western edge of the map, just south of Taldarin. It is a large region, the largest in Amenor in fact. It is an expansive grassland, dotted with mountains, rivers, lakes and forests. Being the largest region in Amenor, it is home to a large percentage of the population in its twenty-two settlements.

 
Geography
Acadia is the largest region in Amenor and most of its landmass is dominated by seas of green grass. There are also a number of mountain chains that rise above the green landscape, and many rivers that bring some blue into the green. A few lakes also dot the landscape and many forests of deciduous trees grow, but the defining characteristic of the landscape is definitely the seas of grass.   In the southern reaches of the region, where it border The Ashlands, lies Cazerni's Hollow. It is the natural land bridge between the two continents, now a no-mans land battlefield filled with broken constructions and slaughtered humanoids.   Northeast of Cazerni's lies a curving mountain chain known as the Gray Smile. The mountains curve to form the faint shape of a U, hence the name, as it looks almost like a smile. These mountains sit just on the shore of Sunlake, the largest lake in all of Amenor.   North of the Sunlake is the region of Acadia that is known as the Evergreen Sea. It is the fields of grass that stretch from north to south and west to east, essentially all the grassland in the region is the Evergreen Sea. However, as the grass fields are mostly uninterrupted by other natural features in the middle of the region, that is what most people tend to refer to when mentioning the Evergreen Sea.   In the western reaches of the Evergreen Sea lies the Wolfwood. It is the second largest forest in Amenor, and remains quite mysterious. It earns its name from the number of beasts that seem to make their home among the woods. This is also the reason why it has remained quite mysterious, as only the most brave of rangers dare to venture within.   In the eastern reaches of the Evergreen Sea, is the Greenshade Descent. It is a winding path of cliffs, flanked by mountains on either side. Though the cliffs themselves are of gray stone, they descend from the spring green lands of Acadia to the dark green lands of Qanya, hence the name.   Northeast of the Wolfwoods lies another woods, just at the foot of a wide mountain chain. These are the Gravewoods and are aptly named. They used to be a woods for the burial of the dead but have now been completely lost as the landscape itself seems to have died.   Finally, in the northern reaches of the region, just south of Macar, is a wide cove that cuts into the green fields of Acadia. This cove is known as Pearlcove and is named for the large amount of pearls and other sea gemstones that can be found in its waters.  
Settlements
There are twenty-two settlements in Acadia, as well as three points of interest. These are listed below.  

    Andesoar, a fishing village on the western coast of Acadia
    Biviul, a small village on the western coast of Acadia.
    Corum, a small fishing village just south of the Gray Smile.
    Elley, a village on the eastern shore of the Sunlake.
    Innhold, a village in the middle of the Evergreen Sea where most land traffic in Acadia passes through.
    Vel'Nar, a small village in the southwestern reaches of Acadia, near Cazerni's Hollow.
    Wastillé, a mining village in the northern reaches of Acadia.
    Te'Phra, a small town in the northern reaches of Acadia.
    Yssoné, a town lying at the end of a river north of the Gravewoods.
    Lordona, a town on the western shore of the lake that lies just south of the Gravewoods.
    Jelore, a town that lies amidst the hills on the eastern side of the Evergreen Sea.
    Monloe, a town that lies on the northern shore of a lake in the western reaches of Acadia.
    Port Imodal, a port town on the southeastern coast of Acadia.
    Wallsmount, a newly established fortification town just north of Cazerni's Hollow.
    Chandé, a harbor town on the coast southwest of the Sunlake.
    Goldwatch, a city of banks at the foot of a mountain west of the Sunlake.
    Elendar, a city on the northern shore of the Sunlake.
    Martee, a coastal city on the western side of Acadia.
    Soizie, a city on the eastern shore of the lake that lies just south of the Gravewoods.
    Gul'Dára, a city just west of the mountains on the border between Acadia and Qanya.
    Pearltide, a city on the shore of Pearlcove.
    Redemount, a mining city at the foot of the mountains just southwest of Pearlcove.
    Sunhaven, the capital of the region, lying on the western shore of the Sunlake.
    The Goldwatch Mountains, the incredibly profitable mountains that lie just west of Goldwatch. Enough gold has been unearthed from within it that a tunnel actually connects Goldwatch with the western part of the Evergreen Sea.
    The Pit, a strange and deep indentation within the landscape of the Evergreen Sea.
    The Merdepths, a small lake at the forking point of a river, rumored to hold intelligent underwater life.
    Nature's Sanctuary, a strange, deliberately made clearing deep in the forest northwest of the Merdepths.

 
Rule
Acadia is a kingdom, with the highest voice of authority naturally being the King. Surrounding the king is an entire court of people, who all perform different functions. There are guards, war advisors, cultural liaisons, court mages and many more people who all assist the king in ruling. In the end though, on any matter that arises for Acadia, the king has final say. Acadia has had this form of rule since its birth and the leader of the kingdom is chosen through bloodline. For the thousands of years that Acadia has existed, there have only been male rulers of the same bloodline, until now. Recently, Hayle D'Varzan, previous Spymaster of the king, took up rule of Acadia, marking the largest change in Acadia's rule since the beginning of time. With Acadia currently engaged in war, there are many who oppose Hayle and his stepping up on the throne, while there are many who support his campaign against the Ashlands.  
History
The history of Acadia is long and complicated, as is perhaps not too unsurprising for such a large region. Acadia's proximity to Taldarin made it among one of the first regions that was settled after The Commence. Furthermore, its habitable and bountiful environments made for a very pleasant place to settle. Combining these factors made Acadia grow like no other region in Amenor and it has remained the largest region, both in terms of landmass and population, ever since.   In the early years of Acadia's growth, before a real monarchy was established, the lands were far more dangerous than they later became. Without anyone to make an official ruling and dole out punishments, crime was rampant and it was stifling the growth of Acadia. Furthermore, the large amount of population made the number crimes quite a bit larger as well.   As such, the amount of new settlements and population growth that Acadia saw was certainly a great blessing but it was not entirely easy. To ensure prosperity for the future of them all, solve the disputes that arose and deal with all other problems that arise when there are many people in one place, Acadia's monarchy was eventually created.   It was during the creation of this monarchy, during the Era of Longevity, that The Ashlands came to Acadia and asked for future assistance, should Ironbellow erupt. Acadia, who had still not quite gotten control of their rampant growth, declined the offer by explaining that they needed to fix their own problems first. Displeased, the Ashlands returned home.   It took a number of years, but eventually Acadia fell into a more natural and safe rhythm of life. All settlements answered to the monarchy, and people could farm, mine or pursue their work without worry of robbery. Acadia continued their quick growth and entered into a great time of growth.   The Era of Growth came and passed without any major problems for Acadia. It had grown to an incredible powerhouse during the years of that era. It's larger landmass made it have no issue finding all manner of natural resources needed for survival, invention, cosmetics and more. This made Acadia an attractive trading partner and made them even richer than they already were. In combination with this, their large population made them the home to not only many soldiers, but also many great minds in all different kinds of fields. All in all, there was very little that Acadia lacked.   However, as the Era of Divine Division came, Acadia would face a massive problem. Beyond just the fact that many of Amenor's evil deities caused trouble throughout the region, Acadia's trade came almost to a standstill during this worrysome period in Amenor's history. Much of the trading that Acadia did was done via ship, almost all of which passed through or near the archipelago of Oricere.   Oricere had seen the Era of Divine Division as a perfect opportunity to strengthen their own position and power, while Neolora was embroiled in a civil war. They then targeted the other strongest region in Amenor; Acadia. Specifically, they began plundering and looting their trade ships. The advanced ship technology that Oricere possessed made them fearsome upon the seas and it took a while until Acadia had a surviving ship who could return and inform the region of the injustice currently being doled out.   Acadia then promptly approached the leadership of Oricere and asked them to rein in their population and replace all that had been lost. To this Oricere promptly answered no, and war was declared that very day. Though Acadia had the larger population and greater military strength, Oricere had spent every day since their infancy upon the waters and thus, the war lasted for many years. As such, Acadia approached the Ashlands much like they had approached them many years ago. Acadia asked for help in the war against Oricere but the new kingdom of Undelór that the Ashlands had become declined them, Acadia was on their own in this war.   Acadia's trade came to a complete standstill and they had to delve into the regions savings and gold to keep up with the Oricere who had become quite rich from all of their looting. During this time, almost all of Acadia was focused on ending this war as quick as they could and return to normal. Oricere, with their skill upon the seas and their overflowing coffers, managed to resist far longer than Acadia expected but they were eventually beat. As their looted riches started to run out, they quickly surrendered and Acadia accepted, hoping to return to the trading that made them so much stronger.   This war, known as The Trade War, lasted for 93 years and at the end of it, Acadia was left with a large standing army after their focus on ending the war as quickly as possible. However, they had not gained any new ground and even though the terms of the surrender left Oricere indebted to Acadia, they were flat broke and could not yet pay. In essence, Acadia was left with a huge money sink and a great deal of people who relied on the war in order to make their living.   Acadia tried to plead with the kingdom of Undelór for help, but they were resilient. They would not offer and jobs, food or help for the people left jobless by the lack of war. As such, there was only one reasonable course of action for Acadia with their large military force: war!   Acadia and Undelór clashed in battle only for a few years. Despite the superior size of Acadia's army, Undelór had a steadfast ally in Jyndera and together, they stood firm against Acadia. The Excess War was a stalemate for all its years and a tenuous peace was soon reached. The war had ended, but the relations between Undelór and Acadia were still shaky, at best.   Then came the Era of Peace and Acadia spent most its early years restarting old trade routes and taking care of people who had become accustomed to war. Eventually though, the region started to return to its former self. This continued all the way until the end of the era, at which point worries started to spread through Undelór about a recent minor eruption of Ironbellow. What this meant for Acadia left its people worried.   Only three years ago, Undelór approached Acadia once more, now with concrete evidence that Ironbellow was going to have a major eruption, soon. A massive diplomatic meeting was arranged to discuss the possibility of Acadia's help.   It was one of the largest diplomatic meetings in the history of Amenor. Correspondents from every region were there. Undelór pled their case unto the king of Acadia, King Erridan and the chamber fell silent. All who where there that day could sense the uncertainty in Acadia's leader, but none could sense the bristling emotion of Undelór IX. He stood across the table from Erridan for the many hours of the meeting, hoping against hope for salvation from the other side of the table. Whether or not that would come though, none will ever know. For before Erridan could give his answer, Undelór lept across the table, dagger in hand and sliced clean open the throat of Acadia's leader. The chamber broke out in absolute chaos and Undelór kept stabbing at the corpse before him with the vigour of something wholly possessed, only stopping when Erridan's second in command relieved Undelór's body of his head. With both kings dead and many of the guard along with them, the chamber fell silent once more. It was eventually broken by Erridan's second in command, Hayle D'Varzan, with one simple word: "War."   He then reached down to the still warm body of his king and pulled a Sending Stone out of his pocket and quietly spoke a few words: "The Ashlanders have sealed their fate. Awaken the beast. May Obiterra guide you." The stone was tossed aside and the remaining few of Undelór's guards were slaughtered while their homeland was set aflame by the greatest volcanic eruption in the history of Amenor.   The war that was birthed that day, known as The Ruiner's War, is still being fought. Even though the Ashlands is severely weakened from the eruption and loss of their king, along with their steadfast ally of Jyndera, they stand firm against Acadia to this day.

title: Dar'Cortya
content:
Dar'Cortya
A document detailing Dar'Cortya, a region of Amenor.

    Dar'Cortya is a region in the northeastern reaches of Amenor. The southern part of it contain a tundra covered in mountains and pine trees, while the northern part is a vast glacier surrounded by tall, snowy mountains. The region is home to six settlements, four on the glacier and two in the tundra.


 
Geography
The geography of Dar'Cortya consists of tundra and ice. In its southern reaches, a tundra surrounds the large lake of Ishir's Depths. The lake and its dark waters are dotted with multiple islands and has a narrow mouth leading to southern lands that has claimed many ships.
  To the west of Ishir's Depths is a large forest of pine trees, the Rimewoods. The frost-covered trees stand tall upon the gray ground and are the point of transition from the gray south to the whiter north of the region. This western part of land is the only reasonable approach towards the glacier in the north as rivers, denser forests and mountains block the eastern approach.
  Just north of the Rimewoods is a pass through the snow-covered mountains that surround and shield the glacier from the rest of Amenor. These mountains, The Frostwall Mountains, stretch across most of the coast of Dar'Cortya, only allowing access into the glacier at a few points.
  The glacier itself, Rimefrost Glacier, is the largest body of ice in all of Amenor. During the winters, the frozen ice and falling snow makes traversing it life-threatening. During the summers, the shining sun melts the fallen snow and reflects in the unearthed ice, creating a blinding traversal.
 
Settlements
There are six settlements in Dar'Cortya, as well as two points of interest. These are listed below.
 

        Kalte, a town in the northeastern reaches of the Rimefrost Glacier.
        Makan & Wokan, two villages in the middle of the Rimefrost Glacier.
        Whitepass, a city in the pass of The Frostwall Mountains.
        Desteré, a town on an island in the lake of Ishir's Depths.
        Gruun, a town east of the Rimewoods.
        Frostcrag Castle, a mysterious castle of ice and stone situated upon icy cliffs in the eastern reaches of the Rimefrost Glacier.
        Shipcutter Estuary, the narrow mouth of Ishir's Depths whose pointed rocks have claimed many a ship.


 
Rule
Dar'Cortya is essentially a kingdom, with one ruler who has final say on any and all matters. The only difference between a kingdom and Dar'Cortya's rule is that the ruler does not have the title of king, but instead Frostbaron or Frostbaroness. Currently, Dar'Cortya is ruled by the Frostbaroness Surina Coldborn who has ruled the region for the last 34 years when she took up the rule after her father. The rule of Dar'Cortya has been passed down the Coldborn bloodline for as long as the region has existed and the rule of each Coldborn lasts remarkably long, thanks to the frost magic that flows through the veins of this family. Surina is surrounded by a court of people who assist her rule but she naturally has final say on every matter, and most people of Dar'Cortya are very pleased with her rule.
 
History
The history of Dar'Cortya is a calm and quiet one. It's ruthless climate and cold environs makes it an unappealing place to settle. Thus, any who do so are those who are determined and desire to live in such cold. One might think that Dar'Cortya was settled from the south and then expanded into the Rimefrost Glacier but such is not the case.
  The first settlement in Dar'Cortya was Kalte, almost settled as far away from the rest of Amenor's civilization as possible. Despite the harsh climate, Kalte prospered and its population grew faster than was sustainable. That, combined with internal leadership struggles led to the emigration of many people from Kalte, people who would evantually form the villages of Makan and Wokan in the gorge of Rimefrost Glacier.
  At the same time, people from southern reaches continued their expansion north. They settled Gruun and Desteré by Ishir's Depths. As they continued to move north, they met up with settlers that moved south from Wokan and Makan. Together, the two groups settled Whitepass, the boundary settlement between the tundra and the glacier.

title: Elezari
content:
Elezari
A document detailing Elezari, a region of Amenor.

    Elezari is a region in Amenor, situated to the southeast on the map. It is a large region that remains mostly unexplored, even now in the seventh Era of Amenor. It's landmass is made up of the mainland, cut up by large, winding rivers, three large island and many smaller ones. The entire region is a jungle landscape with verdant growth and massive trees, making it a natural sanctuary. There are nine settlements in Elezari, with three of them being on the surrounding islands.


 
Geography
The geography of Elezari can be described quite accurately with mountains, rivers and jungle. Jungle makes up over 75% over Elezari's landmass, where the rest is mostly mountains that rise above the treetops and rivers that flow from their faces.
  The rivers that flow through the region are wide and rapid, stretching far into the landscape. These running waters then feed the verdant and varied growth of Elezari's jungles, making the trees grow tall, their canopies wide and the bushes and vines at their base growing thick and large.
  In the northern reaches of the region, where it borders Neolora is the only path that exists throughout all of Elezari. The towering jungle trees have been cut down to allow for unimpeded travel between the capital of Elezari and the region of Neolora. Beyond this one well-tended road to travel, no other paths lead into the large jungles and traversing them is certainly a challenge.
  Most people who travel through Elezari do so via ship, upon the largest river in all of Amenor, the Slithering Serpent. It's waters flow from the southern mountain chain of the Lifespring Mountains and snake their way through the emerald landscape, with smaller rivers diverging from the main "snake". All but two of the settlements in Elezari are settled in connection with a river, making seafaring the most common form of travel in the region.
  To the west of the Lifespring Mountains is a region of the dense jungle that is dotted with hills. This area is known as the Emerald Depths and though it is not the most unexplored or distant part of Elezari's jungles, it is the most mystical and very few have ventured there, with even fewer returning.
  To the north of the Emerald Depths lies an island dominated by large cliffs that divide the island into four sections. The jungle grows both upon and at the base of these cliffs, covering the island in Elezari's characteristic green. North of this island lies Elezari's largest island, Crescent Island, with three different settlements situated along the rivers that flow through the verdant jungle. Like a C, mountains rise around all but the eastern coast of the island, forming what is known as the Crescent Rise which, in turn, give its name to the island.
 
Settlements
There are nine settlements in Elezari, as well as three points of interest. These are listed below.
 

        Diroux, a city on the main path of the region.
        Vitrienné, a town at the foot of a lone mountain in the eastern reaches of the region.
        Faeiere, a town in the southern reaches of the region, where the Slithering Serpent flows into the ocean.
        Iveré, a village at the end of one of the branching rivers from the Slithering Serpent.
        Hotienné, a city on the western coast of the region.
        Gréchen, an isolated village on an island in the middle of the region.
        Foleyn, a town in the northeastern part of Crescent Island.
        Aliendor, a city in between the Crescent Rise.
        Dragonhold, the capital of the region, settled southwest of the neck of the Slithering Serpent.
        Gemgrave, a lone rising mountain known for its plentiful natural resources, east of the head of the Slithering Serpent.
        Jungleheart, the deepest part of the jungles on the northwestern island of Elezari.
        Water's End, a strange cavern at the final bit of the river that bisects the land of western Elezari, lying west of the Emerald Depths.


 
Rule
Elezari is a democracy, ruled by a council of members who represent different skills and cultures. The council, whose name is the Dragonheart Council, has six members who are known as Dragonheads. Each of these Dragonheads represents a different part of Elezari, with there being a representant for warriors, craftsmen, the cultured, merchants, the pious and the scholars. Each of these representants are democratically elected, one each year, meaning that a term as Dragonhead lasts six years. Together, the Dragonheart Council rules Elezari, allocate funds, solve problems and provide for the people who have elected them.
 
History
Elezari has a long history of trouble and chaos, beginning as early as the Era of Settlement. After people began to flood into the lands of Amenor following The Commence, it took quite a few years before the spreading settlers made their way to Elezari.
  In the passing of these years, along with the abundance of plantlife, water and habitable areas in the jungle, many creatures had come to take root in the region. Most notably, the wide rivers, rising mountains, many caves and massive trees provided many places for different types of dragons to lair in. It was these dragons that laid claim to Elezari before the first settlers showed up.
  It was made abundantly clear to these settlers that Elezari was hostile land ruled by dragons when they attempted to establish the first settlement in the region. Though it is exceedingly rare for dragons to work together and they were already competing with each other for control of the largest hoards and hunting grounds, hatred towards these newcomers was shared among all of them.
  As a result of this, the first attempted settlement (which today would be in the place of Cel'Ki) turned out to be a terrible tragedy. Dragons continuously sacked the settlement as it was trying to establish itself, murdering many and destroying even more. It was only the hardiest of settlers and greatest of champions that dared to venture into these dangerous unexplored lands so far from Taldarin though, and their stand against the dragons was legendary. It took almost two years for the dragons to manage to drive these first settlers from their lands, and only after the death of many of their own kin. This legendary first stand was a crucial time in Elezari's history, known as the Dragon's Dream.
  The few people that survived the Dragon's Dream returned to the newly established civilization on Amenor and word spread quickly about the dangers in the jungles to the south. As such, it would be many, many years before any others attempted to return to region and make it their home. It was not until the midst of the Era of Growth that settlers began to spread into the jungles once more and even then, only the most resilient dared to do so.
  The foothold of Diroux was established in the northern reaches of Elezari as the settlers slowly slew their way south, gaining more and more control over Elezari. Their scrambles and skirmishes with the dragons would go on for hundreds of years, with the humanoids making slow and incremental progress. Because even though the humanoid numbers had grown a great amount during the Era of Growth, the dragons had lived undisturbed in Elezari in many hundreds of years, fighting each other and thereby only letting the very strongest emerge.
  To slay these great, hundreds of men were needed and not a battle was fought without casualties. Slowly though, they made their way further south until they eventually came upon the site of the first settlement that was attempted so many years ago. This time of reclaiming the lands of Elezari from the dragons is known as Dragon's Destiny.
  At the site of the first attempted settlement, a heroic last stand to determine the future of Elezari is made. The strongest and oldest of the dragons attack the bravest of humanoidkind and the battle is legendary, even to this day. It is said that the river of the Slithering Serpent did not exist before this battle, but that it was created by the body of the greatest wyrm ever slain. In the end and after many casualties, the settlers claim their victory over the dragons and establish the, now capital of Elezari, Dragonhold. This event is known as Dragon's End.

title: Jyndera
content:
Jyndera
A document detailing Jyndera, a region of Amenor.

    Jyndera is region in Amenor, situated in the southern part of the map. It is a large desert landscape, filled with sandy dunes, jutting cliffs and mesas that rise from the hot ground. The region is one large island, barring one large island in its southern reaches. The desert stretches are rather barren and the region is only home to six settlements, most of them settled by the coast.

 
Geography
Jyndera is a region that is dominated by sand. It is a region consisting of two islands, the main one being larger than Taldarin, filled with nothing but desert, mountains and cliffs. On the eastern and western coast, mountains rise to frame the region while a large island lies in a bay by the southern coast, connected by a thin land bridge. Throughout the region, sand slides down steep cliffs and large rocks jut out from beneath. Mesas dot the orange landscape, rising like pillars far into the sky.   In the western reaches of the desert rises cliffs up from the sand, forming a large elevated circular region. Upon this circular plateau stand many mountains that reach even further. These mountains are knowns as the Sandstone Peaks and though they are the highest point in Jyndera, the mountains are some of the smallest in Amenor.   To the southeast of these mountains lies the small island that is one of the two islands that make up the region of Jyndera. The bay that surrounds this island is known as the Clearwater Bay, simply named so for its very clear waters where one can see far into its depths.   The desert that stretches out from coast to coast and covers almost all of Jyndera's landmass is known as Wynderialdessa's Wastes, named after the only deity who gives any form of beauty to the scorched sands. In the northeastern part of it, the mountains and cliffs narrow into a thin trench that approaches the capital. This approach is known as the Sandtrench.  
Settlements
There are six settlements in Jyndera, as well as two points of interest. These are listed below.  

    Novacera, a city on the western coast, established as a way to strengthen Jyndera's bond with The Ashlands.
    Feir, a small village built into the mountains on the western coast of the region.
    Seand, a town built below the sand level of Wynderialdessa's Wastes, at the bottom of a cliff.
    Echtar, a town settled in the middle of the desert, mostly home to those who withstand the heat better than most mortals.
    Venbaaz, a city settled on the island in the Clearwater Bay, well known for its inventions and innovations.
    Alitrá, the capital of the region, settled on the northeastern coast, just past the Sandtrench.
    The Deep Dunes, the northern part of the small island of Jyndera. This region is dominated by tall dunes and quicksand, with deep caverns running underneath the sands.
    Bloodspout, a tall mesa in the middle of Wynderialdessa's Wastes. Ever since The Exsanguination, the top of this mesa has continually bled.

 
Rule
Jyndera is the most modern form of democracy in Amenor. It is ruled by a parliament of different political parties who all desire to take Jyndera in slightly different directions. These parties are rather small, owing to the small population of Jyndera, and there has never been more than three parties in the parliament at once. This is partly due to there not being enough people in the region to make many more serious political parties and partly due to the three most established parties representing the population quite well.   As such, it is these three parties, which have around 30 members each, who sit in the parliament and vote upon matters of the region. The people of Jyndera vote for these different parties every two years and the ones who get the most votes by the people subsequently have the most votes in the parliament. To grossly simplify the situation, one can say that there is a party who lobbies for Jyndera's inventive nature and are more self-focused, there is a party who is focused on external diplomatic relations and a party who is more cultured.  
History
The history of Jyndera is somewhat lengthy, and tied up in the history of The Ashlands. Jyndera was settled rather quickly after The Commence, by settlers who sailed north out of Jerun in Taldarin. These settlers found a rather barren desert landscape but whether it was simple determination or they saw untapped potential, they still settled the lands.   As settlements started to be built around Jyndera, they were struggling with resources, both for building and for feeding their population. It was around that time that the first significant historical mark in Jyndera's history happened, as Venbaaz invented a way to clear the waters around their island. This quickly led a large increase in the amount of fish that was being caught, helping Jyndera with part of their food problem.   As Venbaaz continued to invent ways to help with the food problem, Jyndera was approached by The Ashlands. They had a problem where they had too much food and natural resources and Jyndera, who had better ways to take care of their food and increase its longevity, saw a perfect opportunity for an alliance. Jyndera received food and resources to build with and The Ashlands received ways to take care of their food. It was in the Era of Settlement that this alliance was formed, an alliance that remains strong to this day.   After these accords, a long time of peace and serenity passed for Jyndera as they continued to grow, invent and conquer the sands. When asked, Jyndera quickly agreed to help The Ashlands in case of an eruption of Ironbellow would occur. This agreement would go many, many years before having a chance to be honored however, and before it would ever occur, The Ashlands fell into ware with Acadia, The Excess War.   Acadia, being far larger and stronger, especially with the army that they had accumulated from The Trade War would quickly crush The Ashlands were it not for Jyndera's help. The inventions of Venbaaz, the hardened people of Wynderialdessa's Wastes and the seafaring skills of the coastals cities made Jyndera a valuable ally in this fight and together with The Ashlands they managed to hold the line against Acadia.   As a peace agreement was signed, Jyndera returned to their lands and prospered once more. This time of peace would last for many years and let the region build up a strong economy through their trading, unique inventions and resources in the desert. It should be noted that their trading with Acadia specifically was rather much less than what one would expect. The relation between The Ashlands and Acadia was shaky and since Jyndera were strongly allied with The Ashlands, they did little trading with Acadia. It also does not help that many people of Jyndera tend to see Acadians as soft, weak people who have everything spoon-fed to them.   Jyndera remained strong and blossoming until just a few years ago, when Ironbellow erupted and war broke out between The Ashlands and Acadia once more that Jyndera was pulled into battle once more. They quickly came to the side of their greatly wounded allies, acting as the shield that is currently still holding back the strikes of Acadia.
Type
Region
Included Locations

    Alitrá
    Echtar
    Feir
    Novacera
    Seand
    Venbaaz

    The desert is a place only for those not faint of heart, but we have mastered it. Our inventions, our people and our knowledge make us masters of our home, something very few in Amenor can say.
    — A common sentiment in Jyndera.

title: Neolora
content:
Neolora
A document detailing Neolora, a region of Amenor.

    Neolora is a region in the eastern reaches of Amenor. It is a large region, characterized by hills and cliffs. A few islands dot the waters around the green region, large and small alike. Neolora is the most densely populated region in all of Amenor, being home to seventeen settlements.


 
Geography
The geography of Neolora is quite varied, having rivers and lakes, cliffs and hills but also large stretches of plains and forests. It is mostly know for it's rougher geography though, the hills and cliffs that make up a large part of the landscape and clearly define the roads around them.
  In the northern reaches of the region, where it borders Dar'Cortya, a spreading river flows south into the region. This many-armed river is named Zeschla's Embrace, after the Webmother herself. Despite its grim name, the largest settlements in Neolora are settled around its flowing waters.
  Zescla's Embrace flows south and then turns west, flowing into the large lake called Triloch. The lake is aptly named as three rivers flow from it and three settlements are situated around it. Though the Triloch is smaller than Ishir's Depths, it has no landmasses within it, thus making it seem considerably larger.
  East of the Triloch are The Howling Cliffs, a region of land that is defined by the large cliffs that cut through the landscape. Sitting just on the coast, strong winds often blow in from the sea and cut through the cliffs stone, hence the name.
  Southwest of The Howling Cliffs is a solitary cliff in the midst of the plains. It rises from the ground to form the shape of a snake and has thus been given the name of Serpent's Skull. Southwest of this lies the Heaten Hills, a stretch of land dominated by bare and large hills.
  In juxtaposition to the Heathen Hills, there is The Faithful Forest which lies just on the other side of a river from the Heathen Hills. The origin of these names are owed to the history of Neolora. East of The Faithful Forest lies The Metal Mountains, a mountain range whose name comes from the Admyl settlement that lies there.
 
Settlements
There are seventeen settlements in Neolora, as well as three points of interest. These are listed below.
 

        Hendas, a village on an island in the northeast reaches of the region.
        Hértaux, a city at the first branching point of Zeschla's Embrace.
        Ilértaux, a daughter settlement to Hértaux, further down the river.
        Kjera, a village settled amidst the steep ground of The Howling Cliffs.
        Penchla, a town on the eastern coast of Neolora with a reputation for money-keeping.
        Celiera, a town and one of three settlements on the shores of the Triloch.
        Bayn, a city and one of three settlements on the shores of the Triloch.
        Adlora, the capital of Neolora and largest of the three settlements on the shores of the Triloch.
        Tarell, a town to the south of The Mark with a focus on tourism.
        Lendech, a small village to the west which lies somewhat isolated.
        Verendar, a town on the coast towards Xelley Island.
        On'Tha, a city between The Faithful Forest and Heathen Hills and the previous capital of Neolora.
        Jazerai, a city settled upon a rock formation bearing the resemblance of a snakes head.
        Taran, a city in The Metal Mountains, entirely dedicated to Admyls.
        Teh'Fal, a village in the southern reaches of Xelley Island.
        Navalja, a town on Xelley Island closest to mainland Neolora.
