        Xell, a city on Xelley Island , located on its most northwestern part.
        The Mark, a large crater left from the Marked Septet's final stand against a members own mother, west of the Triloch.
        Skyward, a mysterious and magical tower stood upon tall cliffs just east of Heathen Hills.
        Deepgorge, a deep valley in The Howling Cliffs that continually spirals down into the earth.
        Redquench, a dormant volcano on an island east of The Metal Mountains.


 
Rule
Neolora is, and has always been, a queendom. Ever since the birth of the region, it has been ruled by a single Queen of the same bloodline. Today, this is queen Elianora Selizerun, a young woman who recently took over the rule after her diseased mother passed in her sleep at only 51 years old. Though Elianora is inexperienced, she has a council of people who assist her in ruling the region but she has the final say on everything. The people of Neolora remain skeptical of her ability to rule, and she remains unmarried, leaving her a lot of room to prove herself.
 
History
The history of Neolora is long and tumultuous. In the early eras after Darkhearts defeat, the region was quietly settled and experienced a calm growth of population and settlements. This peace did not last too long however, as trouble broke out already in the Era of Longevity when people did not age and the consequences of this were major disagreements among the populace.
  Once the Era of War eventually ended and the Era of Growth began, the situation in Neolora was still uncertain. Many were still angry and decided to turn their anger towards the Admyls who had built themselves a settlement in the region. This hostility would tear the region apart slowly and it would be many years before it would start to fade away.
  The largest mark on Neolora's history though, is during the Era of Divine Division. During this time, there were many fanatical groups who sided with nefarious gods and, due to the population density in Neolora, many were active there. It is from this time that the places of The Faithful Forest and Heathen Hills have gained their namesake.

title: Oriceré
content:
Oriceré
A document detailing Oriceré, a region of Amenor.

    Oriceré is a region in Amenor, situated in the middle of the map. Though the region is not relatively small, it has the least amount of landmass of any region in Amenor, on account of it being an archipelago. Lying in the middle of Amenor, the region has influences from most other regions of the world, both geographically and culturally. There are only a total of four settlements in the region.


 
Geography
Oriceré is the smallest region in Amenor by landmass as most of it is made up of the ocean where its islands lie. This archipelago region has a great amount of variety upon its islands though and is certainly the most diverse region in all of Amenor. This diverse geography has been divided into six sections, where five of them are named after gemstones. The last sections is the centre of the region, which holds the capital and is colloquially known as the Capital Heart but is officially unnamed.
  The southern islands of the region are known as the Amber South. These islands are desert landscapes like Jyndera, south of them. They are warm and dry, with dunes of sand and mesas that stretch across a barren land.
  The western islands of the region are known as the Jade West. These islands are grasslands like Acadia, west of them. They are grasslands with far-reaching fields of grass and forests of deciduous trees, shaded by a few mountains.
  The northern islands are divided into two regions, the Tourmaline North and the Peridot North. The Tourmaline North are the northwestern islands of the region and they are marchlands like Qanya, west of them. These islands are wet and filled with insects, with dense willow tree forest that grow out of the muddy ground. The Peridot North are the northeastern islands of the region and they are hilly reaches like Neolora, east of them. These islands have uneven ground, characterized by hills and cliffs, dotted with mountains and forests.
  Finally, the eastern islands are known as the Emerald East. These islands are jungle landscapes like Elezari, east of them. They are dense jungles with verdant growth and vibrant colors, and a few mountains that dot the islands.
 
Settlements
There are four settlements in Oriceré, as well as three points of interest. These are listed below.
 

        Nor'Da, a village in the Peridot North known for its quiet and serene life and a good place to start a family.
        Reylee, a town in the Jade West a diverse town that sees much ship traffic.
        Yldesar, a city in the Tourmaline North well known for its strength in naval military.
        Cel'Ki, the capital of the region settled in the middle of it, as its very heart.
        Mount Searose, a volcano whose name has double meaning, harkoning to both its origin and its beauty.
        The Fluorescent Waters, a lake in the Tourmaline North known for its large population of dangerous sea creatures.
        Wachten, a lone castle in the mountains the northernmost reaches of the region with unknown owner(s).


 
Rule
Oriceré has always been ruled by the one person who has desired it the most. In essence, the region is a kingdom or queendom but with the notable difference of how the rule is transferred. The crown is usually not passed to the next of kin, though this has occurred, but rather to whoever challenges and defeats the current ruler in honourable combat. This somewhat barbaric practice has led to a few selfish rulers but if a ruler is proven to be too tyrannical, they will not last particularly long. This fear of mutiny and rebellion leads to Oriceré's rulers still providing the best for the region. It does however entail that no ruler has ruled beyond the human equivalent of 50, as their bodies start to fail them and younger people take their chance to rule.
  Another difference from a typical king/queendom is that the ruler is not referred to by such a title. Instead, they are known as Wave. Much like a king or queen though, a Wavetamer is surrounded by a council of people who assist in the rule of the region. As one can suspect, there tends to be a little more suspicion and paranoia in such a court though, given how the title of Wavetamer is available to anyone with enough guts. Whatever the rest of Amenor thinks of this practice, it has worked well for Oriceré and its people, as it provides more adaptability than the other regions tend to have.
 
History
The history of Oriceré is rather long, beginning in the Era of Longevity when Cel'Ki and Yldesar were settled in quick succesion. During their early years, these two settlements had a friendly rivalry that urged both of them on to further their innovation within the seafaring space quite greatly. During this time, they were the only two settlements in the archipelago and the region of Oriceré did not even exist yet.
  Even many years later, in the Era of Growth, when Nor'Da and Reylee were settled, there was not much interest in the official formation of an actual region. These four settlements worked closely together and traded freely with the other regions and settlements of Amenor. It was not until the Era of Divine Division that Oriceré would formally become a region of Amenor.
  During this era, the collaboration between the four settlements in the archipelago was tighter than before as the chaos of the era unfolded. Together, they saw an opportunity, brought on by the superiority of their naval forces. No other region had the seafaring skill or resources to stand up to them and so, a plan was hatched. The four settlements banded together and formed Oriceré, becoming an officially recognized region. The first action the region took was to heavily increase the taxes on trade ships that passed through the region.
  This was no small increase either, or a slow increase of tolls and taxes, but rather an outrageous increase clearly meant to line their own pockets with more gold than they knew what to do with. This naturally angered the other regions whose ships all sailed through Oriceré, whether or not that was their final destination to trade with. They did not quite have the resources to deal with both this taxation and the chaos of the era though, just as Oriceré predicted.
  What Oriceré did not predict though, was Acadia's stubborness though. They did not simply lie down and accept the taxes, but instead started to find alternate routes through the region, avoiding patrolling ships and cutting off trade with Oriceré. This was much to the dismay of Oriceré, but their seafaring skills were great enough that they often found Acadia's ships who tried to slip past them. And when they did, there would be no mere taxation but instead outright piracy.
  This was the last straw for Acadia and the beginning of The Trade War. Though it took almost a hundred years for Acadia's might to win against Oriceré's skill, they eventually succeeded. Fortunately for Oriceré, the costs of the war and subsequent peace were still less than what they had earned from the war itself and, even though they lost, they came out the other side a lot better, which is not something Acadia can say.
  Oriceré considered their loss a perfect success and reestablished trade routes with the other regions and, to a lessened degree, with Acadia as well. With their newfound wealth, they could springboard into a very successful mercantile region with great wealth and connections. Though resentment still lingers in the other regions about Oriceré's dirty tactics, especially in Acaida, none can deny its genius, execution and outcome. Since then, Oriceré has been a stable region with a calm history, rich coffers and powerful friends.

title: Qanya
content:
Qanya
A document detailing Qanya, a region of Amenor.

    Qanya is a region in Amenor, located just southeast of Taldarin. It is the second smallest region in Amenor, only being larger than The Ashlands. The region is a marshland, a swamp defined by soft, wet ground, uneven terrain, many small lakes and dense willow tree forests. The region is home to six settlements.


 
Geography
Qanya is a rather small region and its terrain makes life there a daily challenge. The region is very low and lies quite close to the sea level, which is what makes it the wetland that it is. There are a number of islands around the coast of the region, and even the mainland is somewhat fractured, dotted with many lakes and split by rivers, small and large alike. The many lakes around the region make the mossy ground wet and spongy and travel through the region is rather difficult off the beaten path.
  In the southernmost reaches of the region lies the Greenshade Descent. Though these cliffs are actually of gray stone, they refer to the change in ground color when moving from the bright green, grassy plains of Acadia to the dark green marshland of Qanya. Even though Qanya protested naming it the "descent" as it actively ignores Qanya's point of view of the area, Acadia did not consider this plea to have any merit, simply based on the population numbers.
  North of the Greenshade Descent sit the Inverted Mountains, a group of peaks that rise a short height from the middle of the swamp. Sitting in the middle of the swamp, these mountains do not rise especially high and, over time, the mere weight of the mountains has caused them to sink further and further into the swamp. At this point, almost half of the mountains are under the ground, hence the name.
  Northeast of these mountains is a very large and dense forest of willow trees known as the Bogwood. The forest grows along the eastern coast but is large enough to grow quite far inland as well. It is notoriously difficult to traverse due to the density of the forest and amount of lakes, small and large, that the trees rise from. As such, the Bogwood remains rather unexplored, even to this day.
  West of the Inverted Mountains lies Mistmire. It is a large lake with a few small island in its cold waters. Most notably though, the lake is always blanketed in a thick layer of fog which obscures its surface. If one manages to look through the fog however, they would see the waters of the lake moving as of its own accord, no matter how the wind blows. That is just one of the strange phenomena that seems to occur at Mistmire, which has earned it the supernatural reputation that it has today.
 
Settlements
There are six settlements in Qanya, as well as two points of interest. These are listed below.
 

        Aldemar, a small and somewhat isolated town in the northern reaches of the region.
        Juva, a small village just at the foot of a mountain range along the coast.
        Ildros, a town settled on the shore of Mistmire, well known for mystical and spiritual connection.
        Oena, a port town settled on a peninsula along the eastern coast of the region.
        Fe'Dalar, a city settled near the Greenshade Descent with a significant economic class divide.
        Oryen, the capital of the region, settled in the middle of Qanya.
        The Gray Crossing, a large natural jagged cliff that bridges a river in the north of the region. The stone has been hollowed out, allowing for traversal through it though it is not entirely safe.
        The Great Drain, a large sinkhole just south of Juva that seems to swallow everything in the vicinity.


 
Rule
Qanya is a democracy, ruled by a council of members who represent different skills and cultures. The council, whose name is the Avalon Council, has six members who are known as Avelars. Each of these Avelars represents a different part of Qanya, with there being a representant for warriors, craftsmen, the cultured, merchants, the pious and the scholars. Each of these representants are democratically elected, one each year, meaning that a term as Avelar lasts six years. Together, the Avalon Council rules Qanya, allocate funds, solve problems and provide for the people who have elected them.
 
History
Qanya has had a somewhat shaky history throughout its existence. From the early years where the settlements where trying to find their place and a life to lead within the swamp, to the worries that exist today, for example the divide in Fe'Dalar. What has been consistent throughout its history though, is how other regions have underappreciated the region and have often left them to fend for themselves.
  The best example of this is Oryen's multiple leadership changes. When Qanya was first being settled, there was a great deal of collaboration and assistance between each of the settlements. They shared resources between each other freely, lending workers, knowledge and much else without any issues. This assistance was a large reason as to why Qanya even managed to estblish themselves within the tough environment that the swamp is.
  These beneficial alliances continued for thousands of years, where the main agent of help was Oryen. They had the largest population and amount of resources, by far and were instrumental in helping other settlements to continue to grow. This was in fact most of what Oryen did through the Era of Growth and some people started to think that they prioritized other settlements in the region higher than their own city. This belief was not helped by the fact that Oena had recently been settled and Qanya, primarily Oryen, was doing a lot of trade with the other growing regions.
  These feelings and beliefs were nothing but rumors and whispers at this point though, but it would soon become worse. When the Era of Divine Division came bearing its chaos, Qanya began to struggle a bit. It would seem that Oryen had spread itself too thin, trying to provide for every settlement in the region. In trying to care for everyone, they had left themselves a little to vulnerable to unforeseen chaos, something that was very common in Amenor in the Era of Divine Division.
  Schemes from evil deities, people that aligned with them, people unable to perform their normal jobs due to dangers, rampant beasts and dragons, all of these things were not too uncommon in the Era of Divine Division. This ended with Qanya having to do its utmost to stay in one piece to combat all of these threats. Though the different settlements fared differently well, Oryen was certainly hit the hardest, losing almost half of its population.
  When the era came to an end and the situation had stabilized, Qanya could start to pick themselves off their knees and dust themselves off. This work which began in the Era of Peace only had a year or two to proceed before another disaster would strike. The people who believed that Oryen was making mistake after mistake now felt that they had been proven right and many more had joined their cause.
  This group which came to be known as Qandezar's Betrayers, made their move against Oryen on the 24th of Arbamand, 4 ADD, Era of Peace. They had believed for a long time that Oryen's constant benevolence was slowly killing it, in the same way that Qandezar slowly drains all life, and had gotten their name from that. On this night, they assualt Oryen, kill its leadership and install themselves as the new leaders of the capital. As most people in Oryen were on their side at this point, their success was inevitable and though many lives were lost, they had gotten what they wanted.
  Naturally, their first change to the order of business was to completely cut off all other settlements in Qanya from Oryen. They isolated themselves and celebrated their victory as they started to rebuild. With no help from the capital, the other settlements quickly realized the trouble they were in. With Qandezar's Betrayers not interested in the actions of the other settlements, they could begin to rally a counterattack.
  It took multiple years for the other settlements to rally in Fe'Dalar and then assualt Oryen, but eventually they did. Oryen still had the largest population and many of them were still loyal to Qandezar's Betrayers. With some help from those who lived in Oryen but were loyal to all of Qanya though, the rebelling settlements managed to fight their way to the leaders of Qandezar's Betrayers and remove them from power. They were swiftly executed and descendants of the original leaders were installed in Oryen once more as things finally returned to normal.
  This event, known as The Wilting, is by far the largest catastrophe in Qanya's history and its population after these events had shrunken drastically. Despite this warfare and bloodshed, the era came to be known as the Era of Peace, a clear sign of how most of the other regions see Qanya.

title: Taldarin
content:
Taldarin
A document detailing Taldarin, the First World.

    Taldarin, The First World existed before the full creation of Amenor. It is a varied and storied world that is the origin of almost all people that now live in Amenor. It existed for four eras before the creation of Amenor was fulfilled and Taldarin became a part of it.

 
History
After the Nine managed to banish Darkheart, they set about their creation of the world of Amenor. Unfortunately, their plans proved too grand for the time that they had before Darkheart's return. As such, all that they had time to forge was Taldarin. Four eras later, Darkheart was finally defeated and the Nine could finish crafting Amenor in peace. During those four eras however, Taldarin was all that existed in the Material Plane.  
Before Eras
It was, and still is, a diverse land with a rich history. The history of Taldarin began before time had evolved as a concept. Uncountable time passed before Huwander created what today is understood as the passage of time.  
First Era
In 657 BD, the first era of Taldarin began. King George Kandran established a kingdom in his name and rallied all folks of Taldarin, near and far, to his city. This city, Leynel, rose as a paragon of hope in a yet untamed world and the people of Taldarin thrived.   This first era lasted for 1274 years before the city of Leynel and the Kandran kingdom would come crashing down. The stories and the rumors about its downfall were many but it has since been learned that George Kandran and the Nine were responsible for this fall.   After establishing Leynel, George Kandran took nine of his most trusted warriors and roamed the lands of Taldarin, killing all roaming beasts and dangerous monsters they came across. With the land mostly wiped of danger, George desired more attention from the Nine, more power.   They refused his pleas and George grew resentful. When Darkheart started to return, the Nine reached out to George once more, seeking champions to battle this evil. George's resentment was enough for him to deny this honor however, and with the Nine going into hiding and George's mind slowly breaking apart, Leynel fell to the monsters who started to creep out of the shadows, as the first era ended.  
Second Era
The second era of Taldarin lasted for only 303 years. The lands were overrun with monsters and beasts, roaming and slaughtering each other. No settlements remained standing after the end of the first era and very few people survived. Those who did, hid away in dark corners of the world, biding their time and trying to survive.   This miserable existence would persist for a bit more than 300 years before they would be given a chance to return to glory. A strange shockwave washed over Taldarin and rid the lands of most monsters. People came out of hiding and drove off the beasts that remained as they started to focus on building a future.  
Third Era
The third era was the longest of Taldarin's history. People spread across the previously dangerous lands, building settlements and families. This era of growth, mirrored many years later by the 3rd Era of Amenor, saw the construction of Taldarin as it became most of what it is today.   Lasting for almost 1600 years, Taldarin's third era was characterized by peace and growth. People were happy, safe and content. This only ended when a great tower was constructed in hopes of contacting the Nine, who had been absent for so long. This attempt failed and with the subsequent panic, the fourth era was ushered in.  
Fourth Era
The fourth era was characterized by uncertainty and troubled. The Nine had been gone for a long time and no one knew where they had gone. In reality, they had disappeared into hiding due to the imminent approach of Darkheart.   This would come to be the shortest era of Taldarin's history, lasting only a little bit more than 73 years. It was ended when the Nine chose five people to become their champions who did not disappoint. Equipped with the Paragons of the Nine, this group of people (The Marked Ones), made their way to Darkheart's domain and slew him together with the help of the Nine. With its death, Amenor's creation was ready to be finished.  
Geography
Although Taldarin is not as large as Amenor is, it still has much varied geography. Plains, grassland, desert, swamp, jungle, canyon, archipelago are all geographic features that can be found in Taldarin. The continent of Taldarin is divided into four different regions, each with their own rulers.   Furthermore, since Taldarin existed as the entire world before the Nine finished their creation, the climate and weather of Taldarin is different from the rest of the world. The entire region exists within its own ecosystem, climate and weather. This essentially means that, even though Taldarin may be at the same longitude as another region, it will not have the same climate.  
Esteraín
Esteraín is the largest region of Taldarin, a region of plains, lakes, forest and the Elcaren Canyon. People farm the lands, hunt in the forest or fish in the lakes. There are a total of ten settlements in Esteraín, each of them ruled by its own townsmaster. Though the townsmasters are in charge of the cities, they all answers to the Queen of Esteraín. Ever since the founding of the region, the ruler has been the eldest woman of the Esteraín family.  
Macar
Macar used to be by far the richest region of Taldarin. An archipelago focused on trade, with people who love the sound of coin. However, in the wake of Darkhearts defeat and the actions of The Marked Ones, a great political rift was introduced in Macar. It has been mostly resolved since then but their time of uncertainty brought them closer in line with the other regions of Taldarin. Macar is ruled by a council of all six townsmasters, known as the Vanya. Macar is not only know for its love of coins however, as Macarian milk is a well-known delicacy throughout Amenor.  
Idra
Idra is the smallest region of Taldarin, only being home to three settlements. It is located in the southeastern reaches of the continent, a large desert. The harsh landscape of these settlements have driven the Idras to be inventive people. They pride themselves on their creation and inventions and their ruling council, Autá, is made up of the five most innovative families.  
Luvexen
Luvexen may be larger than Idra, but the deep jungles and swamps that make up its region makes it the least populated region of Taldarin. Only four settlements exist here though they are remarkable well-crafted, taking advantage of the geography that surrounds them. In the time before Amenor, Luvexen was ruled by a council of elder men that abused their power. Early in the fourth era of Taldarin, this power structure was shaken and since then, the people on the council have all been democratically elected, making the politics of Luvexen far more stable.  
Rule
The rule of Taldarin has changed dramatically since The Commence. Since it used to be the entire world, there was no need for a singular ruler of all the regions (which is not to say that some didn't try for this anyway). The regions of Taldarin all developed different way to rule themselves and then collaborate, but after the Commence and the creation of Amenor, it quickly became clear that Taldarin itself needed to be one whole region.   While Taldarin retains its own regional division, it now also has a council that represents the entire region of Taldarin in global diplomacy. This council is made up of five members, one from each region of Taldarin. For the regions with singular rulers, the decision of representant was easy, while it has caused a lot of political strife in the regions ruled by councils of people. Nevertheless, this is how the rule of Taldarin has changed. The most notable member in Taldarin's Council is certainly Kelral Yorboth, mayor of Targaraen and a creature who has been alive since long before the Commence.

title: The Ashlands
content:
The Ashlands
A document detailing the Ashlands, a region of Amenor.

    The Ashlands is a region in Amenor, situated in the southwestern corner of the map. It is a small region, consisting of one main, circular island and a bunch of smaller ones scattered around it. It used to be known as the Kingdom of Undelór until rather recently, when King Undelór IX died. Along with that, the large volcano that is situated on the middle of the island had a massive eruption, covering the island in ash, hence the name. 

 
Geography
The geography of the Ashlands is, to no surprise, governed greatly by the activities of the volcano on the island. It used to be that the nutrient-rich soil that the volcanic activity provided made the island a great place for farming. Many farms dotted the landscape, trees and flowers growing tall and proud besides them.   Nowadays however, the Ashlands it no more than a mere shadow of its former self. The recent eruption choked the sky in smoke and ash rained down all over the island. Every part of it is now covered in a layer of gray ash that quickly flies around when disturbed. Whenever heavy winds blow, an ashstorm often follows and when it rains, the ash becomes thick and heavy, making it tough to traverse.   Not only did the eruption spew ash all over the island, it also erupted with enough force to crack the land apart. The stone nearest the volcano is broken and cracked, shards of stone jutting at every which angle. The rest of the saw hills pushed up towards the sky and stone pillars forced up out of the ground, creating a broken and shattered landscape.   In the middle of the island lies Ironbellow, the largest volcano in all of Amenor. Even though it used to bring great bounty and fertile lands, it's recent eruption brought ruin to the landscape surrounding it. When it erupted, it created a great tear in the land all the way out to the sea. The former capital fell in and the rift was quickly filled with water.   To the west of Ironbellow lies Graveshallow, a shallow, three-pointed lake. It is known for its treacherous and dangerous waters, even before the eruption. South of Ironbellow lies a massive, naturally formed cliff. Five massive rocky spires jut out of the cliffs, forming something that looks like a giant hand. It is known as the Hand of Terra.  
Settlements
There are two settlements in the Ashlands, as well as one point of interest. These are listed below.  

    Koenadar, a city on the border towards Acadia.
    Vyr, the capital of the region, nestled in behind large mountains on the eastern side of the island.
    The Riftlands, the landscape neareast Ironbellow that is now broken and shattered, containing many lost places and people.

 
Rule
The Ashlands used to be the kingdom of Undelór and it was this bloodline of Kings that have ruled the region since its birth. However, after Ironbellow's eruption, the region has been utterly devastated and its king has been murdered. Still at war with Acadia, the region has yet to recoved from this tragedy and reestablish a proper form of rule. Currently, the highest voice of authority in the region belongs to the highest ranking war officer but it is unknown how the rule of Ashlands will turn out after the war.  
History
The history of the Ashlands is a long and complicated one. It is closely tied to that of the history of its two neighboring regions, Acadia and Jyndera. After The Commence, it took a few years before people hade made their way through Acadia and found bountiful land upon the island.   Eventually settlements started to be formed though, most of them settled close to Ironbellow and taking advantage of the enriched soil. So fortunate were these settlements that the amount of food gained from the farming started to become greater than the amount of people that needed it.   A better way was needed to ensure the longevity of of their food, or more people who needed it was required. Fortunately, the Ashlands found both of this in their neighboring region of Jyndera. The settlers who made their home in those harsh deserts needed to be very careful with the scarce amount of food they created and so had developed and brought with them from Idra, many techniques and inventions to do just that. An alliance seemed natural and accords were quickly struck. The Ashlands would provide food to Jyndera and Jyndera would provide any and all inventions that helped to managed, develop and sustain that food. These accords formed a strong alliance in the Era Of Settlement, one that stands to this day.   Many years of serenity passed and the desire to keep it so was strong. The biggest risk to the fortune of the Ashlands was naturally the temper of Ironbellow. As such, the Ashlands spoke with their neighbors about their assistance if such an event would come to pass. Given the strong alliance with Jyndera, they quickly agreed to send whatever aid was needed if such a tragedy would strike. Speak with Acadia however, they declined to enter into any agreements with the Ashlands, explaining that they needed to prioritize taking care of their own growing population. This was during the Era of Longevity.   Though this was not a surprising answer, it was a worrisome one. Acadia was far larger, had a far larger population and the lack of any official cooperation caused worry among the Ashlands about the intentions of their grand neighbor. Though they gracefully accepted the declination of the offer, the Ashlands remained wary against Acadia.   Then came the Era of Divine Division and Acadia wound up entangled in a war with Oricere after their pirating efforts. In their moment of crisis, they asked for aid from the Ashlands which had now become the Kingdom of Undelór. Undelór I saw their plea for aid and reciprocated the answer that Acadia had given last, leaving them to their own strife.   Though it took many years, the The Trade War eventually came to an end, an end where Acadia came out victorious, with a large standing army but very little ground gained. With no war left to fight and no places to plunder, many of Acadia's people were left without a job and Acadia started to fall into a post-war regression. Once more, they turned to the Kingdom of Undelór for food and aid sent to their realm. During the negotiations, Acadia stressed that their army was taskless, turning the request for aid into a thinly veiled threat. The negotiations quickly broke down and a promise other than aid was made: war!   Acadia and Undelór clashed in battle only for a few years. Despite the superior size of Acadia's army, Undelór had a steadfast ally in Jyndera and together, they stood firm against Acadia. The Excess War was a stalemate for all its years and a tenuous peace was soon reached. The war had ended, but the relations between Undelór and Acadia were still shaky, at best.   Then came the Era of Peace, and all was calm for Undelór for many, many years. It was not until towards the end of the era that Ironbellow had a small eruption. Any larger disaster was avoided but, after a few years, it was discovered that it was not long before a major eruption. This was but the calm before the storm, a large eruption was coming.   Jyndera had already sworn to assist Undelór in the case of such a crisis but more help was still needed. Once again, now with concrete evidence, Undelór turned to Acadia in order to put aside past trifles and ask for help in the face of this coming disaster.   It was one of the largest diplomatic meetings in the history of Amenor. Correspondents from every region were there. Undelór pled their case unto the king of Acadia, King Erridan and the chamber fell silent. All who where there that day could sense the uncertainty in Acadia's leader, but none could sense the bristling emotion of Undelór IX. He stood across the table from Erridan for the many hours of the meeting, hoping against hope for salvation from the other side of the table. Whether or not that would come though, none will ever know. For before Erridan could give his answer, Undelór lept across the table, dagger in hand and sliced clean open the throat of Acadia's leader. The chamber broke out in absolute chaos and Undelór kept stabbing at the corpse before him with the vigour of something wholly possessed, only stopping when Erridan's second in command relieved his body of his head. With both kings dead and many of the guard along with them, the chamber fell silent once more. It was eventually broken by Erridan's second in command, Hayle D'Varzan, with one simple word: "War."   He then reached down to the still warm body of his king and pulled a Sending Stone out of his pocket and quietly spoke a few words: "The Ashlanders have sealed their fate. Awaken the beast. May Obiterra guide you." The stone was tossed aside and the remaining few of Undelór's guards were slaughtered while their homeland was set aflame by the greatest volcanic eruption in the history of Amenor.   Though the people who live upon the now ruined island still refer to themselves as people of the Kingdom of Undelór, the words of Hayle stuck with the rest of Amenor and the region is now called the Ashlands. The war that was birthed that day, known as The Ruiner's War, is still being fought. Even though the Ashlands is severely weakened from the eruption and loss of their king, along with their steadfast ally of Jyndera, they stand firm against Acadia to this day.
Type
Region
Included Locations

    Koenadar
    Vyr

Related Historical Events

    3320 ADD
    Ironbellow's Fate
    384 ADD
    The Ashland's Proposition
    147 ADD
    The Ashlands & Jyndera Ally

    What we have may be but a shadow of what we used to have but it is not we who made that shadow.
    —  A common sentiment in the Ashlands.

title: The Crooked Tooth
content:
The Crooked Tooth
A document detailing The Crooked Tooth, a region of Amenor.

    The Crooked Tooth is a landmass in the northwestern reaches of Amenor. It is a frigid and cold land, covered in snow and ice. It gains its name from the fractal form that the landmass has, looking like one long, curled tooth. An archipelago of small, snowy islands make naval navigation through the area a challenging task.


 
Geography
The lands of The Crooked Tooth are unforgiving, cold and cruel. A layer of snow lays upon the frozen ground always, only the depth of it waxing and waning throughout the year. Chilly winds blow across the snow-covered tundra and ice floes that slowly drift through the freezing waters.
  The many islands that dot the sea around the main landmass have little variety. They are empty islands of stone and snow that house a sparse forest of pine trees or tall, rocky cliffs. The southern reaches of the main landmass is the only part of The Crooked Tooth that sees the ground during the warmest months of the year. The peaks of the lone mountain here rises to heights high enough that they are eternally covered in snow, always holding the threat of an avalanche.
  The middle part of the main landmass is characterized by a dense forest of pine trees. Their snow-covered barks give a much desired protection against the beating winds. In this dense forest, the layer of snow often grows deep, making traversal through it very difficult if one is not properly equipped.
  The northern reaches of The Crooked Tooth is a frigid wasteland of snow and cliffs. Pine forests stand sparsely across the area, while cliffs dot the landscape and make traversal difficult. Ascending or descending down the icy stone wall cliffs is a quick way to death.
 
Settlements
There are two settlements in The Crooked Tooth, as well as one point of interest. These are listed below.
 

        Wehran, a town in the southern reaches of The Crooked Tooth.
        Caldera, a small village just north of the dense pine forest of The Crooked Tooth.
        The Silo, a ruined and abandoned research facility that used to belong to The Caged Cabal.


 
Rule
The rule of the Crooked Tooth is easy to describe: there is none. The region only consists of two settlements, a village and a town. There only lives people who try their best to get by and the Crooked Tooth has no impact on global politics, in fact it is barely recognized as a region in that aspect. Technically, Wehran has a Townsmaster but they have no impact on a global scale.
 
History
Despite its proximity to Taldarin, The Crooked Tooth was not settled upon quickly. While the harsh environment made it an unappealing place to settle, the major factor was the difficulty of approach. Navigating through the ice floes, many tiny island and jutting rocks was a death sentence for most inexperienced navigators.
  It was not until 303 ADD in the Era of Growth that the first settlement in this frosty archipelago would rise. Approaching from the south, sailing to the main landmass proved much easier and Wehran was established where landfall was made. A few years later, in 330 ADD, the village of Caldera was founded north of Wehran.
  The most notable event in the history of The Crooked Tooth has to do with The Caged Cabal. In the year 977 ADD of the Era of Growth, they establish a research station nicknamed "The Silo" in the northernmost reaches of The Crooked Tooth. This station would stand for 1075 years before it falls into ruin, the cause still unknown to this day.

title: The Grayhold
content:
The Grayhold
A document detailing The Grayhold, a region of Amenor.

    The Grayhold is a region in the northern reaches of Amenor. It is a land of tundra, snow and ice, all of it framed by tall mountains and winding rivers that cut through the gray ground. The region is home to six settlements, making it one of the smaller areas of Amenor. 


 
Geopgraphy
The region of The Grayhold is large enough that its geography is somewhat varied. Though it exists from the northernmost latitude to more southern latitude that would constitute warm summers and grassy fields, this variation does not exist in the Grayhold due to its history.
  In the northernmost reaches of The Grayhold one can find The Broken Stretch. It is a broken archipelago of small islands, surrounding larger oblong landmasses that stretch out over Taldarin. Covered in snow all year round, surrounded by icy waters and cold winds, it is a cold and cruel place.
  To the east of The Broken Stretch lies the Frostreach Glacier. It is the largest glacier on Amenor, stretching from coast to coast of The Grayhold. The ice lies eternal, through winter and summer alike, only the depth of the snow upon it varying throughout the year.
  To the south of the Frostreach Glacier lies almost all of The Grayhold's settlements. Situated upon the coast and along rivers, these settlements may have had their glory days yet they still have much left to give. The Winding Peaks cut through the middle of The Grayhold as its many rivers reach both the eastern and western coast.
  Contributing to the flow of water to the eastern coast is Deeploch. It is a lake situated upon a tall cliff, its waters supposedly reaching far far down, passing even the ground level at the foot of the cliffs. Between the Deeploch and The Winding Peaks lies Grayhold, the capital of the region.
 
Settlements
There are six settlements in The Grayhold, as well as two points of interest. These are listed below.
 

        Dereli, a village in the northern reaches of the Frostreach Glacier.
        Mern, a village just east of The Winding Peaks.
        Kech'Nal, a town southeast of the Deeploch.
        Breakwind, a town in the southwestern reaches of The Grayhold.
        Loftal, a city just west of The Winding Peaks.
        Grayhold, the capital of the region situated between The Winding Peaks and the Deeploch.
        The Cleft, a region of the Frostreach Glacier characterized by deep canyons and cracked ice.
        Winter's Peak, one of the tallest mountains in Amenor that many have foolishly tried to ascend.


 
Rule
The Grayhold is a democracy, where the people vote to elect one single person to lead the region. This ruler, known as Plaguebreaker, is the person who has the last say on anything and everything that concerns the region. They are surrounded by many other people who assist in the ruling of the region, but it is the Plaguebreaker themselves who have final say. Currently, this position is held by Austine Derelar, who has ruled for the last two years, halfway through their term.
 
History
The Grayhold has a turbulent history. Much of the regions geography and climate has changed over the course of Amenor's history. Due to its proximity to Taldarin, it was one of the first places people settled after The Commence. Settlements arose in the southern reaches of the region, in green lands with winding rivers and great bounties.
  The settlements of Loftal and Breakwind were both established in the early years of the Era of Settlement. Over time, people eventually explored more of the land, settling Mern, Grayhold and Kech'Nal as well. The settling of Dereli would not happen for many more years however.
  The Frostreach Glacier and The Broken Stretch existed when people first made their way to The Grayhold. Just south of the glacier was a bit of tundra but south of where Mern lies today, the lands were green and fertile. That was until Puzkera put another of his destructive plans into action.
  He concocted a great plauge and unleashed it upon Amenor, infecting the first humanoid within Grayhold. The great plauge rampaged through the city and quickly spread across The Grayhold. So potent was this plauge that the victims of it succumbed to death before they had the chance to even leave The Grayhold. As such, the plauge was contained to only one region of Amenor where it killed most of the population. Some managed to survive by heading far north, across the Frostreach Glacier where the cold quickly killed the disease. These few survivors settled Dereli and never returned.
  Over time, especially after the end of the Era of Divine Division, The Grayhold would come to be resettled. The abandoned cities and towns would come to house populations of people again. Only now they would be surrounded by gray stone. The fertile fields that used to exist were long dead and the region lost its previous name, The Greenreaches and got its new name: The Grayhold.

title: Divine Realms
content:
Divine Realms
A document detailing the divine realms of Amenor's gods.

    The ultimate fate of a mortal's soul is decided through every look, thought, action and inaction.
    — Kannder, Goddess of Justice.

 
Overview
Arranged in a two great concentric circles, the Inner and Outer Planes make up the cosmology of Amenor. The Inner Planes hold the Material Plane and its two mirror planes, the Feywild and Shadowfell. Surrounding these three planes are the four Elemental Planes: Plane of Water, Plane of Earth, Plane of Fire and Plane of Air. Their relative position does not matter, but imagine them as a clock, with the Plane of Water at 12 o'clock and the subsequent planes three hours later. The Elemental Planes also have overlapping regions with their neighbors, creating the following regions: Plane of Ooze (Water & Earth), Plane of Magma (Earth & Fire), Plane of Ash (Fire & Air) and Plane of Ice (Air & Water). Surrounding all of these planes on all sides is the Ethereal Plane.   The Outer Planes hold the divine realms of all Amenor's deities, as well as the Outlands and Sigil. The distance between the Inner and Outer Planes is immeasurable yet is entirely covered by the Astral Plane. The Outer Planes are also arranged in a circle, with the Outlands and Sigil at its very center. Though their relative position is unimportant, the divine realms are arranged by alignment. Using the clock analogy again, the Lawful Good divine realms would be at 12 o'clock. Moving clockwise, you would move through each Lawful plane (Good, Neutral, Evil), then each Neutral plane (Good, Evil) and finally each Chaotic plane (Good, Neutral, Evil). The true Neutral planes exist, like Sigil and the Outlands, at the center of this circle.   Each of the divine realms have the alignment corresponding to its deity. In addition to the forty-five divine realms that exist, there are also two more that does not belong to a deity in the Amenorean pantheon. These two realms are the Abyss, the realm of demons and the Nine Hells, the realm of devils. The governance of these planes are in constant fluctuation as the demigods therein constantly fight each other for control.  
Alaric - Plane of Dreams
The divine realm of the God of Dreams is the Plane of Dreams. Like the Ethereal Plane, it surrounds the Material Plane, Feywild and Shadowfell. The edges of the plane is called the Waking Dreams and they overlap the Material Plane, Feywild and Shadowfell much like the Border Ethereal does. To get to the Plane of Dreams through the Waking Dreams is no easy task but spells like Etherealness could be modified to achieve it. Most creatures reach the Plane of Dreams through other means though. When most creatures sleep, they walk the Plane of Dreams and their unconscious mind shapes its surroundings.  
Alora - Alderya
The divine realm of the Goddess of Spring, Fertility and Growth is Alderya. It is a realm of constant growth, verdant fields and beautiful landscapes. The realm is in constant change, eternally growing and blooming. The season never changes though, as Alderya is bathed in an eternal spring. Trees rise and fall over in the course of a mortal day and flowers bloom and are replaced just as fast.  
Aqua - Plane of Water
The divine realm of the Goddess of Water and Grief is the Plane of Water. The realm is one entire ocean of endless depth and breadth. The shallow waters hold some civilized life, living on coral reefs and long dead large creatures that drift endlessly through the currents. The deeper parts of the ocean hold massive beasts and strange creatures that can survive the extreme pressure. No matter how shallow or deep one is, there is no sunlight and if a visitor cannot breathe underwater, they are dead within minutes.  
Brilja - Consturos
The divine realm of the Goddess of Constructs and Inventions is Consturos. It is a divine realm of remarkable inventions and endless workshops. Not an inch of grass can be seen anywhere on the plane, and massive cities rise to the point that they should fall over. The sound of pipework and beating hammers fills the sky, along with the soaring airships.  
Cáhlid - Beastlands
The divine realm of the Gods of Beasts is the Beastlands. It is a realm of nature unbound, where the forests grow so thick that barely any light shines through. Beasts roam the lands unfettered, birds fill the sky above the dense canopies and herbivores graze fields that stretch many horizons in each direction. Half of the time the sun shines its bright rays and the other half, rainstorms feed the rivers and vegetation.  
Cazerni - Marchon
The divine realm of the God of War is Marchon. It is a realm of bloody battlefields, red skies and raging fires. The entire plane is divided in two and an eternal war rages between the two sides. Unending sieges are fought on grounds holding ruined cities and massive siege machines constantly unleash balls of flame. No rain feeds the schorched ground, it is only thick, red blood that seeps deep into the dirt.  
Dar'Ishir - Frostfell
The divine realm of the God of Winter and Cruelty is Frostfell. It is a realm of incredible cold and harsh blizzards. Though it shares many similarities with the Plane of Ice, they are two different realms. The blizzards, snow and ice on the Plane of Ice are neutral and uncaring of those who live there. In the Frostfell, the howling winds and blistering blizzards intensify as if to drive creatures off the plane or to entirely kill them. Unending glaciers crack and consume those who travel them and tundras of snow are so unpacked that one sinks deep into death.  
Dilora - Eandalands
The divine realm of the Goddess of Food is the Eandalands. It is a realm of endless production and consumption. Massive orchards, endless vineyards and huge fields stretch across its horizon-bound fields. Every other day the sun shines and the rain pours. The inhabitants of the plane spend the sunny days working the fields and the rainy days cooking and eating.  
Eladrey - Passlands
The divine realm of the God of Autumn and time is the Passlands. It is a realm where time passes differently for each inhabitant. For some, the days pass quickly as they see the sun set early and the leaves pile up on the ground For other, days are unending as they walk through orange and brown-colored forests and fish on large lakes. The Passlands are kept in an eternal state of autumn, time never progressing no matter how its inhabitants have it.  
Elyzari - Taletell
The divine realm of the Goddess of Stories is Taletell. It is a realm of endless adventures and conversation. The threes whisper tales of long-lost kings and the sun itself seems to tell a story through the shadows it casts. The realm is often in a state of twilight, the sun setting on the horizon and countless campfires burning through the landscape. Creatures gather around the flames and share stories and books.  
Firenthra - Etheral Plane
The divine realm of the Goddess of Souls is the Ethereal Plane. It is a plane bordering all other Inner Planes, reflecting the actions and things that exist on those planes. It is a misty, fogbound dimension where one cannot see much further than a few feet. The ethereal plane holds many secrets, lost deep within its swirling fog and raging cyclones.  
Fórein - Deepforge
The divine realm of the God of the Forge and Creation is Deepforge. It is a warm plane located within the depths of a volcano. The sun is never seen but the rivers of lava that flow through the realm lights it up plenty. Wherever one goes, the sound a hammer striking an anvil can be heard. Weapons, armor and other creations line the many tunnels that snake back and forth through the mountain.  
Guinebrut - Crimsonreach
The divine realm of the God of Blood and Slaughter is Crimsonreach. It is a plane of constant fighting and death. The sun shines a deep red color, tinting the skies to match the color of the ground. Blood flows freely through the plane, forming rivers and deep lakes. Abandoned bodies decompose quickly and skeletons litter the crimson fields.  
Ignis - Plane of Fire
The divine realm of the God of Fire and Passion is the Plane of Fire. It is a realm of constant flame and orange hues. A blazing sun hangs at the zenith of a gold-orange sky, never waning. Seas of fire and lava stretch across the plane, broken up by obsidian rock and shale grounds. Meteors streak across the sky and everything is an unbearably hot temperature. The flames of the plane are self-sufficient and require no oxygen to burn. As such, creatures who cannot breathe flame with the help of magic are dead within minutes.  
Irwena - Seraphex
The divine realm of the Goddess of Luck and Fortune is Seraphex. It is a realm of plentiful bounties. The rivers are long and fresh, the fields are big and fertile, the sun is golden and bright. The mountains contain plentiful gems and the lakes hold much fish. In a way, Seraphex is a mirror of the Material Plane, everything is just much easier.  
Jale'Qar - Arcaneum
The divine realm of the God of Magic is Arcaneum. It is a glistening realm of incredible magical potential. The sun never shines, it is only illuminated by magical sources. The night sky is covered in glistening stars and thick prismatic threads are visible as they stretch throughout the skies, representing the Weave.  
Kannder - Justicia
The divine realm of the Goddess of Justice and Law is Justicia. It is a realm of perfect peace and absolute respect. It is much like the Material Plane with varying climates and seasons but unlike the Material Plane, there is a central pillar. This marble pillar rises to unfathomable heights and is etched with countless eyes. These eyes see all across the realm and punish anyone not following the laws of the plane. Any creature that disturbs the peace or disrespects others is immediately punished with a swift death.  
Libram - Noverium
The divine realm of the God of Knowledge is Noverium. It is a realm of endless libraries and unbound knowledge. The realm is filled with libraries connected by wooden hallways. Stained glass windows let sunlight into the book stacks and hallways but are not transparent. The bookcases are tall enough that the libraries have hundreds of levels. Walkways wind around the towering bookcases, letting the inhabitants pick out any book of their choosing.  
Maar - Hades
The divine realm of the God of Death is Hades. It is a realm of gray colors and boundless apathy. No sun shines in the realm and dark grey clouds cover the horizon. Tall grey mountains rise all throughout the realm, creating a single habitable valley. No wind blows and the entire realm is entirely quiet. Maar's reapers walk the streets and ensure that none are here that should not be.  
Mae - Limbo
The divine realm of the Goddess of Cunning and Chaos is Limbo. It is a realm of absolute chaos, one that is near impossible to survive unless one is familiar with it. The plane is entirely subject to the mind power of the creatures who inhabit it. Up, down, left and right exist only when a creature in the plane defines it. If a mortal is thrown into Limbo for the first time, it is likely that their panic and chaotic thoughts bring about their own doom. The plane is also filled with twisting archives, moving paintings and strange books, all somewhat reminiscent of the goddesses past.  
Mornal - Mechanus
The divine realm of the Goddess of Balance is Mechanus. It is a realm of perfectly regimented order. There are equal measures of light and dark, of hot and cold. Great constructions of cogwheels turn the plane in perfect order, operated by modrons. Mechanus is a plane of perfect balance and fully exempt of opinions.  
Nechlar - Avarice
The divine realm of the God of Greed is Avarice. It is a plane of incredible hoarding and wealth. The sun shines eternally in the realm, reflecting off of the copious amounts of gold that exist. Coins lay strewn across the ground and mountains made from pure gold rises in the distance. Gems contiually roll down the hills, magically appearing at the top again once they reach the bottom.  
Obiterra - Gehenna
The divine realm of the God of Disasters and Destruction is Gehenna. It is a realm of eternal destruction, slowly breaking apart at all times. A volcanic mountain dominates the real of Gehenna and smaller volcanoes drift like dragons throughout the sky, erupting often. The ground itself is steep and leads into deep canyons and volcanic fissures.  
Perreni - Arborea
The divine realm of the God of Perfection and Skill is Arborea. It is a plane of extremes, every part of the landscape brought to its greatest state. There are stupendously craggy mountains, unbelievably deep gorges, forest of monstrously huge trees and more. To traverse through Arborea is an extreme challenge that requires great skill.  
Presch - Acheron
The divine realm of the God of Tyranny and Domination is Acheron. It is a realm made of immense iron blocks, bound by massive chains. The chains drag these blocks through a dark void, sometimes colliding with a fearsome clang, sending creatures flying into the dark void only to eventually be caught by an cold steel chain.  
Puzkera - Canzera
The divine realm of the God of Plagues, Poison and Disease is Canzera. It is a realm of deathly sickness, infested to its very bones. No sun shines in Canzera but it is still bright enough that one can see the sickly green coloration of the sky. The ground is made of dark shale and broken stone, large fissures spreading through it. Gas constantly seeps up from these fissures, filling the air with a light green mist and making it difficult to see the giant mushrooms that grow all over the plane.  
Qandezar - Pandemonium
The divine realm of the Goddess of Decay and Rot is Pandemonium. It is a plane of stone tunnels and withering plants. Pandemonium is nothing but a planet-sized ball of stone filled with caverns, underground oceans and winding tunnels. A horrible wind drifts through the tunnels at all times, carrying Qandezar's draining nature as it searches Pandemonium endlessly for any life to absorb.  
Raschal - Necropolis
The divine realm of the God of Undeath is Necropolis. It is a plane consisting of one massive city, built on the bones and bodies of the fallen. The entire realm is lit in a neon green light and undead wander the streets, some with purpose and others aimlessly. The whole plane stinks of death and decay even though no corpse is left unused.  
Ronashand - Tenebraen
The divine realm of the God of Theft and Shadows is Tenebraen. It is a realm of endless shadow and darkness. The entire realm is kept in an eternal state of midnight and any source of light is quickly quenched by the shadows. Tall mountains rise throughout the plane, each decorated with well-defended castles and fortresses that hold large hoards of wealth.  
Serra - Elysium
The divine realm of the God of Gifts and Music is Elysium. The whole realm brims with kindness and joy. Buolic landscapes glimmer with life and beauty, clear water rivers running through miles of farmland and idyllic hills. A soft and calming melody always drifts through the air of Elysium.  
Soyal - Luxon
The divine realm of the God of the Sun and Light is Luxon. It is a realm bathed in eternal golden sunlight. Orchards of apple trees grow amidst large, glittering lakes. Mountains reach high up into the sky, nearly touching the sun in the cloudless sky.  
Terra - Plane of Earth
The divine realm of the God of Earth and Jewels is the Plane of Earth. The realm is a cloud-filled place of massive mountains that rise far out of sight. Large tunnels, excavated by inhabitants and dug by massive burrowing creatures connect the stone cities that exist in the large caverns in the base of the mountains. No oxygen can be found on this plane so if a visiting creature cannot breathe earth, it is dead within minutes.  
Therion - Bytopia
The divine realm of the God of Jewelry and Craftsmanship is Bytopia. The realm is curved into two layers that face each other like the covers of a closed book. Looking up from the bottom layer, one can see up to the top layer. No matter the layer, Bytopia is a heaven of productive and creative work and craftsmanship. Artisans litter the streets of the cities and carefully crafted homes are a common sight.  
Uynga - Carceri
The divine realm of the Goddess of Torture and Pain is Carceri. It is a plane of desolation and despair, completely empty of hope. The realm holds vast jungles, fetid bogs, frigid oceans and more, each place a prison in its own right. No matter the climate a creature finds themselves in, everything in your vicinity wants to harm and capture you.  
Varileth - Arcadia
The divine realm of the God of Fate is Arcadia. It is a realm of absolute intent and perfection. Orchards of perfectly lined trees, ruler-straight streams, orderly fields and immaculate roads are signs of the flawless attention to detail that characterizes Arcadia. The night and day cycle in Arcadia is dictated by a large orb that floats in the sky, rotating to define the time of day.  
Velissara - Desiren
The divine realm of the Goddess of Ecstasy and Hedonism is Desiren. It is a realm of endless pleasures and desires. The realm is surrounded on all sides by a massive mountain range, a single massive city in the center. The city has restaurants for every taste and brothels for every kink. The streets are filled with people pleasuring themselves or others.  
Velyndra & Vortharax - Draconia
The divine realm of the God of Dragons is Draconia. It is a realm that is in constant fluctuation. Whenever Velyndra or Vortharax reclaim control over the body, so too do they regain control of the divine realm. Everything then changes in an instant, from cities with golden streets, shining statues and tall towers, to a hellscape of erupting volcanoes and cracked ground.  
Ventus - Plane of Air
The divine realm of the Goddess of Wind and Change is the Plane of Air. The realm is an endless open sky in all directions, cluttered with clouds. There are no defining features and since the plane stretches endlessly in all directions, there is no concept of up, down, left or right. Air currents blow fiercely through the plane at all times, sending creatures tumbling through the open air.  
Virá - Mount Celestia
The divine realm of the Goddess of Summer, Beauty and Love is Mount Celestia. It is a realm of endless beauty, cities built into the rising side of a massive mountain. The realm is in an eternal state of summer and warmth, the hot sun shining down onto the streets of the cities. Flowers and trees grow boundless throughout the plane, all of them in a pink coloration.  
Wayleen - Outlands
The divine realm of the God of Travel is the Outlands. It is a realm of great differences as one traverses the edges of the plane. The Outlands is vast and contains many unique locations and secrets, as well as gate-towns to each of the divine realms. As opposed to all other divine realms, the Outlands is not unique but instead a mix of all the other divine realms.  
Wynderialdessa - Astral Plane
The divine realm of the Goddess of the Moon and Stars is the Astral Plane. It is a realm of thought and distant stars. It is a great silvery sea, the same above as below, with swirling wisps of white and gray streaking among motes of light. In the distance glimmers countless stars endlessly.  
Xaner - Reeve
The divine realm of the God of Debauchery is Reeve. It is a realm of constant excitement and noise. The entire realm consists of scattered inns and taverns in a landscape of green rolling hills, beautiful forests and still lakes. A fire always burns in the hearths of the inns and a rowdy, inebriated crowd can always be counted on.  
Yllí - The Feywild
The divine realm of the Goddess of Children and Pranks is the Feywild. The Feywild is a reflection of the Material Plane, cast in more extravagant colors and unlikely scenarios. Very little in the Feywild makes much sense and everything is out to get you. Some things are dangerous while others are more harmless, simply having the mischievous soul of a smiling child.  
Zeschla - The Shadowfell
The divine realm of the Goddess of Fear, Despair and Spiders is the Shadowfell. Just like the Feywild, the Shadowfell is a reflection of the Material Plane just a far more sinister one. Everything is clad in shades of grey, the sky is a drab color and no light ever finds its way into the plane. Fear, despair and other emotions run rampant on the inhabitants of the plane, piercing their mind and controlling their thoughts.  
Ze'Tren - ???
It is unknown to almost all if Ze'Tren even has a divine domain. The assumption is made that he would have one given his power level but it is nothing that mortal collections can recount.

    All mortals struggle in one way or another to avoid death, yet they all fail to realize that death is not the end.
    — Maar, God of Death

The divine realms of the gods are the ultimate destination for most mortals on Amenor. Every deity in the Amenorean pantheon has a divine realm. The realms of the Lesser Deities are smaller than that of the Prime Deities but they can still house millions upon millions of souls.
Type
Plane of Existence
  An image showing the cosmology of the Forgotten Realms. The cosmology of Amenor is the same except for the Outer Planes. Instead of there being sixteen Outer Planes, there are forty-seven, arranged by alignment. Lawful Good is at the top of the Outer Planes circle and moving clockwise, one goes through each permutation of Good, Neutral and Evil and then Lawful, Neutral and Chaotic, with the exception that the true Neutral planes exist "above" Sigil.

    Every soul is unique, yet every soul eventually ends up in my hands.
    — Firenthra, Goddess of Souls.

title: Alora
content:
Alora
A document detailing Alora, Goddess of Spring, Fertility and Growth.

    Alora is the Goddess of Spring, Fertility and Growth. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Elfyrean's essences. She if often referred to as Caretaker by her followers for her love of birth and growth. She is a being of of beginnings, new and old alike.

 
Description
Alora's divine form is one that would stand out like a beacon in a crowd. Her form is that of a human woman, standing almost two and a half metres tall. Her skin is fair and light, almost glowing as she seems to absorb the sunlight that shines on her.   Alora is always seen in a very revealing, flowing white dress that drifts behind her as she takes her long steps. A golden band, inscribed with icons of many different plants, tightens the dress to her voluptuous form. Alora's golden blonde hair is curled and reaches down just past her waist, blowing in the wind along with her dress.   A headress of newly blossomed flowers rests upon Alora's head, a collection the colorful spring. Alora's ocean-blue eyes seem to always sparkle with an insatiable lust and wherever she strode, all the vegetation around her seemed to spring up in bloom.  
Personality
Alora is a being that is in love with the beginning and continued existence of life. Whether it be the life of a creature with a soul, or the simple life and blossoming of a flower, Alora considers it sacred. According to her, in the beginning of a life, everything has the same chance to become something great.   With that said however, Alora cares little for the eventual outcome of a life. The birth of a would-be assassin is just as beautiful as the birth of a would-be cleric of her own faith. Not only are those births equal, the growth of life and the journeys these souls take to become themselves are also equally sacred.   Alora is a Neutral Good deity and takes little part in the discussion of morals and ethics, as noted above. Alora believes that the worth of every life is the same, assuming they all appreciate the gift that life is. Alora's love of births and beginnings culminates in her being the matron of the spring. After the flora and fauna of Amenor have been reset by the winter, Alora is the one who ushers in the spring and births a new year.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Alora are divided into two groups. Firstly are the people who love births and beginnings in the way that Alora do. Spring is their favorite season, they cultivate gardens and often have many children. Many farmers also pray to Alora for the well-being and growth of their crops.   Secondly, there are the group of worshipper who are unable to see the difference between lust and fertility. Due to the intrinsic tie between birthing new life and having sex, they mistake Alora's teachings as ones that promote lust and meaningless sex. Though these followers would often be better suited following Velissara, their confusion still makes them hold orgies in Alora's name, for example.   Though Alora acknowledges that their faith is misguided, she is not one to interfere in the life journeys of mortals. As such, she lets them do what they wish, sometimes spreading incorrect teaching of Alora.  
History
As an advocate for new beginnings, Alora has been involved in many of the major historical events of Amenor. While she has neven been the instigator, she is often there at the end in order to try to end the event and let a new Amenor be birthed from the consequences.  
Relationships
As Alora loves the beginning and subsequent growth of a birth, she has done so many time herself. She has had many relations with mortals, birthing half-divine beings and then watching their growth from a distance. For a long time however, she has been in a relationship with Maar, as both of them need one another to be given meaning. Furthermore, as the Goddess of Spring she is naturally connected to the primordial force representing that season. As such, she is also romantically entangled with Ventus, something that Maar is aware of and alright with.   Alora is honest and straightforward and is appreciated by the other good deities of Amenor's pantheon. However, for those deities that crave more nefarious outcome like destruction and death, she is a stain. As a daughter of Arbaraen, Alora also has a strong, sistely relationship to her divine sister, Virá.
Alora's divine form.  

    The birth of a child is a three-fold beginning of a new life.
    — Alora, Goddess of Spring, Fertility and Growth.

Alora, Goddess of Spring, Ferility and Growth is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is a heart wrapped in a bundle of wheat. Her divine realm is Alderya.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Neutral Good
Parents
Arbaraen
Elfyrean

title: Aqua
content:
Aqua
A document detailing Aqua, Goddess of Water and Grief.

    Aqua is the Goddess of Water and Grief. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Elfyrean's and Tyrabane's essences. She is often referred to as Wavemother by her followers for obvious reasons. She is a being of endless depths and waning tides.

 
Description
Aqua's divine form is easy to recognize though often very difficult to spot. Her color scheme is a deep blue coloration that makes her camouflaged in the oceans and thus very hard to see. Even her skin is a dark grey-blue color that gives her a pallid look.   Her divine form is that of a humanoid woman, standing almost two and a half meters tall. For her height, she is very scrawny and her skin sits tightly upon her bones. Her white-blue hair is long and knotted, like tangled seaweed. Her eyes are a deep ocean blue, filled with a sorrowful look.   She wears a long dress that is decorated with small shining stones and reflective scales. The cloth of the dress is tattered and flows freely after her as she swims, giving her the visage of a banshee. Her time spent in the depths of the oceans has made her body shrunken and sallow. There are basically no muscles left on her body.  
Personality
Aqua embodies the idea of Grief in the most literal sense. The oceans she controls never forgets and, by extension, neither does she. As she remembers all that has transpired and all that has been lost, Aqua's grief is eternal, never able to move on from such loss.   Aqua's grief is what defines her, though it often takes different forms. The usual stages of grief; denial, anger, bargaining, depression but never quite acceptance is what characterizes Aqua. As she moves through these stages of grief, her sorrow manifests in different ways. Perhaps the ocean is calm and quiet as she is depressed, and perhaps the waves soar to new heights as she feels anger.   Aqua is a Chaotic Neutral deity. She is a primordial force as much as she is a goddess. She is predictable but chaotic and she is ambivalent towards both mortals and gods. She cares little for what others do, or who the world belongs to, as long as she gets to do what she wants.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Aqua are rather many throughout Amenor. People who have suffered great losses, sailors who pray for safe passage or others who have a love for the oceans. For all the followers that Aqua has however, she would need none of them to remain a Prime Deity. She is a manifestation of a primordial power and she will remain as long as waves wash upon the shores of Amenor.  
History
As a neutral primordial force, Aqua has certainly had effects on major events throughout Amenor's history. The raging sea might have sunk an invading naval force or the calm waves might have made new naval paths available. Though the list can go on, Aqua has had no stake in Amenor's history. She cares little for it, or the creatures involved in it.  
Relationships
Ventus is inexorably tied to the other deities of primordial force, Ventus, Ignis and Terra. Together, the four of them represent the primordial forces surrounding the Material Plane. Just like them, she calls her own plane, the Plane of Water home. Beyond her three brethren, she is also connected to the season that most appreciates her. As such, she is romantically entangled with the God of Winter, Dar'Ishir. His cruelty and her sorrow compliment each other quite well.
Aqua's divine form.  

    The ocean never forgets.
    — Aqua, Goddes of Water and Grief.

Aqua, Goddess of Water and Grief is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of three curving horizontal lines, signifying waves. Her divine realm is the Plane of Water.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Neutral
Parents
Elfyrean
Tyrabane

title: Cazerni
content:
Cazerni
A document detailing Cazerni, God of War.

    Cazerni is the God of War. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Draktyl's and Dwandrin's essences. He is often referred to as God General by his followers due to his connection to war. He is a being that is alive in the heat of warfare and near dead everywhere else.

 
Description
Cazerni's divine form is easily recognizable. It is that of a decorated myrmidon soldier carrying a flaming sword. What differentiates him from a normal soldier is his stature, as Cazerni stands almost five meters tall. In his giant form, he strides across the battlefield with purpose and a fire in his eyes that is only matched by his burning sword.   His plate armor is a dark burgundy, colored by the many litres of blood that have been spilt upon it. The joints are covered in dark red cloths, matched by the fresh blood that splatters across him in the heat of battle, as well as the long cloak that flows behind him.   His massive flaming greatsword illuminates Cazerni's face from below as he charges the frontlines, making the intimidating expression he always has even scarier. A myrmidon helmet protects his face, its dark metal buckled to the point that it barely fits anymore.  
Personality
As the God of War, Cazerni is an easy deity to understand. He cares about little else other than the battlefield and tactics of warfare. In times of peace, Cazerni stature fades and his giant form shrinks to that of a regular man. The flames of his ever-burning sword also fade to nothing more than a slight crackle.   In times of war however, Cazerni stands his full height and awesome stature. His energy is palpable and his lust for battle is unending. His flaming sword burns bright for each blow he delivers in the battle.   Cazerni is a Lawful Evil deity as he has but one goal: to cause as much war as possible. Only in times of war does he feel alive and so he cares little for the repercussions of the war, little for all the lives lost and things destroyed. He only wants to find himself on a bloody battlefield, no matter the cost.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Cazerni wax and wane throughout time. While there are those that worship Cazerni even in time of peace, they are not enough to make Cazerni a Prime Deity. When times of war come around however, most of the soldiers pray to Cazerni for improved warfare prowess. They pray to win the next battle, that their sword may strike true or their armor may deflect many blows.   As such, Cazerni oscillates between a Prime and Lesser Deity more than any other god. In times of peace, he has just enough followers to be considered a Lesser Deity and in times of war, he rises to the position of Prime Deity. At the moment, there is enough warfare in Amenor that Cazerni is considered a Prime Deity.  
History
Due to Cazerni's level of power being so tied to the warfare, his involvement in major historical events has changed greatly over time. With that said however, he used to be a part of almost every mortal war waged upon Amenor.   He would descend from his divine domain and upon the battlefield, fighting with unmatched fervor, uncaring of which side he strikes down. This lead to the fact that mortal war upon Amenor used to be nearly impossible, as Cazerni would descend and eviscerate both sides.   It took a few wars until Cazerni understood how he always was his own demise and changed his tactics. Now he enjoys wars through his worshippers and thus retains his status as a Prime Deity for much longer at a time.  
Relationships
To other good deities, Cazerni is considered a divine stain. With no remorse for the actions of warfare, none of them want anything to do with such a murderer. Other morally ambiguous or evil deities appreciate what Cazerni brings however. In time of war and crisis, there are many opportunities to further ones own goals.
