While Cazerni despises the good deities in the same way they despise him, and appreciates the support of the others, there is one deity that he likes above all. He and Fórein have almost a brotherly relationship. After each battle, Cazerni returns to his friend by the forge and recaps the war while Fórein restores his equipment.
Cazern's divine form.  

    May the enemy swords break upon our shields and our spears pierce their throats!
    — Cazerni, God of War.

Cazerni, God of War is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of a shortsword struck into a shield as the shield splinters and cracks. His divine realm is Marchon.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Evil
Parents
Draktyl
Dwandrin

title: Dar'Ishir
content:
Dar'Ishir
A document detailing Dar'Ishir, God of Winter and Cruelty.

    Dar'Ishir is the God of Winter and Cruelty. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Draktyl's and Tyrabane's essences. He is often referred to as Frostbaron by his followers for his chilly nature. He is a being of incredible cold and unending cruelty.

 
Description
Dar'Ishir's divine form is a chilling sight. It is that of a giant humanoid, with a pale blue skin. A long, fur-covered robe occludes almost all of him, with the exception of his head. A large beard of snow white color obscures the neck and frost forms upon its edges continuously.   A see-through crown of jagged ice adorns his head, causing a chilly smoke to slowly drift down his person. His eyes are dark and sunken to the point where one needs to be very close to see more than two dark pits. Naturally, if one gets that close, they would feel the incredible cold that emanates off of Dar'Ishir.   His blue fingers crack and break as they grip the large staff he is always seen with. It is a staff of unremarkable wood that ends in an elaborate snowflake design. Just like the crown, cold air drifts down the grip of the staff at all times. Wherever Dar'Ishir strides, he leaves behind a patch of snow with a well-defined footprint, no matter the weather.  
Personality
As the God of Cruelty, Dar'Ishir needs little explanation as he serves his domain well. He is cold and cruel in all aspects, caring little of who he affects or how he affects them. For Dar'Ishir, complaining and pleading do little good. The situation that you find yourself in is always of your making so you best find yourself in it, whether the situation be love or torture.   While Dar'Ishir is cruel and uncaring, there is a side to him that can appreciate more than misfortune. As the God of Winter, he is very fond of the arctic landscape. He enjoys snow, blizzards and cold with an uncharacteristically warm heart. Most of all does he love the beauty of ice. According to him, when something becomes encased in ice, it is preserved in its most beautiful state until it melts.   Dar'Ishir is a Neutral Evil deity, one who is comletely untroubled by the strife of others as he pursues his own goals. To those who cross him, he enacts cruel justice, often doling out far more punishment than his opponents transgression should require. While he does not seek others out to inflict cruelty, he never passes on an opportunity to do so when he feels it is needed.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Dar'Ishir are a great range of people. Some worship him for all of his tenets, for both winter and cruelty. Others may worship him for only cruelty, agreeing with his philosophy that all should fend for themselves.   Most of his followers, however, care little for his teachings of cruelty. They are people who love the quarter of the year that Amenor goes cold. People who love the winter and snow, and who see beauty in the arctic just like Dar'Ishir does. People in the colder parts of Amenor often worship Dar'Ishir for guidance through the cold landscape that they live in.  
History
Dar'Ishir's cruelty is evident when one looks at his actions throughout the history of Amenor. He always takes the side of the evil deities and supports them in their attempts to inflict harm upon the mortals of Amenor. Furthermore, he has conjured many long and harsh winters throughout the history of Amenor, most notably The Eternal Cold.  
Relationships
Dar'Ishir's cruel nature make him a natural match with most evil deities. Their means and methods of taking whatever they desire often please him. On the contrary, deities that show compassion and aid others are the exact type of creature that Dar'Ishir despises. Furthermore, as the God of Winter he is naturally connected to the primordial force representing that season. As such, he is romantically entangled with Aqua.
Dar'Ishir's divine form.  

    The cold is unyielding, unending and fair. Unlike most mortals...
    — Dar'Ishir, God of Winter and Cruelty.

Dar'Ishir, God of Winter and Cruelty is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of an elaborate, sixteen-pointed snowflake. His divine realm is Frostfell
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Neutral Evil
Parents
Draktyl
Tyrabane
Related Historical Events

    2816 ADD
    A Final Stand
    2506 ADD
    The Eternal Cold

title: Eladrey
content:
Eladrey
A document detailing Eladrey, God of Autumn and Time.

    Eladrey is the God of Autumn and Time. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created as an extension of Huwander's domain. He is often simply referred to as Fall by his followers due to his innate connection with that season. He is a being that is a constant reminder that everything eventually fades away in time.

 
Description
Eladrey's divine form is strangely mundane in most aspects. Appearing as a male human acolyte, he dresses in long, flowing robes of an autumn orange that hang from his arms. His human body is nothing of remark either, being of average height and build. By all means, Eladrey's divine form is that of someone who could get lost in a crowd.   However, one exception does exist and that is his face. Eladrey has only one face, but it exists in many phases. Being a representation of time itself, Eladrey's face changes over the course of each day. As the day starts, he has the appearance of a young child.   His face then grows older throughout the day. From child to teenager, to young adult, to adult and finally to that of an old person. At the end of each day, his face dies to its rapid aging, only to be reborn young and new on the next day. Each day with the same face, going through all the same phases of aging.  
Personality
As the God of Time, Eladrey is the embodiement of such a concept. Time is cold and uncaring, marching ever onward no matter how much people would like to alter it. Eladrey is the same, a deity that is fully concerned with his divine duty. He rarely takes the time to engage in conversation with the others, focusing on the evermarching time.   Just as Eladrey takes on the concept of time, so does he embody the concept of autumn. There is fading beauty in his words and his looks but it is always bittersweet. Even the simplest things such as his walk bring to mind the end of things, but not without appreciation for all that has been.   Eladrey is a Neutral deity however, as he simply cares little about others. He does not go out of his way to harm them, nor does he go out of his way to help them. Just like time itself, he simply exists as a facet of the universe and he is purely content in such a role.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Eladrey are many, wildly ranging in practices. Some worshippers simply have an affection for autumn and expresses it through their worship, while others may be wizards seeking to understand and transmute time itself.   The bulk of Eladrey's worshippers however are old. Not just human old, but really old. People like archdruid or many hundred year old elves and the like. People who have lived long enough to have experienced time in a manner different from most others express this complicated relationship through their worship to Eladrey.  
History
The history of Eladrey is one that is easy to recount. He has been around for every event, ensuring that divine squabbles cause no strange temporal ripples in Amenor. He has been there, upholding his duty each time but he has never participated. No major historical event in Amenor has had his fingers in the mix.  
Relationships
Eladrey rarely engages in conversation with others, including the divine meaning that he has little relationships to speak of. He cares little for most other gods but he does hold respect for the other neutral deities that also have little stakes in the game, such as Libram.   Furthermore, as the Goddess of Autumn he is naturally connected to the primordial force representing that season. As such, he is romantically entangled with Terra, the only other deity he cares about.
Eladrey's divine form.  

    In time, everything falters and fades.
    — Eladrey, God of Autumn and Time.

Eladrey, God of Autumn and Time is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor. His holy symbol is that of a dark brown, falling maple leaf. His divine realm is the Passlands.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Neutral
Parents
Huwander

title: Firenthra
content:
Firenthra
A document detailing Firenthra, Goddess of Souls.

    Firenthra is the Goddess of Souls. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Gordfein's and Tyrabane's essences. She is often referred to as the Unraveling Weaver for her control over souls. She is a being endless soul manipulation and caretaking.

 
Description
Firenthra's divine form is rather recognizable. It is that of a tall elven woman of regal appearance and bearing. She is on the older side, with a wise face and a soft but knowing smile. On her head is a golden tiara that matches perfectly with the ostentatious earrings that she also wears.   Her white hair is bundled up with beautiful braids and golden and silver pins. She is clad in soft, flowing robes that glimmer and glitter all by themselves. It seems almost as if her clothing is made with starlight itself. Her robes are long, far longer than she is tall yet they do not drag on the ground but float above it instead.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Souls, Firenthra is an incredibly wise entity. Together with Maar and Kannder, she has absolute control over the mortal afterlife. Her mastery over souls is unparalleled and her knowledge of the deepest truths of mortals is exceeded by few.   Firenthra is fascinated by all souls, no matter what body houses it. Regular mortal souls, souls that have defied death, divine souls and broken souls are all equally intriguing to her. Through her control over a creatures soul, she can fully understand all that causes it to exist, as well as enact full domination over it. With that said however, she is fascinated by souls and has no desire to take control over them, she wants to study them just as they are.   Firenthra is Lawful Neutral deity, one that fulfills her place in the universe. Though she most likely could exert total control over most other creatures through her power over souls, she does not do so. She is a fair deity, adhering to the judgement given unto the soul and handling it correspondingly.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Firenthra are many across Amenor. They include people who worship her for an amicable relationship once they move onto the afterlife, people who like the esoteric nature of her or people who actually have a higher degree of control over their own soul. As a matter of fact, a lot of worshippers of Firenthra are monks or otherwise spritually inclined.  
History
As a neutral force that handles the souls that move to the afterlife, Firenthra has little interest in the conflicts of others. Though she finds it interesting to peer into the souls of those caught in conflict to see their true intentions and thoughts, she offers no assistance and take no stance in such cases.  
Relationship
Firenthra cares little for other deities or their plans and thus takes few steps to forming relationships with them. Her divine domain keeps her in close contact with Maarand Kannder however and she does respect the both of them, even if the relationships are simply professional.
Firenthra's divine form.  

    No matter how they dare delay it, all souls will eventually end up in my hands.
    —  Firenthra, Goddess of Souls.

Firenthra, Goddess of Souls is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of a simplified model of an atom, the core being made of a black color and the surrounding circles having the colors of the rainbow. Her divine realm is the Ethereal Plane.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Parents
Gordfein
Tyrabane

title: FóRein
content:
Fórein
A document detailing Fórein, God of the Forge & Creation.

    Fórein is the God of the Forge and Creation. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created as an extension of Dwandrin's domain. He is often referred to as Forgemaster by his followers for his skill at crafting. He is a being of endless creation, new or perfected. 

 
Description
Fórein's divine form is very recognizable as it is the form of a dwarf as large as mortal goliaths. Though he may initially appear gray-skinned, it is nothing but a deep layer of soot. If it were ever scrubbed away, his tan, olive skin would be revealed undeneath.   Fórein is a miniature mountain of muscle, almost being as wide as he is tall. His soot-covered skin shines in the light of the forge and his massive muscles bulge as he works. As a divine deity, he has no reason to sweat but despite that, pearls of sweat often streak down his flexing biceps.   A large braided beard of brown color hides all of Fórein's throat and just like his skin, it is darkened by a layer of soot. His head is bald and he is always shirtless. Fórein's likeness to his divine father, Dwandrin, cannot be denied.  
Personality
As the God of Forge and Creation, Fórein is a deity that enjoys creation of any and all kinds. Everything from jewelry to the crafting of sprawling cities can be an inspiring act in his mind. He has a particular distaste of those who get everything from the ones around them. If you are not able or willing to get your hands dirty, Fórein is not able to respect you.   Fórein is a Lawful Neutral deity. He is unbothered by the plea of others and creates on what pleases him. Sometimes these creations are gifts that others could use but most often they only fill his own collections, assuming that they are perfect enough. Very rarely does he forge something by request.   Fórein seeks perfection in all crafts. If a creation of his is not perfect, it is incinerated into another attempt at the item. To Fórein, it is a failure of the crafter if the craft is not perfect. However, such failure only paves the way for more attempts.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Fórein are many. All across Amenor are people who are trying their hand at crafting a small object as a sign of affection, praying to Fórein for luck. For all of them, there are many who practice crafts as a profession. Blacksmiths, jewelers or soldiers praying that their sword will survive its next strikes are only some of the ones who worship Fórein.  
History
Fórein is mostly concerned with crafting and creating. His incredible skill at this though often leads to others desiring his talents for aid in their conflict. Though he takes distance from the conflicts themselves, his love for creation is so great that he often abides their request. As such, Fórein has had little to do with most major events throughout Amenor's history, though the same can't be said of his creations.  
Relationships
As mentioned above, Fórein is a bit of a lonesome figure. He cares more for the perfection of his crafts than for the company of others, mortal or divine. With that said, he does have a pretty close relationship with Brilja given their mutual love for creation, as well as a brotherly relationship with Cazerni.
Fórein's divine form.  

    To be the birthing hands of a creation is joy unending.
    — Fórein, God of the Forge and Creation. 

Fórein, God of the Forge and Creation is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is a hammer striking an anvil, sparks flying in every direction. His divine realm is Deepforge.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Parents
Dwandrin

title: Ignis
content:
Ignis
A document detailing Ignis, God of Fire and Passion.

    Ignis is the God of Fire and Passion. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Elfyrean's and Dwandrin's essences. He is often referred to as Father Flame by his followers for obvious reasons. He is a being of eternal flame and great passion.

 
Description
Ignis' divine form is easy to recognize, though it may appear very different at times. His form is that of humanoid main, his body entirely caught in flames. His hair and beard are made of crackling embers, chaotically bouncing off in every direction.   His arms are made of moving lava. It pours from his neck, over his shoulders, down to his fingers and then it returns to the tip of the other arm. His legs are made of nothing but roiling flame, eternally burning and crackling. Wherever Ignis steps, scorch marks singe the ground.   The reason that Ignis' divine form is different from time to time is due to the fire that envelopes him. Over the course of a week, Ignis' flames burn him down from the three meter tall giant that he is, to a humanoid of normal size and then finally to a pile of ash. As soon as nothing but powder remains of him, he reforms into his largest form once more and the cycle starts over again.  
Personality
Ignis embodies the ideas of passion and purpose in their most literal sense. Everything he does, he does with an utmost dedication and love for the act. If there is something he is uncertain of, he will not attempt it as he considers it a disgrace to do something one is not entirely sure of.   Ignis regards his domain with absolute and complete respect, but he can often be quite temperamental. Due to his extreme passion and opinions, he is quite prone to erupting in anger. As he is the emodiment of such a destructive primordial power, these tantrums are often quite dangerous. The eruption of a volcano or burning of a forest fire are simple examples of this.   Ignis is a Chaotic Neutral deity. He is a primordial force as much as he is a god. He is unpredictable and chaotic but he is ambivalent towards both mortals and gods. He cares little for what others do, or who the world belongs to, as long as he gets to do what he wants.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Ventus are rather many throughout Amenor. Pyromaniacs, people who have extreme passion for a craft or the like, wizards who study the path of evocation magic are among the worshippers of Ignis. For all the followers that Ignis has however, he would need none of them to remain a Prime Deity. He is a manifestation of a primordial power and he will remain as long as fire rages throughout Amenor.  
History
As a neutral primordial force, Ignis has certainly had effects on major events throughout Amenor's history. A flame might have proved to dangerous to approach. A volcanos eruption might have made lands hospitable. Though the list can go on, Ignis has had no stake in Amenor's history. He cares little for it, or the creatures involved in it.  
Relationships
Ignis is inexorably tied to the other three deities of primordial force, Terra, Aqua and Ventus. Together, the four of them represent the primordial forces surrounding the Material Plane. Just like them, he calls her own plane, the Plane of Fire home. Beyond his three brethren, he is also connected to the season that most appreciates her. Thus, the passion and love of the Goddess of Summer, Virá is his spouse.
Ignis' divine form.  

    Flame and fire burns away all falsehoods and leaves only the raw, desirable truth.
    — Ignis, God of Flame and Passion.

Ignis, God of Fire and Passion is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of three vertical lines that curl at the top, signifying flame. His divine realm is the Plane of Fire.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Neutral
Parents
Elfyrean
Dwandrin

title: Irwena
content:
Irwena
A document detailing Irwena, Goddess of Luck and Fortune.

    Irwena is the Goddess of Luck and Fortune. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created as an extension of Heyla's domain. She is often referred to as Lady Luck by her followers for obvious reasons. She is a lucky being of boundless fortunes.

 
Description
Of all the divine forms of the deities of Amenor, Irwenas's might be the one that is the least recognizable. Were it not for her divine powers, one could easily mistake Irwena for an aasimar woman. With fair skin, long blonde hair and a subtle divine glow, Irwena has the appearance of a middle aged female aasimar.   Naturally, Irwena's divine powers clearly separate her from any mortals and her unfathomable luck make her easy to recognize after all. Beyond that though, her clothing is often quite ostentatious. Black and gold are the primary colors of Irwena and she masterfully blends them in her clothing such that she appears royalty.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Luck and Fortune, Irwena is the embodiment of breezing through life. There is very little that she does not do with ease, despite the lack of actual practiced skill. This ease is much to Irillia's delight, letting the powers she govern guide each of her actions.   Irwena most often wears a smile on her lips, accompanied by a graceful laughter, no matter how dangerous the situation is. After all, it always seems to turn out just fine for her. More than basking in the lucky outcomes of her actions, Irwena enjoys to bask in the promises of fortune. Without question, the resplendent and rich life is her greatest love.   Irwena is a Chaotic Good deity, caring very little for what others think she might do. Irwena does as she feels at all times, paying no mind to how her actions affect others. With that said, she is not ill-tempered or vengeful, nor does she ever seek misfortune for others. She simply only seeks fortune for herself and some luck for others.  
Worshippers
The morality of Irwena's followers vary greatly. There are those who pray to Irwena simply due to their belief that it gives them just a little bit more luck that they need to get through life. There are those who have made their fortunes earnestly and are bashing in the life it brings, praying to Irwena as their wish to keep their life that way.   Then there are those who pray to Irwena for the less savory things that luck and fortune can bring. Those who worship her so that their hands when gambling are just a little bit better. And those who seek to gain fortune by any means that they can. While such an acquiring of fortune is not within Irwena's teachings, they pray to her nonetheless.  
History
Irwena is the eldest god in all of the Amenorean pantheon. She was the first to ascend and she has been a Prime Deity since before Amenor was finished. She used to be mortal, a part of the Marked Septet, the group that defeated Darkheart. She took the place of Heyla as the Goddess of Luck during The Commence.   Throughout the history of Amenor however, Irwena has almost always been a part of the major events in Amenor. Her presence often caused incredible problems for her enemies since her luck is so unbounded that she basically cannot be harmed. Most notably, she singlehandedly withstood a combined assault of gods at the end of the fifth Era.  
Relationships
Due to Irwena's wily nature, she is not particularly beloved by the good deities of Amenor as they find her methods far too chaotic. For the evil deities, they find her aversion to ill-will and malice enough of a disdainful trait that they want little to with her as well.   As a matter of fact, Irwena's lucky nature and indulgence in fortune makes her a little bit of a pain in the ass to deal with. Her luck essentially protects her from anything bad ever happening to her, which can anger others that are struck with strife. Her only notable relationship is with Mae, thanks to their shared history.
Irwena's divine form.  

    Luck is my blessing upon the fearless, the ones whose hearts do not falter in shadows.
    — Irwena, Goddess of Luck and Fortune.

Irwena, Goddess of Luck and Fortune is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of three d6:s stacked on top of each other, all of them having rolled a 6. Her divine realm is Seraphex.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Parents
Heyla
Related Historical Events

    2819 ADD
    Irwena's Punishment
    2816 ADD
    A Final Stand
    8 ADD
    Irwena's Apotheosis

title: Jale'Qar
content:
Jale'Qar
A document detailing Jale'Qar, God of Magic.

    Jale'Qar is the God of Magic. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created as an extension of Gordfein's domain. He is often referred to as Weavemaker by his followers due to his connection magic and the Weave. He is an ethereal being of absolute control over magic.

 
Description
Jale'Qar's divine form is one that is easily recognizable, thanks in large part to his ethereal appearance. While his divine form is detailed, it is not fully material, appearing translucent almost like a ghost. This is due to Jale'Qar's innate connection to the Weave and his divine form being half latched onto the Weave and half a physical form.   His physical form is that of a tall and slim humanoid, standing over three meters tall. His skin is a deep brown and his fingertips always seem to sparkle with some excess magic. A large and beautiful purple robe covers most of his body, accented with golden details and chains.   Just like his clothing, Jale'Qar's skin bears golden details that shimmer in the light. A ring of golden dust always seems to orbit him as well, almost like he is the center of a small solar system.  
Personality
As the God of Magic, Jale'Qar is the divine embodiment of magic, arcana and spells, something that is obvious in his personality. The spells that mortals cast work with their own rules and are reliable, always producing the same effect as long as the mortal properly followed the magical rules associated with the casting.   Jale'Qar is much the same way. He follows the laws and rules of magic and his actions are predictable and understandable. He has no stake in mortal ethics or philosophy, caring not about how spells are used, only that they are used correctly.   Jale'Qar is a Lawful Neutral deity, abiding by all the rules that surround magic and not bothering himself with the eventual consequences of magics usage. With that said, all the rules that surround magic are crafted by himself so, by extension, he follows his own laws.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Jale'Qar are obvious. Those who practice arcane magic often pray to Jale'Qar for help in their understanding of the subject. Naturally, there are those that pursue arcane magic without seeking the favor of Jale'Qar but many do.   While arcane practitioners are granted their magical powers by Jale'Qar, druids and cleric get their magic from other sources such as the land itself, or their patron deity. While that often means that they have no affiliation with Jale'Qar, their magic still abides by the rules that he has set.  
History
Jale'Qar's adherence to the magical flows of Amenor and his connection to the Weave keeps him away from divine conflicts. He concerns himself with the magic that courses through Amenor and ensuring that it is kept intact through divine conflicts and thus rarely has a part in the conflicts themselves.  
Relationships
Jale'Qar's neutrality and love for magic makes him a little bit aloof. He cares more for controlling the Weave and magic of Amenor, spending little time with others. While his domain enables many others, he considers himself far too busy for spending time with anything but the Weave.
Jale'Qar's divine form.  

    There are few situations in mortal life that can not be solved by a well-spoken spell.
    — Jale'Qar, God of Magic.

Jale'Qar, God of Magic is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of three sparkling, overlapping blue diamonds. His divine realm is Arcaneum.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Parents
Gordfein

title: Kannder
content:
Kannder
A document detailing Kannder, Goddess of Justice and Law.

    Kannder is the Goddess of Justice and Law. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created as an extension of Draktyl's domain. She is often referred to as Arbiter by her followers due to her absolute authority. She is a being of merciless justice.

 
Description
Kannder's divine form is quite recognizable, even in the neutral form that it is. Kannder's divine form its that of a peasant woman, clad in a simple white chore dress that is immaculately clean. A brown, many-laced corset covers her stomach, tied to perfection.   As the corset fastens the dress to the divine form, the straps hang loosely just below the shoulders. The divine form is thin, not to the point malnourishment but to the point that it seems surprising that she so easily wields a longsword with just one hand. The other hand holds a set of silver scales, empty and perfectly balanced.   Were it not for the tools that Kannder's divine form holds, one could still recognize her by her bare feet and blindfold. This pure white blindfold ties the curly, dirty blonde hair in the back and completely covers Kannder's eyes in the front, without obscuring justice.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Justice and Law, Kannder is a being of absolute stoicism and coldness. Emotion is never shown from her divine form, maintining an unvawering neutral expression at all times. Whether she decrees an accused should be let go, or she personally slices their head off with a clean swipe of her sword, her expression does not change.   Nothing ever phases Kannder. No matter the crime or the accusation, she passes out judgement as easily as mortals have breakfast. When the accused tells their story and Kannder contemplates the punishment to dole out, she is nigh indistinguishable from a statue.   Kannder is a Lawful Neutral deity, one that does not only follow the laws to a tee, but also enforces them. It is by the laws, whether they be made by mortals or her, that she doles out justice, a never-ending task. Kannder never takes emotion into the decision of justice. To her, a crime is a crime, no matter how good the motivation is. And, of course, a crime should never go unpunished.  
Worshippers
Kannder is worshipped by many across Amenor. Naturally, there are judges, executioners and other public servants that enforce the law that follow Kannder for obvious reasons. Beyond that, there are others who share her neutral and apathetic approach to punishment and follow her for her righteous sense of justice.   Some people consider followers of Kannder to be almost brutal at times. They do not consider the whole story or the conditions that led the accused to the situation, only the punishment to be doled out. In particular, followers of Virá often butt heads with followers of Kannder, calling them ruthless barbarians.  
History
As a neutral arbiter, Kannder has never been involved in any of the great historical events of Amenor. She always lets the conflict come to a close before she can begin her contribution. She assesses the conflict from both sides and doles out punishment accordingly, whether she is judging mortals or divine.  
Relationships
Though many other deities respect Kannder, she cares extremly little for their opinion. She has a job to do, a divine domain to control and that is all she concerns herself with. Even though the chafes of mortals mirror themselves and Virá dislikes Kannder's cruelty, Kannder does little in response.   Furthermore, her role in a mortals passing has made her work closely together with the god of death, Maar and the goddess of souls, Firenthra. Despite that, it is nothing more than work to her and she does not care for Maar or Firenthra.
Kannder's divine form.  

    Justice never sleeps.
    — Kannder, Goddess of Justice and Law.

Kannder, Goddess of Justice and Law is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of a standing silver scale, the two plates perfectly balanced. Her divine realm is Justicia.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Parents
Draktyl

title: Libram
content:
Libram
A document detailing Libram, God of Knowledge.

    Libram is the God of Knowledge. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Huwander's essences. He is often referred to as Keeper of Scrolls by his followers for his incredible hoard of knowledge. Libram is a being of absolute knowledge, quite possible the creature in Amenor that knows the most.

 
Description
Libram's divine form is a unique sight. A humanoid body is covered in simple gray robes that are lined with gold. Two golden pauldrons on either shoulder hold a lot of inscriptions, phrases from each recorded language of Amenor, all with the meaning that "Knowledge is eternal."   Libram's hands appear to be human and they always hold a number of golden scrolls. Wherever the god goes, he carries with him more scrolls than his hands can carry, making them float above. As Libram travels, his focus is entirely on the scrolls he carries with him, always reading.   The head of the god is much less human-looking than the hands. A golden mask covers the face, with a large hole in the back of it. From this hole a pillar of golden smoke emerges, slowly turning into a regular column of black smoke. This smoke-like pillar is a few hundred meters long, wrappign around Libram's form as he walks. It is believed that this smoke is Libram's brain, that it extends so far due to Libram's extensive knowledge of all things.  
Personality
As the God of Knowledge, Libram is a being that is entirely concerned with the gathering and storage of knowledge. No piece of information is too small or old to have a place in his extensive libraries. To Libram, it does not matter if the information is useful or not, it is equally desirable.   Libram is a Neutral deity, one that takes no side in the moral and ethical difficulties of mortal life. He cares only for the information that he could gain. He would equally quickly make a deal with a righteous king as a crooked devil, should the information they offer be of equal value.   While it is true that Libram does not distinguish between the value of information, there is one category of information that he does not care for, which is personal information. Gossip or tales of a mortal's life is a waste of storage, whether it be the storage of one's own mind or his extensive libraries on his own demiplane.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Libram are primarily sages, wizards or artificers, people that value information very highly. Most libraries in Amenor have a small section dedicated to Libram, praying for the safety of their books and gathered knowledge.   Those who worship Libram are generally well-respected scholars or wizards that are experts in their field. Most of them tend to follow the principles of their god, staying out of moral discussions. The worshippers of Libram that do engage in such talk however, are often considered an annoying addition due to their tendency to play the role of the devil's advocate.  
History
Due to Libram's neutrality, he has been involved in very few divine events of the seven eras of Amenor's existence. However, his vast gathered knowledge could be an incredibly valuable asset for those who are caught in conflict. That is exactly what happened in the end of the fifthEra. Beyond that event, Libram has remained entirely neutral within the divine politics of Amenor.  
Relationships
Libram tends to be a rather reclusive god, one that has little interaction with the rest of Amenor's pantheon. He is one to stand nearby and take notes about the events between the gods but never one to interfere. With that said, he finds a lot of mutual traits in Varileth and they have formed a strong friendship.
Libram's divine form.  

    Knowledge is an eternal, unending and eternally joyous reward.
    — Libram, God of Knowledge.

Libram, God of Knowledge is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of a silver book with golden pages that is being flipped through, the pages rapidly turning over. His divine realm is Noverium.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Neutral
Parents
Arbaraen
Huwander
Related Historical Events

    2816 ADD
    A Final Stand

title: Maar
content:
Maar
A document detailing Maar, God of Death.

    Maar is the God of Death. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created as an extension of Tyrabane's domain. He is often referred to as The Great End due to his control over death. He is a being of complete stillness and quiet.

 
Description
Maar's divine form is very distinct among the deities of Amenor. His massive, black and boney wings are unmistakable. They protrude from his back and extend to almost thrice his height and much more than that in width. A hovering "crown" of dark spikes always floats above his head, close enough to almost pierce it.   Between these spikes and Maar's head is a thin white hood that offers little protection. The ragged white hood extends into a torn poncho that covers most of his upper body. Beneath the hood is nearly only darkness. No face can be seen and two glowing purple orbs imitate eyes.   The rest of his body is covered in dark armor and cloth. Broken pieces of plate and chainmail clang, the unmistakeable sound of cold metal striking, as Maae moves. The tattered pieces of ripped cloth flutter ceaselessly in the wind. The only part of Maar's body that is visible are his two skeletal hand, permanently covered in the same form of purple energy that glows from underneath the hood.  
Personality
As the God of Death, many fear Maar for the domain he controls. Many use his name or his title when trying to describe ultimate evil. While such a mistake is easy to make, it is still a mistake. Maar may be the God of Death, but he is not a malicious figure. He and his reapers perform the duties bestowed upon them.   Maar is aware that many others fear him simply for his existence but it matters little to him. He has control over death and, just like the other deities maintain their domain, he maintains his. He ensures that all souls follow the natural cycle of life and that Amenor remains in balance.   Maar is a Lawful Neutral deity. He does not seek the death of others, he simply controls it. He knows when someone's time has come and so he sends his reapers to assist in their passing. He cares little for their pleas or their attempts to stall, Maar deals out death when the time has come, whether or not the subject is ready.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Maar are a varying bunch. There are those that worship Maar out of fear of death, hoping that their faith can stave off such terror. Naturally, there are also those that have accepted that death is a natural part of life and serve Maar because they wish to spread his teaching.   Then, there are those who have defied death and still worship Maar. Necromancers, vampires and other sentient undead creatures may worship Maar as a way to display their own control over death. Though this is in direct opposition of Maar's teachings as they extend their lives beyond what they should have been, these people tend to care little for such details.  
History
Maar's position as the God of Death keeps him quite removed from the plights and fights of the other deities. His neutral nature also means that he cares little about having stakes in Amenor's major events. With that said however, he tends to dislike most major conflicts due to the large influx of death that usually follows them.  
Relationships
Most of the deities respect Maar for taking on the burden of the domain of death. Even then, they often want little to do with such a sorrowful creature. Some deities that are extremely power-hungry may even fear Maar, paranoid that their time may come before their plans are realized.   There is however one relationships that Maar treasures greatly. The birthing of life is only given meaning if it eventually death, a sentiment that both Maar and Alora agree with. Though their domains keep them at opposite ends of Amenor's cycle of life, they have found a strong relationship with each other.
Maar's divine form.  

    Mortals may fight it or run from it but in the end, death comes for them all.
    — Maar, God of Death.

Maar, God of Death is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of a jawless humanoid skull, a pair of black wings extending in the place of the ears. His divine realm is Hades.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Parents
Tyrabane

title: Mae
content:
Mae
A document detailing Mae, Goddess of Cunning and Chaos.

    Mae is the Goddess of Cunning and Chaos. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Chandryl's and Heyla's essences. She is often referred to as The Everchanging by her followers due to her tricksy nature. She is a being of absolute wisdom and complete unpredictability, doing whatever is to her liking in the moment, whether it be at others expense or benefit.

 
Description
Mae's divine form is difficult to describe and to call it anything but uncertain or everchanging would often be incorrect. Given her chaotic and constantly changing nature, it is unusual that Mae is ever seen in the same form twice. However, despite this constant change, there is one form that she prefers above all the others.   The form is that of a striking humanoid woman, standing almost two meters tall. Her skin is smooth but pale and gray, much reminiscient of a young Changeling. She wears an elaborate dark blue dress lined with strips of shimmering gold. Though the dress itself is dark, a constantly changing starscape seems to shimmer upon its everflowing texture.   The face of this form is hidden beneath a richly adorned veil, or so it would appear at least. Observing her face leads one to strange conclusions as it appears like the veil and her dark hair almost appear to be one, blowing in an invisible wind that completely obscures her face. If this wind ever stopped though, one would not see the faint outline of a face beneath the dark veil, but instead just a completely blank slate. No mouth, no nose, eyes or ears can be seen, but the gray skin seems to almost move and swirl, like stirred paint in a bucket.  
Personality
Mae is the Goddess of Cunning and Chaos, a creature with incredible wisdom. However, one does not become a cunning creature without the ability to lie and weave tales to get what you want. Mae is thus not just a shrewd negotiator and dangerous enemy, but also an incredibly good deceiver and liar. More than anything else though, Mae values chaos. In a situation where the course of action is clear, Mae can never be found. In her mind, chaos is the one and only step to evolution and improvement, not to mention fun...   Mae is a Chaotic Neutral deity, one that uses her cunning and wily tricks in order to gain an advantage over her opponents and further her own goals. She is an independent deity, only forming alliances or making deals when it suits her. She cares for little other than herself.   With that said, Mae quite often encourages others to settle their disputes, be they mortal or divine. She enjoys watching the struggle of power between two equally matched opponents, to see which one will make the more clever decisions. That, and in a struggle between two equally strong creatures, there are often things left behind that one can claim for herself, of course.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Mae are many but quite often poorly organized. The overlap between people who pray to the Goddess of Cunning and Chaos, and have strong motivations about earning great power seems to be quite high. As a result, they very rarely flock together and when they do, their organizations often fall to in-fighting.   With that in mind, Mae remains a Prime Deity since so many, although scattered, people pray to her. The followers of Mae include everything from overconfident teenagers, to power-hungry adventurers to wise druids who fight for noble causes using their wisdom and cunning.  
History
Mae is the second eldest god in all of the Amenorean pantheon. She was the second to ascend after Irwena and she has been a Prime Deity since before Amenor was finished. She used to be mortal, a part of the Marked Septet, the group that defeated Darkheart. She took the place of Chandryl as the Goddess of Cunning and Chaos during The Commence, giving up her mortal family for her ascension.   Due to Mae's cunning mind, she has been involved in almost every divine events throughout the eras of Amenor. Whether she is one of the forefront deities or standing behind the fighting parties, she tends to always have a hand in one outcome. Despite her constant involvement, her guile and trickery makes it difficult to say just how large a part Mae has played in the outcome. The easiest event to define is most likely Presch's Retort.  
Relationships
Mae's wily nature is not unknown to any other gods, making her presence quite unwelcome by most. Most every deity on Amenor knows quite well that she only serves herself and therefore they tend to avoid her. Despite that, Mae's cunning often means that she works closely with other gods, evil and good alike.   Interestingly enough, Mae has a rather strong relationship with Libram. As a god that only cares for knowledge, Libram disregards Mae's plays for power as long as he gains information. Similarly, to be the Goddess of Cunning and Chaos, knowledge is the most important tool. As such, Mae and Libram are quite strong allies in the Amenorean Pantheon. Beyond this relation, Mae also has a strong bond with Irwena, given their shared history.
Mae's divine form.  

    Never fear the unknown. Fear the unchanging.
    — Mae, Goddess of Cunning and Chaos. 

Mae, Goddess of Cunning and Chaos is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of a swirling silver amulet, sporadically decorated with lilac gems. Her divine realm is Limbo.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Neutral
Parents
Chandryl
Heyla
Related Historical Events

    2532 ADD
    Presch's Retort
    712 ADD
    Draconic Merging
    8 ADD
    Mae's Apotheosis

title: Mornal
content:
Mornal
A document detailing Mornal, Goddess of Balance.

    Mornal is the Goddess of Balance. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Draktyl's essences. She is often referred to as Divine Fulcrum for her enforcing of absolute balance. She is a being of relentless and unending equivalence.

 
Description
Mornal's divine form is easily recognizable. A well-traveled mortal would probably identify her as the largest, and strangest, warforged that they have ever seen. Her divine form is that of a female warforged with with and golden skin.   Every joint on her body is covered in an intricate golden metal carving while the rest of the body seems to be made from beautiful white marble. Protruding from many small holes in the golden carvings are flowers, but not real flowers. If one investigates more closely, they can find that the white and golden flowers are also made of metal.   Mornal's face is her most distinguishing quality. Her face only reaches up to the nose, at which point it has been cut clean off. The top half of her face which contains her eyes is held in her hand, allowing her to look around independently of wherever she is facing. Furthermore, a golden, spiky, vertical halo rises from her neck and two more faces sit upon it. These faces are full and stare out to her right and left at all times.  
Personality
As the Goddess of balance, Mornal is a very easy deity to understand. She cares only about all life in the universe being in balance. What balance this is, whether it is a 50/50 split between evil and good or if the distribution is different is unknown to almost all but Mornal.   Most of the time, the universe is in balance or manages to right itself out as Mornal rarely intervenes. When she does however, she is quick and effective in rectifying the problem, whether it be divine or mortal. She does not care for any explanations or motivations, it is rumored even that she cannot understand such sentiments.   Mornal is a Neutral deity, the epitome of a neutral force. Unlike other neutral deities that do not concern themselves with conflicts between good and evil, Mornal does. She only does so in order to ensure that the entire universe remains neutral, in her judgement that is.  
Worshippers
There are many worshippers of Mornal throughout Amenor. Some of them simply confuse the concepts of justice and balance and would be better of praying to Kannder. Those who are not confused however, are people who considers themselves neutral but still have faith, people who believe in a balance cosmos and those that see that some evil is needed so that one can see the good.  
History
Mornal is only concerned with the balance of the universe. Most times, conflicts between divine have a way of equalizing and, as such, she very rarely interferes with such events. There is one exception to this however, and it is during the end of the fifth Era. When Irwena singlehandedly withstood three other deities Mornal realized that there was an imbalance. Thus, she quickly rectified this.  
Relationships
As a truly neutral entity that is only concerned with the balance of the universe, Mornal has no interest in relationships. There are certainly deities that she considers to be a bigger threat and those who she need not concern herself with, such as Libram or Wayleen. With that said though, she cares about none of them.
Mornal's divine form.  

    All life in the universe is subject to eternal balance. When all things are summed up, there should be no other answer than 0.
    — Mornal, Goddess of Balance.

Mornal, Goddess of Balance is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of two filled triangles, their bottoms facing each other with a horizontal line separating them, as well as a horizontal line above each of the triangles. Her divine realm is Mechanus.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Neutral
Parents
Arbaraen
Draktyl
Related Historical Events

    2819 ADD
    Irwena's Punishment

title: Qandezar
content:
Qandezar
A document detailing Qandezar, Goddess of Decay and Rot.

    Qandezar is the Goddess of Decay and Rot. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Elfyrean's and Huwander's essences. She is often referred to as Lifedrinker due to her killing nature. She is a being of endless life absorbance.


 
Description
Qandezar's divine form is one that could be seen on stage of a gothic drama play. It is a humanoid form clad in a floor-length black robe that is tied by a single knot at the chest. The fingers that protrude from under the black robe are long and sallow, darker than even the robes themselves.
  That is where the likeness to a drama actor stops however. Everything else about Qandezar is indicative of her rotting nature. Wherever she goes, a lifeless aura follows. Everything around her turns black and white, sucked of all life so completely that even their colors leave. Her touch, if anything ever lives long enough to feel it, is cold like the grave and leaves necrotic wounds on all living things.
  The most striking feature of Qandezar's appearance are her multiple face. Like a normal humanoid, her neck reaches up to a face that appears normal up to the bridge of the nose. Above that, her face turns into a congealed gray smoke that is decorated with sharp obsidian pearls, acting as her eyes. From this smoke, two more faces form, both identical and always softly whispering. This dense smoke then trails off to a sparse cloud that surrounds Qandezar. Cracked hands, drained bodies and broken faces drift through the smoke at all times.
 
Personality
As the Goddess of Decay and Rot, Qandezar is a purely malicious force. The decay and rot that she desperately craves is what provides her life force. Unlike other deities, who exist because people have faith, Qandezar requires both such faith and a constant absorption of life to be able to live herself.
  Beyond the fact that she requires decay and rot to exist, she deeply enjoys to do so. Absorbing the life of a creature, tasting their essence, feeling their soul are things that Qandezar love to do. It is easy to guess Qandezar's desires and hopes, as they all ultimately entail her absorbing more lifeforces.
  Qandezar is a Lawful Evil deity, a god that can always be counted on to do the evil thing that one would expect from the Goddess of Rot. She seeks the subjugation of all living matter, to make all of it slaves to her life absorbance and every actions she takes reflects that.
 
Worshippers
The worshippers of Qandezar are few and far between. Most of the people that worship her do so in secret cults or organizations that do not publicly declare their faith to the Goddess of Rot. There are some people that find beauty in things due to the fact that they do decay, and they then pray to Qandezar though they often have to explain themselves.
 
History
In the beginning of Amenor's history, Qandezar was confined to absorb life from within her own plane. Few things lived there and thus her power remained as a Lesser Deity. When the lack of aging on Amenor started to become a problem, Qandezar saw an opportunity and took it.
 
Relationships
Qandezar knows well enough that attempting to drain the lifeforce of other deities is an action that is immediately hated. Thanks to this, she does have some pretty strong alliances with other deities, even if the more good-aligned gods despise her.
  Together with Puzkera and Obiterra, she makes up an incredibly dangerous and destructive trio of gods.
Qandezar's divine form.  

    Your life is my sustenance, so live well.
    — Qandezar, Goddess of Decay and Rot.

Qandezar, Goddess of Decay and Rot is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of a punctured human heart, a withering black rose growing out of each hole. Her divine realm is Pandemonium.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Evil
Parents
Elfyrean
Huwander
Related Historical Events

    495 ADD
    Aging Begins

title: Soyal
content:
Soyal
A document detailing Soyal, God of the Sun and Light.

    Soyal is the God of the Sun and Light. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Gordfein's essences. He is often referred to as Lightfather by his followers for his brilliant nature. He is a being of endless positivity and illumination.

 
Description
As one can imagine for the God of Light, Soyal's divine form is easily recognizable. However, as easy as it is to recognize, it is much harder to actually see because of its incredible brilliance. At all times, Soyal's divine form shines with the brightness of a star and very few can actually withstand its luminescence to look upon the form.   Assuming one does manage to withstand the brightness, they would see the humanoid form of a male aasimar. A pair of golden silk pants reach to just above the ankles where bare feet are visible. His upper body is unclothed and impressively muscular.   Long, golden-blonde hair is braided multiple times but still reaches down just below the shoulder blades. As it does, it covers where two massive wings protrude from the body. These divine wings are formed of soft white feathers and though they are not that large, spectral light in the form of wings surround them and make them even larger.  
Personality
As the God of Light, Soyal is incredible fond of assisting others. Whenever and wherever he can lend aid, he does. Naturally, he only gives aid to those that desire it and never forces his assistance upon anyone else. It is in his guidance, like a shining beacon, that he takes most pride.   Particularly, Soyal has a fondness for guiding mortals that have lost their way or are confused. To be that guiding light for someone who is fumbling around in darkness is Soyal's greatest passion. Sometimes the blinding light that Soyal represents needs to be used to burn away evil, but it is a task that he does not enjoy. By mortal standards, Soyal would certainly be considered a pacifist.   Soyal is a Lawful Good deity, one that enjoys seeing happiness in others. Though his stance on the divine role as caregivers, protectors and guides is firm, he often shies away from confrontation. He feels that he has nothing to prove and to argue with others is unfruitful.  
Worshippers
There are many worshippers of Soyal, spread all across Amenor. With that said, the places that see more sunlight often have a larger percent of Soyal worshippers compared to the places that see less. Soyal's worshippers are mostly made of people who have found themselves lost or unsure in their lives. People who craved guidance, sought an answer to the next step in life. That is the perfect situation for Soyal to step into.  
History
As mentioned above, Soyal is someone who avoids conflict to a high degree. Throughout Amenor's history, he has not been involved in major historical events. However, the fallout from these events often affect mortals and that is where Soyal can be found. Providing his aid and helping those that are not involved in, but affected by divine conflict.  
Relationships
Soyal has a strong relationship with all other good deities, considering them allies and friends alike. He vehemently avoids the rest of the deities however, considering them the antithesis to his divine philosophy. Furthermore, Soyal has a loving relationship together with Wynderialdessa as they keep the extraterrestrial planets together.
Soyal's divine form.  

    In light there is truth, redemption, salvation, beauty and love.
    — Soyal, God of the Sun and Light.

Soyal, God of the Sun and Light is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of a shining sun, ten waves of rippling heat emanating from the central circle. His divine realm is Luxon.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Good
Parents
Arbaraen
Gordfein

title: Terra
content:
Terra
A document detailing Terra, God of Earth and Gems.

    Terra is the God of Earth and Gems. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Elfyrean's and Draktyl's essences. He is often referred to as Rockbreaker by his followers for obvious reasons. He is a being of endless stability and strength.

 
Description
Terra's divine form is quite recognizable. It is that of an absolute mountain of a humanoid man. His lower body is covered by a gray kilt, detailed with brown linings and many colorful and beautiful gems. Such gems also adorn the belt that holds fast the kilt, as well as the fingerless gloves that protects his massive hands.   Though his clothing is unstained, Terra's body is filthy, covered in dirt and muck. No matter how violent the rain, nothing ever washes off the dirt that cover his muscles. Exempt from this dirt is his face and facial hair. The skin of his face is much lighter than the rest of his body due to the dirt and his hair and beard are the same shock-white color.   Of course, the most distinct thing about Terra is his imposing stature. Standing almost three meters tall, he is perhaps not the tallest deity but that would be the only small thing about him. His muscles are absurdly large, the veins are clear and the ground itself shakes as Terra walks.  
Personality
Terra is an incredibly proud deity. He considers his domain the most impactful of all divine domain and he serves it with incredible respect and control. For the most part, he is a steadfast deity, one that stands strong in the face of adversity and never wavers.   There is one exception to this however, which is when he considers his pride to be insulted. If one mistreats or insults the earth, Terra's rage is easy to notice. Earthquakes, rockslides and other dangerous events are the ways that Terra unleashes his anger.   Terra is a Chaotic Neutral deity. He is a primordial force as much as he is a god. He is proud and reliable, yet chaotic, but he is ambivalent towards both mortals and gods. He cares little for what others do, or who the world belongs to, as long as he gets to do what he wants.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Terra are rather many throughout Amenor. People who work in mines, who search for or appraise gems and most certainly dwarves. For all the followers that Terra has however, he would need none of them to remain a Prime Deity. He is a manifestation of a primordial power and he will remain as long as Amenor's mountains and hills exist.  
History
As a neutral primordial force, Terra has certainly had effects on major events throughout Amenor's history. A major cave-in may have claimed the lives of many miners, a mountain may have broken apart and crashed into a nearby town. Though the list can go on, Terra has had no stake in Amenor's history. He cares little for it, or the creatures involved in it.  
Relationships
Terra is inexorably tied to the other three deities of primordial force, Aqua, Ventus and Ignis. Together, the four of them represent the primordial forces surrounding the Material Plane. Just like them, he calls her own plane, the Plane of Earth home. Beyond his three brethren, he is also connected to the season that most appreciates him. As such, he is romantically entangled with the God of Autumn, Eladrey.
Terra's divine form.  

    The earth is stable and dependable, its beauty hidden deep within itself.
    —  Terra, God of Earth and Gems.

Terra, God of Earth and Gems is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of a rough-hewn diamond, made from ten straight lines. His divine realm is the Plane of Earth.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Neutral
Parents
Elfyrean
Draktyl

title: Varileth
content:
Varileth
A document detailing Varileth, God of Fate.

    Varileth is the God of Fate. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Gordfein's and Heyla's essences. He is often referred to as Fatestitcher by his followers for his weaving of fate. He is a being with eternal knowledge of the future of all.

 
Description
Varileth's divine form is quite unremarkable. It is simply that of an old human man somewhere in his late seventies. His back is slightly hunched and his white-gray beard reaches all the way down to his waist. The case is the same for his hair, which is neatly combed.   What distinguishes Varileth from a normal mortal man is mostly his garb. He wears a long-sleeved robe of a dark aquamarine color. It's edges and frills are all decorated with golden accents but none of them give any weight to the clothing, instead magically hovering just above it.   These accents are not the only golden things that are associated with Varileth. Firstly, a bair of brilliantly shining golden spectacles add to his old man visage. Secondly, a singular golden thread weaves its way around Varileth at all times. It is dull and hard to see but it is the thread of fate that Varileth controls.  
Personality
Varileth may be the second most knowledgable creature in existence, behind Libram. While their knowledge certainly differs, Varileth has seen enough stories and chronicled enough events that he has become incredibly wise.   This is evident in his personality, how he speaks considers every opportunity and outcome of a situation. He never speaks unless he has given his thoughts their due consideration and when he does, he always speaks calmly and collected. There is only one thing that bothers him, which is creatures that act spontaneously or on instinct.   Varileth is a Neutral Good deity, one that aims to provide as much subtle aid as he can. Through soft nudges, sudden inclinations and quiet suggestions he provides guidance to mortals to keep them on the right thread of fate. He never interferes too much though, nor does he ever make the decision for a mortal. He only gives them ideas and thoughts that might help them make a difficult choice.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Varileth are many across Amenor. They consist of many different people but all of them tend to have a special characteristic, which is that they are control freaks. They dislike to leave things up to chance and through their worship of Varileth, they express their desire of control over fate and the future.  
History
As the God of Fate, Varileth is well aware of all the major events of Amenor's history. In fact, he most likely knew about their coming before they even happened. With that said however, he is simply an innocent bystander and not ever involved in these conflicts. He controls the flow of fate and chronicles these events and he is not one to intervene in them.  
Relationship
The burden of knowledge can often be great, which is why Varileth has found a kinship in Libram. Their vast experience and knowledge has made them good friends. In addition to this, he also has a strong friendship with the god of stories, X, due to their mutual love for and knowledge of mortal experiences.
Varileth's divine form.  

    Every single thread of fate is woven of the same material, yet the outcomes remain remarkably unique!
    — Varileth, God of Fate.

Varileth, God of Fate is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of a spinning wheel decorated with pocket watches. His divine realm is Arcadia.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Neutral Good
Parents
Gordfein
Heyla

title: Velyndra & Vortharax
content:
Velyndra & Vortharax
A document detailing Velyndra & Vortharax, God of Dragons.

    Velyndra & Vortharax is the God of Dragons. They are one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Draktyl's and Heyla's essences. They are often referred to as Dawnscale and Duskscale, respectively, by their followers. They are a unique pair of deities since they share the same body and are never conscious at the same time.

 
Description
Though the God of Dragons has two distinct forms that they take, both are very recognizable. Both divine forms are that of a massive dragon, one that would cast a shadow over most of even the largest cities on Amenor. However, it is only in size that they are similar.   Velyndra's divine form is that of gold dragon. She has the same sagacious face, sail-like wings and long, sweeping horns. Her eyes shine with a deep, glimmering golden like, like a coin under sunlight. Wherever she goes, light seems to follow, making her scales glitter in the sunlight.   Vortharax, on the other hand, has the form of a red dragon. He has the same proud bearing, beaked face and spinal frill. His horns are shorter than Velyndra's but much sharper. His scaled are a deep red, like the deepest depths of a lava lake. His massive wings are tinted blue-black along the edge, resembling metal burned by fire.  
Personality
Velyndra and Vortharax share the same body but have opposite ideals. When one is in control, the other is consigned to watch the actions of their counterpart with deep disdain. When one of them takes control from the other, their body transformed back into their chosen divine form while the other is thrust back into the depths of their shared mind.   It is the number of metallic and chromatic dragons that determines who of the two is in control. When Amenor has more metallic dragons, Velyndra is in control. When there are more chromatic dragons, Vortharax is in charge. Due to the fact that the number of chromatic and metallic dragons are so similar, their control fluctuates often as dragon are birthed and killed.   Velyndra is a Lawful Good deity, a shining beacon of virtue and justice. She fights for those who cannot fight for themselves and she helps those that need it, no matter the cost to herself. She is friendly and forgiving, yet blunt and honest. To defend and provide aid are the things that matter most to her.   Vortharax is a Lawful Evil deity, a pillar of strength and destruction. He desires to raze as much as possible, to fight all that he can until something can actually challenge him. The more he razes and conquers, the more his hoard grows, only for it to be given back to the affected whenever Velyndra regains control. Vortharax hungers for Velyndra's death more than anything.  
Worshippers
There are some people among Amenor that worship either Velyndra or Vortharax, even some that may worship both. These are no the prime worshippers of the God of Dragons however, Naturally, most metallic dragons follow Velyndra while most chromatic dragons worship Vortharax. Whether they are a chromatic or metallic dragon, very few dragons are content with the fact that the one they put their faith in often is consumed by their greatest foe.  
History
Velyndra and Vortharax used to be the two oldest dragons in existence, yet still just mortal. Their hoards were vast and their power even greater, yet they lived completely different lives. Velyndra offered protection and help to all mortals that needed it while Vortharax destroyed and raided. The two engaged in battle many times but only ever managed to slow each other down.   Eventually Vortharax would go on a spree of destruction so great that Velyndra seeks help from Aisha, who is happy to grant it. The powers given to Velyndra by Aisha causes the two dragons to merge into one body, ending Vortharax's rampage.  
Relationships
Due to the incredibly different ideals and goals of the God of Dragons, as well as how often they change, there are few deities in Amenor that interact much with Velyndra or Vortharax. Though each of them can certainly count some different members of the pantheon as allies, they have little chance to develop a stronger bond than that.
Velyndra's & Vortharax's divine forms.  

    Strike terror into their hearts, rip apart their flesh and... // Die! Heed his orders and die at my claws!
    — Vortharax & Velyndra, God of Dragons.

Velyndra & Vortharax is one of the Prime Deities. Velyndra's holy symbol is that of the head of a golden dragon in profile, facing right. Vortharax's holy symbol is that of the head of a red dragon in profile, facing left. Their divine realm is Draconia.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Good & Lawful Evil
Parents
Draktyl
Heyla
Related Historical Events

    712 ADD
    Draconic Merging
    688 ADD
    Vortharax's Rampage

title: Ventus
content:
Ventus
A document detailing Ventus, Goddess of Wind and Change.

    Ventus is the Goddess of Wind and Change. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Elfyrean's and Chandryl's essences. She is often referred to as Mother Wind by her followers for obvious reasons. She is a being of constant change and movement.

 
Description
As the Goddess of Wind and Change, Ventus' divine form is often hard to recognize. She very rarely keeps the same divine form for long, switching through multitudes of humanoid shapes. Even when she has taken humanoid form, it is often so translucent and flowing that it can be hard to see.   With that said, there is one form that she favors above the others. Though she does not like it as much as constantly changing, she likes it well enough that is the most common description of her. This divine form is that of a humanoid woman, white and fluffly in appearance.   She has a pale face, with turquoise eyes and a soft expression. Her hair is long, falling down to the middle of the back. It is white and soft as a cloud, always blowing in the wind that forms wherever she goes. Her clothing is a soft white dress that fades into a cloud formation at an indistinguishable point.  
Personality
Ventus embodies the ideas of change in its most literal sense. Nothing she touches ever stays the same and she would have it no other way. This change is something that has included Ventus herself and she is an ever fickle deity due to this.   Ventus changes her appearance, ideals, personality and characteristics on the daily, never quite settling on any one thing. Some days her temper is foul and the harshest winds blow. Other days she finds herself in a calm mood and only a soft and quiet breeze blows. The only thing that never changes with Ventus is change itself. You can always be certain that the wind will never blow the same way again.   Ventus is a Chaotic Neutral deity. She is a primordial force as much as she is a goddess. She is unpredictable and chaotic but she is ambivalent towards both mortals and gods. She cares little for what others do, or who the world belongs to, as long as she gets to do what she wants.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Ventus are rather many throughout Amenor. People who can't decide who they should be, people who are going through middle-life crises, druids who worship nature are among the worshippers of Ventus. For all the followers that Ventus has however, she would need none of them to remain a Prime Deity. She is a manifestation of a primordial power and she will remain as long as wind blows throughout Amenor.  
History
As a neutral primordial force, Ventus has certainly had effects on major events throughout Amenor's history. The wind might have blown strong during a siege, rendering ranged defenses futile. It might have dispersed a gathered fog, revealing an ambush. Though the list can go on, Ventus has had no stake in Amenor's history. She cares little for it, or the creatures involved in it.  
Relationships
Ventus is inexorably tied to the other three deities of primordial force, Ignis, Terra and Aqua. Together, the four of them represent the primordial forces surrounding the Material Plane. Just like them, she calls her own plane, the Plane of Air home. Beyond her three brethren, she is also connected to the season that most appreciates her. As such, she is romantically entangled with the Goddess of Spring, Alora. She cares little for Maar's position in this relationship, and little for him at all.
Ventus' divine form.  

    The winds of change wait for no one, nor do they ever bend.
    — Ventus, Goddess of Wind and Change.

Ventus, Goddess of Wind and Change is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of three horizontal lines that curl at the end, signifying wind. Her divine realm is the Plane of Air.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Neutral
Parents
Elfyrean
Chandryl

title: Virá
content:
Virá
A document detailing Virá, Goddess of Summer, Beauty and Love.

    Virá is the Goddess of Summer, Beauty and Love. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Tyrabane's essences. She is often referred to as Lover by her followers due to her warmth. She is a being of incredible beauty and endless love.

 
Description
Virá's divine form is one of unfathomable beauty, one that makes mortal men and women alike fall in love at the mere sight of her. An aasimar woman of average height, Vira is wrapped in a light-pink silken dress that naturally rests upon her fair skin like a soft kiss.   Her divine form has a youtful face that always holds a warm and friendly smile. Her pink eyes glimmer with acceptance and attention, giving the feeling that nothing else in the world matters beyond what Virá looks at. Wherever Virá goes, she floats softly above the ground as she leaves a trail of pink roses.   Virá's hair is white with a soft pink tint, and it is many times longer than she is tall. Even as she stands still, her hair floats like cotton clouds around her, obscuring parts of her body that her dress does little to conceal. Atop her head is a tiara of pink flowers, a splash of concentrated pink crowning her soft pink appearance.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Love, Virá is the embodiement of warmth and acceptance. She is kind, loving, supportive and beautiful, the most perfect partner a mortal could ever ask for. She is forgiving and accepting, being able to look past mistakes and see the best in everyone, deity and mortal alike.   For as beautiful that Virá is, she knows it. As kind and loving she is, she is equally vain, often basking in her own beauty. She is fully aware of the effect that she has on others and she is not above using it to get what she wants. With that said, she rarely wants more than the love and happiness of others.   Virá is a Lawful Good deity, an entity that does all she can for others to be happy. She believes that everyone should get to experience beauty and that everyone should be loved. To her, almost no one is beyond redemption, almost everyone can show love if love is shown to them. There is however one group of people that she considers beyond redemption, one action that enrages her unlike anything else: adultery.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Virá are many, spread all across Amenor. Generally, the worshippers of Virá are people who are kind, loving and happy people. People who like to help or guide others often give prayers to Virá. Furthermore, a priest or cleric of Virá is often present at wedding ceremonies, signifying the unending love that is being committed to.   However, beyond people that worship the love that Virá stands for, there are also others who worship her for beauty. These are often people who are incredibly vain and shallow, caring only about beauty and forgetting or dismissing the rest of Virá's teachings.  
History
Virá has been a staunch protector of mortals and their lives throughout the history of Amenor. She has been instrumental in keeping evil forces away from the people of Amenor, being involved in almost all major events throughout Amenor's history.  
Relationships
As a beacon of love and positivity, Virá is absolutely insufferable to the evil deities of Amenor. Simultaneously, good deities appreciate the warmth and love that she bring to Amenor while neutral deities often can find her a bit too intense.   Virá has a strong relationship with her sister, Alora. Both of them being children of Arbaraen makes them similar in a lot of aspects and that has led to a strong sisterly bond. Furthermore, as the Goddess of Summer she is naturally connected to the primordial force representing that season. As such, she is the wife of Ignis.
Virá's divine form.  

    To experience beauty and to be loved are mortal rights.
    — Virá, Goddess of Summer, Beauty and Love.

Virá, Goddess of Summer, Beauty and Love is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of two beautiful pink roses on either side of a heart. Her divine realm is Mount Celestia.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Good
Parents
Arbaraen
Tyrabane

title: Wynderialdessa
content:
Wynderialdessa
A document detailing Wynderialdessa, Goddess of the Moon and Stars.

    Wynderialdessa is the Goddess of the Moon and Stars. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Elfyrean's and Heyla's essences.
