She is often referred to as The Cosmic Matriarch for her influence over the cosmos. She is a being of reliability, glittering beauty and hidden aid.

 
Description
Wynderialdessa's divine form is a towering figure that is very recognizable. On nights when the sky is clear it is often possible to see her massive form among the stars, never in the same place. Her divine form is vaguely feminine but many many kilometers tall.   Formed entirely of stardust, her divine form is a congealed mass of purple and pink cosmic colors. As she strides across the nigh sky, faintly glimmering starlight trails behind her. Though it is often difficult to see from such great distances, Wynderialdessa's face is well defined by a light purple color, resembling that of a human woman.   Her hair consists of swirling purple stardust encircling a constantly flaring supernova. It expands and shrinks, blinking like a soft light on the distant horizon at all times. Whenever the sun is up, Wynderialdessa's glow is to faint to be seen on the sky but as night falls, she becomes visible once more.  
Personality
As the God of the Moon and Stars, Wynderialdessa is a steadfast creature. The moon may move upon the night sky and the stars may glimmer differently each night, but they will always be there. It is this reliability and fortitude that Wynderialdessa prides herself upon.   Wynderialdessa is a calm and quiet deity. She controls the movement of most celestial bodies, ensures that the moon rises each night on Amenor and controls her domain without interacting with mortals. Despite how her domain has few interactions with mortals, she is utterly fascinated by their lives and stories. She is never a prominent figure in the history of Amenor, but with her control over the celestial bodies she provides mortal with as much hidden aid as she can.   Wynderialdessa is a Lawful Good deity, one that enjoys seeing happiness in others. The scope and scale of her work is so grand that she has found a love for the minute details of human life. Within the scopes of her domain, she provides as much guidance as she can, serving her followers as a steadfast and reliable deity.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Wynderialdessa are many, spread all across Amenor. Her eternal guidance and benevolent nature have drawn many into her fold. One should not underestimate the influence that her visage has had either. Many people who simply need a little guidance or a little help often find themselves staring up at the night sky and Wynderialdessa's distant divine form, speaking towards it. Though the words may not reach her, being able to see the deity you are communicating with is comfortable for many.  
History
Wynderialdessa's fascination with mortals and hidden aid has been a constant throughout the history of Amenor. She has been involved with most major events, striving against the evil forces that seek to subvert and destroy Amenor. Most notably has she stopped a disaster created by Obiterra.  
Relationships
Wynderialdessa's divine domain keeps her a large distance from most other deities and their corresponding planes. Her time in the eternal and infinite cosmos has not been lonely however. Wynderialdessa spends much of her time together with her counterpart, Soyal. Together, they have a strong and loving relationship, bounded eternally in the cosmos.
Wynderialdessa's divine form.  

    The tides may ebb and flow, but the stars in the sky shimmer just as bright each night.
    — Wynderialdessa, Goddess of the Moon and Stars.

Wynderialdessa, Goddess of the Moon, and Stars is one of the Prime Deities. Her holy symbol is that of the moon, illuminated by seven sparkling stars around it. Her divine realm is the Astral Plane.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Lawful Good
Parents
Elfyrean
Heyla
Related Historical Events

    2273 ADD
    The Consuming Flame

title: Ze'Tren
content:
Ze'Tren
A document detailing Ze'Tren, God of Secrets.

    Ze'Tren is the God of Secrets. He is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Draktyl's and Huwander's essences. Due to his extremely mysterious nature, his followers have nothing they refer to him by. It should also be noted that while Ze'Tren does have a few worshippers throughout Amenor, most of his existence and divine rank is supported by the mere existence of secrets in Amenor.

 
Description
???  
Personality
???  
Worshippers
There are some people throughout Amenor that pray and give worship to Ze'Tren. These people value secrets and confidentiality highly and these are aspects that Ze'Tren also embodies. Unfortunately for his followers, he embodies them so strongly that these mortals barely know what they are praying to.  
History
???
Relationships
???
Ze'Tren's divine form?  

    ...
    — Ze'Tren, God of Secrets.

Ze'Tren, God of Secrets is one of the Prime Deities. His holy symbol is that of an old scroll, caught aflame. His divine realms is ???.
Divine Classification
Prime Deity
Alignment
Neutral
Parents
Draktyl
Huwander

title: The Athar
content:
The Athar
A document detailing the magic-teaching organization of The Athar.

    To delve deep into the realm of magic is to gain a greater understanding and control of our enchanted world. Without magic, we would be lost to barbaric impulses and solutions. Magic is suffused with the very roots of our world, making it not only our future, but also our present and past.
    — A common sentiment for members of The Athar.

 
Agenda
The Athar is an institution focused on learning and educating those with magical capabilities and prowess. It is a purely magical organization which has no room, nor interest, for dealing with matters and problems in a conventional way, instead always using magical solutions.   To liken The Athar to a magical university is very apt. The teachers and professors of the organization are well-versed within their magical fields, often performing their own study and research on the side of their teaching work. The students who attend classes are people with magical capabilities, whether innate or taught, who wish to hone their skills over such a mystical art.   It is also important to mention the differences between a normal university and The Athar however. Unlike a normal university, The Athar is a plane-wide spanning organization, having places of learning in almost every larger settlement of Amenor. Unlike a normal university, there are no planned educations and everyone's journey of self-improvement and knowledge gathering is individual, governed by the classes that are available to them.  
Membership
Membership in The Athar is a possibility to all people of Amenor, given a few factors. Firstly, every student of The Athar is expected to pay a tuition to be able to attend lectures, access the libraries and such. This tuition is individual and depends on the performance of the student, though it tends to be lower for first-years, allowing more people to try their hand at magic.   Secondly, two weeks a year, entry exams are held for joining the organization. Anyone who wishes to study at The Athar simply need to pick a school of magic, show up at their corresponding Athar institution and complete the entry exam which consists of members asking a variety of questions, along with the applicant having to show off some form of magical control aligned with the chosen school.   Both of these weeks are also followed by internal exams held the following weeks where the members of The Athar need to prove that they still have what it takes to remain a student. Their performance during this exam is what determines the price of their tuition, with a poor performance raising the cost of the tuition.   Once one has become a member of The Athar, you can remain such for as long as you desire and continue to prove yourself. Your level of experience is determined by your elders and the chance for promotions also arise during the internal exams. The levels of membership, ranked in ascending order of seniority, are list below:  

    Student - The most common rank among The Athar. This ranges from the ones who have just passed their entrance exam to those who are about five years deep in their studies.
    Apprentice - Those who have shown skill and magical profiency through their studies. Most of them are commited to a Weaver as an apprentice, being taught privately and assisting in teaching.
    Weaver - The teachers and professors of The Athar, educating others while performing their own research on the side. For most people, this rank requires at least a decade of studies before it is achieved.
    Archmage - The most powerful mages of The Athar. At any time, there are only eight of these, one for each school of magic. They have the final say in any matter and organize the school. Due to magic like Clone, the turnaround on these positions are very low, essentially only occuring when an Archmage considers their time fulfilled.

 
Culture
The culture of The Athar is a typical university culture, spiced up by the use of magic. The students and apprentices are often worked to the bone in order to keep up with their peers and this pressure results in a typical, "work hard, play hard" environment.   That is not to say that the grounds of any Athar institution is a degenerate hive of partying and drinking. This aspect certainly shines through on the tenends but it is not the reputation of The Athar. The typical grounds of The Athar are filled with students walking by with their head in a book that is carried by their Mage Hand, or a group of apprentices honing their magical skill by playing arm wrestling with their Bigby's Hand.   Naturally, the location of the institution will also influence the culture present on the grounds. The culture of the settlement will often make its mark upon the students and people at The Athar, blending with their own. That being said though, The Athar is a well-respected, prestigious place of learning for those with magical capabilites, no matter where in Amenor you are.   Their slogan of sine magia, stagnatio, which is Celestial for "Without magic, stagnation" speaks for itself on their relation to magic. The people of The Athar believe that magic is the way to further the world, to move forward in time and evolution and to solve more and more problems.  
History
The Athar is the eldest organization in all of Amenor, originally founded way back in the 3rd Era of Taldarin. Since then, the organization has gone through a number of major changes that has led to it being what it is today. Included in these changes are things like the lifting of the ban on life-extending magic or the removal of teaching in subjects like math and physics.   Both of these decisions, which are the largest changes to the organization, happened in 73 BA, 4th Era, just after the point of Darkheart's death. Many of the Archmages back then had been swayed to the side of Darkheart but were fortunately put down by The Marked Septet. Only three of the original Archmages remained when all was said and done: Iardec Wuldhall, Archmage of Enchantment; Sylleth Shivi, Archmage of Necromancy; Sirxus Gadius, Archmage of Evocation.   It was these three that made the above decisions and many more that altered the framework of the organization. The most controversial change has certainly been the lifting of the ban on life-extending magic and though they point to the failure of the organization prior to this change, it is hard to argue with the personal gain from such a change. Especially considering that now, over 4400 years later, all three of them are still among the ruling Archmages of The Athar.   Beyond the shaky history of The Athar that occured before The Commence, the organization has had a rather quiet past. For the entirety of Amenor's existence, it has simply been a respected place of learning for those with magical powers. They have remained neutral in any and every larger conflict and kept their business to themselves, with one major exception.   During the late years of the Era of Divine Division, tensions had been building between The Athar and The Reliquary. The amount of magical artifacts that found their way into the world during this Era had been unprecedented and many of them had found a home, either with The Athar or The Reliquary.   Naturally, they wanted them for different reasons which led to disagreements on who got to keep even what they found. This tension kept growing over the era until eventually it bubbled over completely and a small group of mages from The Athar assaulted the Arcane Lock in hopes of stealing a number of artifacts. Though all the perpetrators were stopped and put to the sword, some of the artifacts were lost in the skirmish and has not been seen since. After the incident, The Athar would publicly claim that it was a group of independent mages who acted and that they despised their actions. Given the amount of gear that these "independent mages" were given however, it is a difficult claim to make. Ever since then, the event known as The Arcane Breach, The Athar and The Reliquary have remained in opposition, disagreeing on the best use of magical artifacts.


"Sine magia, stagnatio"
The crest and slogan of The Athar.
Founding Date
112 BA, 3rd Era
Type
Education, Magic
Alternative Names
The Bookworms
Location
Taldarin
Related Historical Events

    2638 ADD
    The Arcane Breach

title: The Caged Cabal
content:
The Caged Cabal
A document detailing the knowledge-seeking organization of The Caged Cabal.

    The rite of information gain is much like the right of information gain. Only those who dare to take the deepest plunge will learn the greatest secrets.
    — A common saying in the Caged Cabal.

 
Agenda
The Caged Cabal is a research institution that is focused on the acquirement of new knowledge and sciences. They are primarily a non-magical faction though there are a number of wizards and artificers among their ranks. The Caged Cabal strive to further humanoid understanding of the non-magical aspects of the world.   They are physicists, chemists, biologists and the like that desire to understand all natural phenomena that occur in the world. This includes everything from understanding the formation of earthquakes to analyzing the energy spectrum of atomic particles.   The knowledge gleaned by the members of the Caged Cabal is published in papers that are kept within the headquarters of the organization. While these papers are technically public records and anyone may view them, the amount of hoops that one needs to jump through in order to do so often means that the knowledge of the Caged Cabal is kept within its walls and between its members.  
Membership
To be a member of the organization is to have an unquenchable thirst for knowledge. Your desire to unearth the secrets of the natural universe should know no bounds and your work should reflect that. Furthermore, if you take an interest in understanding or researching magical matters, you are better suited at other organizations.   In order to become a member of the Caged Cabal, there are primarily three things that are needed. Firstly, you need to have previous publications from another university that show an interest in these matters. Secondly, you need to have someone within the Caged Cabal vouch for you. In most scenarios, this means that you need to already know them. Thirdly, you need to complete a brutal entrance exam of which only 5% of the participants are accepted.   The second point of this journey to membership is one that is heavily critized by others. In most cases, this familiarity comes in the form of family members. As such, many of the people who are granted membership within the Caged Cabal are done so by nepotism. The Caged Cabal deny such accusations, claiming that anyone with enough of a drive can be admitted into the organization.  
Culture
The Caged Cabal value information and knowledge over all else. This is evident by their slogan, Cognitio Quaerenti, which is Celestial for "Seeker's Knowledge". Essentially, knowledge will only come to those who seek for it.   This pursuit for knowledge is imprinted upon the members of the Caged Cabal as well. For example, members that desire to take a leave of absence in order to raise a newborn child are often terminated instead, seeing as their priorities are clearly misplaced.   It is this ruthless pursuit of knowledge that gives the Caged Cabal a grim reputation. They are commonly referred to as "The Cruel Cabal" instead, given how easily they discard matters that do not entail the gain of new knowledge.  
History
The Caged Cabal was founded in the year 12 ADD of the Era of Longevity. It is one of the oldest organizations of Amenor as it is the first organization that was established after The Commence.   Throughout history, The Caged Cabal has been slowly growing, adding more and more members to their ranks. Their pursuit of knowledge has yielded some great improvements in humanoid understanding of the natural world. What they are best known for however, is probably their greatest failure.   In the year 977 ADD of the Era of Growth, they established a research station in the northern reaches of The Crooked Tooth, by the name of The Silo. It stood for more than a thousand years before it fell into ruin and was abandoned by the Caged Cabal. To this day, no official explanation beyond "harsh weather" has been given as a reason for its destruction.

Cognitio Quaerenti"
The crest and slogan of The Caged Cabal.
Founding Date
18th of Chandran, 12 ADD, Era of Longevity
Type
Educational, Scientific Institute
Alternative Names
The Cruel Cabal
Demonym
Cabal
Location
Elendar
Related Historical Events

    2554 ADD
    Destruction of The Silo
    1479 ADD
    Establishing of The Silo
    222 ADD
    Establishing of The Caged Cabal

title: The Emerald Blades
content:
The Emerald Blades
A document detailing the mercenary organization of the Emerald Blades.

    Amenor is a paradoxical place, being a plane of great beauty but also great dangers. Our job is to ensure that the common person only ever experiences the beauty. The monsters and enemies that lurk outside our walls shall feel the sting of our swords and learn to fear the Emerald Blades!
    — A common quote among members of the Emerald Blades.

 
Agenda
The Emerald Blades is what one could describe as a mercenary guild. They are a collection of adventurers, mercenaries, bandits, retired guards and the like, anyone who has decided that they would wish to use their sword to not only help themselves, but also others.   The organization is concerned with helping people with taking care of dangers that they are not capable of dealing with themselves. It could be any sort of danger, everything from rats in the basement, to cougars in the basement. You might need protection when traveling, help dealing with wolves attacking the livestock or anything of the like.   All of these helpful acts have a price, of course. The Emerald Blades are the very definition of swords-for-hire. If you have a problem that you would like to stick a sword in, the Emerald Blades keenly oblige, charging an amount based on the perceived danger of the mission.   It is interesting to note that the agenda of the Emerald Blades is pretty much evenly divided between helping the people of Amenor and earning money. They are equally happy to help and equally happy to receive a pouch of coins. Furthermore, their moral scruples are very few and, should you pay enough, they will happily perform shakedowns or deliver very clear threats.  
Membership
Though the members of the organization come from many different walks of life, they now fit under the umbrella that is "mercenary". Their skill and experience with the blade varies greatly across all the members but you do need to have some skill in order to join.   The Emerald Blades welcomes almost anyone into their fold, should they pass the entrance test. A prospective member has to show off their skill in combat, fighting members of the Emerald Blades. They start by battling the most inexperienced member and then move as far up the chain of experience as they can before they get defeated. Assuming that they win at least one fight, their requested membership is approved.   This welcoming procedure makes it seem like the Emerald Blades has a clear hierarcical structure, but that is not the case. All members have the same rank, no matter the experience or number of years as a member. Ranks naturally form within this kind of organization due to the varying levels of skill but none are officially recognized.   The salary of each member is entirely up to the member themselves. Many of them are traveling mercenaries who seek out any kind of work, wherever it can be found. Others keep to the guild houses in various settlements, waiting for people to come with prospective work and then leaping at it before another member can. That is not to say that all work is done solo though. For example, if a client desires the death of a dragon that has been sacking trade routes, many Emerald Blades often band together for the mission and decide to split the loot.   As previously stated, almost anyone is allowed to join the Emerald Blades. The exception is magic-wielders. You need to defeat another member of the organization in martial combat, no spells are allowed. This is an incredibly strict rule, applying to paladins, hexed warriors, rangers and the like as well. If you rely on or amplify your battle prowess with magic, there is no place for you among the Blades.  
Culture
The culture of the Emerald Blades is very varied among its members. Since there are no official ranks nor any obligatory tasks for a member, many do not really affiliate themselves with the organization more than a way to provide legitimacy to their mercenary services.   Some members spend years on the road. Some members are drafted into wars and enjoy a steady source of income, the life of a member varies greatly. The ones that do spend time in a guildhouse and affiliate themselves strongly with the organization are often brutish characters. Men and women who prefer a strong ale and a bloodied sword to any other kind of activity. Proud and loud, they often mirror their dragons of the organizations origins.   This abrasive nature is well captured by the organizations slogan: sanguis, viscera et cerevisia, which is Celestial for "blood, guts and ale". The slogan is simple and catchy, describing the favorite things of a typical Emerald Blades member.  
History
The Emerald Blades was founded after the settling of Dragonhold. When the heroic fighters managed to defeat even the greatest of wyrms and claim the region of Elezari, they found themselves purposeless. They had slain the great beasts that they set out to kill and there was nothing left for them.   It did not take long for them to realize however, that many parts of Amenor still house dangerous creatures lurking in the dark. It was then that they created the Emerald Blades, an homage to the jungle city that they had helped create.   The reputation of the heroes that slew the dragons helped to grow the organization a lot in its early days. Since then, it has reached a steady state where the number of new members match the number of members that hang up the sword or are permanently employed by wealthy merchants or warring nations.   It goes without saying then also, that current times have been very good for the Emerald Blades. In times of peace, there is often not quite enough work for every member but in times of war, there is plenty. Both sides of the current conflict pay large sums to employ the most skilled of fighters in the Emerald Blades, which quickly fills the pockets of these members.


"Sanguis, viscera et cerevisia"
The crest and slogan of the Emerald Blades.
Founding Date
27th of Chandran, 1114 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Guild, Fighter / Mercenary
Alternative Names
The Dragonslayers
Location
Dragonhold
Related Historical Events

    1617 ADD
    Founding of the Emerald Blades

title: The Golden Trust
content:
The Golden Trust
A document detailing the money-handling organization of the Golden Trust.

    Has your family had a stroke of bad luck? Have all your sheep been eaten by wolves in the night? Did the purchasing of that engagement ring turn out to be a mistake? Whatever your fiscal ailment might be, have no fear, for the Golden Trust is here! We will aid you through the tough times and stand by you in the good.
    —  A Golden Trust advertisement often seen on notice boards.

 
Agenda
The Golden Trust is an organization focused on the handling of money and valuables, where anyone can go for financial help. They fill many purposes for the people of Amenor, being a pawn shop, a bank, a safekeeping place and more. They aim to provide financial aid for people, to let them not have to worry about money, whether they need loans or simply a safe place to store their wealth.   Individuals can pawn things to the Golden Trust, loaning away valuable items for a time, in return for money. They can take out a loan, being put on a payment plan but allowing them to have some money up front. The Golden Trust has services even for those who are not financially troubled, allowing wealthy individuals to pay to store part of their wealth within the large vaults of the organization. All interactions and information shared with the Golden Trust is kept entirely confidential as well, never shared with anyone else, letting none but you and them know what secrets you desire to hide in their vaults, to lessen the chance of stealing.   The Golden Trust is a well-respected organization with a good public relation. They are known to give generous sums of money for people who pawn, remarkably low interest rates on loans and much more. Few know how they manage to keep their services and rates so cheap but even fewer complain. No matter your species, appearance or fiscal opportunities, as long as you can speak the Golden Trust is always willing to believe in you and help you, however stuck you might be in your ways.   This is seen in their slogan as well, divitiae sine molestia, which is Celestial for "wealth without vexation". They aim to provide all people, us or them, with financial independence and to do so easily and plainly, without having to vex the general populace with the services they provide. As they often say: "Our goal is simple. We simply wish to give and help, make people get a second chance, no silver lie only Golden Trust!"  
Membership & Culture
Membership in the Golden Trust amounts to working for the organization. The Golden Trust is a world-spanning organization with many different locations and positions to cover so being a member entails different things depending on your title.   There are tellers, bankers, security, janitor, management and other positions that keep the Golden Trust up and running. Becoming a member of the Golden Trust is rather standard, following a process of interviews where relevant experience is desired. No matter the position however, it is critical that members of the Golden Trust believe in second chances and are good-hearted by nature, true altruists. This is a crucial step for any member and great lengths are taken to see through any faking applicants.   These qualities are what best describe the culture of the Golden Trust. Whether you find yourself in one of its gleaming halls to take out a loan or by a simple stand in the local market to pawn something, the air of acceptance emanates from the Golden Trust. Members of the Golden Trust are friendly, happy to see you and will always help.  
History
The Golden Trust has a remarkably quiet history. In their entire existence they have had no qualms with other organizations or ruling powers. People have generally always been happy to see the Golden Trust expand to new settlements as they have slowly spread their services across all of Amenor.   The organization was established back in the Era of Growth and has since then simply grown from a single building to become a world-spanning organization, providing all of Amenor with monetary assistance.
"Divitiae sine molestia"
Crest and slogan of the Golden Trust.
Founding Date
25th of Elfyrend, 933 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Financial, Bank
Alternative Names
The Golden Goose
Location
Goldwatch
Related Historical Events

    1435 ADD
    Establishing of The Golden Trust

title: The Greengrove
content:
The Greengrove
A document detailing the nature-protecting organization of the Greengrove.

    Amenor is a place of great natural beauty, where many wondrous sights can be beholden. Yet, like most beautiful things, Amenor's nature can be fragile. It is up to us to ensure that the natural beauty and order is preserved against all threats that would seek to jeopardize it.
    — A common quote amongst the members of the Greengrove.

 
Agenda
The Greengrove is an order of druids that seeks to preserve all of Amenor's nature. They are people who find that the greatest beauty and wonder that Amenor has to offer can be found in its forests, deserts, mountains and other landscapes. They then do their utmost to ensure that these parts of Amenor are not harmed.   The Greengrove is an altruistic organization, one that works without funding or support from others. Though there are some sponsorships from wealthy lords and ladies, the Greengrove is mostly an autonomous order that does not concern themselves that greatly with their public image. The work of the organization is wide and varied, performing tasks like removing non-native species, caring for flora and fauna and defeating predators that grow too dangerous for the regions they roam. Particuarly, they do quite a bit of work in hunting dragons whose hoards have grown too large.   Though the Greengrove's work only concerns themselves with Amenor's nature, they have no opposition against manmade settlements. They are not ignorant or stubborn enough to stand against such developments, though most members would agree that the less settlements there are, the better. Where the Greengrove do make a stand though is against wars and extensive exploitation of natural resources. They realize that wars always leave no room for concern about the fields upon which they are fought. Wars also require many more resources than in times of peace, making it a two-fold transgression against Amenor's nature.  
Membership
Anyone who wishes can be a member of the Greengrove, though a member has certain expectations upon them. To join the order because you care greatly for your garden at home is not quite enough cause, nor does it fulfil the expectations upon a member of the order.   Being a member of the order entails taking care of the wildlife and nature in a region that you wish to protect. It entails keeping track of the activities in the area, and knowing about the families of beasts and animals that live there. It entails taking care of any predators that grow too powerful, or renegade hunters or others that would seek to harm the region.   Though the Greengrove is an organization that spans the entire Material Plane, with thousands of members, it only has three different membership, which are described below.  

    Fledgling - The lowest rank of the organization. It is afforded to those that recently joined the organization or those that might not have magical powers and thus preserve much smaller pieces of landscape.
    Mosswalker - The most common rank of the organization. This rank is reserved to the druids that either walk the lands of Amenor and perform their work wherever or have larger land areas in their charge. Everyone in this rank has magical abilities granted by primal powers.
    Rootweaver - The highest rank of the organization, reserved for a select few archdruids. These members run the organization, make any and all official decisions and are the ones who decided if a prospective member can join. There are eleven of these members, each one of them representing a different region of Amenor.

 
Culture
The culture of the Greengrove is easily understood, it is one that appreciates nature most of all and will strive to protect it. It is relatively rare for members to gather in any larger groups as most druids keep to their own part of the landscape that they protect.   This somewhat solitary nature of members means that the cohesion in the organization is not particularly strong. Naturally, the views of most members are aligned and they get along well whenever they do meet but since such meeting are rare, it is upon each member of the organization to further its goals and views.   Among the members, faith varies a lot. Some simply believe in the power of the land, some only wish to protect creatures who cannot protect themselves and other are religious. As such, the amount of people in the organization that follow deities only around half but almost all of them follow Cáhlid.   This solitary but appreciative nature of the organization is well captured by their slogan: Natura est optima comes, which is Celestial for "Nature is the greatest companion". This slogan well describes how members of the Greengrove put nature above most and spent much of their time among it.  
History
The Greengrove was founded during the Era of Settlement, only a few years into the migration from Taldarin. In these early days, the organization often served the function of scouting prospective lands before settlers ventured into unknown lands.   Over time though, as most corners of Amenor became settled, the Greengrove was slowly shaped into the role that they have today, as guardians of nature. Their staunch belief that nature is sacred and should always be protected has led to a few conforntations in the organizations history.   Notably, whenever Puzkera has forced Amenor to succumb to one of his concotions, the Greengrove have always been the main agent in fighting such disease. To this day, the greatest failure of the Greengrove is The Ravaging of The Greenreaches.   The Greengrove has also clashed with settlements and monarchies about how they are treating the land. Most notably, during The Trade War, Acadia was cutting down a lot of forest in order to make more warships. The Greengrove vehemently opposed this but Acadia disregarded their warnings and focused their efforts on the war. This led to the event now referred to as Nature's Wrath, wherein the Greengrove assualted one of Acadia's cities with the beasts displaced by the deforesting. This confrontation led to Acadia's army taking a significant hit and their warship production slowing as the Greengrove started to dictate what wood they could use, thus extending the Trade War many years.
"Natura est optima comes"
The crest and slogan of the Greengrove.
Founding Date
24th of Arbamand, 10 ADD, Era of Settlement
Type
Druidic Circle
Alternative Names
The Green Guardians
Location
Martee
Related Historical Events

    2130 ADD
    Nature's Wrath
    18 ADD
    Establishing of the Greengrove

title: The Inventorium
content:
The Inventorium
A document detailing the inventing organization of The Inventorium.

    The progress and advancement of technology is what sets us apart from base beasts. Without the comforts and inventions of today, the ones we are so used to, we would be no more than scavenging vultures. It is the invention of such technology and the betterment of society that we strive for.
    — A common sentiment among The Inventorium.

 
Agenda
The Inventorium is an organization focused on the improvement of society through inventions and technology. They are a, mostly non-magical, collective of great minds and artificers that work together to solve problems of civilization and improve the standard of living for any and all.   The inventions of The Inventorium stretch from great, world-spanning, life-changing inventions to simple things exclusive to a single household. It can be something as simple as a pair of specialized wind chimes to something as complex as an airship.   Though there are artificers among the ranks of The Inventorium, the number of inventions that combine techonology and magic are very rare compared to the number of inventions that lack magic. This fact is one of the key differences between The Inventorium and the Gnomish Love Machines Guild, which is almost exclusively artificers.  
Membership
The other big difference between the two organizations is the membership policy. Since the GLMG only accepts gnomes and it has thus stayed a relatively small organization despite being older than The Inventorium. Meanwhile, anyone is welcome to try their hand at being a part of The Inventorium.   Any member of the organization is granted permission to welcome any other person into their fold. This is of course most likely done when a prospective member shows off an invention that they have been working on but in theory, it means that anyone can become a member of The Inventorium, even without a vetting process. However, membership is not made official until one sends a letter to the headquarters of the organization. This letter requires one to state their name and place of residence as well as whatever invention and person gave them their membership. If the letter contains this information, it is accepted and a pin with the crest is sent back to the prospective member, making them a member officially.   In order to ensure that the organization does not have too many slacking inventors, there is a pretty hefty yearly membership tax as well, making it somewhat hard for young and inexperienced inventors to become a part of The Inventorium.  
Culture
The culture of The Inventorium is one of extreme pride. Almost all inventions that are made by members bear a small sticker with their crest and the inventors name, claiming it as a piece of the important work that The Inventorium is performing. Their slogan, invenire est progredi, which is Celestial for "Invention is progress", speaks clearly to what they wish to achieve. In order to progress mortal life, build greater civilization and live better, one needs to invent.   They take great pride in their inventions and great interest in each others inventions as well. Members nerd out over the inventions about other members, asking detailed technical questions and being awed at every new piece of technology that they come across.   The loose nature of membership makes The Inventorium a rather unorganized collection of members. There are no defined ranks among the members themselves and there are few organized moments that members meet each other. It is not for members to happen upon each other in the streets and delve into lively discussion before moving on with their day and perhaps not meeting another member for many more weeks.  
History
The Inventorium is one of the few organizations in Amenor's history that has had a rather uncomplicated history. The largest conflict in its history technically predates its birth since it is what caused its birth. In the Era of Growth, a group of non-gnomish inventors were fed up with the fact that they were not able to join the Gnomish Love Machines Guild.   In response, they created their own invention-focused organization and thus The Inventorium was born. Since then, the organization has grown into a staple of Amenor and the stickers of their crest can be found on many inventions all around the world.


"Invenire est progredi"
The crest and slogan of The Inventorium.
Founding Date
24th of Chandran, 73 ADD, Era of Growth
Type
Guild, Craftsmen
Alternative Names
The Everyone Else Also Loves Machines Guild
Location
Venbaaz
Related Historical Events

    575 ADD
    Establishment of The Inventorium

title: The Lifelight
content:
The Lifelight
A document detailing the life-giving organization of The Lifelight.

    While the world grows rough, the humanoid body remains ever soft. The duty we take upon us is to mend the wounds and ailments of the physical form, so that any and all can live their full life.
    — Common saying in The Lifelight.

 
Agenda
The Lifelight is an organization dedicated to the preservation and maintenance of life. They seek to treat all the problems that humanoid bodies can incur, everything from lost limbs to deadly diseases. Though their care is primarily focused on that of humanoids, their compassion is quite great and they can often be seen treating pets and other animals that are injured.   The Lifelight has both magical and non-magical members among their ranks. Skilled physicians, surgeons and remedial alchemists practice their crafts without access to magic. Meanwhile, clerics and druids make use of their innate magic to heal injuries and treat ailments.   Though the Lifelight has their headquarters in Grayhold, they are certainly not constrained to the city. Their healers and doctors travel all through the lands of Amenor and offer their aid. A local remedial building belonging to the Lifelight can be found in most settlements in Amenor.  
Membership
To become a member of the Lifelight is rather simple. One only needs proof magical ability or physical skill and head to a local remedial building. The bookkeepers there can guide one through the proper procedures and paperwork in order to become a Lifebringer. They are always accepting new members, as there can always be more kind souls willing to lend a hand.   Membership in the Lifelight is certainly not lucrative, nor is it a glorious life. The payment a Lifebringer receives is just about enough to allow them to travel through the lands and stay at inns. This is because of the cost of services that the Lifelight offers. To cure diseases and treat wounds can unfortunately not be free but the costs are as low as they can be, allowing most to receive this aid.   Most members of the Lifelight are good at what they do, but do have their limits. It is rare to run into a Lifebringer that is able to performs services such as raising people from the dead. Even if you do, their services are expensive and require a bit of persuasion around the ethical dilemmas regarding death.  
Culture
The Lifelight value life above all else. They yearn to see everyone have equal opportunities to live their lives, to not have them hampered by any ailments. This sentiment is echoed through their members who all tend to have boundless compassion and acceptance.   Little competition exists between the members of the organization. Whether you manage to help one or ten people per week, your efforts are still worthy of respect. Their friendly nature often also leads to Lifebringers traveling together and helping each other administer aid to others.   Though the Lifelight does not consider their organiztion to be one of faith and faith is not a necessity for membership, many members are still believers. Many clerics in the service of good-aligned deites perform duties and lend aid through the organization. Most prominent of the deities prayed to among the ranks of the Lifelight is Virá.  
History
The first recorded history of the Lifelight is quite a long time before their actual founding. Just a few years before The Ravaging of The Greenreaches in the Era of Divine Division, recovered records show that a similar organization was in the first stages of being founded. Unfortunately, the Ravaging halted this founding and its inception fell into dust.   Many years later, in the Era of Peace, people started to resettle the region of The Grayhold. As the new settlers searched through the ruins of the lost cities, they found the beginning traces of an organization like this. Given the nature of The Greenreaches fall, the Lifelight was quickly founded and it has been a well-respected and appreciated organization since then.

Vivere et vivere"
The crest and slogan of The Lifelight.
Founding Date
24th of Gordrand, 459 ADD, Era of Peace
Type
Corporation, Medical
Demonym
Lifebringer
Location
Grayhold
Related Historical Events

    3275 ADD
    Establishing of The Lifelight

title: The Reliquary
content:
The Reliquary
A document detailing the magic-seeking organization of The Reliquary.

    Magical potential takes many forms, from simple tricks to world-threatening things. The greater magical creations are much like a bun in the oven. If one keeps an eye on it, it can be made to perfection. Lose sight of it however, and a great flame may break out.
    — Common saying in the Reliquary.

 
Agenda
The Reliquary is an organization dedicated to the safekeeping of the world through the control of dangerous magic. One can consider them a mix of a security organization and a museum, a group of people that collect dangerous items and secure them as well as display and inform about them.   They aim to decrease the amount of deaths in Amenor that is caused by uncontrolled magic, or dangerous magic that has fallen into the wrong hands. They, just like most adventurers, know far too well what powerful magical abilities are capable of, should they be left unchecked.   As such, the members of the Reliquary wander Amenor and the planes beyond, searching for all manner of magical artifacts. Those that are deemed dangerous enough are collected and brought to the Arcane Lock, the magical tower that is the headquarters of the Reliquary. There, the item is inspected, neutralized if needed, secured and then finally displayed within the halls of the Arcane Lock.  
Membership
Anyone is welcome to try their hand at becoming a member of the Reliquary, it is a simple manner of entering and filing an application. In order to be a member, two things are looked for in a person. Firstly, a like-minded view. A prospective member needs to share the ideas of the Reliquary, agreeing that "magic without supervision is destruction". Secondly, some manner of experience in the field of magical items is required. Whether it be through the act of studying or first-hand experience matters not, but a young farmhand will have a hard time finding a place in the organization.   Due to these two qualities, the most prominent type of member of the Reliquary are retired adventurers. People who have interacted with many magical items and seen the threat they can pose with their own eyes, whether they have been scorned by magic or realized it themselves.   Once you are a member of the Reliquary, there are four different roles that can be filled. These vary in entry requirements but are described below:  

    Keeper - Members that keep the Arcane Lock clean, cook food for other members and so on.
    Sentinel - Members that keep the Arcane Lock safe, protect members of the Reliquary along with the displayed items.
    Seeker - Members that travel Amenor and the planes, searching for magical items to bring back to the Arcane Lock.
    Sealer - Highest ranking members, with years of experience with magical items and often magical capabilites that allow them to understand most artifacts.

 
Culture
The Reliquary desire magical control over all else, which is evident by their slogan. Their slogan, magia sine imperio est destructio, is Celestial for "Magic without control is destruction". This sentiment is reflected in the members of the organization, who are adverse to seeing powerful magic not under their control.   One should not mistake the Reliquary's desire for magical control as a disdain for all magic. They have no problems with powerful spellcasters so long as they do not use their powers nefariously. In fact, most Sealers of the organization are powerful mages themselves, as magic is a great help, a necessity even, in controlling other magic.   The culture of the Reliquary is easy for any of Amenor to partake in. The Arcane Lock is open to most to come and view the magical items and artifacts that are on display there. As long as you pay the entrance fee, you can see for yourself the culture of the Reliquary and experience the difference between uncontrolled and dangerous magic, to that of well-intentioned, powerful magic.  
History
The Reliquary has a vary shaky history, and even now in the Current Era, not everyone has a positive attitude towards the organization. It was founded a very long time ago, back in 621 BA, 1st Era, by a powerful magic user known as Azan.   Back then, the organization looked nothing like it does today, but since the Reliquary was born from this organization, that time is still considered its first birth. Azan's organization was known as The Veiled Court and, through the Eras of Taldarin, it came to be more of a cult that searched for powerful magical items that would allow Azan to return to the Material Plane after having been banished to the Shadowfell by Arbaraen.   Azan's search for power led him into the path of The Marked Septed who defeated him and ended The Veiled Court there and then. The few remaining members who had not been a part of the final confrontation scattered across Taldarin, leaderless and hopeless. As The Commence eventually happened, these people made their way to Amenor and decided to turn their magical-seeking skills into something good.   That was the second birth of the organization, early in the Era of Settlement. The name of The Veiled Court remained and the order answered to no one as they took it upon themselves to regulate magic. The organization existed in this form for a long time, halfway between cult and regulating order.   Over time however, it would come to be known that the methods of regulating magic that The Veiled Court were using were not very ethical. Furthermore, their magical control did not stop with items and artifacts but also extended to people. Specifically, they considered Wild Magic Sorcerers to be a large threat to civilized life and would often hunt them down and incarcerate them. Though this happened over many years and eras, the most notable event was The Veiled Dispelling, during the Era of Growth.   The Veiled Court perform a surgical strike against a group of sorcerers that they consider to be a danger to civilized life. Most of the sorcerers are friendly members of society, well in control of their own magic. Before this, the actions of The Veiled Court were always in question but after, they were denounced by the population of Amenor. Eventually, The Veiled Court would fall to criticism and protests, disbanding less than 200 years later   For many years after this, no magic-regulating organization existed in Amenor. Eventurally however, as the world turned to the Era of Divine Division and The Divine War began, the number of dangerous artifacts that made their way onto the Material Plane grew exponentially. 195 years into the Divine War, The Reliquary was established to take the role of a much-needed magical regulator. Though their existence was much appreciated in the early days of the organization, they have begun to face more and more critique in the Era of Peace, being compared to their predecessors.


"Magia sine imperio est destructio"
The crest and slogan of The Reliquary.
Founding Date
27th of Chandran, 197 ADD, Era of Divine Division
Type
Corporation, Security
Alternative Names
The Requirers
Location
Alitrá
Related Historical Events

    2638 ADD
    The Arcane Breach
    2139 ADD
    Establishing of The Reliquary
    1087 ADD
    The Veiled Court Disbands
    1082 ADD
    The Veiled Dispelling
    15 ADD
    Reestablishing of the Veiled Court

title: The Righteous Soul
content:
The Righteous Soul
A document detailing the versatile organization of the Righteous Soul.

    Within each and every one of us is the capability to do good. Every creature on every plane should be afforded the chance to prove what is in their heart, but be punished accordingly should they prove malicious.
    — A common saying in the Righteous Soul.

 
Agenda
The Righteous Soul is a versatile organization that performs many different tasks throughout Amenor. They are an order of righteous monks with a strong sense of justice who primarily assist with government and other officials work. They fulfill many different roles, including jury, judge, warden, rehabilitators and more.   The Rigtheous Soul often works in close proximity with government and other leadership, with most rulers having an agreement with the Righteous Soul to be able to hire their services. The monks of the organization are often used as wardens or protective escorts, guarding a vault or a particularly important shipment. Though some choose to hire mercenaries like The Emerald Blades for tasks like these, the Righteous Soul brings another level of professionalism and respect.   The monks are not only skilled fighters though, but their insight and knowledge of souls, whether it be their own or others, make them perfect arbitrators in criminal matters. They often serve as both judge and jury to decide the fate of those who have commited transgressions and sullied their heart. Finally, the Righteous Soul also serve as rehabilitators, both physical and mental. Many of the criminals they judge are sentence to rehabilitation within their grounds, as a way to try to atone for their sins.  
Membership
This rehabilitation is the primary way in which the Righteous Soul gets new members. They believe that changing a heart and a mind and shaping it from the ground is the best way to get a valuable member into the organization. With that said however, anyone is welcome to apply to the Righteous Soul.   Their diverse work makes it so that most people can find a place within the organization. The type of work that an applicant is looking for determines the type of interview they must undertake, with roles such as wardens requiring a proof of physical prowess. However, there are civilians and commoners with no particular skills to speak of who enjoy being part of a jury and they too are welcome in the organization. This makes the Righteous Soul one of the only organizations in Amenor who have unremarkable commoners in their employ.   The diverse work of the Righteous Soul makes it so that there are multiple ranks within the organization. These are detailed in order below. Any subsequent rank can serve as any rank prior to their own, but you can serve "up" so to say.  

    Rehabilitator - The rank of the organization which indicates that this member can serve as a rehabilitator. Unlike the other ranks, this one requires professional experience and an education in physical or mental therapy and is not immediately available to any higher ranking member.
    Juror - The rank of the organization which indicates that this member can serve as a member of a jury. The most common rank of the organization, which also includes many commoners.
    Judge - The rank of the organization which indicates that this member can serve as a judge in a court. A rank awarded to most Jurors after a few years of experience.
    Warden - The rank of the organization which indicates that this member can serve as a warden. Having proven their sense of justice as both Juror and Judge, they can be allowed to fight for the organization.
    Soulheart - The final rank of the organization, indicating leadership. There are only four of these members in the organization, one leader for each rank and each of these members have an extensive amount of years within the organization.

 
Culture
The Righteous Soul value truth, respect and redemption very highly. They seek to know every part of a matter before they dole out judgement upon criminals. Furthermore, if an employer, whether private or official, hires them under false pretences or withholds information about the work, they consider it a grave offence and will often refuse to do further work with them.   Members of the organization are taught to practice respect to its utmost. Only when the person before you has clearly shown that they have no intention of respecting you are you allowed to answer in kind. Until then, you practice respect, simple as that. The Righteous Soul also believes that there is good within every heart and mind. They are often lenient in their judging, should it be the criminals first offense. Though they clearly believe in redemption and second chances, they also believe in taking accountability for ones actions are not afraid to dole out harsh punishments if one does not learn from their mistakes.   The slogan of the Righteous Soul, Bonum in omni corde est, is Celestial for "There is good in every heart". This clearly symbolizes how the Righteous Soul believes that anyone and everyone can prove themselves to be good, which is a core value of the organization.  
History
The Righteous Soul were settled in the Era of Divine Division. During this chaotic time in Amenor, many individuals were coaxed by scheming deities to perform horrendous crimes and it was then that people began to realize that there were not quite enough judges and jurors to give each and every one of these criminals the proper chance to explain themselves.   That is what formed the Righteous Soul, as a way for those who may have been swayed by powers beyond their control to explain themselves and get a second chance in life. Since the organizations inception, it has grown to take on other roles, such as as wardens and rehabilitators but beyond that, the organization has had a rather quiet history.   That is, with one exception. The Righteous Soul has always worked alongside governments and officials to help them properly dole out justice to individuals within their regions. They have never been a part of a regions official business though and their warden services are not available for hire during times of war. As a matter of fact, the Righteous Soul takes a very distinct step away from wars, refusing to serve as arbitrators or wardens for any part involved.   This has always been known about the Righteous Soul and they have been respected for their abstinence and neutrality. Recently however, this streak would break. When Ironbellow erupted and The Ruiner's War began, the Righteous Soul officially denounced Hayle D'Varzan and Acadia. They sent many members to The Ashlands to serve as protectors and fighters, defending the now ruined lands that Acadia seeks to invade. It is the first time the Righteous Soul has ever taken a side in a war and it has basically entailed them cutting ties with Acadia, their previous biggest employer.
"Bonum in omni corde est"
The crest and slogan of the Righteous Soul.
Founding Date
27th of Chandran, 55 ADD, Era of Divine Division
Type
Civil Services
Alternative Names
The Soul Searchers
Location
Vyr
Related Historical Events

    3322 ADD
    Righteous Denunciation
    1997 ADD
    Establishing of the Righteous Soul

title: The Veilguard
content:
The Veilguard
A document detailing the extraplanar organization of the Veilguard.

    The planes beyond our own are realms poorly understood. To explore them, delve into the depths of their secrets and magic is dangerous work. Whether the realm is good or evil, lawful or chaotic, it always has its own agenda. Only one who can freely traverse the planes of existence like commoners do the streets of their home can consider themselves safe against extraplanar powers.
    — A common sentiment among the members of the Veilguard.

 
Agenda
The Veilguard is an organization that is entirely focused on the exploration and studies of extraplanar forces and other dimensions. They are based in the Material Plane, with most of their research performed in Amenor as well, though most of their findings are kept within pocket planes of their own creation.   The Veilguard aims to better understand the other realms that exist in the cosmology of Amenor. Be it a chaotic or lawful realm, a neutral or evil realm, the Veilguard seeks to study it in great detail. Some of their work is creating and compiling written works on the planes of existence. Beyond understanding just the planes themselves, the Veilguard actively seeks out and records crossings into other planes, keeping an eye on these planar portals.   The bulk of their work however, is the travel to and exploration of other planes of existence as well as the guarding of otherworldly threats invading our own. The members of the Veilguard use their powerful magic to cross into other planes of existence in order to map these out. They also establish barriers and protective magic in order to bar fiends, celestials and the like from crossing into the Material Plane.  
Membership
Membership in the Veilguard is extremely exclusive and there are very few members. This is easily explained by the fact that the magic required for this sort of organization is incredibly powerful and difficult to learn. Beyond the few administrative and scholarly positions that exist, there remains only positions filled with people versed in plane-crossing magic.   Anyone with relevant experience is welcome to apply to the simpler positions, such as janitor or researcher. The researchers are people who often are obsessed with the other planes of existence and spend their days writing, discussing and reading about the different planes.   The most important members of the organization though, the ones that keep it running, are the Planewalkers. These are the people who are versed in spells such as Gate or Plane Shift, and can use them to enter and leave other planes at their will. These Planeswalkers explore and document all that they can on the different planes that they visit.   The Veilguard is a well-known organization in Amenor due to their important and impressive work. Despite this, the danger of the work and the skill required keeps the number of members extremely low. Counting all members, including the researchers and administrative staff, the number of members is not even triple-digit.  
Culture
The culture in the Veilguard is one of extreme professionalism. Their work is equal parts dangerous and important and letting down your guard for even a second while exploring another plane could mean the death of you. This constant vigil that the Planeswalkers have to keep while doing their job is kept while on the Material Plane, giving off a sense of professionalism.   Beyond vigilance, the members of the Veilguard values teamwork greatly. A Planeswalker never enters another plane of existence alone, for multiple reasons. Firstly, it happens more than never that a plane of existence is so hostile that it rips you apart seconds within entering it and in that case another person is needed to immedately return. Secondly, having someone watch your back is infinitely valuable in these dangerous places.   The slogan of the Veilguard is ultra intellegendum est, which is Celestial for "the beyond must be understood". It defines clearly the values of the Veilguard and how they aim to understand the planes beyond our own, not only in order to further knowledge but also to better defend ourselves against extraplanar entities.  
History
The Veilguard is a rather young organization, founded after the Era of Divine Division, in the Era of Peace. After the events of the Era of Divine Division, many agreed that not knowing what goes on in other dimensions can clearly prove dangerous. It is this sentiment that led to the creation of the Veilguard, in the hopes of protection the Material Plane from any possible future transgressions.   Since their inception, the Veilguard has change very little. When one has access to the powerful magic needed in order to perform the duties of a Planeswalker, natural death is a thing that only occurs if one desires it. This means that many of the members who originally founded the organization are still active in it, despite it being almost 1300 years ago.   The small size and incredibly specialized nature of the Veilguard has meant that they have had a very quiet history. No other organization does what they do or have any reason to compete with them. As such, the Veilguard has slowly been carrying on their work for over a millenia now, without any major historical incidents.
"Ultra intellegendum est"
The crest and slogan of the Veilguard.
Founding Date
9th of Drakwhite, 316 ADD, Era of Peace
Type
Guild, Mages
Alternative Names
The Extraplanar Explorers
Location
Sunhaven
Related Historical Events

    3132 ADD
    Establishing of the Veilguard

title: Hayle D'Varzan
content:
Hayle D'Varzan
A document detailing Hayle D'Varzan, King of Acadia.

    Hayle D'Varzan is a person almost everyone in Amenor knows. He used to be the right hand man and spymaster of King Erridan, the previous king of Acadia. Hayle was the one to avenge Erridan's death, and subsequently took up the rule afterwards. He has ruled Acadia and steered it through The Ruiner's War for three years now.

 
Description
Hayle D'Varzan is a human man in his early fifties. Though he is becoming elderly, his body does not betray this at all. He still maintains an impressive physique and a young face. It is no more than the white hair that has started to sneak its way into the black that gives away his years. His salt and pepper hair is tied into a small ponytail his scarred face sports a beard of the same color.   Hayle is a tall man, standing just shy of two meters. He has a lean and dextrous physique and though he is a muscular man, he is no mountain. Despite this, he is still an imposing figure. He carries himself not only with the pride of all his people, but also with an air of confidence that almost makes it seem like he knows more about you than you do yourself.   Though he is the current king in the monarchy of Acadia, he never wears a crown. He dresses in noble garbs of red and black, decorated with gold but the armor is always worn underneath. His face is sharp and his jawline always set in determination. A simple earring pierces his right ear, a reminder of times past.  
Personality
Hayle is a determined, skilled and tough person. When there is something he sets his mind on, he will do his utmost in order to achieve it. Come wind and rain, storm and lightning, Hayle will persevere and push through it all in order to achieve his desired outcome.   Paranoi and caution keep Hayle away from the general populace of Acadia. Even though Hayle mostly interacts with his advisors in these times of war, his love for his region and its people is well known. He fights for the future of Acadia and its people, that a bright future may still be claimed.  
History
Hayle was born on the 11th of Arbamand, 453 ADD, Era of Peace. He was born as the first son and fourth child into the D'Varzan family, a wealthy noble family. The D'Varzan had always been close friends with King Erridan's family and bloodline and Hayle's three elder sisters spent most of their lives in court.   Hayle did the same, though almost exclusively near the walls of the huge ballrooms his sisters danced in. From an early age, Hayle was raised as a soldier and palace guard. His skill with the blade was quickly apparent and his lust to prove himself made him temper not just his body but also his mind.   In his teenage years, Hayle started to supplement his fighting with his self-taught magic, making him even more deadly in a fight. His ability to learn and consume knowledge did not go unnoticed and he began to be taught the ways of Spymaster. He spent his days learning, training and looking out for his sisters and it was not long before he found himself at the side of King Erridan. Over the years, his apparent skill made him more and more valuable to the king and the honor he brought the D'Varzan family name was apparent to all in Acadia's upper circle.   By the time of Ironbellow's Fate, he had been serving as Spymaster for a few years already. It was during this meeting that his life would take a most unexpected turn and, though he walked into the meeting as Spymaster, he walked out as a King.

    "The Ashlanders have sealed their fate. Awaken the beast. May Obiterra guide you."
    — The eternal words of Hayle after the death of his King.

Age
51
Date of Birth
11th of Arbamand, 453 ADD, Era of Peace
Presentation
Masculine
Height
193 cm
Weight
72 kg
Related Historical Events

    3322 ADD
    Righteous Denunciation
    3320 ADD
    Ironbellow's Fate

title: Tara
content:
Tara
A document detailing Tara, the first sentient Admyl.

    Tara, Eldest Admyl was the first Admyl to gain sentience. Intially created by the curiosity of Barry Longfeet, it has since then borne the species of sentient of Admyl that exist on Amenor today. It is a very old and wise construct, with a great deal of compassion but little patience.

 
Description
As one of the oldest Admyl in all of Amenor, Tara is very recognizable even though its stature is common. Its metallic body stands almost two meters tall and the grey metal is covered in wrapped bandages and beautiful silver details that come from the absorption of fine armor.   Its eyes are a light blue and the face has a feminine shape with a kind look. The rest of the body is androgynous and there are no discernible features that distinguish between sex. Most often, Tara is dressed in a simple white robe with a hood. The only thing that indicates her to be the leader of Admyls is the small blue gem that is embedded into the hood.  
Personality
Tara is a confident, wise, calm and impatient person. It has been alive for a very long time and does not suffer exhuastion in the same way that most mortals do. As such, it has collected a great deal of information over the course of its life, rendering it very knowledgable.   Beyond just gathering knowledge, the fact that it is so old makes it rich with experiences and wise from all that it has encountered. Tara's calm demeanour is one of its most noticeable traits. Having seen so much, very little phases it. With that said however, it has little patience for narrow-minded people, especially those that take an issue with the Admyl soul matter.   Tara is a Neutral Good person, one that endeavors to protect its people and strives for good relations with all others. It has a friendly attitude and desires nothing but lasting peace between its people and other species, but it is quick to anger if someone cannot consider the plight of others.  
History
Tara was first born on the 14th of Arbamand, 468 ADD, Era of Longevity. It was initially created by Barry Longfeet's curiosity. He had made many Admyl, but none of them were sentient. Combining all schools of magic at the same time into the robotic frame is what birthed Tara.   Since then, Tara has wandered the world to understand its new form of life. It extended beneficence to most other Admyl that existed at that time, granting many of them sentience as well. Together with those that chose to follow it, the first 73 sentient Admyl built a home for themselves in Amenor.   After that, Tara has served as Eldest Admyl, a title that makes it lone sovereign over all Admyl that live in their city. With its wisdom and experience, it protects and furthers the interest of all Admyls across Amenor.
Tara, Eldest Admyl.  

    This world is for all of us to partake in. No one species has a special right to any of Amenor's bounties and all shall be treated with equal kindness, soul or not.
    — Tara, Eldest Admyl.

Alignment
Neutral Good
Age
3955
Date of Birth
14th of Arbamand, 468 ADD, Era of Longevity
Presentation
Feminine
Height
1.91 m
Weight
121 kg
Related Historical Events

    501 ADD
    Settling of Taran
    482 ADD
    Admyls Become Sentient
    469 ADD
    Tara's Enlightenment
    468 ADD
    The First Sentient Admyl

title: Xelley Lightforged
content:
Xelley Lightforged
A document detailing Xelley Lightforged, freedom fighter.

    Xelley Lightforged was a well-known figure in the history of Neolora. It was he who first attempted to claim independence for Xelley Island, during The Mirrored War. Though his attempt failed, he remains an important figure for many of the people living on the island that now is named after him.

 
Description
Xelley Lightforged was an aasimar man in the prime of his life when he was active. Standing almost two meters tall, he was an imposing figure when he was clad in silver and golden plate that he often wore. Though he was tall, he was not a man of brute strength but rather slender dexterity and speed.   His skin was fair and his face stern. With chiseled cheekbones, slim dark eyebrows and a thin, pursed mouth, he wore his slender appearance quite visibly. His long and curly hair was as white as the light that reflected off his armor and weapons. He was rarely seen not adorned in armor and wielding a sword and shield.  
Personality
At the very center of Xelley's personality lay the wandering soul of a perfectionist. He was always displeased with stagnancy and refused to let matters rest or things be until they were perfected. He always sought to improve matters, for any and all, whether it was by hand, word or sword.   Though he had little problem speaking and persuading others on his point, he often relied on the cold steel of his swords tip, believing that it solved matters quicker and easier than drawn-out discussions. He would never shy away from a confrontation and that is likely what led to him earning his name. Very few today remember his family name, instead only remembering the one that was given to him by those he fought for: Lightforged.   Xelley was a Chaotic Good freedom fighter who did not concern himself that much with greater moral scruples. He did what he considered to be right, fighting for those who he believed to be at the bitter end of injustice.  
History
Xelley was born on the 27th of Arbamand, 20 ADD, Era of Divine Division. His early life is not particularly interesting, raised in a middle income family and eventually taking up work as a mercenary. He traveled eastern Amenor for many years before returning back home around 150 ADD.   In this time, the rift between Xelley Island and mainland Amenor grew larger by the year. The overwhelming sentiment on the island was a plea for independence, and idea that Xelley himself shared. Throughout the years of their existence, the settlements on Xelley Island have continually sought for independence through political means but they have always been denied.   When The Mirrored War began, the political efforts to split off from the mainland came to a grinding halt. This was the final straw for most people, including Xelley, who took up his sword and declared the island to be independent, much to the disdain of mainland Neolora. They combated their islands declaration with what forces they could spare from their own internal crisis, creating three different sides in this conflict.   First there were the believers who were based in The Faithful Forest. These were the people who, despite The Divine War, believed that the deities of the Amenorean pantheon are the guiding forces of this universe and that they still deserve prayer and reverence from all mortals.   Then there were those that did not believe in the divine. They believed that if the gods could fight just like mortals do, they are no better than them and do not deserve such faithful belief. They opposed the believers in The Faithful Forest and instead staged their war from the Heathen Hills.   Finally there were the people of Xelley Island. Most of them had no stake in this religious war that was being waged on the mainland and only saw it as yet another reason to seek independence from that place. The island rallied behind the word and sword of Xelley as he declared the island free from the mainland. Though both the people of The Faithful Forest and Heathen Hills refused to stop their warring, both of them despised this declaration.   Over the remaining years of The Mirrored War, both groups skirmished with the island, attempting to bring it back under control. It was during these endless skirmishes that Xelley earned his name as a freedom fighter and protector, forged in light to defend what is right. This time was known as the The Tectonic Skirmishes and ended after peace was reached on mainland Neolora such that both factions could assualt the island together. They execute Xelley Lightforged but cannot quench all ideas of independence as indicated by the name of the island and its largest city.

    In the shadow of the oppressor's chains, even the brightest stars shine dull!
    —  Xelley Lightforged.

Alignment
Chaotic Good
Age
145
Date of Birth
27th of Arbamand, 20 ADD, Era of Divine Division
Date of Death
27th of Arbamand, 165 ADD, Era of Divine Division
Presentation
Masculine
Height
198 cm
Weight
91 kg
Related Historical Events

    2101 ADD
    The Tectonic Skirmishes

title: Alaric
content:
Alaric
A document detailing Alaric, God of Dreams.

    Alaric is the God of Dreams. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, not created by a merging of the Creator Deities essence, but instead a god of his own making. He is often referred to as The Eternal Nightmare by his followers for his connection to dreams. He is a being with absolute control over dreams and nightmares.

 
Description
Alaric's divine form is easily recognizable, not only in appearance but also through context. Being the God of Dreams, he almost entirely exists in dreams and thus, whenever he appears, the environment around him is often quite different from the waking world.   Beyond that, his pink and purple color scheme is so strong that the sky takes on a similar hue wherever he is. His divine form is that of a humanoid shape, seemingly clad in purple and pink robes. His form is however so etheral that it is difficult to tell whether or not he actually wears clothing or it is simply drifting energy that surrounds him.   His arms do not end in normal hands, but instead in three long, claw-like extensions. From his sides, two more of these arms extend even further and as he moves, these grasp immaterial threads and pull him forward. His face is completely obscured by shadow, nothing but a dark void.  
Personality
Alaric is a power-hungry deity, one that vies for absolute control and power. His apotheosis being brought not from the Nine, but from his own making is a testament to his patience, power and skill. Despite this success, Alaric is never satisfied with the power he has. According to him, one can always strive for more.   While Alairc's hunger for power is unquenchable, it can nearly be matched by his enjoyment of abusing his power. Though Alaric is a Lesser Deity, his power over and in dreams is comparable to that of a Prime Deity or even greater. As such, he rules the Plane of Dreams with an iron fist and makes sure that he is opposed by none.   Alaric is a Chaotic Evil deity, one that does whatever he desires at any point. While he always strives for more power, he may spontaeously decide that some of his followers have been around long enough that they have garnered too much favor. In response, he suddenly and swiftly executes them. In essence, Alaric's ultimate goal is easily predictable but his methods are much less so.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Alaric are scattered across Amenor. Some are drawn to worship him for their fascination with dreams. People like scholars, wizards and philosophers who wish to understand the power of dreams often worship in Alaric. There are other however, tyrannical bullies and people who see dreams as a way to exert control over others, just like Alaric does.
