History
Alaric is an ancient creature, having existed since the First Era of Taldarin even. Back then, he was mortal yet equally power-hungry. After a failed grasp at power, he faded to obscurity for many years, not dead but unable to take physical form. Eventually however, this would change.   A member of the Marked Septet, slayers of Darkheart, would prove to bring this change for Alaric. Voniss Von Vakri and his kalashtar family had a distant relation to Alaric and this proved enough for Alaric to contact Voniss. Alaric and Voniss pacted, Alaric promising the return of Voniss' brothers life.   Naturally, Alaric was simply lying. He had no intention of returning the brother and instead just used Voniss to garner enough souls that he could eventually take a material form once again. Then, in a final deception against Voniss, Alaric sent the Dreaming Dark after him while he himself escaped into the Plane of Dreams.   Alaric was thus free and with material form once more. He strode through the Plane of Dreams and slowly grew his influence and power. Over the course of Amenor's formation and its subsequent eras, Alaric managed to grow to the power-level of a Lesser Deity in the fourth era, as he became the strongest creature on the Plane of Dreams.  
Relationships
Alaric constant struggle for power and his desire to control and dominate is well known by the other deities. The good-aligned gods despise him for his tyrannical way, while the more evil deities want nothing to do with him as he would simply seek a way to supplant them. As such, Alaric remains a lone deity, something that is entirely fine by him.
Alaric's divine form.  

    Your dreams are not of your own making. I own your mind!
    — Alaric, God of Dreams.

Alaric, God of Dreams is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a crude flower, overlapped by four rings. Each of these rings has a spike piercing out from the middle and the left side of the symbol holds a crescent moon. His divine realm is the Plane of Dreams.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Evil
Related Historical Events

    668 ADD
    Alaric's Ascension

title: Brilja
content:
Brilja
A document detailing Brilja, Goddess of Constructs & Inventions.

    Brilja is the Goddess of Construct and Inventions. She is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Dwandrin's and Huwander's essences. She is often referred to as Lady Brilliant by her followers. She is a being of great creativity, applicable intelligence and she is the patron deity of Admyls. 

 
Description
Brilja's divine form is a curious sight. Most of it is a feminine humanoid body, but in place of flesh there is instead a yellow-brown metal. Her body is covered in a poofy white dress that is weighed down by metal plates, causing the fabric to outline her metallic body.   Thin and long chains are strapped over her clothing, accessorizing her outfit. Each chain is connected to a different metallic plate of her body and then strapped around each of her ten fingers. This gives her the ability to remove, switch or upgrade her body at a moments notice.   The most notable feature of Brilja's appearance is her head. Instead of a humanoid head, her metallic neck stretches into a perfectly square golden object. This metallic cube is always perfectly still, no matter how the body its sits upon moves. Many cracks run through the surface of the cube, repaired by a lowly shining golden light.  
Personality
Brilja is a brilliantly smart deity with a deep love for the act of invention. She believes that the betterment of Amenor, and the creation of the future, is one that comes by when people put their minds to making new things. The Admyls is a perfect example of this sentiment. Just as they have come to take Brilja as their patron deity, has she grown fond of them.   Brilja is a Lawful Neutral deity that abides by laws and regulations, keeping her inventions as things that lack the ability to destroy or cause harm. While she does not officially endorse stress and tight situations, she recognizes the ability that such times have to create new, brilliant inventions.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Brilja are often easy to spot. People who have a penchant for creating and innovating, and a predisposition to care a little bit too much about their mediocre inventions. While Brilja has followers of every race, the curious mind of gnomes make them overrepresented in the demographics of Brilja's followers.   Furthermore, almost all Admyls worship Brilja as their patron deity. It is thanks to their worship that Brilja is not only the goddess of inventions, but also the goddess of constructs. The love that most of the Warforged of Amenor hold for Brilja is mutual.  
History
Brilja's neutrality and predisposition towards invention tends to keep her away from most conflicts. However, it is not uncommon that those caught in conflict plead or threaten her for an invention that could help them turn the tide of their fight. Most of the time, Brilja stays neutral and disregards them but it has happened that she has agreed to help. For example, she was instrumental in defeating the Sanguine cult and Guinebrut The Exsanguination.  
Relationships
Brilja is a somewhat lonesome deity. Beyond what is mentioned above, she has had little interaction with other deities, Greater or otherwise. She is perfectly content with this however, as she can spend more time focusing on her inventions, something that interests her much more than other gods. With that said, she does have a pretty close relationship with Fórein given their mutual love for creation.
Brilja's divine form.  

    The future happens when you are busy inventing.
    — Brilja, Goddess of Constructs and Inventions.

Brilja, Goddess of Constructs and Inventions is one of the Lesser Deities. Her holy symbol is that of two gears latched together. Her divine realm is Consturos.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Parents
Dwandrin
Huwander
Related Historical Events

    1890 ADD
    The Exsanguination
    764 ADD
    Brilja Becomes the Matron Deity of Admyls

title: CáHlid
content:
Cáhlid
A document detailing Cáhlid, God of Beasts.

    Cáhlid is the God of Beasts. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Gordfein's and Huwander's essences. He is often referred to as Great Shepherd by his followers for his many friendly beasts. He is a being of incredible patience and a great embrace.

 
Description
As the God of Beasts, Cáhlid does perhaps not have the divine form that one would expect. Instead of it being the shape of some impressive beast, he most often takes the form of a thick oak tree. With many large but short branches and a very robust stump, this oak tree is wide but not particularly tall.   Instead, the low and dense canopy creates a natural umbrella under which small and large beasts alike can gather during bad weather. Whenever a beast does approach, a humanoid face forms out of the bark, the skin being the same brown color as the bark.   This humanoid face takes the shape of an old human mean. His hair is gnarly and only part hair, with the other parts being leaf and bark. His long and bushy beard is a dark green moss that covers his throat, making it hard to see where the body once again is consumed by the bark.  
Personality
As the God of Beasts, Cáhlid is a mild-mannered, patient and warm deity. His mere presence has a calming affect on nearby beasts, small and large alike. Predatores and herbivores sit side by side under his thick canopy as he fixes the broken wing of a bird.   Cáhlid loves the beasts that he cares for and spends time with and in his presence they are calm and well-mannered company. In Cáhlid's experience, mortals are not the same. They rush about, scream and shout, kill and come into unresolvable conflict. As such, he dislikes mortals and their chaos.   Cáhlid is a Neutral Good deity, one that does all he can to provide safety and help to the denizens of the forest. Though he dislikes mortals, he holds no grudge against them, he simply does not concerne himself with them. His only focus are those who have a much harder time taking care of themselves.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Cáhlid are many throughout Amenor. Though they appear slightly different throughout the world, given the different climates, they are similar in most ways. Those who follow Cáhlid are mil-mannered, friendly people who care a great deal about the nature surrounding the cities, much more than the life that goes on in the cities themselves.  
History
Cáhlid protection and love for the beasts of nature keeps him away from most major events of Amenor. When someone does disrupt the natural balance, or destroys the homes of the beasts he loves so dearly, he does not retaliate. He grieves what has been lost but does not seek revenge. It would take a lot to uproot Cáhlid and send him on the path of vengeance. With that said though, his calming presence has helped keep mortals safe from rampaging beasts many times such as in the fourth Era.  
Relationships
Cáhlid treasures his friendships with his beasts greatly and finds little room for relationships with anyone else. With that said though, he always enjoys whenever Wayleen pays him a visit, as he considers him a good friend.
Cáhlid's divine form.  

    Every little critter is a life that needs to be protected, so that it may blossom just like the flowers that surround it!
    — Cáhlid, God of Beasts.

Cáhlid, God of Beasts is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a pawprint with five claws, a large tree growing in the pad of the paw. His divine realm is the Beastlands.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Neutral Good
Parents
Gordfein
Huwander

title: Dilora
content:
Dilora
A document detailing Dilora, Goddess of Food.

    Dilora is the Goddess of Food. She is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Heyla's and Dwandrin's essences. She is often referred to as Hearthmother for her connection to cooked meals. She is a being of incredible culinary skill and an endless appetite.

 
Description
Dilora's divine form is quite easy to recognize, due in large part to it's size. Her divine form is that of a halfling woman standing at just over a meter tall. However, the amount of baskets, bags, backpacks and pouches that she wears, she reaches up to a normal humanoid height.   These many containers make her incredible wide however. Each of them are filled to the brim with different kinds of foods; fruits, vegetables, meats, baked goods and much more. The mere presence of Dilora makes these creations stay fresh and always have the right temperature.   Beyond the bags that widen her stature, Dilora herself is no small halfling. Her face is round and chubby, her dimples always visible from her smiling expression. Her body is rotund and covered not only with a simple beige and green dress, but an apron bearing her own holy symbol as well.  
Personality
Dilora is a happy and joyous deity. Her love for food is two-fold, both the creation and consumption of it is something that brings her great happiness. According to her though, food is nowhere as good if it is enjoyed alone. As such, she aims to share as much of her happiness and food as she can.   Despite her culinary skill and warm heart, Dilora can often be consumed in her own creations. Whenever she learns of a new recipe or makes one herself, she often spends many months perfecting it. As she says, every meal deserves your undivided attention, whether you are making it or eating it.   Dilora is a Chaotic Good deity, one that strives to share as much of her creations and happiness as possible. What she will make, and whether she finds herself in a hungry mood and thus eats it herself, is often hard to predict. In the end however, her food is always a welcome surprise.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Dilora are many across Amenor. There are those who simply have a love for food and eating, while there are those who enjoy making the food itself. Innkeepers and farmers are the most of the types of people that worship Dilora.  
History
Dilora has had little impact on the history of Amenor. She has not really been involved in any major events throughout its history. However, she can often be found giving out food to the starving survivors of a disaster, even though she distances herself from the conflict itself.  
Relationships
Even though none of the deities in Amenor's pantheon need to eat, Dilora's food still makes many friends. She finds most of the evil deities unsettling and she keeps her distance from them. She does have a strong friendship with the other deities though, and they all appreciate her in turn.   Most notably of her relationships is the one she has with Xaner. His self-destructive tendencies and legendary benders are often supplemented by some food by Dilora, something that he really appreciates. Together, the two of them have something akin to a mother-son relationship.
Dilora's divine form.  

    A hearthy, home-cooked meal heightens hope and helps heroes!
    — Dilora, Goddess of Food.

Dilora, Goddess of Food is one of the Lesser Deities. Her holy symbol is that of a bowl of symmetrically placed fruit. Her divine realm is Eandalands.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Parents
Heyla
Dwandrin

title: Elyzari
content:
Elyzari
A document detailing Elyzari, Goddess of Stories.

    Elyzari is the Goddess of Stories. She is one of the Prime Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Chandryl's and Huwander's essences. She is often referred to as Taleweaver by her followers for her crafting of stories. She is a being with an unending appetite for stories.

 
Description
Elyzari's divine form is simple yet quite recognizable. It is that of young human woman in her twenties. Her skin is fair and her long, braided hair is a golden blonde. It is complemented by the gold-accented robes that she wears, along with the golden tiara upon her head.   What makes her divine form so recognizable are the small golden lights that always hover around her. Like a group of fireflies that slowly fly around her form wherever she goes. Though they may appear as nothing more than lights to others, they are much more to Elyzari herself. Each of these little lights are actually books. Every one of them contains stories that Elyzari has documented and then kept with her.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Stories, Elyzari has an unquenchable appetite for stories and tales. Whether they be sorrowful, prideful, joyous, world-spanning or village-spanning, Elyzari loves them all. She spends her time collecting as many stories as she possibly can, experiencing them all with fascination.   Over the course of her existence, Elyzari has come to learn that the stories of gods are predictable and she thus finds them boring. In opposition to that, the stories of mortals are far more interesting to her. Fascinated with their bonds and decisions, she absorbs as many mortals stories as she can.   Elyzari is a Chaotic Good deity, one that does always seeks to make the story as interesting as possible. She often finds herself subtly nudging the protagonist of whatever story she is experiencing, just to make it more interesting. Though she believes there should be danger and strife in a story, ultimately it should end with a happy ending.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Elyzari tend to find each other and form small groups. They are people who enjoy reading or writing books, discussing rumours and reviewing what they have read. There are naturally some, like story-telling bards, who find themselves outside of these groups but most tend to agree that stories should be shared.  
History
A long time ago, Elyzari found herself fascinated with the major events of Amenor. The conflicts of the gods were still new and she took a step back, observing the unfolding story with great fascination. Over time though, she grew bored of the continual plans for domination and she has had a part in the most recent conflicts, often being quite good at predicting the moves of her enemies.  
Relationships
Elyzari has good relations with most of the good-aligned deities of Amenor while she dislikes the ones who have more evil schemes. In particular, she has a strong bond with Varileth due to their shared experiences of mortal life. She does take some care in her relationship with him however, as his domain can quite easily spoil the stories she follows so closely.
Elyzari's divine form.  

    Every life is a unique thread, and every thread tells an unforgettable story!
    —  Elyzari, Goddess of Stories.

Elyzari, Goddess of Stories is one of the Lesser Deities. Her holy symbol is that of an open book, golden sparks rising up out of it. Her divine realm is Taletell.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Parents
Chandryl
Huwander
Related Historical Events

    2532 ADD
    Presch's Retort
    2524 ADD
    The Divine Chessmatch

title: Guinebrut
content:
Guinebrut
A document detailing Guinebrut, God of Blood & Slaughter.

    Guinebrut is the God of Blood and Slaughter. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created not by the merging of essences by the Creator Deities, but by a fanatic group of blood worshippers during The Exsanguination. He is often referred to as Butcherer by his followers. He is a being of absolute slaughter, seeking blood and savagery wherever he goes.

 
Description
Guinebrut's divine form is a horrifying sight. He appears like a gray-furred hound that quickly stalks across the ground. He moves with five limbs, four clawed hands and legs that drip with crimson blood at all times and one extra, muscular arm that protrudes from his torso and seemingly moves freely. As he runs, the arm grabs ahold of the ground and propels him even faster forwards.   Guinebrut gray fur is scabby and uneven. In many places it is cut away, revealing the pulsating flesh that beats underneath. What remains of the fur is matted and wet, soaked in blood from endless killings. The smell that Guinebrut's blood-soaked fur exhumes is nauseating. Small tufts of gray fur, like frills, extend from the back of the creature and blow in the wind as he rushes forward.   Guinebrut is covered in many ages of stale and old blood that has congealed into his fur. Beyond that, fresh blood always runs from his mouth, dripping onto the ground. As he rushes around, his long tail always drags through the spilled blood, absorbing it back into his own body.  
Personality
Guinebrut is a simple creature to understand. He was birthed in The Exsanguination, formed as a deity of extreme malice and an unsatiable bloodlust. Wherever Guinebrut goes, he slaughters. He cares little for the opponent and engages in any combat with a ferocity.   Guinebrut is a Chaotic Evil deity, one that has no regard for moral quandries or questions. He is entirely driven by his bloodlust and nothing will stop him from attempting to satisfy it. He would slaughter children and adults alike, only driven more mad by the slaying of a child due to the lesser amount of blood in the kill.  
Worshippers
The amount of worshippers that Guinebrut has now is unknown. After The Exsanguination Guinebrut fell from the title of Prime Deity to Lesser Deity due to the cult being stopped. A few remaining members of the cult scattered and believers of Guinebrut still exist, just many fewer.   A worshipper of Guinebrut is easy to recognize as they take extreme pride in their worship. No follower of Guinebrut is capable of concealing their faith and are thus not members of civilized settlements. Those that do worship Guinebrut live in concealed places, practicing their worship in bloodthirsty cults.  
History
Guinebrut was first created during The Exsangination and that was the time he had the most influence and impact upon Amenor. For a few years he was one of the most feared entities in the entire multiverse but through smart use of Admyls, Guinebrut and his cult was stopped.  
Relationships
Guinebrut is not one for allegiances, not with any other god, no matter their domain. He is a ferocious beast, a lone wolf and he has no allegiances or kinship. He only feels malice towards all the gods that can bleed and he hungers for that blood greatly.
Guinebrut's divine form.  

    I will devour your innards and bathe in your blood!
    — Guinebrut, God of Blood and Slaughter.

Guinebrut, God of Blood and Slaughter is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a sanguine drop of blood. His divine realm is Crimsonreach.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Evil
Related Historical Events

    2816 ADD
    A Final Stand
    1890 ADD
    The Exsanguination

title: Nechlar
content:
Nechlar
A document detailing Nechlar, God of Greed.

    Nechlar is the God of Greed. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Dwandrin's and Gordfein's essences. He is often referred to as The Great Hoarder for his manic collection. He is a being of endless greed, seeking to own as much as he can.

 
Description
Nechlar's divine form is a recognizable and terrible sight. Even if one was unfamilliar with the God of Greed, it would clearly be visible by his mere appearance. The amount of jewelry that he wears is not only heard from far away but it also shines like a disco ball.   Nechlar's divine form is that of a very overweight humanoid, dressed in a simple black robe and cloak. As mentioned however, this robe is covered in as many details as possible and more jewelry than a mortal's neck could handle hangs around his.   Nechlar's head is barely visible behind the masses of jewelry around his neck, the piercings on his face and the crowns and diadems that rests upon it. His hands are short and stubby, always holding two large bags that are filled to the brim with gold and gems.  
Personality
As the God of Greed, Nechlar is a simple creature to understand. He desires nothing else other than to grow his hoard, by any means possible. Though he prefers convential wealth such as gold and gems for their glittering properties, his greed does not stop at that.   Nechlar collects anything that he can get his hands on that he would consider particularly valuable. Everything from rare rocks, to special foods, to strange animals and even noteworthy mortals. Nechlar's greatest treasures however are magical items. It matters little to him how strong the magic is or what its purpose is, he desires it with a burning lust. His collection of magical item are displayed in a massive museum on his home plane, undoubtedly the largest collection of magical items anywhere in Amenor.   Nechlar is a Neutral Evil deity. He cares only for growing his hoard and extremly little for the original owners of the items he seeks. When Nechlar sets his sights on an item that he desires, nothing will stop him from getting it. He would walk over a mound of corpses of his own making, only to collect a Ring of Water Walking.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Nechlar are most often just as one-dimensional as himself. They are people who have extreme riches and want to keep them or desire even more, people who search all over Amenor to complete their collections. Beyond that, there are a few worshippers of Nechlar that are simply greedy. They may be dicks who mostly care about profits, but they are able to control their greed.  
History
Nechlar's incredible greed has been the catalyst for many conflicts, some large and some small. His desire to keep growing his hoard makes him a threat for anyone who ever comes into possession of something valuable. Though he cares little for the squabbles of other deities, he himself has been the cause of large conflicts when stealing from mortals.  
Relationships
As Nechlar desires nothing beyond growing his hoard, he holds no amicable relations with any other deities, whether they be good or evil. In fact, if given the chance, Nechlar would not hesitate to add them all to his own personal collection, something that the other deities despise.
Nechlar's divine form.  

    More... More! MORE, I NEED MORE!
    — Nechlar, God of Greed.

Nechlar, God of Greed is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of three stacks of many coins, shining their golden light. His divine realm is Avarice.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Neutral Evil
Parents
Dwandrin
Gordfein
Related Historical Events

    1944 ADD
    The Divine War

title: Obiterra
content:
Obiterra
A document detailing Obiterra, God of Disasters and Destruction.

    Obiterra is the God of Disasters and Destruction. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Elfyrean's and Gordfein's essences. He is often referred to as Doombringer by his followers for his destructive nature. He is a being of endless destruction and ruin.

 
Description
Obiterra's divine form is as easily recognizable as it is easy to spot. A five meter tall humanoid creature clad in dark armor and with large wings is what you see when you look upon him. Not even an inch of skin is exposed and perhaps that is for the best, as Obiterra's divine form is permanently on fire.   The flames wreathe his darkened armor, leaving endless scorch marks upon its jagged surface. The wings are boney, extended by walls of flame. The helmet has two curving, pointed horns and the only thing that breaks it midnight appearance are two globules of flaming light.   Despite all the flame that constantly surrounds Obiterra, he is not a bright creature. The flames are a normal orange color but they lack a lot of light. As Obiterra walks, the dancing flames cast no shadows, giving no indication of his terrible approach.  
Personality
As the God of Disasters and Destruction, Obiterra is a name that strikes fear into the most zealous and superstitious. He searches evermore for a way to make Amenor fall to a natural disaster, to wreak havoc and destruction upon all living things of this world.   Though Obiterra is wreathed in flame, he has no particular affinity to such disasters. He simply prefers the natural disaster that fares the best, whether it be volcanoes, floodings, blizzards, earthquakes or many others. Any disaster that destroys all that mortals have created is sufficient for him.   Obiterra is a Chaotic Evil deity, always seeking endless and senseless destruction. While it is easy to understand that he desires the destruction of mortal kind, it is much harder to attempt to predict how he will do it. Obiterra is not satisfied with simple targeted destruction, he wants all of Amenor to fear the powers he brings, not just one place.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Obiterra are easy to recognize once they show themselves. Cults that believe Amenor to be too populated follow Obiterra faithfully, awaiting his next disaster such that Amenor can stabilize, according to them.  
History
Throughout the history of Amenor, Obiterra has always sought ways to envelop the entire Material Plane in a world-ending disaster and he has been close a few times. He also often steps in and supports other evil deities when they have plans of domination or destruction.  
Relationships
Obiterra's sensless destruction makes him quite disliked by the good deities. For the evil, or those who see opportunity after crisis, Obiterra is a great ally. Some consider Obiterra a worthy ally while others consider him the perfect distraction.
Obiterra's divine form.  

    All shall crumble, burn and be reduced to ash!
    — Obiterra, God of Disasters and Destruction.

Obiterra, God of Disasters and Destruction is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of two massive waves, sweeping up on either side of a raging fire. His divine realm is Gehenna.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Evil
Parents
Elfyrean
Gordfein
Related Historical Events

    2816 ADD
    A Final Stand
    2273 ADD
    The Consuming Flame

title: Perreni
content:
Perreni
A document detailing Perreni, God of Perfection and Skill.

    Perreni is the God of Perfection and Skill. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Heyla's and Huwander's essences. He is often referred to as Greatmaster by his followers due to his extensive skills. He is a being whose actions are always skillfully perfected.

 
Description
Perreni's divine form is one that incredibly recognizable. It is a humanoid form clad in beautiful white robes with golden trim. The arms and legs of the robes are just short enough that Perreni's hands and feet are visible. Despite his humanoid form, Perreni has no other similarities to a humanoid.   His skin is made of glossy, dark grey metal that is constructed to liken a muscular humanoid. No mouth or nose can be seen on his face and two dark, horizontal slits are all the resembles eyes. His white robes are fastened to his head via an elaborate crown-like detail.   Perreni never walks. Wherever he goes, he sits cross-legged just a few feet above the ground and floats forward at a steady pace. While he has two normal arms, eight more protrude from his back, each of them holding objects pertaining to a different skill.  
Personality
As the God of Perfection and Skill, Perreni seeks these values in all that he encounters. He desires perfection not only in himself, but in all others that he interfaces with as well. With that being said, he is not an oppressive deity that attempts to force perfection unto others. Instead, he is much more akin to a supporting parent, one that believes in their own teaching ability.   Perreni is one of the deities of Amenor that is fascinated with mortals and mortal life. He often watches mortal life, observing their customs and traditions. Most importantly, he searches for new professions or competitions. When he finds such an activity he learns it, often in the guise of a mortal form, before he practices and perfects his skill in it.   Perreni is a Neutral Good deity. He seeks to improve the skill of mortals and himself, helping as many as he can in the process. While he is not one to break rules or disobey laws, he can't help but feel respect towards a mortal that might commit the perfect murder.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Perreni can often be described as neurotic. Not all of them are of course, but many who worship Perreni seek perfection much in the same way that he does. Without the divine skill that he has, they are often left as people who are incredibly particular about how things should be done.  
History
Perreni's immense skill makes him a formidable enemy in everything from combat to war planning. As such, he is most often involved in greater conflicts that take place in Amenor, fighting to protect mortals. He most often takes the role of a trusted advisor and/or military captain.  
Relationships
Perreni holds a strong relationship to most of Amenor's deities. While he tends to shy away from the ones who's domains are more nefarious, he still often respect how well they control their domain. With the more amicable deities, he holds a friendly relationship as he respects them greatly, considering deities to be the ultimate expression of skill in their respective domains.
Perreni's divine form.  

    Perfection should be sought in all things, whether they be actions or people.
    — Perreni, God of Perfection and Skill.

Perreni, God of Perfection and Skill is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a three-bladed flower with an extra detail between the petals. His divine realm is Arborea.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Neutral Good
Parents
Heyla
Huwander

title: Presch
content:
Presch
A document detailing Presch, God of Tyranny and Domination.

    Presch is the God of Tyranny and Domination. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Chandryl's essences. He is often referred to as Willtaker by his followers for his dominating nature. He is a being who endlessly seeks to control all.

 
Description
The divine form of Presch is as recognizable as it is horrible. It is that of a large humanoid form, standing just above two meters tall. Though it has the shape of a humanoid body, with two arms, a head and is bipedal, there is very little else that likens it to such a form.   His body is made of a collection of iron chains and tattered cloth. No skin is visible anywhere and the hood completely covers the face, leaving just a dark void where it should be. Presch's upper body is wrapped in chains and spikes, making him look like an iron cactus.   The chains that are wrapped around him hang loosely off his arms and legs. As he walks, they drag and clink behind him, marking his approach. The chains on his arms bleed into a finer and finer links until they eventually become the tattered cloth that covers his body with no sign of an edge.  
Personality
As the God of Tyranny, Presch is easy to understand. He seeks to control everyone and everything. For him it is not necessarily about power, but instead about subservitude. He wants everything that he can see to bow to him, so that he may exact his will without consequence.   Presch cares little about how his rule comes about. It can be through violence, threats or fear, it matters little to him why others would serve him. With that said though, he does have a predisposition towards using his chains to physically control people.   Presch is a Lawful Evil deity, one that has a singular goal and all his actions work towards fulfilling it. He will destroy, kill, maim and ruin everything and everyone that stands in his way. Ultimate domination and unending servitude under a cruel and cold master is what awaits those who fall to Presch.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Presch are cruel and uncaring, seeking positions of power wherever they can find it. Most worshippers of Presch tend to keep to themselves, as it never quite works out when they form groups. Cults that follow Presch often fall to infighting as every member seeks to take a leadership position and all are cruel.  
History
Presch has sought domination over mortals since his very first breath. Though his strength and iron will may be prominent, his planning and intelligence is not his strongest suit. As such, despite his many efforts, he has repeatedly failed to enact domination. Most notably there was a four year period where Elyzari kept predicting his moves, hindering him from making any progress.  
Relationships
Presch despises all good-aligned deities of Amenor for their weakness and failure to exert their power. He also tends to dislike the other deities as well, as they prove too powerful to be dominated by him. As such, he is most often a solitary deity though he sometimes allies himself with other destruction-seeking deities as a way to gain more control and influence.
Presch's divine form.  

    Your free will is undeserved! You need me to rule you, to control you!
    — Presch, God of Tyranny and Domination.

Presch, God of Tyranny and Domination is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a three-pointed crown, chains wrapped around it. His divine realm is Acheron.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Lawful Evil
Parents
Arbaraen
Chandryl
Related Historical Events

    2816 ADD
    A Final Stand
    2532 ADD
    Presch's Retort
    2524 ADD
    The Divine Chessmatch

title: Puzkera
content:
Puzkera
A document detailing Puzkera, God of Plagues, Poison and Disease.

    Puzkera is the God of Plagues, Poison and Disease. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Chandryl's and Tyrabane's essences. He is often referred to as Plaugebearer by his followers for his diseased nature. He is a being of endless rot and decay.

 
Description
Puzkera's divine form is easy to recognize and equally easy to fear. Simply laying your eyes upon it causes your stomach to churn and your throat to clam up. His divine form wears a simple gray travelers cloak over a gray set of robes. Both are equally torn and tattered and flutter after him in the wind.   The exception to the torn clothing is the hood. It is fully intact and completely obscures Puzkera's face. It is also the only part of his exterior that is not covered in some form of growth. Whether it be something simple as mushrooms or something far worse, like bubbling puss, Puzkera is almost completely covered in these various growths.   Wherever he goes, deadly mushrooms seem to flourish while other plants wither and die. He could stride across cobblestone and mushrooms would still break apart the rocks and grow out of it. Puzkera emanates a deadly stench at all times, a smell so foul that you would immediately retch upon smelling it.  
Personality
As the God of Plagues, Puzkera is simple to understand. He desires little more than one thing: to see the world contracted by a plague of his making. He wishes for all Amenor to fall into sickness and death at the hands of one of his foul creations.   Puzkera believes that the mutating powers of sickness and disease are the most pure powers in the universe. Those who fall under the effect of a plague and have their body mutate a protection or a solution, those are the only ones worthy of life in his eyes. Mutated life, that is. Along these lines, Puzkera despises life that is unaffected by disease and poisons, such as Warforged ARTICLE EHRE.   Puzkera is a Lawful Evil deity. He never stops seeking a new plague that will bring Amenor to its knees. It does not matter to him who is hurt in the process, or at the hands of such a sickness. It is only when the plauge has done its work that Amenor has reached its true potential.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Puzkera are rarely public figures. It is extremely uncommon for a worshipper of Puzkera to pronounce such a fact in public, due to his teachings being what they are. With that said however, there are enough people in Amenor that at least partially agree with Puzkera. People that belive a new plague is needed to cut down on the population. People that believe that only the strongest shall inherit Amenor, while the meek should be buried.  
History
Throughout the history of Amenor, Puzkera has unleashed many plauges upon the people. Some have not been harmful enough, others have killed their host too quick to spread but some have made their way across Amenor, causing great destruction. Most notably is a plauge unleashed during the fourth Era, when Amenor was growing boundlessly.  
Relationships
Puzkera is a solitary figure. While other malice-aligned deities can respect his craft and goal, even they do not desire to be too close to Puzkera. As he always surrounds himself with plagues and diseases, it is only sensical that no one else wants to be in his proximity.
Puzkera's divine form.  

    All shall be plauged, rot and wither!
    — Puzkera, God of Plagues, Poision and Disease.

Puzkera, God of Plagues, Poison and Disease is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a glass vial containing a bubbling green liquid, marked with a skull and crossbones. His divine realm is Canzera.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Lawful Evil
Parents
Chandryl
Tyrabane
Related Historical Events

    2348 ADD
    The Ravaging of The Greenreaches
    1634 ADD
    The All-Consuming Plauge

title: Raschal
content:
Raschal
A document detailing Raschal, God of Undeath.

    Raschal is the God of Undeath. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging Heyla's and Tyrabane's essences. He is often referred to as The Unending by his followers for his eternal false life. He is a creatures of many deaths, none of them permanent.

 
Description
Raschal's divine form is very recognizable, at least once one gets closer. From a distance one would see a humanoid shape clad in golden clothing with beautiful accents. The decorated robe gives a wholly regal appearance and the golden crown borne atop the head matches that look.   High-laced boots and heavy leather gloves cover the feet and hands while the robe obscures the rest of the body, making no skin visible. Of course, as one approaches, they would quickly make the realization that there is no flesh to be seen. Underneath the golden-lined hood hides a skeletal visage with empty eye sockets yet a full mouth of teeth.   A soft and slowly moving mist of luminescent blue continuously escapes from one of the eye sockets, like an unattended leak. The mist coalesces into ghastly faces and tortured bodies that slwoly surround Raschal. Despite his nature, no stench of decay or rot emanates from Raschal.  
Personality
As the God of Undeath, Raschal finds the time given in one life to be limiting. Whether it be ended by the natural span of a mortal life, or the blade of a rival, it is not enough time to accomplish all that one should. As such, he considers undeath the natural next step.   While others may consider undeath an affront, Raschal simply views it as another chance to continue ones work from their previous life. Of course, undeath is a fine art and it can often go wrong, leaving one but a husk of themselves. Raschal considers this the beauty of undeath, that it can be expressed very differently simply based on ones ambitions.   Raschal is a Neutral Evil deity, an entity that would see all of Amenor embrace undeath. According to him, if everyone in Amenor would become undead once their time was up, they could accomplish so much more. He wants the natural cycle of life and death broken, and he will stop at very little to see that done.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Raschal are almost exclusively those that agree with him. Wizards whose pursuit for knowledge cannot be sated in one life or vampires who have existed for millennia are among his followers. Then there are also a few, more good-hearted people that follow Raschal due to their extreme fear of death and their desire to continue living, in any sort of form.  
History
Raschal has been a constant problem for the good deities of Amenor. His unwillingness to die makes him an incredible tenacious foe and his intelligence makes him very dangerous. His raising of the dead has also caused trouble upon Amenor multiple times, most notably in the fourth Era.  
Relationships
Raschal finds the other evil-aligned deites to be a bit crude. However, their endless violence and slaughter often provides him ample new followers and bodies to experiment upon. As such, he allies himself with them and feigns friendship through disdain.   For the good deities of Amenor, Raschal is another endless problem. He refuses to die and he refuses reason, caring only for his own cause. As such, most of them despise him greatly. There are none among them that hate him more than Alora however. As she represents new beginnings, Alora sees Raschal's work as an affront to her most sacred tenets. Raschal himself enjoys her fury with glee.
Raschal's divine form.  

    The first life of a creature is always so limiting. It is not until the fourth of fifth death that one can find their true potential.
    — Raschal, God of Undeath.

Raschal, God of Undeath is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a hand holding a skull while vapor escapes from the eye sockets. His divine realm is Necropolis.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Neutral Evil
Parents
Heyla
Tyrabane
Related Historical Events

    3264 ADD
    The Gravewoods Wither
    1702 ADD
    The Construction of Necropolis

title: Ronashand
content:
Ronashand
A document detailing Ronashand, God of Theft and Shadows.

    Ronashand is the God of Theft and Shadows. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Chandryl's and Gordfein's essences. He is often referred to as Darkskulker by his followers for his concealed nature. He is a being of darkness and deceit.

 
Description
Ronashand's divine form is incredibly difficult to see, but very recognizable if one does manage to spot it. It is that of a masculine humanoid form, shaped entirely of shadow. As he moves through shadow, he completely blends into his surroundings, only becoming visible in sharp light.   When not in shadow, one can see that his form is made of the same darkness as the shadows themselves. They make up his form, slightly drifting behind him, giving him a mostly ethereal appearance. While the shadows create a humanoid form, the most distinct feature are his two eyes. Cold as winter and shining like diamonds, two white pearls give him sight and are the only thing that can reveal him in shadow.  
Personality
As the God of Theft and Shadows, Ronashand desires much but shows little. If there is something he desires from mortals, he can easily steal it for himself, like taking candy from a baby. Ronashand's hubris knows no bounds however. His many thefts against mortals has made him very bored with their ease.   As such, he has turned his attention to attempting to steal from other deities. He sees this not as an affront to them, but rather as a challenge. In some ways, Ronashand is honorable as he mostly never steals from those who are unsuspecting. If he intends to steal from someone, he will declare so to them before he attempts it. He sees it as an honorable challenge: if they can stop him, they deserve to keep their things.   Ronashand is a Neutral Evil deity, one that cares very little about the opinions or matters of others. He values his solitude extremely highly and always prefer to stay out of the affairs of other creatures. Ronashand does what he desires at all times, never swayed by the influence of others, and always up to some crime.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Ronashand are most often loners, people who value solitude and seclusion above most other things. This means that there are few congregations in Ronashand's name, which is perhaps for the best given his scrupulous teachings. Naturally, there are many thieves and rogues across Amenor that consider Ronashand to be their patron deity.  
History
Ronashand has never been seen on the forefront of conflict, though he does appreciate its existence. During major times of crisis on Amenor, gods and mortals alike are often distracted, giving Ronashand the perfect opportunity to perform a heist he has been planning.  
Relationships
As previously mentioned, Ronashand prefers solitude and thus does not have any strong relationships with other deities. He also does not pay any mind to what the other deities think of him, even though most evil deities respect his ways.   It is worth noting though, that Ronashand and Nechlar have an intense rivalry. Of all the deities, Nechlar is certainly the one that has the most appetizing valuables to steal, something that gladdens Ronashand. Nechlar of course despises Ronashand for his continued attempted thefts of his precious items.
Ronashand's divine form.  

    The real world lies in the shadows of Amenor.
    — Ronashand, God of Theft and Shadows.

Ronashand, God of Theft and Shadows is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of an outstretched hand, holding an overflowing pouch of gold, shadows swirling around it. His divine realm is Tenebraen.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Ronashand
Parents
Chandryl
Gordfein

title: Serra
content:
Serra
A document detailing Serra, God of Gifts and Music.

    Serra is the God of Gifts and Music. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Chandryl and Dwandrin essences. He is often referred to as Joybringer due to his giving nature. He is a being of endless joy, which he always shares.

 
Description
Serra's divine form is a rather simple one. A human man of average height with rather pale skin and dressed in simple white pants, he could blend into a crowd. His well-sculpted upper body is covered in much golden jewelry and a blue cloak that blows around him, whether or not the wind is moving.   His brown hair is voluminous and curly, falling just past his shoulders. He always carries with him a flute of pure gold that plays the most beautiful tunes. Even more gold adorns his bare ankles as he joyfully skips forward, playing his soft music.  
Personality
As the God of Gifts and Music, Serra is an absolutely joyous creature. He delights in making others delight, spreading his joy to all those who wish to receive it. While he enjoys gifting material possession, whether they be valuable or sentimental, his favorite way of spreading joy is through his music.   To Serra, nothing can cheer a soul up faster than a carefully chosen and softly played tune. While he considers any instrument capable of doing this, his absolute favorite is the flute. The golden flute he always carries with him plays music like none other that mortals have ever heard, in the hands of Serra.   Serra is a Chaotic Good deity, one that spreads his joy by surprises and tricks most often. None of his performances or gifts are ever straight-forward, they need to always contain some form of trickery. To Serra, the greatest amount of joy is bestowed when one cannot predict the moment it is given.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Serra are many across Amenor. There are those who simply worship Serra for his joyous nature, something that they want to partake in. There are others who worship Serra as they pursue a career in music, such as bards or minstrels. Finally, there are also good samaritans that worship Serra for his gift-giving ways.  
History
Serra has mostly stayed out of the greater events or conflicts that have befallen Amenor, preferring instead to bring joy to those who are affected by the crisis. As such, he is most often participating in some corner but never actually a part of the conflict itself.  
Relationships
His joyous and gift-giving nature means that Serra very easily makes friends. At least with the good-aligned deities, that is. They respect and appreciate him a lot. On the contrary, Serra's joy is something the more evil gods find absolutely despicable, making them stay far away from Serra.
Serra's divine form.  

    With a tune carefully sung and a gift considerately given, any frown can turn upside down!
    — Serra, God of Gifts and Music.

Serra, God of Gifts and Music is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a finely carved mahagony lute, a blue bow strapped at its neck. His divine realm is Elysium.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Parents
Chandryl
Dwandrin

title: Therion & Liora
content:
Therion & Liora
A document detailing Therion, God of Jewelry and Craftsmanship, and his mortal wife Liora.

    Therion is the God of Jewelry and Craftsmanship. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Dwandrin's essences. He is often referred to as Jewelfather for his exquisite craftsmanship. He is a being of incredible crafting skill and all his wondrous creations are gifted to his mortal wife, Liora.

 
Description
Therion's divine form is one of the few that would not be the central point of attention in a cloud. It is a simple form, that of a handsome human man in his late thirties. He is well-dressed and a bit taller than the average man as well as a bit more built.   His face is clean-shaven and his jawline is sharp while his brown and curly hair is short. He wears a number of shining rings, necklaces and ear piercing but it is nothing compared to his wife. Liora's form is that of a female elf in her mid two-hundreds, but unlike her husband, she is mortal.   The golden dress she often wears complements her olive skin well, but is often so decorated with gems and jewels that its color fades compared to these stones. Therion showers Liora with such endless and great gifts that her entire person is covered in them. Her hair is barely visible underneath all the jewelry and her hands are heavy with rings.  
Personality
As the God of Jewelry and Craftsmanship, Therion is the embodiement of crafts. Unlike Fórein whose forge makes weapons of war and larger objects, Therion is entirely focused on jewelry and smaller crafts. While he respects Fórein's skill, he considers his talent wasted on such crude designs. According to Therion, why make something that can kill when you can simply make something that will stop the hearts of those who see it?   Therion loves only one thing more than crafting and that is his wife, Liora. Their relationship formed around a hundred years ago and has since been as strong as ever. Liora's mortality may be a long-term problem, but it is not something that either of them are concerned with right now. Liora herself was swept off her feet from her normal life, into something far more magical and she has not locked back since.   Therion is a Neutral Good deity, one the dislikes strife and conflict. He would much rather have peaceful times filled with beautiful creations. With that said however, he rarely takes a strong stance on ethical matter, preferring to stay out of them and remain with his wife instead.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Therion are often very different people. There are those that pursue crafts such as painting, jewelmaking or other small crafts that worship Therion for his skill. Then there are also those that worship Therion simply because they appreciate material beauty or because they are infatuated with Liora's story. Some of these people not only pray to Therion but also to Liora, meaning that she is slowly garnering a following as well.  
History
Over the course of history, Therion has mostly been staying out of major events. He dislikes these conflicts but his domain gives him little power to affect change and so he focuses on his craft instead, putting the conflict out of his mind. The last hundred years or so since he and Liora found each other, he has had little influence on Amenor as he spends time with his wife.  
Relationships
Therion's most important relationship is rather obvious. Beyond that though, he has respect for Fórein but due to the fact that he dislikes how he uses his skill, he does not consider him a friend. On the other hand, Virá's love for beauty and Therion's love and mastery over material beauty has forged a strong friendship.
Therion's divine form with his mortal wife, Liora.  

    There is beauty in jewelry, but its true essence is only unlocked once the right person wears it.
    —  Therion, God of Jewelry and Craftsmanship.

Therion, God of Jewelry and Craftsmanship is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a beautiful golden ring, engraved with images of flowers. His divine realm is Bytopia.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Neutral Good
Parents
Arbaraen
Dwandrin
Related Historical Events

    3314 ADD
    Therion & Liora meet

title: Uynga
content:
Uynga
A document detailing Uynga, Goddess of Torture and Pain.

    Uynga is the Goddess of Torture and Pain. She is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Dwandrin's and Tyrabane's essences. She is often referred to as the Lady of Chains due to her pain-inflicting nature. She is a being who endlessly revels in dealing out pain.

 
Description
There is no mistaking Uynga's divine form, it is immediately recognizable. It is a humanoid form wrapped in enough bandages that only one foot and the head are visible. These bandages are all blood-soaked, always dripping fresh blood as Uynga floats forward.   At weird angles, various weapons are thrust into the bandages and surely into the humanoid form beneath. A sword here, a dagger there, a rapier there, all of them thrust through the bandages such that they emerge out the other side, covered in blood.   The only foot that extends out of the bandages is pale and lifeless, looking gray and decayed enough that it could fall off at any moment. Finally, her head is securely fasted by a set of four chains that wrap tightly around her neck. Though the chains have no anchor and just float in the air, they are pulled taught. A dirty and unwashed mop of blonde hair spills down the front of Uynga's face, covering everything except for the pair of shining purple eyes.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Torture and Pain, one can imagine what Uynga's favorite activity is. She can find pleasure in all forms of torture, performed on all forms of people, given that they feel enough pain. To her, it does not matter if the person is innocent or not, or if the torture has the goal of extracting information, she simply delights herself in the torture itself.   Uynga especially enjoys going through all the different torture methods and tools that she has until she can find the one that brings the most pain for her recipient. When the victim experiences maximum pain, Uynga experiences maximum pleasure.   Uynga is a Chaotic Evil deity, one that inflicts as much pain as she wants, whenever she wants. She never relents or lets one of her victims go and she never cares about the consequences of her torture. If someone has a problem with who she has tortured, she has simply found another person to torture. Afterall, if you can torture them physically and mentally at the same time, that is ideal according to Uynga.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Uynga are split into two groups, both incredibly sinister. Firstly, there is a debaucherous bunch, the type of people that inflict pain and torture as a means of ecstasy and pleasure. Then there are also psychopaths that simply enjoy inflicting pain upon others. Either way, both of these groups tend to be quite discreet and very rarely openly worship Uynga, unless they are members of a cult.  
History
Uynga is a deity that mostly keeps to herself and her own pleasures. Occasionally however, her incredible skill at extracting information is desired by others. This has happened a few times throughout history, after all, there is no greater feat than torturing a god. Most notably, Uynga tortured Elyzari at the request of Presch in order to give him an advantage in invading Amenor.  
Relationships
Uynga utterly despises the good deities of Amenor. She considers them boring and cowards, not willing to see the pleasure and fun that can be had in her favorite activites. Meanwhile, she also dislikes the deities that enjoy destruction and ruin as she considers their methods way too crude.   There is one exception however and that is Guinebrut. His endless desire for blood and her precision carving that makes so much blood is a perfect combination. They work so well together that they have been in a romantic relationship ever since Guinebrut was created. This "romance" is perhaps not what mortals would consider love, but their bond is strong nonetheless.
Uynga's divine form.  

    Let the cold embrace of my chains, my knife, my touch, be your ultimate fate!
    — Uynga, Goddess of Torture and Pain.

Uynga, Goddess of Torture and Pain is one of the Lesser Deities. Her holy symbol is that of a dagger wrapped in chains, blood dripping from its tip. Her divine realm is Carceri.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Evil
Parents
Dwandrin
Tyrabane
Related Historical Events

    2532 ADD
    Presch's Retort

title: Velissara
content:
Velissara
A document detailing Velissara, Goddess of Ecstasy and Hedonism.

    Velissara is the Goddess of Ecstasy and Hedonism. She is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Huwander's and Tyrabane's essences. She is often referred to as Midnight Mistress by her followers for her pleasurable ways. She is a being of absolute self-indulgence and bliss.

 
Description
As the Goddess of Hedonism, Velissara has two different divine forms in order to maximize ecstasy. She has one female form where she takes the shape of a succubus and one male form where she takes the shape of an incubus. Both forms are rather revealing and incredibly sexy.   Her female form is scantily clad, wearing nothing but a choker, high heels, velvet stockings, a red thong and a bra that is way too small. Her black hair is long and unkempt, flowing freely underneath her two devilish horns. Her makeup is expertly done and she is rarely ever seen without a suggestive smile on her lips.   Her male form is equally scantily clad, wearing nothing but a thin, grey kilt fastened with a golden belt. His exposed upper body is slightly sweaty at all times and he is unreasonably lean and muscular at the same time. Though her horns are different in this form, the hair is kept the same.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Ecstasy, it is rather easy to understand Velissara's intentions. She desires pleasure, ecstasy and bliss for herself and everyone that she meets. Beyond granting such pleasures to herself and others, she also really enjoys to tease and toy with those that desire it.   To Velissara, all forms of ecstasy and self-indulgence are welcome. If it is gained through fine wine, good food, nice company or more unusual things such as torture or self-harm, she is equally happy. With that said though, she prefers sex as a deliverance of bliss due to its ability to share ecstasy to so many at a time.   Velissara is a Chaotic Good entity, one that aims to spread much joy and pleasure. By no measure are her means to do so conservative or regular, but she still does bring good to those who desire it. That is, when she does not find herself in a playful mood and simply teases instead.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Velissara are a wide range of people, young and old people alike. Some people who follow Velissara keep their worship secret, ashamed of it while others are more confident and openly worship her. Most of her followers however, tend to have a very specific kink and worship her in hopes of seeing such fantasies fulfilled.  
History
As the Goddess of Hedonism, Velissara has no interest in combat or conflict. As such, she has kept a safe distance from most major events in Amenor's history, instead focusing on her own pleasure while others fight. With that said however, she does not despise war and hate as there is pleasure to be gained from that as well.  
Relationships
Velissara keeps a distance from the more evil deities as most often desire things that would not be stopped by a safe word. With that said however, most other good and neutral deities are a bit more prude than Velissara and she does not really fit in with them. There is one exception however, and together with Xaner, Velissara has a passionate relationship.
Velissara's divine forms, female and male.  

    Come a little bit closer, and I'll show you my namesake...
    — Velissara, Goddess of Ecstasy and Hedonism.

Velissara, Goddess of Ecstasy and Hedonism is one of the Lesser Deities. Her holy symbol is that of an overfull glass of wine, drops spilling out on the left side. Her divine realm is Desiren.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Parents
Huwander
Tyrabane

title: Wayleen
content:
Wayleen
A document detailing Wayleen, God of Travel.

    Wayleen is the God of Travel. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Arbaraen's and Heyla's essences. He is often referred to as Wanderlust by his followers for his traveling nature. He is a being of endless wanderlust.

 
Description
Wayleen's divine form is certainly not easy to recognize. It is simply that of a humanoid man of average height and an inoffesive look. His short, brown hair is often matted and wet and his beard is short but equally unkempt. His eyes are kind and he always speaks calmly.   Upon the fact that Wayleen's divine form has no distinguishable characteristics, it is often hard to even see his full form. He always wears a simple leather armor, strapped to which are many bandoliers, bags and pouches. Over the armor he wears a long, deep green cloak with a hood that is always pulled up over the head.   Even his divine presence, the power he exerts by simply existing, is often muted. There are few mortals who could tell that they have passed him on the road. Most would simply assumed that they just met a mild-mannered human male vagabond. In truth, they would not be entirely incorrect.  
Personality
Wayleen is a quiet and lonely creature. He cares little for the affairs of others, whether he is involved in them or not. There are few things that would pull Wayleen from his wanderings. He is of the opinion that, even in times of great distress, there are many others who are better suited to help.   Wayleen is remarkable in terms of Amenor's deities. He has his own divine realm, just like all the other gods do, but he spends no time there. Instead he spends all of his days on Amenor, simply wandering across the land and seeing all that he can. Though he has now wandered Amenor many times over, he still continues to do so.   Wayleen is a Neutral deity, one who prefers to be left out of all debate and conflict and be left to his own devices. To him, nothing is more serene than seeing the sun rise over Amenor while on a long journey. On the contrary, nothing is more obscene when one is forced to ally with some gods and fight others.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Wayleen are spread out all over Amenor. While there are those that admire Wayleen's free spirit and worship him for it, his worshippers are almost exclusively comprised of wanderers. People who want to explore every nook and cranny of Amenor, see every road and river and never be shackled to a home, those are the people that worship Wayleen.  
History
As described, Wayleen simply wants to travel through Amenor and keep to himself. He does not desire to be a part of any major event or contribute in any way. During times of crisis, other gods have petitioned him for assistance but he has declined every time, stating that it is simply not his problem.  
Relationships
As one can imagine, Wayleen has very few relations, being the lone wanderer that he is. Among the divine there are very few that Wayleen desire any sort of interaction with, much less a relationship. With that said however, he does enjoy the company of Serra. His lively nature and beautiful music is good company on Wayleen's journeys.
Wayleen's divine form.  

    The road is long and ever-winding. If one wishes it, they can see it wind into a circle.
    — Wayleen, God of Travel.

Wayleen, God of Travel is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a compass with four needles, each one pointing in a different latitude. His divine realm is the Outlands.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Neutral
Parents
Arbaraen
Heyla

title: Xaner
content:
Xaner
A document detailing Xaner, God of Debauchery.

    Xaner is the God of Debauchery. He is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, representing revelry and party. He is often referred to as the Life of the Party by his followers for his lively nature. He is a being of unbridled joy and without self-control.

 
Description
Xaner's divine form is not the most recognizable, for good reason. His divine form is that of a sculpted elven man with light brown skin. Many different tatoos of red and black cover the entirety of his arms and most of his remaining skin. Enough amulets hang around his neck that his walking causes them to rhythmically clang together.   His ripped white pants are way past their normal lifetime, now with far more holes than they first had. A belt of red cloth strap them to his waist, the color matching the red armbands he wears. A leather vest with a white, puffy collar is all that he wears, clearly showing off his impressive physique.   His brown hair is scraggly and long, often covering the tatoos on his forehead. His pointy elven ears are pierced and the metal hoops that hang from them are only two of the many rings upon his person. Finally, even though it is technically not part of him, he is so seldom seen without an ale in his hand that it may as well be considered a part of his divine form.  
Personality
Xaner is a very easy deity to understand. If one understands the concepts of revelry and debauchery, they have understood Xaner. He is an endless source of drunken joy, terrible ideas, bad breath and unbelievable stories. A mortal in the presence of Xaner would surely walk away with enough drunken tales for a lifetime.   Xaner is a curiousity among the deites. He is one that recognizes that he, just like the mortals that worship him, was once birthed. Though most gods considers themselves beyond that concept due to it's close connection to death, Xaner does not. He is fully aware that he is immortal and, despite that, he considers each day a gift since "you only get one life".   Xaner is a Chaotic Neutral deity that cares little for anything beyond a good time and personal freedom. His extent of helping people includes handing them some strong liquor and seeing where the night takes them.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Xaner are often younger people who have a greater zest for life than those who have lived a bit longer. Students and drunkards are the primary demographic of Xaner's followers. Fortunately for him, there are enough of them in Amenor that he can consider himself a Lesser Deity.   Due to Xaner's rather unruly nature, there are very few temples in Amenor that hold a shrine to him. Those that did often learned their lesson as drunken teenagers stumbled into the temple, spilling beer on the other people who were trying to pray to calmer gods.  
History
Xaner is not one to partake in great debates or historical conflicts. As such, he has had little impact on the major historical events of Amenor. With that said however, you can be very certain that Xaner found himself at whatever celebration was held to commence the end of such strife.  
Relationships
As the God of Debauchery, Xaner spends far more time meddling in mortal affairs than he does socializing with other gods. Most deities also dislike Xaner for his unserious nature and drunken demeanor. There are two exceptions to this however: Dilora, who often gets him back on his feet after a long bender as well as Velissara with whom he has a passionate relationship.
Xaner's divine form.  

    There's no such thing as "one too many"!
    — Xaner, God of Debauchery.

Xaner, God of Debauchery is one of the Lesser Deities. His holy symbol is that of a frothing mug of ale. His divine realm is Reeve.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Neutral

title: Yllí
content:
Yllí
A document detailing Yllí, Goddess of Children and Pranks.

    Yllí is the Goddess of Children and Pranks. She is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Chandryl's and Draktyl's essences. She is often referred to as Aunt Fey, due to her silly nature. She is a being of endless joy and entertainment.

 
Description
Ylli's divine form is easy to recognize, though it often is much harder to see. Firstly, her divine form is the size of a pixie and is thus quite tiny. Secondly, she often flattens herself into nearly two dimensions, appearing as nothing more than a simple sticker or painting, before popping out as herself.   Her tiny divine form is that of a pixie with orange hue. Orange, red and yellow are all the colors that make up her appearance, only her light skin breaks that combination. Her orange locks are dotted with red and yellow roses and her pale face is colored with red eye shadow.   She wears a long dress of the same color as her wings. It fades off into a collection of autumn flowers and leaves that leave a sparkling trail behind her as she flies. Very rarely is she ever seen without a smile on her face, always bringing joy wherever she goes.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Children and Pranks, Yllí often acts as the children she so deeply loves. She is temperamental and quick to anger, but rarely strikes back hard. Her tantrums are often just to prove a point, never to actually injure or oppose anyone.   She is deeply fond of pranks and laughter, specifically that most innocent of laughs from newborn children. Her pranks are chaotic but harmless and always made in good spirit. Above all, Yllí enjoys spreading joy and happiness wherever she goes, especially to mortal children.   Yllí is a Chaotic Good deity, one that cares about little else other than her domain. She intends only to spread joy through chaos. If she finds someone who dislikes her or her pranks, she quickly moves on to somewhere she would be more appreciated. Like a true child, she only seeks happiness and does not care for any deeper conversations or significant events.  
Worshippers
Yllí's chaotic but endearing teachings has given her a good number of followers. People who work with children, such as teacher, babysitters and others are often worshippers of Yllí. There are however also people who simply follow her faith due to them liking happiness and not seeking such rowdy company as Xaner.  
History
Much like Soyal, Yllí stays away from confrontation and conflict. As such, she has not been involved in any major events throughout Amenor's history. When such conflict spills over onto Amenor and affects mortals directly however, it is a safe bet that one can find Yllí amid those affected, cheering up the children.  
Relationships
Yllí is a unique deity in that her domain makes less serious than most other deities. Her childlike nature has made many evil deities try to take advantage of any gullible actions. Unfortunately for them however, despite her juvenile nature, she is no fool. Still though, even the good deities that she respects tends to look down on her a little bit, not quite seeing her as a peer, even if they do like her.
Yllí's divine form.  

    Every child is just a few laughs away from undying love!
    — Yllí, Goddess of Children and Pranks.

Yllí, Goddess of Children and Pranks is one of the Lesser Deities. Her holy symbol is that of small child with fairy wings, wrapped in a blanket. Her divine realm is the Feywild.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Parents
Chandryl
Draktyl

title: Zeschla
content:
Zeschla
A document detailing Zeschla, Goddess of Fear, Despair and Spiders.

    Zeschla is the Goddess of Fear, Despair and Spiders. She is one of the Lesser Deities of Amenor, created by the merging of Draktyl's and Gordfein's essences. She is often referred to as Webmother by her followers due to her intrinsic connection to spiders. She is a being of endless emotional torture and the matron deity of Shades.

 
Description
Zeschla's divine form is one of the most recognizable among the deities of Amenor. Her form is incredibly large, standing almost ten meters tall. Though the form is that of a mostly humanoid woman, it is severely mutilated. Her clothing is black, thin and tattered. As she moves, it barely seems to cling to her, raising the question of whether it is actually cloth or simply the shadows that wrap around Zeschla.   Her head is a bulbous mass of many tiny and moving spiders. Cobweb stretches across all features of her face, only giving the outline of a humanoid face. Spikes pierce through her head, protruding both out of the top of her head and out from the underside of the mouth, piercing into the flesh just between the clavicles.   From her back protrudes ten large, black legs. More spikes protrude from the chitinous flesh of them and they are covered in a thick dark fur. Though her massive form has two humanoid legs, Zeschla most often moves by cracking her back and tiling her head an unnatural amount while she scitters forward using the spider legs on her back.  
Personality
As the Godess of Despair and Fear, Zeschla has only one goal, which is to inflict as much emotional torment as she possibly can. She thrives when others are terrified or hopeless. She takes great pleasure in controlling those lesser than her by infecting their mind with her horrors.   Zeschla uses her beloved spiders for much that she does. They are messengers, torturers, confidants, spies and much more for her. Though she simply loves their nature, the terror that they seem to inflict in many mortals is an added cherry on top.   Zeschla is a Lawful Evil deity. Through her well-known methods of control via fear and despair, she hopes to inflict as much torment as possible. In particular she enjoys toying with those that have no chance to resist her immense presence and tortuous fear.  
Worshippers
The worshippers of Zeschla are spread throughout Amenor but do very rarely announce themselves as such. Secret organizations and cults worship Zeschla without the knowledge of the authorities. Some of these people are ones who enjoy fear, both receiving and dealing it out. Others are mere bullies who like to control others through any means possible.   Beyond the normal humans that worship Zeschla, there are the Shades. A creation of hers that now exists outside her control, they are creatures of shadow, having most of their soul replaced by shadowy essence from the Shadowfell. Though the hearts and minds of Shades vary, most are nefarious creatures who openly worship their matron deity.  
History
Zeschla constantly seeks avenues to seep her terror into the Material Plane where there are so many minds ripe for the taking. Throughout history she has succeeded multiple times but has quickly been beaten back. During the fifth Era however, her spiders and fear managed to infest the Material Plane.   In an attempt to create more creatures that inspire fear and despair, Zeschla created the first Shade. Woven together by the essence of her own divine domain, The Shadowfell, they are maliscious and scheming creatures that are one with darkness. After their first creation and subsequent perfection, Zeschla managed to make them cross over into the Material Plane when her spiders infested it. Since then they have become autonomous creatures, but most of them still worship Zeschla fervently and enjoy inflicting fear and despair into others.  
Relationships
Zeschal despises the good deities of Amenor and avoids them at all costs. They are most often uncaring of her intimidating ways and they are certainly not easy to control, something that Zeschla hates. She has a strong relationships with the other nefarious deities, though especially with Uynga. The terror one feels at the end of her blades is something Zeschla admires, while Uynga admires Zeschla's methods of controlling others. They have a very strong, almost sisterly, bond.
Zeschla's divine form.  

    Everyone fears something different, but everyone fears something!
    —  Zeschla, Goddess of Fear, Despair and Spiders. 

Zeschla, Goddess of Fear, Despair and Spiders is one of the Lesser Deities. Her holy symbol is that of a spider, the four outermost legs cut off. Her divine realm is the Shadowfell.
Divine Classification
Lesser Deity
Alignment
Lawful Evil
Parents
Draktyl
Gordfein
Related Historical Events

    2816 ADD
    A Final Stand
    2452 ADD
    The Arachnid Infestation
    786 ADD
    The First Shade Is Born

title: Admyls
content:
Admyls
A document detailing Admyls and their history.

    Your past is a storied one. Born first as Warforged, you were nothing but robots at the whim of greater schemes. Then you lent protection to those who could not protect themselves.
