Now, you are no less of a person than all other races of Amenor, this is my decree! The Admyls of Amenor are under my wing.
    — Brilja, Goddess of Constructs and Inventions.

 
The First Birth
 
Al Dioerte Tynale
The initial origin of Admyls is one that can be traced all the way back to Taldarin, the First World. Throughout time, their manner of creation has changed a great deal but the idea has remained the same. The first Admyl was created by a high elf man named Al Dioerte Tynale.   He was a powerful artificer of the Athar, the greatest magical institution in Taldarin. For all his power though, he was equally ambitious and spiteful. He saw the possibility to create mechanical bodies for people whose regular bodies were failing. Together with a colleague named Cayel, he spent many years creating the first mechanical body.  
The Transfer
Though Cayel and Al Dioerte would succeed in creating a first prototype for a mechanical host, the matter of transferring the person into the body still remained. Al Dioerte, blinded by ambition, desired to use necromantic magic to essentially trap the soul into the new body. Cayel disapproved of this idea, advocating further research to find a safer approach.   Al Dioerte had no intention of waiting however. One day, he would ambush and betray his partner, performing the necromantic magic on him without his approval. Al Dioerte essentially ripped the soul out of Cayel's human body and inserted it into the robotic body. To Al Dioerte's delight, it worked wonders.   Cayel quickly gained control of his new body and, in his distraught state, fled. Al Dioerte considered it a success and left the Athar before his deeds would be found out, establishing a research lab in the northwestern reaches of Taldarin.  
The Daughter
While Al Dioerte's actions may seem harsh, he considered himself to have a good reason. His spiteful and venomous heart had been softened by the passing of his wife. When she died, he was left as the sole caretaker of their daughter, a person he had paid little attention to before. As her tears fell during her mothers funeral, she sought solace in her father embrace and it warmed his heart a great deal.   Al Dyra Tynale was her name, and in the years following her mothers death, she herself would fall ill. She was doomed to live no longer than 15 winters and so Al Dioerte's ambition for creating robotic life had motivation. With what he had learned from Cayel's experiment, he was able to transfer his daughters soul from her dying form into a new metallic body and save her life.  
Warforged
After that, months passed by with Al Dioerte and Al Dyra spending time together creating more constructs. With their great minds combined, they could make many new steel bodies, having the ultimate goal of saving others whose normal bodies were failing them.   It is never that easy however. Al Dioerte saw this for what it was: the greatest scientific breakthrough of all time. They were already tucked away in the snowy reaches of Taldarin, but he would not risk anything to have someone take his research for him. As such, he needed guards but in order to activate his new constructs, they needed souls.   After managing to convince his daughter, the two of them ventured to the cities and gathered up a few sick, ill and dying. They brought them back to their island and used the necromantic magic to bind them to the constructs that obeyed Al Dioerte's every command. And so, these creatures were born with the sole purpose of protecting research and destroying any who would interfere. Though their soul was inside, it had no influence on the actions of the body. Nothing of the original person remained. Al Dioerte named them aptly: Warforged.  
Ambitions
Al Dioerte and Al Dyra continued their work, making more and more Warforged. Over time, Al Dioerte's ambitions grew. His Warforged could provide a body for those who needed it, and their combat prowess could keep cities safe in times of worry.   These ambitions drew him away even from his own work and to that of a gnome and dwarf in the nearby capital of Jerun. Two men there, Barry Longfeet and Admyl Drunkkind had created a new weapon: a musket. Al Dioerte saw it as the perfect tool for his Warforged to have and so he desired it greatly. He sent his daughter to steal the blueprints from Admyl's home and though she succeeded, this would eventually prove to be the end of Al Dioerte's machinations.  
The Marked Ones
As it turned out, Barry Longfeet came to be a part of the Marked Ones. When he heard of the theft of the blueprint, he brought his friend back home with him to investigate. After a month of two of other pressing matters attended to, the Marked Ones would find Al Dioerte's hideout with their airship.   Al Dioerte had now kidnapped Admyl Drunkkind since he was having trouble understanding the blueprints. The Marked Septed came to his aid and confronted the mad inventor. In the confrontation, the airship would be destroyed and Admyl's life would be lost but Al Dioerte and his daughter would be stopped.   With the threat of Darkheart looming, the Marked Ones turned their attention elsewhere. Months passed but eventually they would defeat this great enemy also and Barry would be able to continue the work on the Warforged. While the Nine crafted Amenor, Barry would continue the work of the Warforged.  
The Second Birth
 
The First Admyl
Though he based his work on the blueprints of Al Dioerte, Barry would make many alterations. Like the high elf before him, he sought to provide an army that could protect, but he did need to work with any souls. Together with Draktyl and Dwandrin, he created the first metallic constructs that could act as a protection force.   Though his first model was tampered with by a jealous inventor, it would prove to be nothing but a small bump in the road. Eventually Barry would create many of these construct as well as imbue them with magic. He created eight different types of them, one for each school of magic. This introduced the first difference between the robots though they still had no sentience.   In an act of remembrance and respect, Barry named his creations Artificially Disciplined Mechanical Yearning Lightbringers, or Admyl.  
The Third Birth
 
Sentience
Barry's work had improved the lives of many and his Admyls were strong, obedient and protective. During his life, the great inventor made a few more than a hundred of these and maintained those that already existed. Through his membership in the Gnomish Love Machines Guild (GLMG), he also taught many of his colleagues of the creation and maintenance of the Admyls, ensuring that this great creation would live on after his own passing.   Before Barry died however, he had one unusual idea. Each Admyl was imbued with a different school of magic, but what would happen if one tried to imbue the same body with the all the schools of magic at the same time? It was an experiment that he carried out towards the end of his life, in the company of most of the Marked Ones once again.   To Barry's great surprise, fusing all schools of magic into a particularly strong frame made it rise and speak within seconds of its birth. It addressed it's creator and pondered the state of its existence. After a long conversation and investigation, it was confirmed beyond any doubt that Barry had just created the first sentient Admyl. This happens in 258 ADD of the Era of Longevity. Barry Longfeet passes peacefully some years later, in 15 ADD of the Era of Growth.  
Growth
After the gained sentience, the newborn Admyl names itself Tara and spends many months conversing with Barry and others to learn the state of things. It learns of its own nature, of life and of many other things. While its mind may have been created fully formed, it was still empty upon creation.   After some time, Tara bids Barry farewell and starts to wander Amenor to learn things that cannot be taught through conversation. It wanders for more than 13 years before it considers its learning completed for the moment.   It concludes that sentience is a great gift, one that should be shared and enjoyed together. It has made a decision: it should not be the only sentient Admyl. For the next twenty years, it wanders the world and grants sentience to most Admyls, as it was taught by Barry. Some Admyls find their new life far too strange to comprehend and leave, just like Tara did itself. Some it cannot approach as it is under the ownership of others that do not wish for it to gain sentience.   In the end however, Tara and 72 other sentient Admyls now exist upon Amenor. They find a little piece of Amenor to claim for themselves and begin construction on their new home as they claim themselves as a new people.  
Souls
Now existing as just another species of sentient creatues upon Amenor, Admyls live their own life just like all other species do. They mostly keep to themselves in their part of Amenor and interact very little with other species.   Over time however, their inability to age means that their numbers are always growing, even if it be slowly. Eventually Admyls begin to make their way into the rest of Amenor, searching for homes, work and lives in other settlements than the one that their people have created.   This does not go over well with most other people however. The origin of Admyls are well known and many people simply see them as robots. The fact that Tara took many of these construct fighters away in a time of war is also seen as an insult to the worth of a "normal" life.   Admyls, created by the merging of all the schools of magic are technically soulless beings. Should their body perish, they are simply destroyed and nothing moves onto the afterlife. This is known by the species with souls and this difference is enough to make the Admyls unwelcome in most places.   This injustice and obvious racism does not go unnoticed by Brilja who, in 262 ADD of the Era of Growth makes the declaration that she is the patron deity of Admyls and that they should be treated with as much respect as all other races are.  
Today
Today Admyls are widely seen as just another species that shares Amenor and its bounties. With that said however, there are many who still consider Admyls strange or freaks due to their lack of a soul. Some belive that without a soul, one cannot have reason or compassion.   It would be somewhat easy to disprove this however. One would only need to look at Tara, the first sentient Admyl and her wise mind and compassionate nature. Alas, the world is not that simple.


Scientific Name
Artificially Disciplined Mechanical Yearning Lightbringers
Lifespan
No theoretical maximum, though the body gets slower over time
Average Height
1.9 m
Average Weight
135 kg
Related Historical Events

    1890 ADD
    The Exsanguination
    482 ADD
    Admyls Become Sentient
    468 ADD
    The First Sentient Admyl
    1 ADD
    The Creation of the First Admyl

title: Shades
content:
Shades
A document detailing Shades and their history.

    Shades are a unique type of creature, initially created by Zeschla, that can be found both in the Material Plane and in the Shadowfell. They are creatures who lack or have substituted most of a soul for shadow essence that ties them directly to the Shadowfell. They maintain their own agency and thoughts but tend to be maliscious creatures. 

 
History
Back in the Era of Growth, it was not only the people of Amenor that would grow. Gods would expand their portfolios and increase their scheming against or for Amenor itself. A good example of this is when Brilja chose to take up the mantle of matron deity for the Admyls.   While every deity would take notice of this act of Brilja's, it would particularly resonate with Zeschla. She too desired to be the matron deity of a species, to have mortal creatures in eternal servitude to her. She would star working on a new species immedately and, after 22 years, she would finish.   In 284 ADD Era of Growth, Zeschla would create the first Shade. Woven together by shadowstuff from her divine realm, the Shadowfell, it was a mostly soulless creature. Zeschla, proud of her invention, made a few more Shades and then let this initial bunch loose in the Shadowfell.   The Shades wandered the dreadful lands and terrorized all they could find, inflicting fear and despair on the behalf of Zeschla. She had managed to create a perfectly subservient species that furthered her cause. Now, only the matter of bringing them to the Material Plane remained.   All divine beings of Amenor may do whatever they desire inside their own realm, but to affect the Material Plane is far harder to do. As such, it would take 1666 years before Zeschla had the opportunity to spread her Shades. From 510 to 528 ADD, Era of Divine Division, Zeschla managed to infest the Material Plane with her spiders and terrorize the people of Amenor. In the chaos, she took the opportunity to sneak her Shades onto the Material Plane. When she finally was beat back, she was still content, having achieved what she desired.   What Zeschla did not expect however, would be how the Shades changed when they finally left Zeschla's realm of influence. Most of them remained terror-inflicting creatures that serve Zeschla dutifully, but not all of them. The Material Plane changed some of them and made them much more amicable.  
Personality
Today Shades are considered a unique and rare species in Amenor. There are enough of them to make their personalities very varied. Their nefarious origin and connection to Zeschla is something that not all Shades acknowledge, striving instead to live a normal life like the other humanoid species.   There are mainly two types of Shades in Amenor today, divided by their birthplace. There are those who are born in the Shadowfell, either from a union of two Shades or Zeschla herself, known as Children of the Plane of Shadow. Then there are also those who are born in the Material Plane, from a union of at least one Shade, known as Dark Ancestry Children.  
Children of the Plane of Shadow
The children of the Plane of Shadow are almost all of a single mind. Born in the bleak and creeping shadowy landscape that is the Shadowfell, these Shades are devoted to Zeschla. In her domain, her control over them is complete and there are very few who dissent from her teachings, even fewer the even desire to do so.   These Shades are nefarious creatures that strive to inflict as much fear and despair as possible. They are brutal and ruthless, worshipping Zeschla with great devotion. They also usually work closely with Zeschla's spiders and they can often be seen using them as mounts.  
Dark Ancestry Children
The Dark Ancestry Children, born in Material Plane are a much more varied bunch than the Children of the Plane of Shadow. They have inherited their umbral legacy from one or two of their parents and they, like their parents, are not subject to Zeschla's influence while on the Material Plane.   This makes these Shades much like any other humanoid species, striving to live a normal life. With that said though, their origin and dangerous brothers and sisters often makes them the subject of much judgement, making a normal life much harder to lead.   It is not completely without reason that people judge and distrust these Shades either. For as much as they strive for a normal life, a part of their soul is woven with shadow essence from the Shadowfell. This manifests differently in Dark Ancestry Children, from everything like anger issues, dangerous profession to not at all.  
Appearance
The appearance of a Shade depends on the two ancestries that make up their lineage. A Shade that has a child with another species will always produce a Shade child, though with more of their soul disconnected from the Shadowfell. A Shade child born from the union of two Shades has more of their sould tied to the Shadowfell.   A child with parents of different species is known as Half-Souls to the Children of the Plane of Shadow, and the Dark Ancestry Children that considers that to be unnatural. A Half-Souls appearance is influenced by the other species of their parentage. They take on the features of whatever species is most prominent in their lineage.   As such, Shades are very varied in appearance. What is common for them though is their colors. Their skin tone ranges from gray to black, often growing darker as they get older. Their hair is typically devoid of colour, often white, gray or black and so are their eyes.  
Sacrament of Gloom
The creation of Shades is not entirely limited to the birth of Children of the Plane of Shadow or Dark Ancestry Children. There exists a dark ritual, the Sacrament of Gloom, that can turn any creature into a Shade.   This profane ritual is highly secretive and almost only available to those most devout to Zeschla. It is not uncommon that the most devout of her worshippers are allowed to undergo the ritual near their time of death, in order to take part of the halted aging of Shades and thus serve Zeschla eternally.


Lifespan
Unknown, as their aging stops after 400 years
Average Height
1.76 m
Average Weight
65 kg
Related Historical Events

    2452 ADD
    The Arachnid Infestation

title: Titles
content:
Titles
A document detailing the various titles one can encounter in Amenor.

    Come now boy. Soon you will learn the way of this wicked world, where your title matters more than your true experience.
    — A wisened old man imparting wisdom.

 
Avelar
Avelar is a title given to the members of the Avalon Council, the ruling council of Qanya. These are the people who rule Qanya and seek to make it prosper. There are officially only six of these titles in all of Amenor.  
Dragonhead
This is the title for the members of the Dragonheart Council, the ruling council of Elezari. These are the people who rule Elezari and seek to make it prosper. There are officially only six of these titles in all of Amenor.  
Eldest
This title is held by the leader of the Admyls, Tara. It is the only person in all of Amenor with this title, at least the only person whose title is officially recognized by the region they reside in.  
Frostbaron/baroness
The tile of frostbaron or frostbaroness is held by the ruler of Dar'Cortya. They are the singular ruler of this region, with the title clearly describing its biting cold. There is officially only one of this title in all of Amenor.  
King/Queen
The tile of king or queen is very self-explanatory. It is the singular ruler of a kingdom or queendom, the highest voice of authority in such a region. There officially only two of these titles in all of Amenor, the king of Acadia and the queen of Neolora.  
Lord/Lady
The title of lord and lady is a symbol of status, power and money. It is taken by those who have enough wealth to own land, employ servants and hire others for menial tasks. Many successful businesses are often owned by a lord or lady who owes their title to the success of their company. While the title officially comes with no political power, it still brings a lot of status and is significant in many personal interactions.  
Mayor
The title of mayor is somewhat self-explanatory. Most cities have a person who is in charge, doing their best to ensure the prosperity of the town and this is the mayor. While not every city has such a person, they are very common and are almost always democratically elected. Furthermore, while they have final say on matters regarding the city, they have a group of people who assist them in ruling.  
Plaguebreaker
This is the title of the ruler of The Grayhold. The origin of the title comes from the unfortunate history of the region and is seen as an honorific title that can turn things back to the way it used to be. This title is officially only held by one person in all of Amenor.  
Townsmaster
The title of townsmaster is rather self-explanatory. Most towns have a person who is in charge, doing their best to ensure the prosperity of the town. While not every town has such a person, they are very common and are almost always democratically elected. Furthermore, while they have final say on matters regarding the town, they have a group of people who assist them in ruling.  
Organizations
Many of Amenor's organizations have different titles for the ranks of a their members. These are described in their relevant documents.  
Wavetamer
The title of Wavetamer belongs to the ruler of Oriceré. The title refers to a persons ability to conquers the seas and tame the waves, the ever-shifting politics and dynamics of the world. This title, while it often trades hands, is officially only held by one person in all of Amenor.

    Why man, he doth bestride the narrow world like a colossus, and we petty men walk under his huge legs and peep about to find ourselves dishonorable graves.
    — A poets description of a loved king.

title: Arbaraen
content:
Arbaraen
A document detailing Arbaraen, Goddess of Life.

    Arbaraen is the Goddess of Life and Aasimars. She is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. She is often referred to as Mother due to her nurturing nature. She is a being of radiant virtue, one that emanates an absolute love and lust for life.

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Arbaraen. When she does manifest her divine form however, it is a recognizable sight.   Arbaraen's physical form is that of a tall and young Aasimar woman. Her skin is the color of the rising sun and a permanent radiance shines from it, illuminating all the shadows in her presence. Her hair is long and blonde, falling freely like a veil far beneath her waist. She wears a beautiful white, flowing summers dress that seems to almost meld with the ground as she walks.   Her eyes are a deep golden color, her mouth always wearing a warm smile. Those who lay their eyes upon her soft face could not help but smile brightly. Wherever Arbaraen walks, the soft tune of a plucking harp seems to follow. Arbaraen's physical form is the divine embodiment of beauty.  
Personality
Arbaraen is a fierce protector of everything that lives, a Lawful Good deity. Every life should have the opportunity to enjoy the beautiful journey of being alive. She cherishes all that is to love in life. The birth of a baby, newfound love, rekindled love, found purpose and appreciation are only a few of the things that Arbaraen stands for. Arbaraen believes that all life is of equal worth, every living creature should be treated with the same kindness. No matter the faults or failures of a life, all lives are sacred.   Arbaraen loves the journey of a life, from birth to growth through strife, to contentment and ultimately death. It is her belief that all life should be given the chance to partake in these beautiful and pivotal moments. She is aware of the time that each life is alloted and all the importants moment of it and she follows these stories closely. Arbaraen is not opposed to death, on the contrary. Death is not the ending of a life, but a necessary part of it, an equally important part of the journey of life.   Arbaraen bitterly despises any who oppose this natural journey of life. Those who seek the means of extending their lives beyond the time that they have been given are the worst sinners in her eyes. As such, necromancers, liches and the like are eternal enemies of Arbaraen's.

    The beauty of life is a gift.
    —  Arbaraen, Goddess of Life.

Arbaraen, Goddess of Life and Aasimar is one of the nine creator deities. Her holy symbol is an intricately carved silver decanter showing a group of people dancing and laughing. It is overflowing with swirling, crystal-clear water.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Lawful Good
Children

    Virá
    Therion & Liora
    Alora
    Mornal
    Soyal
    Presch
    Wayleen
    Libram

Related Myths

    The Creation of Amenor

Related Historical Events

    8 ADD
    The Commence

    Amenor is our gift to you. Live as many lives here as you desire.
    — Arbaraen, Goddess of Life.

title: Chandryl
content:
Chandryl
A document detailing Chandryl, Goddess of Chaos.

    Chandryl is the Goddess of Chaos and Changelings. She is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. She is often referred to as Unmaker due to her chaotic nature. She is a being of controlled chaos, one that revels in strife.

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Chandryl. Even when Chandryl takes physical form, it is rarely ever the same as she has taken before. In her chaotic nature, Chandryl always takes a different physical form when she manifests. However, her true divine form is always the same, even though it is incredibly rare to see.   Chandryl's divine form is something that most people would compare to an undead, strange-looking fortune teller. The form is femine, with an impossibly thin waist and spiky shoulder blades. The face is disfigured and lacks any normal facial features such as eyes or a mouth.   The skin is dark and charred, as if it has been eternally burned. Most of the skin is covered in an elaborate dress of dark blues and purple however. Golden details adorn different parts of the dress, erratically placed upon it's soft silk. The arms end not in fingers, but a sprout of solified energy, almost like a spread-out nervous system.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Chaos, Chandryl endorses one way of life: controlled chaos. It's important to make a distinction between chaos and controlled chaos. While Chandryl enjoys senseless chaos and unpredictability, it is no more than a mere distraction for her. What matters in a life is controlled chaos.   Controlled chaos, in Chandryl's eyes, is the result of a well-developed mind and it's product is a toughened creature. Chandryl sees chaos as an inevitable consequence of the complicated process that is life and she believes that those that can handle it become stronger and smarter because of it.   Chandryl is a Chaotic Good Godess that uses chaos and strife in order to force creatures to adapt. Though it may be to their detriment at the moment, in the end, that chaos makes them a superior creature to one that never has to overcome the chaos.
Chandryl's divine form.  

    In chaos, there is growth.
    — Chandryl, Goddess of Chaos.

Chandryl, Goddess of Chaos and Changelings is one of the nine creator deities. Her holy symbol is a three-eyed skull of a humanoid. The third eye is placed in the center of the forehead and, unlike the other two, contains a purple eye with an elongated pupil.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Children

    Mae
    Elyzari
    Ventus
    Serra
    Yllí
    Ronashand
    Presch
    Puzkera

Related Myths

    The Creation of Amenor

Related Historical Events

    8 ADD
    The Commence

    Amenor is our gift to you. Unleash havoc upon it!
    — Chandryl, Goddess of Chaos.

title: Draktyl
content:
Draktyl
A document detailing Draktyl, God of Justice.

    Draktyl is the God of Justice and Dragonborns. He is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. He is often referred to as Judicator due to his justice-dealing nature. He is a being of absolute justice, doling out punishment for even the smallest crime.

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Draktyl. When he does manifest his divine form however, it is a recognizable sight.   Draktyl's divine form is that of an armor-clad soldier. An incredibly detailed armor of plate, it's edges lined with shining gold, covers almost all of his form. A set of spiky gauntlets covers his hands and with them he wields a golden longsword, at all times. The sword seems to almost shimmer in the light, as if the blade itself is only partly real. The crossguard holds carvings of a set of balanced scales.   Draktyl's face is eternally covered by a hood or helm, no visible features at all. From the back of the muscular body protrudes two massive wings of white. Their feathers are large and tightly clustered, forming the two wings that block out the light as Draktyl hovers above you. His mere presence seemed to cause your mind to drift to your worst sins.  
Personality
As the God of Justice, Draktyl is a relentless avenger for all misdeeds. It was he who drafted the first laws upon Taldarin, the First World and he enforced them with extreme justice. No crime or mistake was too small for Draktyl to pursue.   It was very rare that Draktyl ever let a transgression go. As long as it had inconvenienced or troubled another, Draktyl was sure to deliver vengeance. Draktyl believes that the only way that mortals can coexist is by following a strict set of laws. He believes that if such a framework is not in place, mortals will descend into chaos and anarchy.
Draktyl's divine form.  

    Justice is a neverending task.
    — Draktyl, God of Justice.

Draktyl, God of Justice and Dragonborns is one of the nine Creator Deities. His holy symbol is a set of golden scales, connected with the finest string, perfectly balanced.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Children

    Ze'Tren
    Dar'Ishir
    Velyndra & Vortharax
    Kannder
    Mornal
    Yllí
    Terra
    Zeschla
    Cazerni

Related Myths

    The Creation of Amenor

Related Historical Events

    8 ADD
    The Commence

    Amenor is our gift to you. Ensure that all lives there are lived justly.
    — Draktyl, God of Justice.

title: Dwandrin
content:
Dwandrin
A document detailing Dwandrin, God of Creation.

    Dwandrin is the God of Creation and Dwarves. He is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. He is often referred to as Forgemaster for his creative nature. He is a being with an absolute mastery over all things crafted. 

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Dwandrin. When he does manifest his divine form however, it is a recognizable sight.   Dwandrin's divine form is that of a massive dwarf, standing just above two meters tall. Despite his height, he retains the dwarven quality of being considerably wide for his height. He is a mountain of muscles, every part of his body a bulging muscle, covered in sweat from the forge.   A large braided beard of a dark orange color covers most of his upper body. He wears nothing more than a pair of a thick leather gloves, eternally darkened by the soot of the forge, and a pair of thick leather pants that are held up by a pair of braces strapped over his massive shoulders.   Wherever he strode, he carried around a forge hammer in his left hand. The soot and smell of burning steel and iron seemed to follow him wherever he went.  
Personality
As the God of Creation, Dwandrin is an instatiable creator. He enjoys the crafting of everything from the smallest jewelry to the most elaborate building. It was he who first crafted the Paragons with which the Marked Ones managed to defeat Darkheart.   Not only does Dwandrin enjoy the art of crafting and creating, he also enjoys watching others create. The crafting of civilizations and cities is foremost pride of mortals, according to Dwandrin. He could find beauty in every craft, in every creation.   Dwandrin bitterly despises destruction of almost any kind. The necessity of smelting crafts in order to create something new, something improved is something that he approves of. However, any other kind of destruction, especially wanton destruction, caused the hammer of the forge to ring loudly.
Dwandrin's divine form.  

    The forge never sleeps.
    — Dwandrin, God of Creation.

Dwandrin, God of Creation and Dwarves is one of the nine Creator Deities. His holy symbol is a smoldering hammer, help up by a pair of standing iron tongs, smoke emanating from the metallic connection.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Lawful Good
Children

    Uynga
    Fórein
    Therion & Liora
    Brilja
    Nechlar
    Serra
    Cazerni
    Dilora
    Ignis

Related Myths

    The Creation of Amenor

Related Historical Events

    8 ADD
    The Commence

    Amenor is out gift to you. Create all that your heart desires here.
    — Dwandrin, God of Creation.

title: Elfyrean
content:
Elfyrean
A document detailing Elfyrean, Goddess of Nature.

    Elfyrean is the Goddess of Nature and Elves. She is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. She if often referred to as Bloom due to her connection to nature. She is a being of absolute connection and love to the land.

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Elfyrean. When she does manifest her divine form however, it is a recognizable sight.   Elfyrean's divine form is that of the most beautiful elven woman that a mortal could image. She is eternally young and slender, her long and curly bown hair falls down to the midpoint of her legs. She always wears a crown of pink and white flowers atop her head and the flowers almost seem to move softly in the crown.   Flower-sprinkled vines grow along her arms, forming a sort of natural bracers. She wears a long, flowing green dress that seems to be held up by the ground that she walks on. It is intricately decorated with beautiful patterns of leaves and bushes. With her mere presence seemed to come a soft and invigorating breeze that blew around her.  
Personality
As the Goddess of Nature, Elfyrean is a fierce protector of natural life and environments. While she understands the necessity of cities and is not opposed to it, they must be built with the surrounding environment in mind. Nature provides so much for those that live in it and, according to her, it is the job of a mortal to provide back to nature.   Though Elfyrean has a special love for the forests, all environments are under her protection. Everything from vast ice-fields to murky swamps is an important and necessary part of nature that is to be cherished. Those that would seek to abuse or corrupt the natural order and way of things are eternal enemies of Elfyrean's.
Elfyrean's divine form.  

    All beauty in the world stems from nature.
    — Elfyrean, Goddess of Nature.

Elfyrean, Goddess of Nature and Elves is one of the nine Creator Deities. Her holy symbol is a beautifully blooming white flower.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Children

    Alora
    Obiterra

    Qandezar

    Ignis

Related Historical Events

    8 ADD

    Amenor is our gift to you. Make it bloom!
    — Elfyrean, Goddess of Nature.

title: Gordfein
content:
Gordfein
A document detailing Gordfein, God of Magic.

    Gordfein is the God of Magic and Gnomes. He is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. He is often referred to as Weavemaker due to his intrinsic connection to magic. He is a being of absolute connection with magic and the Weave. 

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Gordfein. When he does manifest his divine form however, it is a recognizable sight.   Gordfein's divine form is that of the grandest mage one could imagine. He wears a grandiose robe of white, blue and gold at all times. The edges of it transition into a dark blue while details of gold run up and down the robe, making it shimmer in the light. A beautiful chestplate of gold is fastened onto the robe, rising like branches on either side of the head.   The head is always hidden underneath a golden mask of an incredible sheen. With a permanent stoic impression, the golden mask and it's starry eyes give complement the robe perfectly. Every movement that Gordfein takes appears slow, as mutliple ephereal images follows every little stride he takes.  
Personality
As the God of Magic, Gordfein has an absolute control over the Weave and all magic. He regards all life with curiosity but those who have the ability to twist the Weave to their use are the ones he respects. No matter the school of magic, everything from Abjuration to Necromancy are noble pursuits of knowledge in Gordfein's mind.   Gordfein encourages the pursuit of magic in all aspects. Whether it be a young student attempting to understand the incantations of a Magic Missile spell or an Archmage attempting to create an entirely new spell, they are both allies of Gordfein's.
Gordfein's divine form.  

    With the Weave, anything is possible.
    — Gordfein, God of Magic.

Gordfein, God of Magic and Gnomes is one of the nine Creator Deities. His holy symbol is a golden mask without a mouth or nose, where the eyes have been replaced by two glimmering stars.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Neutral
Children

Related Historical Events

    Amenor is our gift to you. Make magic within it!
    — Gordfein, God of Magic.

title: Heyla
content:
Heyla
A document detailing Heyla, Goddess of Luck.

    Heyla is the Goddess of Luck and Halflings. She is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. She is often referred to as Lady Luck due to her lucky nature. She is a being that has absolute control over that which no one can control.

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Heyla. When she does manifest her divine form however, it tends to not be that recognizable of a sight. Heyla enjoys taking the shape of an unassuming Halfing, man or woman. Her divine form however, is anything but unassuming.   Heyla's divine form is that of a beautiful young Halfling woman. She wears a long dress made of enough bright and screaming colors that it constitutes a rainbow. Her hair is long, curly and pink, covering most of her rotund body. On her head she wears a crown decorated with many colorful gems, matching her colorful dress.   No matter where Heyla goes, she always wears a big grin on her face, like that of a child who had just received candy after a long wait. The soft sound of a plucking lute seems to always be heard in her presence as well.  
Personality
As the God of Luck, Heyla enjoys most things in life that entail some uncertainty. Games of skill or chance are her favorites but she also enjoys some grander things in life such as stories of love.   Heyla is a being that has absolute control over that which no one else can control. Things like chance and risk that a normal person has to undergo is something that she never has to concern herself with as she is always able to choose her desired outcome.
Heyla's divine form.  

    A little bit of luck goes a long way.
    — Heyla, Goddess of Luck.

Heyla, Goddess of Luck and Halfings is one of the nine Creator Deities. Her holy symbol is a golden, five-sided clover that lacks a stalk.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Chaotic Good
Children

Related Historical Events

    Amenor is our gift to you. You must feel rather lucky, right?
    — Heyla, Goddess of Luck.

title: Huwander
content:
Huwander
A document detailing Huwander, God of Time.

    Huwander is the God of Time and Humans. He is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. He is often referred to as Father Time due to his intrinsic connection to time. He is a being with complete control over the flow of time.

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Huwander. In fact, there are no remaining reports on the divine form of Huwander.   Some believe him to have no such form, instead being one with the flow of time. Some take the speculation even further, claiming that he has no divine form because he is time itself. Some stand by the fact that all the Creator Deities have a divine form should they need it, but that Huwander has never used his.   No matter what the truth of Huwander's divine form is, there still exists many attempts at rendering it. The image to the right is one of the most common themes, that of a large and grandiose God, one that would be outside the range of fathom for most mortals without a visual aid.  
Personality
As the God of Time, Huwander has a hand in most things that happen. As almost all actions and events are subject to the flow of time, Huwander is attached to almost all things that transpire on Amenor.   He is a being of absolute control over time which makes his existence much more nebulous than the other Creator Deities. The fact that he has never appeared in his divine form has also made the accounts of his personality far fewer. Simply put, little is known about Huwander.
Huwander's divine form.  

    Given enough time, anything is possible.
    — Huwander, God of Time.

Huwander, God of Time and Humans is one of the nine Creator Deities. His holy symbol is a golden stopwatch where the second arm has ticked a singular second from the top.
Children

    Ze'Tren
    Perreni
    Eladrey
    Cáhlid
    Elyzari
    Velissara
    Brilja
    Qandezar
    Libram

Related Myths

    The Creation of Amenor

Related Historical Events

    8 ADD
    The Commence

    Amenor is our gift to you. We hope that you will spend much time here.
    — Huwander, God of Time.

title: Tyrabane
content:
Tyrabane
A document detailing Tyrabane, God of Death.

    Tyrabane is the God of Death and Tieflings. He is one of the nine Creator Deities that made both Taldarin and Amenor. He is often referred to as Reaper due to his intrinsic connection to death. He is a being with absolute control over, and an absolute connection to, death.

 
Description
It is very rare that any of the Creator Deities take physical form and manifest on Amenor, a fact that includes Tyrabane. When he does manifest his divine form however, it is a recognizable sight.   Tyrabane wears the old divine form of Darkheart, the creature whose death ushered in the creation of Amenor. It is a dark form, the skeleton of a massive humanoid held up by many overlapping dark wings. The bones are broken and shattered in many places, the jaw hangs loose and the massive eye-holes are vacant.   The shadows that eternally flit around the divine form create six wings of dark energy with which Tyrabane moves. Wherever he flew, the cold touch of death seems to follow him. Tyrabane took up the divine form of Darkheart upon it's death as an extra measure of protection, a way to ensure that Darkheart's essence had no vessel to return to, should it ever do so.  
Personality
As the God of Death, Tyrabane has absolute control over all that is to do with death. He is aware of the ultimate fate and end of all creatures that walk Amenor. As almost all things eventually die, Tyrabane is connected to almost every creature on Amenor.   In the years of Taldarin, Tyrabane also had the task of acting as reaper and shepherd for all those that died. It was a task which he despised but as Darkheart was defeated, one of the champions of the Nine that defeated it took on this role, serving as a reaper under Tyrabane's guidance.
Tyrabane's divine form.  

    Death is a fate in which you all share.
    — Tyrabane, God of Death. 

Tyrabane, God of Death and Tieflings is one of the nine Creator Deities. His holy symbol is a skeletal hand clutching a wilting rose, the petals turned black.
Divine Classification
Creator Deity
Alignment
Lawful Neutral
Children

    Uynga

    Dar'Ishir
    Raschal

Related Historical Events

    The Commence

    Amenor is our gift to you. May it die only when you do.
    — Tyrabane, God of Death.
